Browser lacks capabilities required to play.

Upgrade or switch to another browser.

Loading…
You walk into the modest abode, freed from any cargo, as most of what you’ll need for your future duties has already been relocated there. You can't believe the day has finally come! You'd like to say that day after day of bending over backward, studying, training, and a significant amount of ass-kissing has <b>finally</b> paid off, but while that… perhaps had something to do with it, you also know the main reason of your promotion is the disappeareance of Elisa, leaving a little void. Because sure, you are not an awful hunter by any means but you are certainly not ready to face the formidable horrors that Lauren is probably expecting you to all alone. You will totally not regret your poor life choices when a demon twice your size shoves your head up your own rectum, no. No way, no how. You finally have a place to call home, a place from which you'll be able to act independently, like one of the big names that came before you! Sure, it's not very big, and the pocket dimension granted to you it's... far from welcoming; a small island floating in the middle of the vast interplanar void, a desolate landscape that stretches out in all directions full of floaty rubble and ruin. But hey, it's yours nonetheless! And from here, you’ll be able to carry out your own hunts… and to choose your contracts yourself, instead of the ones Damlon so considerately forced onto you. And boy do you have some ideas for that… The travelstones The Eye produces are more often than not completely flawed, leading to harmless creatures trying to live their lives. Cute things that can't possibly pose a threat to humanity in any significant way, just living their lives when a random dude wrapped in elaborate robes and wielding arcane weapons steps out of the shadows and ends their existence in a quick display of wanton, spectacular violence… before disappearing again. Hell, some of them inhabit worlds where humans are not even a thing, and no chance they would be able to stand a planar shift, even if they managed to figure out how to. Its a grim business, but one that you believed had to be done... but lately, you discovered that these gems don't actually work the way you thought they did. They don't require you to kill the creature, as they have no way to know if the target its dead or alive. They just change color once they come close enough to your prey, and your order assumes that, if the hunter came back after being close to one of these monsters, then the deed had to be done. Which is perfect for you. Because, over the years, you began to develop… something quite contrary to everything your order had so ceaselessly tried to indoctrinate you into believing. The beings that have long been regarded as evil horrors, taboo to even think in any way other than dead, somehow got warped into the subjects of your fantasies. You lamented the workings of your mind, for where others saw only monsters, you saw exquisite creatures with which… you absolutely wouldn't mind spending some... <b>intimate</b> time with. Instead of a fearsome harpy, a fluffy thing to cuddle with, instead of a mighty dragon, a nice, toothy maw to shoot your load all over. If any of the inner circle had even suspected this, you would have lost your life long ago, which is why you can't still wrap your head over the idea that they actually promoted you to High Hunter, giving you Elisa's old home, and the legendary Essence Feaster, the weapon that once belonged to Korvaax himself! Having already tested it out, you were pleasantly surprised to find out that the weapon seems to be tailor-made for you. The demon bound to the ominous onyx blade gives you the ability to manifest spectral chains that swiftly drain the vitality of any creature they come in contact with. An ideal tool for your wicked endeavors! With this, you won't have to shatter every bone in a monster's body before you can fuck it. You doubt many of them would be willing to have sexual intercourse with a random human coming out of nowhere... You absolutely cannot wait to start your new life as a High Hunter, now that you already have all that you need to cross the dimensions, and (s)lay as many of those adorable creatures as you see fit. Hell, you'll even get paid for it! Not that money matters too much in such a small community, but its a way to keep track of your success. Getting paid means getting work done for the order, after all. Where the others bring death, you will bring love! Perhaps even life... Aren't you such a saint? <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Check the interior |Intro 2]]</div>\ENDINGS and persistent data <<set $ending1 to 0>> <<set $ending2 to 0>> <<set $vaelNyxaStone to 0>> <<set $fromPortal to false>> <<set $cumNaafiri to 0>> <<set $dissapointedVael to false>> //combat <<set $currentMonsterHp to 120>> <<set $currentMonsterExhaustion to 0>> <<set $baseNightmareChance to 6>> <<set $dreamstoneChance to 20>> <<set $canSee to false>><<nobr>> <div class="iconWatcher"> <img src="Images/Other/WatcherIcon.png" width="70" height="60"> <span><strong>By ItThatWatches</strong></span> <hr class="separator-line"> </div> <div class="stat-container"> <img src="Images/Icons/Coins.png" width="60" height="60"> <span class="stat stat-value">$money</span> </div> <div class="stat-container"> <img src="Images/Icons/General.png" width="60" height="60"> <span class="stat stat-value">$hunterSkill</span> </div> <div class="stat-container"> <img src="Images/Icons/Heal.png" width="60" height="60"> <span class="stat stat-value">$healSkill</span> </div> <div class="stat-container"> <img src="Images/Icons/MindMagic.png" width="60" height="60"> <span class="stat stat-value">$magicSkill</span> </div> <div class="stat-container"> <<if $karma <= $karmaToEvil>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/EvilKarma.png" width="60" height="60"> <<else>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/Karma.png" width="60" height="60"> <</if>> <span class="stat stat-value">$karma</span> </div> <div class="stat-container"> <img src="Images/Icons/PregIcon.png" width="60" height="60"> <span class="stat stat-value">$cumSkill</span> </div> <div class="stat-container"> <img src="Images/Icons/Charisma.png" width="60" height="60"> <span class="stat stat-value">$charismaSkill</span> </div> <<if $inventoryOpen is false>> <<link "Open Inventory">> <div class="stat-container"> <span class="stat stat-value">Open Inventory</span> </div> <<set $lastPassageName to passage()>> <<set $inventoryOpen to true>> <<goto "Inventory">> <</link>> <<else>> <<link "Close Inventory">> <div class="stat-container"> <span class="stat stat-value">Close Inventory</span> </div> <<set $inventoryOpen to false>> <<goto $lastPassageName>> <</link>> <</if>> <</nobr>> <div class="iconWatcher"> <hr class="separator-line"> <div class="patreon-container"> <a href="https://www.patreon.com/MonsterSlayer969" target="_blank"><img src="Images/Other/Patreon.png" alt="Patreon" width="60" height="60"></a> <a href="https://discord.gg/PBYmT4eQqE" target="_blank"><img src="Images/Other/Discord.png" alt="Kofi" width="60" height="60"></a> </div> </div> You step into the small home, the main and perhaps only place worth checking right now. It's the only structure that doesn't seem to be a literal ruin. And to your surprise, it seems Elisa bothered to put the place in order, the interior at least. The thought of her crosses your mind for a moment. You barely knew her, but she seemed like a decent enough person. But… hey, had she not been given dead, you most likely wouldn't be here, so you are not exactly mourning her loss. Your gaze sweeps across the room, taking in the worn but comfortable furniture, a simple oak desk in the middle of the room, and a crackling fireplace that will cast a warm glow as soon as you light it. Moving further into the abode, you explore the adjoining rooms. At your disposal is the portal room, a small but homely bedroom, an underground basement, and a little workshop. The most important one is the portal room, situated upstairs. An ominous obsidian disk rests at the center of said room, surrounded by a ring of ancient symbols etched into the floor. A small, circular recess in the center of the disk, waiting for the arcane spark of a travelstone to open up a dimensional rift to its destination. Every last corner of the universe is part of your hunting grounds so long as you happen to own the key… and The Eye provides them at a constant speed. You should have no short supply of them. Speaking of which... On a stone altar beside the ominous, inactive stone disk, you spot a letter beneath a smooth travel stone, left for you by Lauren herself it would appear. <div class="playerSpeech">"Shit… not even a day to make myself comfortable huh?"</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Your first mission? |Intro 3][$azriStone = true]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedItem.png"></div>\You take the letter in your hands, feeling an increasing dread as you realize what kind of job it is she has in store for you. <div class="laurenSpeech"><em>"$playerName, As you are well aware, in your new role as a High Hunter, it is expected of you to embody an unwavering shield, always prepared to safeguard humanity from the deadly creatures that lurk in the dark corners of this and all realities. Though what I'm about to ask may seem a daunting endeavor, your track record and reputation speak for themselves, assuring me that you have demonstrated your worthiness of such a responsibility. Let's not waste any more ink. As you surely know, Azri'Elis has spent the last century contained in a dimensional sanctum ubicated in Darthax, but her corrupting influence is wearing down even such a secure prison. The sentinels have requested our guidance for her recontainment, and that is exactly what you will do. She is still bound, but it won't be long until she manages to break free. You won't let that happen. Execute her, or reseal her prison, I care not so long as she is rendered harmless. May the hunt be ever in your favor. Lauren."</em></div>\ Well... fuck. It was to be expected that you'd get a mission like this sooner or later, but you had hoped it would be later! Where are the cute harmless creatures you have waited so long to ~breed~ fight!? You have heard about Azri'Elis before, an extremely powerful arcane-wielding demoness that… that will surely reduce you to ash and dust in a matter of seconds. You take a look at the little stone, a smooth, fairly unremarkable round gem, power untold contained within. This one especially… <div class="important">Travelstones are like fuel rods for the gates you use to travel the realms, when they inevitably run out of charge, their user is recalled into their own, original world.</div>\ Usually, you wouldn't have a lot of time once you go on a hunt using one of them. Enough to give your prey a quick death and disappear. There are more secure, permanent ways of traveling through dimensions, but... those methods require extensive preparation, resources, and support that makes it much much harder to pull off. The travelstone in your hand is not like most, however. This one will literally tear open the space, linking this reality between Darthax and this room, so for this particular mission, you'll have as much time as you need… which may be even worse, all things considered. If things go sour, with regular travelstones you know that you'll dissolve and disappear after a while, so hiding and waiting is an option… So, what now? If you go to Darthax immediately, you're almost certain that Azri'Elis will simply scour you from existence. One does not simply fix these dimensional prisons with planks of wood and nails, you'll most likely need to reduce her before moving her somewhere else… But if you don't go, you'll undoubtedly face Lauren's wrath. Well... you are pretty sure she wasn't expecting you to hop right away, you should have some time to prepare, right? Right? <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |LivingRoom]]</div>\<div class="title">Choose your name</div>\ <<textbox "$playerName" $playerName>> <div <<set $killedWolf to false>> <<set $killedFury to false>> <<set $killedNaafiri to false>> <<set $killedAzri to false>> <<set $killedWolyrn to false>> <<set $killedWyvern to false>> <<set $nyxaState to "dontKnow">> <<set $nyxaTravels to 0>> <<set $damlonNyxaStone to 0>> <<set $orionNyxaStone to 0>> <<set $vaelNyxaStone to 0>> <<set $fromPortal to false>> <<set $cumNaafiri to 0>> <<set $dissapointedVael to false>> <<set $currentMonsterHp to 120>> <<set $currentMonsterExhaustion to 0>> <<set $currentMonsterMaxHp to 120>> <<set $currentMonsterMaxExhaustion to 100>> <<set $canSee to false>> <<set $possibleDreams to []>> <<set $possibleNightmares to ["base"]>> <<set $baseDreamChance to 0>> <<set $baseNightmareChance to 6>> <<set $dreamstoneChance to 20>><div class="title">Choose your starting boon</div>\ <<nobr>> <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Arcane Scholar">> <<set $healSkill += 1>> <<set $magicSkill += 1>> <<goto "ReadIntro">> <</link>> <img src="Images/Icons/RedMagic.png" > <img src="Images/Icons/RedHeal.png"> </div> You have read the books the great library had to offer, and you have spent long hours practicing the arts described in those tomes. You are no master, but your efforts are starting to pay off. <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png" ><<link "Experienced Killer">> <<set $hunterSkill += 2>> <<set $karma -= 1>> <<goto "ReadIntro">> <</link>> <img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"><img src="Images/Icons/RedEvilKarma.png"> </div> Even before becoming a High Hunter, you already knew your way around the business of death. You've killed countless creatures in the service of The Eye, and your expertise shows. <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png" ><<link "Born To Breed">> <<set $cumSkill += 1>> <<set $charismaSkill += 1>> <<goto "ReadIntro">> <</link>> <img src="Images/Icons/RedPreg.png" width="4%" height="4%"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedCharisma.png" width="4%" height="4%"> </div> You may not be the smartest or most skilled hunter, but you know that one thing you do very, very well... <<if $deaths >0>> <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png" ><<link "Enlightened">> <<set $eyeSeeing += 2>> <<set $canSee = true>> <<set $baseDreamChance += 10>> <<set $baseNightmareChance -= 10>> <<goto "ReadIntro">> <</link>> <img src="Images/Icons/RedEye.png" width="4%" height="4%"> </div> The void carries her whispers... can you hear her voice? <</if>> <<if $nyxaPartnered is true>> <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Nyxa's favorite hunter">> <<set $healSkill += 3>> <<set $awakenedHeal to true>> <<set $nyxaState to "partner">> <<set $nyxaPoints to 10>> <<goto "ReadIntro">> <</link>> <img src="Images/Icons/RedHeal.png"> </div> You have befriended Nyxa, and spent some time learning her skills and tricks. You will never reach her level, but you are now able to heal a decent wound. <</if>> <<if $exalted is 1>> <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Exalted Deathbringer">> <<set $hunterSkill += 0>> <<set $darkDevotion to 1>> <<goto "ReadIntro">> <</link>> <img src="Images/Icons/RedDevotion.png"> </div> You have felt it, her voice, calling out for slaughter, for death. You shall answer the call. <</if>> <<if $vaelEffigy is 1>> Use the effigy Vael gave you to immediately gain two ability points in a certain skill. This will consume the effigy. You can select your class in addition to it. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Use it |VaelLevelUp1][$azriStone = true]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedItem.png"></div> <</if>> <</nobr>><div class="title">Would you like to read the intro?</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Yes |Intro]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[No |LivingRoom][$azriStone = true]]</div>\<div class="title">LIVING ROOM</div>\ You stand in the living room. From here, you can access all the other rooms in this house. <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go to the portal room |PortalRoom]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTeleport.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go to your bedroom |Bedroom]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedSleep.png"></div> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Inspect your surroundings |InspectLivingRoom]]</div> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go outside |Outside]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go to the basement |Basement]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go to the workshop |Workshop]]</div>\ <div class="title">FRONT DOOR</div>\ You stand outside, even if you know there is not a whole lot to do there. The already familiar vista of the dead dimension greets you once again; rubble and ruin idly strewn about as far as your eyes can see, like dull stars shimmering their last in the empty blackness that surrounds them. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Inspect your surroundings |InspectOutside]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go to the courtyard |Courtyard]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back inside |LivingRoom]]</div>\<div class="title">BASEMENT</div>\ The basement of the house. As one might expect, it's dark, but surprisingly devoid of the junk and clutter you’d usually associate with basements. The walls are made of sturdy stone or concrete, and there's an almost eerie sense of emptiness... This space has the potential for something more than just storage, maybe you could use it to keep one of the cute creatures you will surely encounter. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Inspect your surroundings |InspectBasement]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back upstairs |LivingRoom]]</div>\<div class="title">BEDROOM</div>\ The bedroom is a rather unassuming space, but it serves its purpose. It has everything you need; A bed, a nightstand and a small wardrobe. It would probably be nicer with some decorations, or some better lighting... but then again, no sun shines in this dimension. <<if $playerEnergy is "rested">>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Sleep|Sleep]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedSleep.png"></div>@@\ <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Sleep|Sleep]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedSleep.png"></div>\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Inspect the room |InspectBedroom]]</div>\ <<if $cumNaafiri is 0 && $darkinDagger is true>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Masturbate to the darkin dagger |MasturbateDagger]]</div>\ <</if>> <<if $cumNaafiri is 1 && $darkinDagger is true>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Masturbate to the darkin dagger |MasturbateDagger2]]</div>\ <</if>> <<if $dreamcatcher is 1>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Place the dreamcatcher |PlaceDreamcatcher][$baseNightmareChance -=30]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedItem.png"></div>\ <</if>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go to the living room |LivingRoom]]</div>\ <div class="title">WORKSHOP</div>\ As with the rest of this place, the workshop is functional but leaves much to be desired. Just the bare necessities and tools needed to fix whatever's broken, and not a whole lot else. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Inspect the workbench |InspectWorkshop]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back to the living room |LivingRoom]]</div>\ <div class="title">COURTYARD</div>\ The courtyard... or what remains of it. What once was a garden of sorts has been reduced to a barren patch of gray dirt, not a hint of vegetation anywhere. The only detail to break the awful monotony of the place is the crumbling fountain, sitting near the center. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Inspect the fountain |InspectFountain]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back inside |LivingRoom]]</div>\ <<nobr>> <<set _randomInt to Math.floor(Math.random() * 100)>> <<set $dreamVariable to _randomInt>> <<set $dreamChance to $baseDreamChance>> <<set $nightmareChance to $baseNightmareChance>> <<set $dreamChance += (3 * $eyeSeeing)>> <<set $nightmareChance += (4 * $eyeSeeing)>> <<if $dreamChance >=50>> <<set $dreamChance to 50>> <</if>> <<if $nightmareChance >=50>> <<set $nightmareChance to 50>> <</if>> <<set $totalChance to $dreamChance + $nightmareChance>> <</nobr>> <<set $onlyOnceSleep = false>>\ <<if $nyxaState == "niceDay4" || $nyxaState == "readyForNight1"|| $nyxaState == "readyForNight2">>\ <<switch $nyxaState>>\ <<case "niceDay4">>\ <<goto NightDay4Nyxa>>\ <<case "readyForNight1">>\ <<goto SleepNyxaFirst>>\ <<case "readyForNight2">>\ <<goto SpendNightNyxa>>\ <</switch>>\ <<else>> <<switch $playerEnergy>> <<case "library">>\ You may have not exercised your body much today, but you have certainly strained your mind. You take off your clothes and climb into bed, eager to get some rest... <<case "damlon">>\ You are pretty tired after the friendly sparring session with Damlon, and some sleep would do your weary body good... <<case "dayOff">>\ Maybe it has not been the harshest day you remember, but the hunt is exhausting, both mentally and physically. After a quick dinner, you retreat into the privacy of your room, the comfort of the bed a welcome pleasure. <<case "nyxaTravel">>\ Today wasn't the most productive of days, but travelling between worlds is tiresome one way or the other... <<default>>\ After a long day venturing through the corners of the universe, you crave sleeping more than anything else. You take off your clothes and climb into bed. Your weary muscles relax and your mind slowly slips away into the sweet, soothing embrace of sleep. <<set $onlyOnceSleep = false>>\ <</switch>>\ <<if $nyxaState == "niceDay3">>\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">\ <<link "Continue">>\ <<goto NyxaNight3>>\ <<else>>\ <<set $yuumiChance to random(1, 7)>>\ <<if $VaelIntro == "afterFeragon">> <<set $VaelIntro to "afterFeragon1">> <</if>>\ <<if $fuckedFeragon is true>> <div class="linkIcons">\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">\ <<link "Continue">>\ <<goto BadEndVael>>\ <</link>> </div>\ <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons">\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">\ <<link "Continue">>\ <<if $yuumiChance == 1 && $foundYuumi is false>>\ <<goto YuumiStart>>\ <<else>>\ <<goto SleepDreams>>\ <</if>>\ <</link>> </div>\ <</if>> <</if>> <</if>> <<if $blessing == 0 && $darkDevotion >= 2>>\ During the night, you dream of blood, of corpses. Your patron is pleased with your efforts so far, and her blessing will allow you to slay your enemies with even greater efficiency. <div class="passCheck">\ +1 <img src="Images/Icons/General.png"></div>\ <<link "Continue">>\ <<goto Bedroom>>\ <<set $hunterSkill +=1>>\ <<set $blessing +=1>>\ <</link>>\ <<elseif $blessing == 1 && $darkDevotion >= 4>>\ During the night, you dream of blood, of corpses. Your patron is pleased with your efforts so far, and her blessing will allow you to slay your enemies with even greater efficiency. <div class="passCheck">\ +3 <img src="Images/Icons/General.png"></div>\ <div class="passCheck">\ +1 <img src="Images/Icons/MindMagic.png"></div>\ <div class="passCheck">\ +1 <img src="Images/Icons/Heal.png"></div>\ <<link "Continue">>\ <<goto Bedroom>>\ <<set $hunterSkill +=3>>\ <<set $healSkill +=1>>\ <<set $magicSkill +=1>>\ <<set $blessing +=1>>\ <</link>> <<else>>\ <<if $dreamVariable < $dreamChance>>\ <<if $possibleDreams.length > 0>>\ <<set _randomIndex to Math.floor(Math.random() * $possibleDreams.length)>>\ <<set $selectedDream to $possibleDreams[_randomIndex]>> The night stretches on, your consciousness slipping into the realm of dreams, or so it seems. What begins as an empty, dreamless void, dark and infinite, soon shifts and twists, like ripples on the surface of still water. Subtle images flicker at the edges of your mind, blurry and distant at first, but they grow clearer, more insistent. They feel vivid—too vivid. <<switch $selectedDream>>\ <<case "wolf1">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |Dream Wolf 1]]</div>\ <<case "wolf2">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |Dream Wolf 2]]</div>\ <<case "wolf3">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |Dream Wolf 3]]</div>\ <<case "azri1">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |Dream Azri 1]]</div>\ <<case "feragon1">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |Dream Feragon]]</div>\ <<case "fury1">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |Dream Fury 1]]</div>\ <<case "fury2">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |Dream Fury 2]]</div>\ <<case "wolyrn1">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |Dream Wolyrn 1]]</div>\ <<case "deer1">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |Dream Deer 1]]</div>\ <<case "naafiri1">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |Dream Naafiri]]</div>\ <<case "wyvern1">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |Dream Wyvern]]</div>\ <<case "nyxa1">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |Dream Nyxa]]</div>\ <<default>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |Bedroom]]</div>\ <</switch>>\ <<else>>\ <<if $insectDream is false>>\ The night stretches on, your consciousness slipping into the realm of dreams, or so it seems. What begins as an empty, dreamless void, dark and infinite, soon shifts and twists, like ripples on the surface of still water. Subtle images flicker at the edges of your mind, blurry and distant at first, but they grow clearer, more insistent. They feel vivid—too vivid. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |Dream Insect 1][$insectDream = true]]</div>\ <<else>>\ No dreams, no nightmares. You wake up after some time, renewed and ready for the day. <<if $quickSleep is false>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |Bedroom]]</div>\ <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |PortalRoom]]</div>\ <</if>>\ <</if>>\ <</if>>\ <<else>>\ <<if $dreamVariable < $totalChance>>\ <<if $possibleNightmares.length > 0>>\ <<set _randomIndex to Math.floor(Math.random() * $possibleNightmares.length)>>\ <<set $selectedNightmare to $possibleNightmares[_randomIndex]>>\ The night stretches on, your consciousness slipping into the realm of dreams, or so it seems... you find yourself turning and tossing... <<switch $selectedNightmare>>\ <<case "base">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |NightmareBase]]</div>\ <<case "maze">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |NightmareMaze]]</div>\ <<case "house">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |Nightmare: house]]</div>\ <<case "thing">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |Nightmare:Thing]]</div>\ <<default>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |Bedroom]]</div>\ <</switch>>\ <<else>>\ No dreams, no nightmares. You wake up after some time, renewed and ready for the day. <<if $quickSleep is false>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |Bedroom]]</div>\ <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |PortalRoom]]</div>\ <</if>>\ <</if>>\ <<else>>\ No dreams, no nightmares. You wake up after some time, renewed and ready for the day. <<if $quickSleep is false>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |Bedroom]]</div>\ <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |PortalRoom]]</div>\ <</if>>\ <</if>>\ <</if>>\ <</if>>\ <<if $onlyOnceSleep is false>>\ <<run setup.passDay()>>\ <<set $onlyOnceSleep = true>>\ <</if>>\You reach and grab it, of course. Inside the crumpled envelope, you find a small piece of paper and a <div class="item">travelstone</div>. <div class="playerSpeech">"Great"</div> It appears to be just an old contract, probably one that Elisa had postponed and never got the chance to complete. You quickly read between the lines, and you realize it may be an interesting one after all. The contract describes a rather peculiar target – human from the waist up, and deer from the waist down, apparently sighted by the locals of a far away world. She's described as agile and very elusive, but the reward offered is not half bad, 90 for what you assume will be a really easy job. Hell, you'd probably go even if the reward was nothing, just because it looks like a nice opportunity to get your dick wet. <div class="passCheck">You stuff the piece of paper and the travel stone into one of your many pockets.<img src="Images/Icons/Item.png"></div> <<set $deerStone to true>> <<set $foundWorkshop to true>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |Workshop]]</div>\ <<if $foundHall is false>> \ The Hunter's Hall serves as your order's primary hub, both for business and social interactions. No matter how far away, no matter what kind of inhospitable world one finds oneself in, this sanctum remains the only true constant. You won't even need a travelstone to access this place; The necklace you wear is the key that will allow you to travel directly to the hall.<</if>> You step into the disk and mutter an old incantation, and when you open your eyes again, you are no longer standing in your room, but in another chamber filled with many other similar disks and portals. You will forever be grateful for how innocuous traveling from and to the Hunter's Hall is. No dizziness, no nausea, no pain. If only the other travels were as comfortable... <<set $haiderIntro to false>>\ <<if $furyJobState ==0 && $reputation >=1 && $foundHall is true>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Walk into the main hall |Hunters Hall Haider]]</div>\ <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Walk into the main hall |Hunters Hall]]</div>\ <</if>> ‎ @@.title;HUNTER'S HALL@@ <<if $foundHall is false>> You step into the round chamber, the main hub where your fellows come together to share their stories, prepare their hunts, rest between their expeditions, and find new contracts to take care of. Some eyes turn to you, and greet you with smiles and waves. @@.haiderSpeech;"And speaking of, there's the man himself..."@@ Haider comes to greet you, his gloved hand stretching yours. @@.haiderSpeech;"Congratulations on the promotion. Let's hope you last more than Elisa!"@@ The both of you laugh at that. Hopefully you will... You exchange some pleasantries before you are left alone. Now, what should you do? <<set $foundHall to true>> <<else>>\ The round chamber greets you with its familiar hustle and bustle, full of people as it usually is. Now, what should you do? <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Meditate and level up |LevelUpLuminarch]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedLevelUp.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go talk to Nyxa |NyxaMenu]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedHeal.png"></div>\ <<switch $VaelIntro>>\ <<case "afterFeragon">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go talk to The Forgemaster |VaelUnavailableFeragon]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedItem.png"></div>\ <<case "lostDuel">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go talk to The Forgemaster |VaelIntroDuel]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedItem.png"></div>\ <<case "afterFeragon1">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go talk to The Forgemaster |VaelIntroFriendly]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedItem.png"></div>\ <<default>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go talk to The Forgemaster |VaelIntro]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedItem.png"></div>\ <</switch>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go talk to Damlon |DamlonIntro]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go to the library |LibraryIntro]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedMagic.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back to your own dimension |PortalRoom][$returnToPortal = true]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTeleport.png"></div>\ @@.title;THE FORGE@@ <img src="Images/Hunters/VaelFixed.png" class="resizable-image" alt="Vael Image"> <<if $metVael is false>>You decide to go and talk with Vael, The Forgemaster. An insuperable blacksmith, and an archivist of all kinds of trinkets, truly an invaluable resource. If you need something, chances are Vael has it. Hell, you could say he truly is a self-made man... It's a shame he is so much of a self-centered prick. He is almost as old as Lauren herself, so it makes sense that he's a bit tired of dealing with the new blood. But it's not an excuse for his behavior, or so you tell yourself. You descend a couple of sets of stairs, to find him hard at work, his long, blade-like fingers closed around a heavy hammer as he deftly shapes a longsword. No matter how many times you see him, you never get used to his appearance. He is a hulking steel monstrosity, and if he was once a human, then there's no trace left of it. Four arms, eight legs, and a head that's nothing more than a metal skull, with a set of lenses and lights where his eyes should be. His slow descent into insanity is finally starting to show, in the form of the unusual choice of clothing that adorns his otherwise completely naked steel body. It looks like some sort of ceremonial robe, only that instead of silk or any other normal material, it's made from living flesh, connected to the rest of him by thin tendrils of tissue. He is clearly aware of your presence... but he keeps focused on his task, hammering at the red-hot sword, seemingly unperturbed. You clear your throat and try to get his attention. @@.playerSpeech;"Vael? Can I have a word?"@@ @@.vaelSpeech;"I'm... listening"@@ He grumbles back.<<set $metVael to true>><<else>> You make your way to the Forgemaster's chambers. You find him there, as always, working on the forge. @@.vaelSpeech;"What is it that you want now, Hunter?"@@ He grumbles, without stopping his work <</if>>\ <<if $askVaelWeapon == false>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask him about the weapon |AskWeapon]]</div>\<</if>>\ <<if $feragonJobState != 1>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["Do you have a job for me?" |AskJob]]</div>\<</if>>\ <<if $feragonJobState is 1>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["Im ready to retrieve that feragon" |FeragonStart]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTeleport.png"></div>\<</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["I would like to purchase some wares from you" |PurchaseWares]] <img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["I would like to sell you something" |SellWares]] <img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\ <<if $repVael <= -1>><<if $lostVaelDuel != 0>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Fight The Forgemaster |FightVael]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\<</if>><</if>>\ <<if $fromPortal is false>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |LeaveVael]]</div>\ <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |PortalRoom]]</div>\ <</if>>\@@.title;HEADQUARTERS @@ <<set $temporaryReputation to $reputation>>\ <img src="Images/Hunters/DamlonFixed.png" class="resizable-image" alt="Damlon Image"> <<if $continueDamlon is false>>\ <<if $metDamlon is false>>\ You decide to pay a visit to Damlon, the veteran tracker of the order. Too old to properly hunt himself, he now serves as a liaison, sorting and facilitating knowledge and contracts to fellow hunters. But hey, he is one of the few high hunters who managed to finish their duties without even suffering a tragic fate or trascending mortality through magic or technology. As you step into his office, you find him huddled over a table, examining various maps and papers, before glancing up to acknowledge your presence. His grizzled appearance and scar-covered face make him an intimidating figure, but he's a good man, and he has treated you well before. @@.damlonSpeech;"Ah, it's you,"@@ Damlon says in his gravelly voice. @@.damlonSpeech;"What brings you here?"@@ <<set $metDamlon to true>>\ <<else>>\ @@.damlonSpeech;"You again"@@ Damlon grumbles as he glances away from the maps and papers. @@.damlonSpeech;"What is it this time?"@@ <</if>>\ <<elseif $continueDamlon is true>>\ Damlon watches you from his desk, as if impatient to continue whatever he was doing. <<set $continueDamlon to false>>\ <</if>>\ <<if $nyxaState is "partner" && $damlonNyxaStone is 0>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask Damlon for a spare travelstone |AskTravelstoneDamlon]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedReputation.png"></div>\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask for a job |AskDamlonJob]]</div>\ <<if $activeDamlonContract == "wyvern">><<if $wyvernJobState == 2>><div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Claim the wyvern's bounty (60) |ClaimBountyGeneric][$money += 60]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\<</if>><</if>>\ <<if $activeDamlonContract == "wolf">><<if $wolfJobState == 2>><div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Claim the wolf's bounty (50) |ClaimBountyGeneric][$money += 50]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\<</if>><</if>>\ <<if $activeDamlonContract == "deathclaw">><<if $deathclawJobState == 2>><div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Claim the deathclaw's bounty (90) |ClaimBountyGeneric][$money += 90]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\<</if>><</if>>\ <<if $activeDamlonContract == "wolyrn">><<if $wolyrnJobState == 2>><div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Claim the wolyrn's bounty (120) |ClaimBountyGeneric][$money += 120]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\<</if>><</if>>\ <<if $deerJobState == 2>><div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Claim the deer's bounty (80) |ClaimBountyDeer][$money += 80]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\<</if>>\ <<if $naafiriJobState == 2>><div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Claim Naafiri's bounty (60) |ClaimBountyNaafiri][$money += 60]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\<</if>>\ <<if $darkinDagger is true>><div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Tell Damlon that you took care of naafiri, and give him the blade (300) |ClaimBountyNaafiri2][$money += 300]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\<</if>>\ <<if $damlonTrain is false>>\ <<if $reputation >=2>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask Damlon to train you |AskDamlonTrain]]<div class="numberStat">2</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedReputation.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask Damlon to train you |AskDamlonTrain]]<div class="numberStat">2</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedReputation.png"></div>@@\ <</if>>\ <</if>> <<if $fromPortal is false>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |Hunters Hall]]</div>\ <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |PortalRoom]]</div>\ <</if>>\@@.playerSpeech;"Um... what's with the sword you are making there?"@@ He stops forging for a moment, slowly straightening his body, and turning to face you with a menacing look in his lenses. @@.vaelSpeech;"A replacement... for when you inevitably lose your life, and the priced weapon Lauren inexplicably thought a good idea to put in your inexperienced hands."@@ He says, his voice oozing contempt. @@.vaelSpeech;"I'm not up for idle chatter, and neither should you, <i>High Hunter</i>. So would you be kind enough to tell me what you want and stop contaminating my sacred forge with your presence?"@@<<set $repVael -= 1>> <<set $askVaelWeapon to true>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ignore the taunt |IgnoreJab]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Comfront him |NotIgnoreJab]]</div>\You sigh. Not worth it. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |VaelIntroReduced]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"I know you never liked me, Vael, and to be honest I don't like you either. Whether you like it or not, I'm now a high hunter, and as such, you are going to treat me with the respect I deserve."@@ @@.vaelSpeech;"Respect has to be earned, child. Your premature promotion is no achievement worthy of praise."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |VaelIntroReduced]]</div>\<<if $feragonJobState == 0>> @@.playerSpeech;"I wanted to ask if you have any jobs for me, Vael."@@ He chuckles, a dry, metallic sound, and then turns to face you. <<if $azriActive is true>>\ @@.vaelSpeech;"I do believe you already have quite an important job ahead of you. Lauren herself tasked you with dealing with Azri'Elis, did she not?"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"I'm still preparing for that. I'm looking for other jobs in the meantime."@@ He ponders for a second, considering if you're even worth the effort. @@.vaelSpeech;"I do. It should surely be an easy enough job, for one such as yourself. High Hunter... what a joke."@@ He scoffs at the very end as if the words taste foul. <<else>>\ @@.vaelSpeech;"I would belive you would have more than enough already...."@@ He ponders for a second, considering if you're even worth the effort. @@.vaelSpeech;"I do, however, have a job for you. It should surely be an easy enough job, for one such as yourself. High Hunter... what a joke."@@ He scoffs at the very end as if the words taste foul. <</if>>\ @@.playerSpeech;"..."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["Tell me more about this job" |FeragonJob]]</div>\ <<else>><<if $feragonJobState == 4 || $dissapointedVael == true>> @@.playerSpeech;"You wouldn't happen to have another job, would you, Vael?"@@ @@.vaelSpeech;"You dare ask for another job after your pitiful performance? One chance was far more than you deserved."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |VaelIntro][$continueVael = true]]</div>\ <<else>> <<if $sixEightJobState == "">> Vael thinks for a moment, something is on his mind. @@.vaelSpeech;"I do have a... request. A task that would require a bit of... muscle. If you are interested. I wouldn't have asked you, but since you proved your skills on our last... job together, I thought I'd offer it. I simply need you to retrieve a sample of a certain... creature." @@ You raise an eyebrow, curious. If Vael is complimenting you, then chances are this is a death trap. @@.playerSpeech;"You flatter me. But if there's money in it, I'm in. What's the catch?" @@ He shakes his head, amused by your suspicion. @@.vaelSpeech;"Of course, the catch. Said creature cannot be killed, a true, genuine immortal entity. Its flesh regenerates in seconds, and its mind is too twisted for any sort of... reasoning. Furthermore, it is capable of adapting to any attack or strategy used against it, rendering most conventional methods useless."@@ Vael’s tone is matter-of-fact, but there's an edge to his words, a hint that he's not telling you everything. You can’t help but feel a prickle of unease at the thought of what this creature might be. @@.playerSpeech;"So, let me get this straight. You want me to go up against something that can’t be killed, can’t be reasoned with, and adapts to any attempt at harm? And you want a piece of it. Sounds like a suicide mission."@@ @@.vaelSpeech;"The adaptation process is not instantaneous, and a sample is all that I need. It is not particularly fast, so you should be able to retrieve it before it fully adapts to your attacks. And yes, the reward will be... substantial." @@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Take this job |TakeJob682][$sixEightJobState = "accepted"]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[You would rather not |NotTakeJob682]]</div>\ <<else>> @@.playerSpeech;"You wouldn't happen to have another job, would you, Vael?"@@ @@.vaelSpeech;"Not at the moment. But I will let you know when I do... you make for a good errand boy."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |VaelIntro][$continueVael = true]]</div>\ <</if>> <</if>> <</if>>@@.playerSpeech;"Tell me more about that job, then."@@ He stops what he is doing and turns around, now fully facing you, his four hands crossed. @@.vaelSpeech;"Listen closely. I don't like to repeat myself…"@@ Bullshit, you know just how in love the man is with his own voice. @@.vaelSpeech;"Feragons are truly… remarkable creatures. Tough, smart, strong, but their most important quality has to be their scales. They are made of a unique alloy, impervious to most weapons, and even magic."@@ You nod along. @@.vaelSpeech;"These scales are crucial for crafting weapons and armor that can withstand the deadliest of foes. My last feragon met an untimely end during the latest harvest, and I need a replacement. I have identified and located the surviving mate and hatchling of the last one, still living by themselves, away from civilization as it was all those years ago."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"It’s relatives... do they recall our order? If so, I doubt they will be friendly."@@ @@.vaelSpeech;"That would be correct. The creature's mate fought alongside him and knows us all too well. The hatchling's memories on the matter are… undisclosed."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["The hatchling seems like the correct target, then" |FeragonJobHatchling]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedItem.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedCross.png">@@.fadedLink;[["The larger feragon would surely have more scales" |FeragonJobMother]]@@</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"The hatchling seems like the correct target, then. What can you tell me about it?"@@ @@.vaelSpeech;"Scared of an adult feragon, hunter?"@@ He lets out another cold laugh. @@.playerSpeech;"If you intend on using it as livestock, then the hatchling is the most reasonable option, is it not?"@@ You answer, a bit annoyed. @@.vaelSpeech;"Of course. The most reasonable option for someone of your... capabilities."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"..."@@ You ignore him. Again. @@.vaelSpeech;"The hatchling is twenty-three years old. Has a habit of wandering off, so you should be able to catch it alone."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"And how am I supposed to bring it to you? I don't think it will come willingly."@@ @@.vaelSpeech;"You will use this..." Vael goes to rummage through a chest full of random trinkets and pulls out a black collar, runes inscribed on its surface. "It employs the same draining hex your weapon does, tenfold. This will keep the beast under control, no matter how fierce."@@ <div class="passCheck">You take the collar and give it a good look. The runes on its surface glow faintly, pulsing with magic.<img src="Images/Icons/Item.png"></div>\ @@.vaelSpeech;"I will shift you directly to where the beast resides. When you have it subdued, feed the collar a spark of magic and I will shift you both back."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["How do I know you will not double-cross me" |DoubleCrossVael]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["What do I get from this" |PriceHatchling]]</div>\You raise an eyebrow. @@.playerSpeech;"How do I know you will not just leave me stranded, or shift me to my death?"@@ He chuckles at that. @@.vaelSpeech;"If I wanted you dead, hunter, I wouldn't resort to any kind of… trickery or elaborate scheme."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["What do I get from this" |PriceHatchling]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"And what do I get from this?"@@ @@.vaelSpeech;"Fair enough. What is your price, hunter?"@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["10 gold coins" |10coinsHatchling]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["100 gold coins" |100coinsHatchling]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["1000 gold coins" |1000coinsHatchling]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"10 pieces of gold."@@ He gives a small snort. @@.vaelSpeech;"Ten pieces? I didn't know you were so cheap. So shall it be, ten pieces of gold, if you bring me the hatchling unharmed."@@ <<set $feragonJobState to 1>> <<set$continueVael to true>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |VaelIntroReduced][$paymentVael = 10]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"One hundred."@@ @@.vaelSpeech;"One hundred, you say... fair price. I shall pay you that price upon delivery of the creature."@@ <<set $feragonJobState to 1>> <<set$continueVael to true>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |VaelIntroReduced][$paymentVael = 100]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"One thousand."@@ @@.vaelSpeech;"One thousand? You must think quite highly of yourself. I am not paying you anything close to that, hunter."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["10 gold coins" |10coinsHatchling]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["100 gold coins" |100coinsHatchling]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"I'm ready to catch that feragon, Vael."@@ @@.vaelSpeech;"Preparation is a sign of wisdom, hunter."@@ He says, a mocking tone to his voice. @@.vaelSpeech;"But very well. Make your way to the portal array, and I shall shift you immediately."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Get ready for the shift |ShiftFeragon]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTeleport.png"></div>\ <<set $feragonAskedJob to false>> <<set $playerEnergy to "tired">> <<set $temporaryImpregnations to $impregnations >>\ <<set $temporaryKills to $playerKills >>\ <<set $newDreams to ["feragon1"]>> <<set $possibleDreams to $possibleDreams.concat($newDreams)>>@@.playerSpeech;"I would like to purchase some of your wares."@@ @@.vaelSpeech;"What is it you are looking for... hunter?"@@ <<set$continueVael to true>>\ <<if $boughtDeerPh is false && $deerStone is true>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Buy a flask of concentrated deer pheromones(30) |deerPh]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\ <</if>>\ <<if $firePotion is 0>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Buy a fire resist potion (10) |fireResistPotionVael]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\ <</if>>\ <<if $azriExplosive == 0 || $azriAmulet ==0>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask for something that could help you with Azri'Elis |AskSomeAzriElis]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Buy some "Elixir of Exertion" (10) |elixirOfExertion]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\ <<if $nyxaState is "partner" && $vaelNyxaStone is 0>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask Vael for a spare travelstone |AskTravelstoneVael]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\ <</if>>\ <<if $dreamcatcher is 0>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask for a magical dreamcatcher (60)|BuyDreamcatcher]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\ <</if>> <<if $dreamstone is 0>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask for a dreamstone (40)|BuyDreamstone]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\ <</if>> <<if $pendant is 0>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[A curious-looking pendant (50)|Pendant]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\ <</if>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |VaelIntroReduced]]</div>\ @@.playerSpeech;"I would like to sell some items of mine."@@ The Forgemaster's lifeless eyes flicker on and off, as if blinking, and he turns to face you. @@.vaelSpeech;"Do you have any artifact of interest?@@ <<set$continueVael to true>>\ <<if $deerAntlers is true>><div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Sell the deer girl's antlers (10) |sellAntlers]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\<</if>>\ <<if $wyvernEgg is true>><div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Sell the wyvern's egg(40) |sellEgg]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\<</if>>\ <<if $wolfMedallion is true>><div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Sell the wolf girl's medallion (5) |sellMedallion]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\<</if>>\ <<if $wolfBow is true>><div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Sell the wolf girl's broken bow (1) |sellbow]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\<</if>>\ <<if $lasRifle is true>><div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Sell the laser rifle (50) |sellRifle]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\<</if>>\ <<if $darkinDagger is true>><div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Sell the darkin dagger (350) |sellDagger]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\<</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |VaelIntroReduced]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"That would be all." You say, turning around and leaving.@@ @@.vaelSpeech;"Then begone."@@ You exit the forge, and climb the stairs, reaching the top floor. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |Hunters Hall]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"You don't like me, I don't like you. Let us solve our differences like men."@@ You growl, readying your sword. @@.playerSpeech;"A duel, Forgemaster."@@ Vael's gaze falls upon you, and you feel a shiver down your spine. @@.vaelSpeech;"Is this a joke?"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"I don't know, Vael. Is it?"@@ @@.vaelSpeech;"You must be beyond madness. Will you fight me with the weapon I myself created? You believe I have not fought my fair share of battles? I was there when creation itself burned to the ground, hunter."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Seathe the blade |NoFightVael]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["Are you scared of me, old man?" |FightVael2]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\@@.playerSpeech;"..."@@ You seethe, trying to stay calm, and put the weapon back in its sheath. Deep down you know you could never stand a chance against him. @@.vaelSpeech;"You are more of a fool than I thought, hunter."@@ He laughs and quickly goes back to work. <<set $lostVaelDuel to 1>> [[Continue |VaelIntro]] @@.playerSpeech;"Scared of me, old man?"@@ @@.vaelSpeech;"Fine."@@ He hisses. @@.vaelSpeech;"If it will satisfy your need for a quick humiliation."@@ The Forgemaster puts down his hammer and slowly makes his way to you, an aura of raw, unbridled power radiating from him. Hidden runes of power glow in his otherwise smooth plates, their purpose unknown. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Charge The Forgemaster |NormalFightVael]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\Sword in hand, you charge towards Vael, aiming for the neck. Regardless of what he may say, he looks slow and cumbersome, surely an easy opponent. On the other hand, The Forgemaster calmly stands there, watching you come, until the moment your blade is about to reach him. The contempt he feels for you is evident in the lack of emphasis he is putting on his movements. He is not taking you seriously, not one bit. The Forgemaster is of course bound to keep some of the best pieces of his many creations for himself, and indeed, before your sword can even touch his body, a series of runes become visible on his metal body, and your blade bounces off of his chest with so much force that you are launched across the room, landing hard on the floor. @@.vaelSpeech;"I'm not surprised this is no real challenge for me. I'm surprised you thought we were on equal footing in the first place. A wise general is the one that picks their battles carefully…"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Fuck you..."@@ You curse under your breath as you stand back up. The Forgemaster raises his arms, another set of runes igniting on his chest, and then, you feel his magic slam against your mind like a tidal wave. It's overpowering, and overwhelming, and you quickly fall to your knees as your body ceases to answer your commands. You can only watch in horror as Vael slowly approaches, looming over you, a malicious look in his lenses as he considers what to do with you. @@.vaelSpeech;"Bow."@@ He growls, and your body obeys, bending your spine, your forehead touching the floor. @@.vaelSpeech;"Stay."@@ You remain in the same position, your mind reeling. From here you can smell the metallic scent of his body, the cold, unforgiving steel coupled with the warm, living tissue, creating an unnatural, nauseating sensation. @@.vaelSpeech;"Your stupidity is like no other, child."@@ He mocks. @@.vaelSpeech;"How is it that someone as dim-witted as you managed to become a High Hunter... poor Lauren must be losing her mind as well."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"..."@@ Your mouth refuses to move. @@.vaelSpeech;"It would be easy, to just crush you here and now."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"..."@@ @@.vaelSpeech;"But I do not wish to soil my hands or my sacred forge with your worthless blood, nor do I wish to make Nyxa's day more miserable by sending her your broken body for her miraculous fixing."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"..."@@ @@.vaelSpeech;"You will leave. Never show yourself before me again. Are we clear?"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Y-Yes..."@@ You finally manage to mutter. @@.vaelSpeech;"Good. Now begone. May this be the last time you disgrace my presence."@@ He steps away from you, and the pressure lifts, your limbs suddenly coming back under your control. You stand up, shame filling your body, and leave without a word. Challenging one of the most powerful people in your order to fight was, surely, a foolish move. <<set $VaelIntro to "lostDuel">> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Make your way upstairs |Hunters Hall][$reputation -=1]]</div>\<<if $activeDamlonContract == "">> <<if $returnDamlonContract is false>> @@.playerSpeech;"Well, I was looking for a job."@@ You tell him. @@.playerSpeech;"Anything interesting, maybe?"@@ Damlon raises an eyebrow, his lips curling into a small smirk. <<if $azriActive is true>>\ @@.damlonSpeech;"Really? Here I thought Lauren herself had assigned you to Azri'Elis' impending… compromise of containment"@@ He snickers. @@.damlonSpeech;"Done with that already?"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"No, no... I haven't done it yet. I just want to make sure I'm ready for it, so... yeah. Wanted to practice first with something less dangerous, and more... profitable. Need some gold."@@ @@.damlonSpeech;"Sure you do, kid. Sure you do. Well, let's see what we can do..."@@ <<else>>\ @@.damlonSpeech;"You are a restless one... but that is how it should be."@@ <</if>>\ He shuffles around a few papers on his desk, before finding what he was looking for. @@.damlonSpeech;"I have some that may interest you. Should be quick work for a high hunter such as yourself..."@@ He says, and you can certainly feel the sliiight sarcasm in his voice. He throws a the papers he has recently fetched at you, and you feign interest as you read between the lines to select those that may have a cute creature you’d be willing to dick up. <<else>> @@.playerSpeech;"You know what?"@@ You shake your head. @@.playerSpeech;"On second thought... I think I will go with something else"@@ <</if>> <<set $returnDamlonContract to false>> <<if $wolyrnJobState ==0>><div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[A warrior wolyrn. She's already had contact with your order, she will be prepared for the encounter |wolyrnContract]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedItem.png"></div>\<</if>>\ <<if $wolfJobState ==0>><div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[A young wolf girl. A dead-eye shot with her bow...|wolfContract]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedItem.png"></div>\<</if>>\ <<if $wyvernJobState ==0>><div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[A small wyvern that has been spotted inhabiting the crumbling remains of a castle |wyvernContract]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedItem.png"></div>\<</if>>\ <<if $naafiriJobState ==0>><div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Naafiri, the hound of a hundred bites |naafiriContract]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedItem.png"></div>\<</if>>\ <<if $deathclawJobState ==0>><div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[A deathclaw, she's been spotted in a desolate, ruined city |deathclawContract]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedItem.png"></div>\<</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["On second thought..." |LeaveNoContract]]</div>\ <<else>> @@.damlonSpeech;"I believe you already have enough as it is. I respect you wanting to do more, but I won't hand you over any more contracts until you finish the one I have given you already."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["I will, I will." |DamlonIntro][$continueDamlon= true]]</div>\ <</if>> @@.playerSpeech;"Nevermind... I think I have plenty of contracts already"@@ He looks at you dead in the eye for a moment and then goes back to his paperwork. @@.damlonSpeech;"Figured. Don't use more of my time then."@@ You go back to the main chamber and look around, thinking about what to do next. <<if $fromPortal is false>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |Hunters Hall]]</div>\ <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |PortalRoom]]</div>\ <</if>>\@@.playerSpeech;"Tell me more about this... wolyrn."@@ You ask. @@.playerSpeech;"It looks like an interesting job"@@ @@.damlonSpeech;"And a high-paying one, too"@@ Damlon nods. @@.damlonSpeech;"This one has some... history with our order. The Eye marked her family long, long ago, and while her other relatives were successfully dealt with, she herself managed to escape."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"The work of an inexperienced hunter, I'd assume"@@ @@.damlonSpeech;"Korvaax. Back when he still worked with us..."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"I... can't imagine how she managed to get away, then"@@ @@.damlonSpeech;"I can. You know how much he enjoyed tormenting his victims before dealing the killing blow. Perhaps he ran out of time. Perhaps he wanted her to live in fear..."@@ He sighs. @@.damlonSpeech;"It is not important. What matters is that The Eye has marked her once again, and that we need to take her down. But you can be completely sure she hasn't forgotten the encounter. Be ready for a fight."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Take this contract |TakeContract][ $activeDamlonContract = "wolyrn"]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedItem.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Check one of the other contracts |AskDamlonJob][$returnDamlonContract = true]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"Tell me more about this one,"@@ You say, pointing at the contract for the wolf girl. @@.playerSpeech;"A bow-using beastkin sounds like a threat to be taken seriously"@@ Damlon raises an eyebrow, not sure if you are serious. She may be a threat, but certainly not to a High Hunter. @@.damlonSpeech;"We don't know much about her, not really. The Eye marked her, and that is all we need to know. It appears she hunts down humans in a forested area, using a longbow. She's still young, so it should be easier to take her down now. Before she has the time to grow into her skills and become a problem."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Take this contract |TakeContract][ $activeDamlonContract = "wolf"]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedItem.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Check one of the other contracts |AskDamlonJob][$returnDamlonContract = true]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"A wyvern, huh?"@@ You ask, taking a closer look at the paper. @@.damlonSpeech;"Yes."@@ Damlon nods. @@.damlonSpeech;"Not too big, about the size of an elephant by our estimations. It is currently inhabiting the ruins of an old castle, far away from any human settlement, but that could change. The Eye has marked it, so it needs to be dealt with as soon as possible."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Any remarkable abilities?"@@ @@.damlonSpeech;"Fire breath, claws, and fangs. It's a wyvern, nothing you haven’t fought already."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Take this contract |TakeContract][ $activeDamlonContract = "wyvern"]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedItem.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Check one of the other contracts |AskDamlonJob][$returnDamlonContract = true]]</div>\After a moment's consideration, you nod. @@.playerSpeech;"Alright. I will do it."@@ @@.damlonSpeech;"Excellent."@@ <div class="passCheck"> Damlon hands you the contract, and the required travelstone, which shimmers in the light. <img src="Images/Icons/Item.png"></div>\ @@.damlonSpeech;"Best of lucks, $playerName. May the hunt be ever in your favor."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"May The Eye guide my steps"@@ You respond, before departing. <<switch $activeDamlonContract>>\ <<case "wyvern">>\ <<set $wyvernStone to true>>\ <<set $wyvernJobState to 1>>\ <<case "wolf">>\ <<set $wolfStone to true>>\ <<set $wolfJobState to 1>>\ <<case "wolyrn">>\ <<set $wolyrnStone to true>>\ <<set $wolyrnJobState to 1>>\ <<case "naafiri">>\ <<set $naafiriStone to true>>\ <<set $naafiriJobState to 1>>\ <<case "deathclaw">>\ <<set $deathclawStone to true>>\ <<set $deathclawJobState to 1>>\ <<default>>\ <<nobr>><<script>>\ alert("what kind of horrible spaghetti code brought us here...");\ <</script>><</nobr>>\ <</switch>>\ <<set $tookDamlonContract to true>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue|Hunters Hall]]</div>\<<switch $activeDamlonContract>> <<case "wyvern">>\ <<set $wyvernJobState to 3>>\ <<case "wolf">>\ <<set $wolfJobState to 3>>\ <<case "wolyrn">>\ <<set $wolyrnJobState to 3>>\ <<case "deathclaw">>\ <<set $deathclawJobState to 3>>\ <<default>>\ <<nobr>><<script>>\ alert("what kind of horrible spaghetti code brought us here..."); <</script>><</nobr>>\ <</switch>>\ <<set $activeDamlonContract to "">>\ "I would like to claim a bounty," You say, taking the required document with you and handing it over, along with the travel stone that indicates that indeed, the creature has been successfully 'brought low'. @@.damlonSpeech;"I see..."@@ Damlon mumbles, as he looks over the documents, and then the travelstone. @@.damlonSpeech;"Good hunt, $playerName. And the payment."@@ He hands over a pouch of coins, that you gladly take. Its weight feels so good in your hands... hell, it's the main reason you still use these antique coins. As they only really have value within the order itself, you could pretty much use any other, more convenient form of currency to account for your performance... but it seems there is something inherently exciting about a pile of coins. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |DamlonIntro][$continueDamlon= true]]</div>\ <<set $reputation to $temporaryReputation +1>>@@.playerSpeech;"I would like to claim a bounty,"@@ You say, taking the required document with you and handing it over, along with the travel stone that indicates that indeed, the creature has been successfully 'brought low' @@.damlonSpeech;"I see..."@@ Damlon mumbles, as he looks over the contract. @@.damlonSpeech;"This is an old, old contract. Last assigned to... well, Elisa, before her tragic passing."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Found it in Elisa's old house, yes. She would have liked to see it done, so I decided to finish it."@@ @@.damlonSpeech;"Well, a noble gesture."@@ Damlon nods. @@.damlonSpeech;"A High Hunter such as yourself should have more important matters to attend to, but… hey, one less threat trotting about."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Oh, It didn't take long."@@ You say with a smile. @@.playerSpeech;"Took her it by surprise. It was quick and easy"@@ @@.damlonSpeech;"Mhm."@@ By his tone, you can tell he doesn't care too much. @@.damlonSpeech;"Here is your payment, then. Best of luck on your other hunts."@@ <<set $deerJobState to 3>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |DamlonIntro][$continueDamlon= true]]</div>\ <<set $reputation to $temporaryReputation +1>>You present the pair of antlers to The Forgemaster, who looks them over with a critical eye. @@.vaelSpeech;"Taken from the corpse of one of your hunts, I presume"@@ He mumbles, taking one of them and running his fingers over the tines. @@.vaelSpeech;"They are in good condition, but they are not useful for their qualities, nor for their rarity."@@ He takes a moment to think before turning his attention back to you. @@.vaelSpeech;"Ten gold is a fair price for the set."@@ <<set $deerAntlers to false>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |SellWares][$money += 10]]</div>\You present the pendant to the forgemaster, who looks at the item from a distance. It's almost as if he is disgusted by the lacking quality of the metal, afraid it will somehow contaminate his body. @@.vaelSpeech;"This is a very crude and poorly made piece... and peridot is not a particularly rare stone. Smelting it down is the only real value it holds..."@@ He pauses, his seven eyes fixated on the piece as if waiting for some sort of change. Finally, after several seconds, he speaks. @@.vaelSpeech;"I detect some traces of magic left in the piece. Perhaps a lingering blessing... still, ten pieces of gold is all that I am willing to offer."@@ <<set $wolfMedallion to false>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |SellWares][$money += 10]]</div>\You know you can't just come in and ask for this kind of item without a reasonable explanation to back up your purchase. This item can surely be used to get close to that dragon, but the sexual nature of the product might arouse some suspicion. @@.playerSpeech;"I have been tasked with dealing with a dragon, and its mate. I had hoped you would be able to provide me with some... aid."@@ The Forgemaster just lets out a sigh. @@.vaelSpeech;"Just state what you want and stop dancing around it."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"I had thought a flask of concentrated pheromones would help me get close to them without too much trouble."@@ <<if $boughtDeerPh is true>> @@.vaelSpeech;"Pheromones again, hm?" The Forgemaster remarks, a hint of curiosity in his voice. @@.vaelSpeech;"Aren't there other methods suitable for this kind of job? One that isn't so... distasteful to produce?" He looks directly at you, scratching his spiky chin thoughtfully. @@.vaelSpeech;"I can shut down my sense of smell when needed, but you... don't you find the stench revolting?"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"It's not very pleasant, but last time it worked like a charm. If it ain't broken, don't fix it."@@ @@.vaelSpeech;"I suppose. Fine. Same as before, thirty pieces of gold for this other flask."@@ <<else>> @@.vaelSpeech;"I see. And yes, I can provide what you are looking for, but it is not cheap. Producing this kind of elixir is always a most... unpleasant business..."@@ He grumbles, one of his metallic fists clenching as if he just remembered something particularly distasteful. @@.vaelSpeech;"Thirty pieces of gold for a vial of... concentrated extract."@@ <</if>> <<if $money >= 30>><div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Buy the item (30) |BuyDrg]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\<<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">@@.fadedLink; [[Buy the item (30) |BuyDrg]]@@</div>\<</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |PurchaseWares]]</div>\You know you can't just come in and ask for this kind of item without a reasonable explanation to back up your purchase. This item can surely be used to lure that deer, but the sexual nature of the product might arouse some suspicion. @@.playerSpeech;"So, I found this particular contract Elisa didn't deal with before her... unfortunate disappearance."@@ You begin, pulling out the contract for The Forgemaster to read. @@.vaelSpeech;"I don't have all day. What do you want?"@@ He says, not bothering to look at the paper. @@.playerSpeech;"These creatures are particularly elusive, I had thought a flask of concentrated pheromones would help me get close enough to deal the killing blow."@@ <<if $boughtDragonPh is true>> @@.vaelSpeech;"Pheromones again, hm?"@@ The Forgemaster remarks, a hint of curiosity in his voice. @@ @@.vaelSpeech;"Aren't there other methods suitable for this kind of job? One that isn't so... distasteful to produce?"@@ He looks directly at you, scratching his spiky chin thoughtfully. @@.vaelSpeech;"I can shut down my sense of smell when needed, but you... don't you find the stench revolting?"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"It's not very pleasant, but last time it worked like a charm. If it ain't broken, don't fix it."@@ @@.vaelSpeech;"I suppose. Fine. Same as before, thirty pieces of gold for this other flask."@@ <<else>> @@.vaelSpeech;"I see. And yes, I can provide what you are looking for, but it is not cheap. Producing this kind of elixir is always a most... unpleasant business..."@@ He grumbles, one of his metallic fists clenching as if he just remembered something particularly distasteful. @@.vaelSpeech;"Thirty pieces of gold for a vial of... concentrated extract."@@ <</if>> <<if $money >= 30>><div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Buy the item (30) |BuyDeer][$money -=30]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\<<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">@@.fadedLink; [[Buy the item (30) |BuyDeer]]@@<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\<</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |PurchaseWares]]</div>\The Forgemaster disappears from sight for a good couple minutes, but soon you hear his metallic legs making their way back to you. On his hand, a good meter away from the rest of his body, is a small, clear bottle with a cork on top, filled with some kind of viscous, translucent liquid. "Here. Take it." <div class="passCheck">You pay Vael his 30 gold coins and take the vial, it has a rather strong, musky scent.<img src="Images/Icons/Item.png"></div>\ <<set $boughtDragonPh to true>> <<set $dragonPheromones to true>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |PurchaseWares][$money -=30]]</div>\The Forgemaster disappears from sight for a good couple minutes, but soon you hear his metallic legs making their way back to you. On his hand, a good meter away from the rest of his body, is a small, clear bottle with a cork on top, filled with some kind of viscous, translucent liquid. @@.vaelSpeech;"Here. Take it."@@ <div class="passCheck">You pay Vael his 30 gold coins and take the vial, it has a rather strong, musky scent.<img src="Images/Icons/Item.png"></div>\ <<set $boughtDeerPh to true>> <<set $deerPheromones to true>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |PurchaseWares]]</div>\<<nobr>> <<set $dreamChance to $baseDreamChance>> <<set $nightmareChance to $baseNightmareChance>> <<set $dreamChance += (3 * $eyeSeeing)>> <<set $nightmareChance += (4 * $eyeSeeing)>> <<if $dreamChance < 0>> <<set $dreamChance to 0>> <</if>> <<if $nightmareChance < 0>> <<set $nightmareChance to 0>> <</if>> <div class="header-container"> <div class="left-section">Level: $playerLevel</div> <div class="middle-section">INVENTORY</div> <div class="right-section"><img src="Images/Classes/FighterClass.png"></div> </div> <div class="long-container"> <div class="left-long-container"> <div class="stat-container-inventory"> <img src="Images/Icons/Coins.png" > <span class="stat-inventory">Gold: $money</span></div> <div class="stat-container-inventory"> <img src="Images/Icons/General.png" > <span class="stat-inventory">Hunter's Expertise: $hunterSkill</span></div> <div class="stat-container-inventory"> <img src="Images/Icons/Heal.png"> <span class="stat-inventory">Curative Magic: $healSkill</span> </div> <div class="stat-container-inventory"> <img src="Images/Icons/MindMagic.png"> <span class="stat-inventory">Mind Magic: $magicSkill</span> </div> <div class="stat-container-inventory"> <img src="Images/Icons/PregIcon.png" > <span class="stat-inventory">Cum Potency: $cumSkill</span> </div> <div class="stat-container-inventory"> <img src="Images/Icons/Charisma.png"> <span class="stat-inventory">Charisma: $charismaSkill</span> </div> <div class="stat-container-inventory"> <img src="Images/Icons/Dream.png"> <span class="stat-inventory">Dream Chance: $dreamChance </span> </div> </div> <div class="right-long-container"> <div class="stat-container-inventory"> <img src="Images/Icons/Days.png"> <span class="stat-inventory">Days: $days</span> </div> <<if $darkDevotion <= 0>> <div class="stat-container-inventory"> <img src="Images/Icons/Death.png"> <span class="stat-inventory">Kills: $playerKills</span> </div> <<else>> <div class="stat-container-inventory"> <img src="Images/Icons/Devotion.png"> <span class="stat-inventory">Dark Devotion: $darkDevotion</span> </div> <</if>> <div class="stat-container-inventory"> <<if $karma <= $karmaToEvil>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/EvilKarma.png"> <<else>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/Karma.png"> <</if>> <span class="stat-inventory">Karma: $karma</span> </div> <div class="stat-container-inventory"> <img src="Images/Icons/Pregnancies.png"> <span class="stat-inventory">Impregnations: $impregnations</span> </div> <div class="stat-container-inventory"> <img src="Images/Icons/Reputation.png"> <span class="stat-inventory">Reputation: $reputation</span> </div> <div class="stat-container-inventory"> <img src="Images/Icons/Eye.png"> <span class="stat-inventory">Cosmic Insanity: $eyeSeeing</span> </div> <div class="stat-container-inventory"> <img src="Images/Icons/Nightmare.png"> <span class="stat-inventory">Nightmare Chance: $nightmareChance</span> </div> </div> </div> <<if $theIndomitableHumanSpirit is true>><div class="inner-container"> <img src="Images/Icons/Karma.png" class="panel-image"> <span class="panel-text">[[The indomitable human spirit |IndomitableSpirit]]</span> </div> <<else>>\ <<if $elixirExertion is 1>><div class="inner-container"> <img src="Images/Icons/Potion.png" class="panel-image"> <span class="panel-text">[[Elixir of Exertion |ElixirExertionInv]]</span> </div>\<</if>>
