<<if $deerStone is true>><div class="inner-container"><img src="Images/Icons/Travelstone.png" class="panel-image"> <span class="panel-text">[[Travelstone (Deer Girl) |Travelstone]]</span> </div>\<</if>> <<if $wolfStone is true>><div class="inner-container"> <img src="Images/Icons/Travelstone.png" class="panel-image"> <span class="panel-text">[[Travelstone (Wolf Girl) |Travelstone]] </span> </div>\<</if>> <<if $sixEightStone is true>><div class="inner-container"> <img src="Images/Icons/Travelstone.png" class="panel-image"> <span class="panel-text">[[Travelstone (SCP-682) |Travelstone]]</span> </div><</if>> <<if $azriStone is true>><div class="inner-container"> <img src="Images/Icons/Travelstone.png" class="panel-image"> <span class="panel-text">[[Travelstone (Azri'Elis) |TravelstoneAzri]]</span> </div>\<</if>> <<if $wolyrnStone is true>><div class="inner-container"> <img src="Images/Icons/Travelstone.png" class="panel-image"> <span class="panel-text">[[Travelstone (Wolyrn) |Travelstone]]</span> </div>\<</if>> <<if $wyvernStone is true>><div class="inner-container"> <img src="Images/Icons/Travelstone.png" class="panel-image"> <span class="panel-text">[[Travelstone (Wyvern) |Travelstone]]</span> </div>\<</if>> <<if $furyStone is true>><div class="inner-container"> <img src="Images/Icons/Travelstone.png" class="panel-image"> <span class="panel-text">[[Travelstone (Light Fury) |Travelstone]]</span> </div><</if>> <<if $naafiriStone is true>><div class="inner-container"> <img src="Images/Icons/Travelstone.png" class="panel-image"> <span class="panel-text">[[Travelstone (Naafiri) |Travelstone]]</span> </div><</if>> <<if $deerAntlers is true>><div class="inner-container"> <img src="Images/Icons/Item.png" class="panel-image"> <span class="panel-text">[[Deer Girl's Antlers |Antlers]]</span> </div><</if>> <<if $deerPheromones is true>><div class="inner-container"> <img src="Images/Icons/Potion.png" class="panel-image"> <span class="panel-text">[[Flask of Deer Pheromones |FlaskDeer]]</span> </div><</if>> <<if $wyvernEgg is true>><div class="inner-container"> <img src="Images/Icons/Item.png" class="panel-image"> <span class="panel-text">[[Wyvern Egg |WyvernEgg]]</span> </div><</if>> <<if $wolfMedallion is true>><div class="inner-container"> <img src="Images/Icons/Item.png" class="panel-image"> <span class="panel-text">[[Wolf Girl's Medallion |WolfMedallion]]</span> </div><</if>> <<if $voidEye is true>><div class="inner-container"> <img src="Images/Icons/Item.png" class="panel-image"> <span class="panel-text">[[Shard of Sight |VoidEye]]</span> </div><</if>> <<if $essenceFeaster is true>><div class="inner-container"> <img src="Images/Icons/EssenceFeaster.png" class="panel-image"> <span class="panel-text">[[Essence Feaster |EssenceFeaster]]</span> </div><</if>> <<if $orderArmor is true>><div class="inner-container"> <img src="Images/Icons/OrderArmor.png" class="panel-image"> <span class="panel-text">[[Armor of the Order |orderArmor]]</span> </div><</if>> <<if $firePotion is 1>><div class="inner-container"> <img src="Images/Icons/Potion.png" class="panel-image"> <span class="panel-text">[[Fire resist potion |FireResistPotion]]</span> </div><</if>> <<if $lasRifle is true>><div class="inner-container"> <img src="Images/Icons/LasRifle.png" class="panel-image"> <span class="panel-text">[[Laser Rifle |LaserRifle]]</span> </div><</if>> <<if $sixEightJobState is "small">><div class="inner-container"> <img src="Images/Icons/Item.png" class="panel-image"> <span class="panel-text">[[Sample of flesh (small) |FleshSample]]</span> </div><</if>> <<if $sixEightJobState is "medium">><div class="inner-container"> <img src="Images/Icons/Item.png" class="panel-image"> <span class="panel-text">[[Sample of flesh (medium) |FleshSample]]</span> </div><</if>> <<if $sixEightJobState is "big">><div class="inner-container"> <img src="Images/Icons/Item.png" class="panel-image"> <span class="panel-text">[[Sample of flesh (big) |FleshSample]]</span> </div><</if>> <<if $darkinDagger is true>><div class="inner-container"> <img src="Images/Icons/Item.png" class="panel-image"> <span class="panel-text">[[The Darkin Dagger |DarkinDagger]]</span> </div><</if>> <<if $dreamcatcher is 1>><div class="inner-container"> <img src="Images/Icons/Item.png" class="panel-image"> <span class="panel-text">[[Dreamcatcher |Dreamcatcher]]</span> </div><</if>> <<if $dreamstone is 1>><div class="inner-container"> <img src="Images/Icons/Travelstone.png" class="panel-image"> <span class="panel-text">[[Dreamstone |Dreamstone]]</span> </div><</if>> <<if $pendant is 1>><div class="inner-container"> <img src="Images/Icons/Item.png" class="panel-image"> <span class="panel-text">[[Curious-looking pendant |PendantI]]</span> </div><</if>> <<if $vaelEffigy is 1>><div class="inner-container"> <img src="Images/Icons/Item.png" class="panel-image"> <span class="panel-text">[[Odd effigy |Effigy]]</span> </div><</if>> <</if>> <<if $flowerItem is true>><div class="inner-container"> <img src="Images/Icons/Item.png" class="panel-image"> <span class="panel-text">[[Bunch of flowers |Flowers]]</span> </div> <</if>> <div class = "close-Inventory"> <<link "Endings">> <div class="stat-container"> Endings </div> <<goto InventoryEndings>> <</link>> </div> <div class = "close-Inventory"> <<link "Contracts">> <div class="stat-container"> Endings </div> <<goto Contracts>> <</link>> </div> <div class = "close-Inventory"> <<link "Close Inventory">> <div class="stat-container"> Close Inventory </div> <<set $inventoryOpen to false>> <<goto $lastPassageName>> <</link>> </div> <</nobr>>This travelstone can be used one time to link a portal from your home to the world where its assigned marked target lies. [[Go back |Inventory]]This bigger, more powerful travelstone can be used to create a portal between your home, and Darthax, the prison where Azri'Elis is contained. You will have as much time as you need. [[Go back |Inventory]]A gold amulet, masterfully crafted by Vael. In the center of it lies a perfectly round stone that reflects no light whatsoever... This amulet can give the wearer faint visions, glimpses and feelings of other realities, but these rarely stick in the memory. [[Go back |Inventory]]The Essence Feaster, the weapon assigned to you when you officially became a High Hunter. This dreadful piece of art was, like many others in your order, forged by Vael. The blade is long and sharp, but the true threat comes from the demon that was bound to it long ago, which, when unleashed, can be used to siphon and feast on the energy of living beings, rendering them docile and ready for slaughter. [[Go back |Inventory]]A suit of armor that despite its size and composition seems to weigh nothing. A hunter must be able to move quickly and quietly, and a lot of their equipment was forged to do just that. It doesn't matter how many layers of enhanced steel you wear if a dragon's fire breath can cook you alive before you can react. [[Go back |Inventory]]A pair of antlers, still bloody and wet with the blood of the creature who once owned them. They are soft, covered in velvet. [[Go back |Inventory]]A flask of concentrated deer pheromones, tightly sealed and filled with a transparent liquid that somehow manages to smell strong even through the glass. [[Go back |Inventory]]Composed of a brass chain and some kind of green gemstone. It still carries the soft scent of its last wearer, and a couple of grey strands of fur stuck here and there. [[Go back |Inventory]]<<if $playerEnergy == "rested">> <<switch $currentContract>> <<case "wyvern">>\ Not the first time you’ve faced wyverns… seems like something you can do. You take the corresponding travelstone and insert it into the circular socket in the middle of the floor. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Use the travelstone |TravelBasic]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTeleport.png"></div>\ <<case "reptile">>\ Time to take care of another of Vael's little pet projects. What kind of monstrosity awaits you this time? <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Use the travelstone |TravelBasic]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTeleport.png"></div>\ <<case "wolf">>\ This contract should be easy money, and hopefully, fun to do as well... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Use the travelstone |TravelBasic]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTeleport.png"></div>\ <<case "wolyrn">>\ You take out the travelstone, you are quite ready for a new adventure. Hopefully, it won't be the last..\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Use the travelstone |TravelBasic]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTeleport.png"></div>\ <<case "lightfury">>\ You take out the travelstone, you are quite ready for a new adventure. Hopefully, it won't be the last..\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Use the travelstone |TravelBasic]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTeleport.png"></div>\ <<case "deathclaw">>\ A deathclaw... the name is quite ominous, but you hope its more a title rather than an accurate description of the creature. Oh well...\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Use the travelstone |TravelBasic]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTeleport.png"></div>\ <<case "naafiri">>\ You take out the travelstone, and examine it for a moment. Damlon said this contract would be easy, but somehow, you have a bad feeling about it... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[On second thought... |PortalRoom]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Use the travelstone |TravelBasic]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTeleport.png"></div>\ <<case "deer">>\ This contract has been up for too long, so… it's about time you give it closure. You take the corresponding travelstone and insert it into the circular socket in the middle of the floor. You brace yourself as the room fills with a bright light and you begin to feel the pull of the planar shift. The world around you is distorted and changes in color, and soon, you are surrounded by the cold void. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |TravelBasic]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTeleport.png"></div>\ <<case "azri">>\ <<if $days is 0>>\ Azri'Elis it is, then. The mere prospect of facing a creature as powerful as her is enough to make you sweat all over. But your admission as a high hunter has been a rushed decision, surely taken because of Elisa’s disappearance, and you are afraid not performing the way you are expected will result in another taking your place. And you don't have an intention of going back as a meager second-class hunter. <<else>>\ Azri'Elis is it, then. You won't deny that the prospect of facing a creature as powerful as her is not a particularly pleasing one, but it has to be done. You've had plenty of fun as it is, but you know you also have real duties to attend to. If she manages to escape her prison and you are not there to face her, you are as good as dead anyway. <</if>>\ You prepare your essential equipment, and soon you are ready to depart. The travelstone you are about to use is not like regular ones; this one is used as a key between two different receptors, so you won't be recalled after a short while like with the other ones. Whatever happens, you’ll be stuck in Darthax until you make your way back, one way or another. The portal room seems more silent than usual, the ancient stone disk and its runes glowing in eerie, faint light. It's as if it knows of the great danger you are about to face... @@.important;...Are you truly ready?@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Yes |AzriYes]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTeleport.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[No |AzriNo]]</div>\ <<default>>\ <<nobr>><<script>>\ alert("what kind of horrible spaghetti code brought us here...");\ <</script>><</nobr>>\ <</switch>>\ <<else>> <div class="failCheck">\ You are feeling way too tired for another interdimensional adventure. You should get some sleep first. <<set $currentContract to "">> <img src="Images/Icons/FailSleep.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |PortalRoom]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go directly to sleep |Sleep][$quickSleep = true]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedSleep.png"></div>\ <</if>>@@.title;AUNDUIR, OUTER FOREST@@ <<set $deerJobState to 2>>\ <<set $playerEnergy to "tired">>\ <<set $deerStone to false >>\ <<set $temporaryImpregnations to $impregnations >>\ <<set $temporaryKills to $playerKills >>\ <<set $deerStone to false >>\ <<if $voidEye is true>> <div class="passCheck">\ Elina trotted through the lush forest, her delicate deer hooves barely making a sound as she looked around for that juicy, juicy, fruit she knew grew nearby. Many didn't dare venture so far away from their home, but it had been decades since they had seen the last human, or other equally fearsome predators. The Ailthron forest was a safe haven, and she wasn't scared to wander around a little. Besides, she appreciated the moments to herself away from the rest of the herd and their sometimes obnoxious nonsense. Here, all alone, she could let her mind wander, she could focus on her thoughts and simply enjoy the tranquility of the forest, without having to worry about anyone. <img src="Images/Icons/Item.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ <<if $eyeSeeing >=3>>\ <div class="passCheck">\ Elina trotted through the lush forest, her delicate deer hooves barely making a sound as she looked around for that juicy, juicy, fruit she knew grew nearby. Many didn't dare venture so far away from their home, but it had been decades since they had seen the last human, or other equally fearsome predators. The Ailthron forest was a safe haven, and she wasn't scared to wander around a little. Besides, she appreciated the moments to herself away from the rest of the herd and their sometimes obnoxious nonsense. Here, all alone, she could let her mind wander, she could focus on her thoughts and simply enjoy the tranquility of the forest, without having to worry about anyone. <img src="Images/Icons/Eye.png"></div>\ <</if>>\ <</if>>\ The shadows of the void dissipate around you, and you find yourself standing in the midst of a lush forest. The trees here are tall and thick, their branches curving gracefully toward each other like a canopy over your head. The ground is covered in mossy green grass, and the endless chirping of birds and insects muffle every other sound. @@.playerSpeech;"Oh well, this is not half bad,"@@ you think out loud. It looks like a greatplace to set up a picnic indeed! Also a great hunting ground… Speaking of which, you can feel your prey's mark already, seems like even after all this time the travelstone’s link to her hasn't been lost. She cannot be far away from you. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Try to get her attention; Call out for help |CallOutHelp]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Follow the trail |FollowDeerTrail]]</div>\ @@.title;REYIL, SWIFTBROOK'S OUTSKIRTS@@ <<set $wolyrnStone to false >>\ <<set $wolyrnJobState to 2>>\ <<set $playerEnergy to "tired">> <<set $temporaryImpregnations to $impregnations >>\ <<set $temporaryKills to $playerKills >>\ <<if $voidEye is true>> <div class="passCheck">\ It was another tranquil night in Swiftbrook, as they usually were. The gentle sun had given way to a beautiful starry sky, the moon rising slowly and bathing the village in its silvery glow. The air, clean and sweet, carried the light scent of the many wildflowers that bloomed in the meadows nearby. And yet... she couldn't shake off a creeping feeling of foreboding, an unshakeable sense that something terrible would happen that very night. She didn't really have any reason to believe such a thing; since she had been taken into this little village as a child, nothing even remotely dangerous had happened, nothing even remotely close to the horror she had experienced all those years ago. She of course had not forgotten it, and even now, she still woke up in the middle of the night, drenched in sweat, crying and screaming for a few seconds until the visions finally dissipated. Tonight, that dreadful dream had returned to her, with full force. She had seen it all again, the shadow-clad knight that had butchered her family and her whole village, the cold, lifeless, glowing red eyes, and the crimson blade that had cut through flesh and bone alike. Korvaax was its name… and it killed everything... and everyone. She had been the sole survivor, for some reason, she had been spared by the monster. The creature did not explain why, but she knew. She knew it had just been playing with her, she knew it just wanted to let her live with the horrible memories… until it came back for her one day. Nelia gritted her teeth and took a deep breath before wrapping herself in her blankets one more time. Hard as she tried, sleep seemed to elude her. Something felt… wrong. It was as if the nightmare had followed her back this time. She had to make sure everything was alright. So, in an effort to quieten her mind so that she could go back to bed, she got dressed and made her way toward the northern edge of the village, and indeed, barely a moment later, she felt it. They say that scent is the most powerful trigger of memories, and this one was simply unmistakable— sulfur and smoke, death about to be delivered. As a tidal wave, more painful recollections surged forth and quickly engulfed Nelia's heart in a torrent of anguish, transporting her back to that fateful night, forever carved in her mind. Its eyes... It had been so long ago that details as important as the exact way her family used to like had become fuzzy. But those eyes... remained etched in her mind with chilling clarity. Nelia hesitated for a moment, her trembling fingers tightening around the handle of her kamas blade before she took a deep breath, more of the unmistakable scent filling her nostrils. She had prepared for this moment all her life, and now that the time had finally come, now that she had the chance to fight back, to avenge her family and her village... she was scared. She had every reason to be. But what if she tried to run? She couldn't risk it coming anywhere near her new home. How many would perish, this time? The young woman swallowed, and then, started walking toward the source of the smell, her muffled footsteps the only sound that could be heard through the dead of night. <img src="Images/Icons/Item.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ <<if $eyeSeeing >=3>>\ <div class="passCheck">\ It was another tranquil night in Swiftbrook, as they usually were. The gentle sun had given way to a beautiful starry sky, the moon rising slowly and bathing the village in its silvery glow. The air, clean and sweet, carried the light scent of the many wildflowers that bloomed in the meadows nearby. And yet... she couldn't shake off a creeping feeling of foreboding, an unshakeable sense that something terrible would happen that very night. She didn't really have any reason to believe such a thing; since she had been taken into this little village as a child, nothing even remotely dangerous had happened, nothing even remotely close to the horror she had experienced all those years ago. She of course had not forgotten it, and even now, she still woke up in the middle of the night, drenched in sweat, crying and screaming for a few seconds until the visions finally dissipated. Tonight, that dreadful dream had returned to her, with full force. She had seen it all again, the shadow-clad knight that had butchered her family and her whole village, the cold, lifeless, glowing red eyes, and the crimson blade that had cut through flesh and bone alike. Korvaax was its name… and it killed everything... and everyone. She had been the sole survivor, for some reason, she had been spared by the monster. The creature did not explain why, but she knew. She knew it had just been playing with her, she knew it just wanted to let her live with the horrible memories… until it came back for her one day. Nelia gritted her teeth and took a deep breath before wrapping herself in her blankets one more time. Hard as she tried, sleep seemed to elude her. Something felt… wrong. It was as if the nightmare had followed her back this time. She had to make sure everything was alright. So, in an effort to quieten her mind so that she could go back to bed, she got dressed and made her way toward the northern edge of the village, and indeed, barely a moment later, she felt it. They say that scent is the most powerful trigger of memories, and this one was simply unmistakable— sulfur and smoke, death about to be delivered. As a tidal wave, more painful recollections surged forth and quickly engulfed Nelia's heart in a torrent of anguish, transporting her back to that fateful night, forever carved in her mind. Its eyes... It had been so long ago that details as important as the exact way her family used to like had become fuzzy. But those eyes... remained etched in her mind with chilling clarity. Nelia hesitated for a moment, her trembling fingers tightening around the handle of her kamas blade before she took a deep breath, more of the unmistakable scent filling her nostrils. She had prepared for this moment all her life, and now that the time had finally come, now that she had the chance to fight back, to avenge her family and her village... she was scared. She had every reason to be. But what if she tried to run? She couldn't risk it coming anywhere near her new home. How many would perish, this time? The young woman swallowed, and then, started walking toward the source of the smell, her muffled footsteps the only sound that could be heard through the dead of night. <img src="Images/Icons/Eye.png"></div>\ <</if>>\ <</if>>\ After an excruciatingly long instant traveling through the vast emptiness of the void, you are more than relieved to find yourself standing in a beautiful field of silvery grass and glowing flowers that would be enough to illuminate your surroundings had the moon not already done so. A light breeze rustles the tall blades of grass, carrying with it the fragrance of the flowers that fill the air with a pleasant aroma... <<set $wolyrnJobState to 2>>\ <<if $flowerItem is false>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Pick up a bunch of flowers |PickUpFlowers2][$flowerItem = true]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedItem.png"></div>\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go find your prey |GoFindWolyrn]]</div>\@@.title;THE ISLE OF BERK@@ <<set $furyJobState to 2>>\ <<set $playerEnergy to "tired">>\ <<set $furyStone to false>>\ <<set $temporaryImpregnations to $impregnations >>\ <<set $temporaryKills to $playerKills >>\ <<set $currentImageContract to "Fury">>\ You emerge from the interdimensional void right before a quiet, moonlit cave. The sound of crickets and other insects can be heard outside of it and looking back, you are greeted with a peaceful forest scene; the trees swaying gently in the night breeze. The travelstone guides you to the interior of the cave, though. It makes sense, dragons seem to have a thing for caves, natural constructions fit for their large forms. Luckily, you don't need any source of light to see in the dark, for the cave roof is full of holes and the rays of the moonlight filter in through them, illuminating the entire hollow in an almost romantic manner. Compared to the many other hellholes you’ve been… this one does look slightly inviting, in a way. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Descend deeper into the cave |DescendCave]]</div>\@@.title;AUNDUIR, RIVATHOL RUINS@@ <<set $playerEnergy to "tired">>\ <<set $wyvernJobState to 2>>\ <<set $wyvernStone to false>> <<set $temporaryImpregnations to $impregnations >>\ <<set $temporaryKills to $playerKills >>\ <<set $currentImageContract to "Wyvern">>\ Emerging from the void, you find yourself standing amidst a vast plain of grass, stretching as far as the eye can see. The moon is high in the sky, casting a silver glow over a small, ruined castle not far from your current location. Once a great fortress, now all that remains are a few open towers, crumbling walls, and dusty courtyards. But even if this decadent place would be a depressing home for even the most meager of nobles, life has still found its way inside it would seem. This must clearly be the ruins Damlon talked about… if for no other reason, this seems to be the only structure within miles. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Make your way to the ruins |CastleWyvern]]</div>\ @@.title;AUNDUIR, SPRINGLEAF GLADE@@ <<set $wolfStone to false >>\ <<set $currentImageContract to "Wolf">>\ <<set $temporaryImpregnations to $impregnations >>\ <<set $temporaryKills to $playerKills >>\ <<if $voidEye is true>>\ <div class="passCheck">\ She ran as fast as she possibly could, ignoring the dense vegetation that brushed against her legs and the tangled roots that threatened to trip her. Their howls echoed through the forest, and they were getting closer by the second. Her heart raced as she pushed herself harder and harder, her injured leg throbbing with every step, and longbow bouncing awkwardly across her back, its massive size and weight causing her discomfort with each stride. She tried her best to ignore it all, she knew she had to keep going, had to find a way to escape before they caught up to her. The panic was getting to her, so she risked a glance behind. The wolves were gaining in on her. Fear surged through her, and she redoubled her efforts, but her tired legs couldn't keep up with the demands she was placing on them. @@.wolfSpeech;"No no no no no! Stay back!"@@ She yelped in fright, her voice barely audible over the sound of her heavy breathing and the snapping of branches. Just how had she managed to end up in this predicament? How could she be so stupid? Distracted as she was by the terrifying sight, she failed to notice a tree dangerously close to her, and although she could easily avoid it before barging into it, she didn't take into account the enormous longbow on her back… and it collided with the tree trunk, violently jerking her forward and causing her to lose her balance. She tumbled to the forest floor with a sharp cry of pain as her leg bore the brunt of the impact, and her head spun from the rough fall. She realized many things then. Firstly, the longbow was now broken in half, and the sight made her frightened heart drop. It was her father's longbow, and it had been the only tool they had to hunt in these unforgiving woods. Secondly, her injured leg was now in excruciating pain, throbbing with each heartbeat, and as she incorporated, she almost immediately fell down again. It became obvious she wasn't going to be able to walk. Lastly, and most pressingly, the wolves were quickly closing in, their growls growing louder until she could see them, their eyes gleaming in the darkness as they circled around her. The bow would be the last of her problems, for most likely, her father would never know of her again. Tears welled up in her eyes, mixing with the dirt and grime. @@.wolfSpeech;"Please, please, d-don’t hurt me…"@@ She stuttered, hoping they would somehow understand her language, but the wolves just growled at her, their tongues lolling out of their mouths as they took steps forward, a glimmer of… desire in their eyes. Then, the miracle happened. As if an invisible sword had sliced through space itself, a crack opened before them, a wound in the fabric of reality. It pulsed for a moment, nothing happening, and then a figure popped out of it, looking completely disoriented and dizzy. Stumbling and nearly falling, it managed to catch itself and stand up, swaying slightly. @@.wolfSpeech;"...My savior..."@@ The girl whispered weakly, fascinated by what seemed to be divine intervention. It seemed to be a human, just like her adoptive father! <img src="Images/Icons/Item.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ <<if $eyeSeeing >=3>>\ <div class="passCheck">\ She ran as fast as she possibly could, ignoring the dense vegetation that brushed against her legs and the tangled roots that threatened to trip her. Their howls echoed through the forest, and they were getting closer by the second. Her heart raced as she pushed herself harder and harder, her injured leg throbbing with every step, and longbow bouncing awkwardly across her back, its massive size and weight causing her discomfort with each stride. She tried her best to ignore it all, she knew she had to keep going, had to find a way to escape before they caught up to her. The panic was getting to her, so she risked a glance behind. The wolves were gaining in on her. Fear surged through her, and she redoubled her efforts, but her tired legs couldn't keep up with the demands she was placing on them. @@.wolfSpeech;"No no no no no! Stay back!"@@ She yelped in fright, her voice barely audible over the sound of her heavy breathing and the snapping of branches. Just how had she managed to end up in this predicament? How could she be so stupid? Distracted as she was by the terrifying sight, she failed to notice a tree dangerously close to her, and although she could easily avoid it before barging into it, she didn't take into account the enormous longbow on her back… and it collided with the tree trunk, violently jerking her forward and causing her to lose her balance. She tumbled to the forest floor with a sharp cry of pain as her leg bore the brunt of the impact, and her head spun from the rough fall. She realized many things then. Firstly, the longbow was now broken in half, and the sight made her frightened heart drop. It was her father's longbow, and it had been the only tool they had to hunt in these unforgiving woods. Secondly, her injured leg was now in excruciating pain, throbbing with each heartbeat, and as she incorporated, she almost immediately fell down again. It became obvious she wasn't going to be able to walk. Lastly, and most pressingly, the wolves were quickly closing in, their growls growing louder until she could see them, their eyes gleaming in the darkness as they circled around her. The bow would be the last of her problems, for most likely, her father would never know of her again. Tears welled up in her eyes, mixing with the dirt and grime. @@.wolfSpeech;"Please, please, d-don’t hurt me…"@@ She stuttered, hoping they would somehow understand her language, but the wolves just growled at her, their tongues lolling out of their mouths as they took steps forward, a glimmer of… desire in their eyes. Then, the miracle happened. As if an invisible sword had sliced through space itself, a crack opened before them, a wound in the fabric of reality. It pulsed for a moment, nothing happening, and then a figure popped out of it, looking completely disoriented and dizzy. Stumbling and nearly falling, it managed to catch itself and stand up, swaying slightly. @@.wolfSpeech;"...My savior..."@@ The girl whispered weakly, fascinated by what seemed to be divine intervention. It seemed to be a human, just like her adoptive father! <img src="Images/Icons/Eye.png"></div>\ <</if>>\ <</if>>\ @@.playerSpeech;"...I hate interdimensional travel,"@@ you mutter in annoyance, trying to steady yourself as the world spins around you. @@.playerSpeech;"Now... where the fuck is… where the fuck I…"@@ you look around, suddenly becoming aware your surroundings. Your prey is right before you, lying wounded on the forest floor, and a pack of wolves is circling around you both. Turns out that the ‘dangerous marksman’ is nothing more than a frightened girl, comically small compared to you, and her bow. You have a hard time imagining just how she is supposed to use it. Her fur is silver and her eyes are bright orange... she is quite beautiful, even in her current state, with fear clearly written all over her face and mud caked into her silvery hair. <img src="Images/Monsters/WolfFixed.png" class="resizable-image" alt="WolfImage"> Another of the many inaccurate markings from The Eye, it would seem, because there is no way this adorable anthropomorphic wolf can be a threat to anyone other than herself. Oh well. You won't complain. Your hand instinctively goes to the hilt of your Essence Feaster, but you hesitate for a moment. Surprised you were able to sneak in on them, the wolves bark and growl, their teeth bared as they try to scare you off. You assess the situation, unsure of what to do. Dealing with a pack of ordinary wolves is child's play to you but the devil inside you whispers other… <strong>tempting ideas.</strong> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Fight the wolves |FightWolves]]<div class="numberStat">0</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Just watch the scene unfold |WatchWolves][$karma -=1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedEvilKarma.png"></div>\ <<set $wolfJobState to 2>>\ <<set $playerEnergy to "tired">>\No, not yet. You step back from the ominous disk on the floor and put the travel stone back in its place, away from the socket that would have sent you to the depths of Darthax. Surely the prison that has been containing Azri'Elis for a century can hold her just a bit longer? Just long enough for you to properly prepare... <<if $days is 0>>\ Because let's face it, what difference would you make by yourself right now? <</if>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue|PortalRoom]]</div>\Yes, yes you are ready. This shouldn't be postponed any longer. You introduce the sparkling gem into the circular socket in the middle of the disk and brace yourself as the room fills with a bright light and you begin to feel the pull of the planar shift. The world around you is distorted and changes in color, and you are taken far away. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |AzriIntro]]</div>\@@.title;DARTHAX@@ <<set $temporaryImpregnations to $impregnations >>\ <<set $temporaryKills to $playerKills >>\ <<set $azriStone to false>>\ Your vision returns and the world around you is once more familiar, yet different. You have not been here in a long while, and you had forgotten just how utterly alien this prison is. Truthfully, it was not made by humans; you merely appropriated and repurposed the ancient structure, and no amount of decoration and modification could ever cover up its true, inhuman origin. You find yourself in a circular room, standing above a rune-inscribed disk, not unlike the one in the portal room back in your own personal pocket dimension. Unlike the cramped, insignificant room you come from, this one is vast, much, much bigger than it really needs to be. The gigantic size only further amplifies the sense of emptiness and isolation that clings to every last corner of this prison. The walls are a smooth, dark gray, with nothing to indicate their true material, and the ceiling is almost invisible in the distance. Heavy, echoing footsteps bring you out of your trance, and you turn around to face the ancient guardians of the prison, what remains of them at least. Once humans not unlike you, now little more than husks, animated suits of ancient armor that don't hold even an echo of the life they once had. It was... necessary, or so you’ve been told. No mortal can survive long here, not just because of the countless things that lurk within the deepest confines of the prison, but also because of the creeping madness that seems to come out from the structure itself. They aren't undead, but they certainly aren't alive anymore. Automatons, made of steel and bound souls. The two guardians approach you, but their weapons are not drawn. They bow in unison, their plates of armor scraping against each other, and one of them speaks with a hollow, metallic voice. @@.armorSpeech;"Hunter. It has been long. You are needed. Once more."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |ContinueDarthax]]</div>\ <<set $newDreams to ["azri1"]>> <<set $possibleDreams to $possibleDreams.concat($newDreams)>>You make your way to the castle, grass rustling in your wake all the way to the entrance, and there you stay low. A wyvern can be a very dangerous foe, after all, so you'd prefer for her to remain unaware of your presence, at least until you can get a feel of the situation. But as you focus on your senses, you immediately catch something, a succession of groans and whimpers, coming from inside the ruined main hall. @@.playerSpeech;"So she is hurt..."@@ you mutter to yourself, as you quietly slip through the gate and into the hall. Before you stands… or rather lies, the source of the noise, and your prey. A small wyvern; her body is not bigger than a horse, even if her wings make her seem larger. Her scales are a soft red, and only her black horns and claws contrast that image. <img src="Images/Monsters/WyvernFixed.png" class="resizable-image" alt="WyvernImage"> She is in evident pain, on her back, legs spread wide, claws digging into the cracked stone as she searches for leverage. It becomes apparent to you why she is in such discomfort. She's trying to lay an egg, but it seems to be too large for her to pass. You can see the effort she is putting to push the enormous object out, muscles taut and trembling with effort, breathing ragged, and her eyes squeezed shut. She's so focused on the task she hasn't even noticed you yet, and you are fairly close. You take another step forward and it's then that she realizes she is not alone, her big, slitted eyes widening in shock before they narrow once more. The creature opens her muzzle and hisses at you, hoping it will somehow intimidate you. She can't even breathe fire, and she is in no position to fight... When that, obviously, does not work, she tries to recoil and squirm away from you, but her strength is failing her after hours of painful labor, and she only manages to wriggle weakly. Eyes filled with fear despite the little act she is trying to put up she is perfectly aware of just how dire the situation is for her. She knows just how dangerous humans can be, and now that she can't even fly, her primary evasion tool, she is entirely at your mercy. If you wanted to end her life, you could easily do so… <<if $karma <= $karmaToEvil>>\ @@.fadedLink; <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedCross.png">[[Help her push the egg out |PushEgg]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedHeal.png"><img src="Images/Icons/RedKarma.png"></div>@@\ <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Help her push the egg out |PushEgg]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedHeal.png"><img src="Images/Icons/RedKarma.png"></div>\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Use your weapon to bind her |BindWyvern]]<div class="numberStat">0</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"><img src="Images/Icons/RedEvilKarma.png"></div>\But you have no such intentions. Even if a voice inside of you is screaming to just take advantage of the poor creature while she is helpless and vulnerable, you can’t help but feel a sense of pity. You raise up your hands, and lower to your knees, trying to look as non-threatening as possible. @@.playerSpeech;"Hey, don't be afraid. I'm here to help"@@ You are aware that she may not comprehend your words, but you hope that your tone and demeanor are enough to calm her down. As you move closer, one tiny step at a time, her hissing and growling fade into quiet, labored breathing. She never takes her wary eyes out of you, still unsure of your intentions, but seeing you are not stepping back, she just resigns to whatever fate you may bring. (Until you are within biting range at the very least…) You reach out a hand, placing it gently on her side, just below where her wing joins with the torso. The wyvern flinches at your touch but doesn't lash out, only making another whimpering noise. @@.playerSpeech;"It's alright,"@@ you say in your most soothing voice. @@.playerSpeech;"I'm not going to hurt you. Really."@@ You stroke the wyvern's scales gently, hoping to reassure her. As you feel them underneath your palm you realize the intense, almost feverish heat that seems to radiate from them. Slowly, wanting her to be fully aware of every single one of your movements and not to scare her, you guide your hand toward the wyvern's tail, and then you apply just the slightest bit of pressure to spread the horizontal slit from where the egg needs to come out. You can see it inside, an enormous object of hard, opaque material. Just how on earth can this creature gestate an egg of such size? You can't even begin to figure out the series of events that led her here. Focusing on the task ahead, you keep one of your hands firm there and move the other over to her abdomen, where you press down gently, feeling the egg move ever so slightly. The wyvern grits her teeth, letting out a low whine, but doesn't attack you. You are not sure if it's because she is too tired to fight or simply because she knows you mean well, but either way, you decide to keep going. The sooner is out the better for her. @@.playerSpeech;"Just give me a few more moments and I'll get this thing out of you, alright?"@@ She doesn't give out a response you can pick up as a positive, but her compliance is enough. You climb up atop her, sitting on her chest and using your knees to keep her legs spread apart before you press down harder on her abdomen, your whole weight being applied for more pressure. The distressed animal underneath you lets out a loud cry of pain and bucks her body, but you hold on tight, determined to get it out as soon as possible. @@.playerSpeech;"I know it hurts, but I need you to try and stay still for me."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |HealCheckWyvern][$karma +=1]]<div class="numberStat">1</div></div>\ With a dark grin, you take a menacing step toward the poor creature, her pitiful state not enough to stir even a sliver of pity within you. Your thoughts seem to somehow creep into her, as the creature attempts to scare you off with another hiss, her sharp teeth bared, and her body tensed, even more so. @@.playerSpeech;"That's all you can do? All bark, no bite… "@@ You say, chuckling darkly as you aim your Essence Feaster at the creature. @@.playerSpeech;"You don't even breathe fire, do you?"@@ <div class="passCheck">\ With a flick of your wrist, you set free the demon bound to your blade, and a flurry of ethereal chains soon entangle the pregnant beast. The crackling tendrils of energy coil around her wings and hindlegs, pinning her to the ground. She wouldn't have been able to fight you before, but now she’s just completely helpless, frozen in place like an insect on display. <img src="Images/Icons/General.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Get the object out, and then rape her |RapeWyvern][$karma -=1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedEvilKarma.png"></div>\ <<if $darkDevotion <=0>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Kill her |KillWyvern][$karma -=1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png"><img src="Images/Icons/RedEvilKarma.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Kill her, in the name of the Dark Lady |KillWyvernDarkLady][$darkDevotion +=1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png"><img src="Images/Icons/RedDevotion.png"></div>\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Shove the egg back inside her |TortureWyvern][$karma -=1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedEvilKarma.png"></div>\ Your grin only widens as you step closer to her, delighted by her palpable fear, until you are close enough to reach out to her partially spread slit, your fingers diving inside until they rest on the hard shell of the egg stuck inside her. @@.playerSpeech;"That looks painful... don't you think it would be best if we just get it back inside you?"@@ You whisper to the trembling beast before you start to apply a little pressure to it. Not much, just enough to keep it from moving any further despite her strong labor contractions, and considering just how sore her insides are by now, any minimal amount of force is more than enough to cause a considerable deal of pain. You stroke her belly with your other hand, rubbing your thumb over her soon-to-be full womb, and as her screams grow louder, you simply push harder. The oval-shaped form now begins to reverse direction sliding back down inch by agonizing inch until it bumps against her exhausted cervix, which has been forced open painfully wide not too long ago to pass it. @@.playerSpeech;"That's it, take it back inside."@@ The wyvern's eyes flutter close as she lets out a series of agonized screeches, no longer hostile, just terrified. Her suffering does nothing to deter you, though, if anything, it only makes you exert more force, pushing the object further into her body until, during one of her uterine contractions, you finally manage to make it slip inside her with an audible, wet pop. Almost immediately, the wyvern lets out a piercing screech, almost fainting from the intense, sudden pain that nearly overwhelms her senses. The egg is now safely nestled in her womb, right where it belongs and you take a moment to appreciate the sight of her bump. Your hand rests gently on her swollen belly, giving it a gentle pat. The wyvern simply crying and whimpering at your feet, what progress she'd managed to accomplish undone by your foul actions... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Rape her |RapeWyvernTorture]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedEvilKarma.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Keep torturing her |TortureWyvern2][$karma -=1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png"><img src="Images/Icons/RedEvilKarma.png"></div>\You don't feel like wasting any of your time with this creature. Just an easy contract, so you'll just kill her, and then move on. <div class="passCheck">\ She seems to realize that, her eyes widening in fear as the glinting edge of your Essence Feaster descends upon her. There is nothing she can do to stop you, and a quick swing of your weapon is enough to end her life. <img src="Images/Icons/Death.png"></div>\ <<set $playerKills to $temporaryKills +1 >>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Return to your dimension |ReturnWyvernBad]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Take the egg |TakeEggDead]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedItem.png"></div>\ <<set $killedWyvern to true>>\You are here for a reason, and that is to get your dick wet. Right now, the biggest impediment to achieving that goal is that egg-shaped boulder obstructing her hole, so, you decide to take it out. The egg is slick and slimy with the fluids of her body, and you have a hard time getting a firm grip on it, so you resort to forcing your weight down on her scaly belly, using your entire body to pry it loose, and you are anything but gentle. Her shrill cries grow even louder and more desperate as you keep pushing, and a sharp pain shoots through her body when the egg finally comes out, leaving a large gaping hole between her hind legs. You waste no time filling it back, this time with your cock, which of course has no problem fitting into the wyvern's newly emptied cunt. There is ample space, and whatever fluids were coating the egg are still dripping from her walls, which is enough to make your entry smooth and painless. In fact, she doesn't even seem to mind the new situation. With the object out, her breathing has slowed down considerably, head resting on the ground and eyes closed, exhausted from the whole ordeal. Sure, her walls are still a bit sore and it hurts a little when you push your cock hard against her, but the pain is nothing compared to what she has just experienced. If anything, she is just grateful you have relieved her of the agony of bearing that strange object inside her. She doesn't resist, doesn't scream, and doesn't growl, just lays there, completely motionless except for her soft breathing and the occasional twitch of her tail when you bottom out. You would have perhaps preferred something a bit more interactive; her hole is evidently quite loose after giving birth, and some kind of reaction would have made the sex at least a bit more exciting, but oh well. It's quite pleasant either way, a thousand times better than your hand, or any toy for that matter. And the feeling only seems to grow as the two guardians lead you through the final gate, and into a circular room with two more guards standing in their eternal vigil. The room is similar to most of the prison; not unlike the one you arrived in. This one however does have a truly remarkable feature, in the form of the sphere of black, rune-etched stone suspended in the middle of the round chamber, floating in place effortlessly despite the thousands of tons it must surely weigh. Your gaze is glued to the enormous orb, and you feel the oppressive, almost tangible presence of the creature within, and for a moment, your confidence wavers. The room is clearly damaged, with cracks and irregularities running through the once-smooth surface of the walls and ceiling. Without warning, you turn and sprint down the corridor in the opposite direction, hoping to lose the guardians and explore the prison on your own terms. However, and much to your dismay, the creatures respond almost immediately, their heavy, armored bodies moving with an unnatural speed that belies their size and form. In but a split second, they catch up to you, crackling blue lightning sparking in their gauntlets and empty sockets as they grab you and slam your body into the cold, stone wall. You struggle to get free, but it's to no avail. They may be not the brightest, but what they lack in intelligence and... free will, they make up in strength. @@.playerSpeech;"Alright, alright, you got me"@@ You groan, still held firmly in place.
