Hopefully, you'll be able to beat her ass and execute her without too much trouble. And you'll have the four guardians to aid you. You communicate your plan to them, and they seem to accept it. Not that much as agree to it, as their only response is an almost imperceptible nod before they step back, forming behind you. You suppose you have full agency to do whatever you please when it comes to Azri'Elis. And so, the wait begins. You are not sure how long it will take for her to emerge, so, you take a seat on the cold floor and prepare yourself for a potentially long wait. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |WaitAzri2]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTime.png"></div>\There is a reason you are a hunter, even if this last promotion is not completely justified. You are a warrior, a fighter. And this is the kind of approach that fits you best. @@.playerSpeech;"Release her from her prison"@@ You command the guardians. @@.playerSpeech;"I will end her, here and now."@@ The armored beings say nothing, but they obey. With no hesitation, they move closer to the colossal, rune-etched stone sphere and begin to manipulate the intricate markings on its surface. The room trembles as the process begins, and the cracks in the floor and walls seem to spread outward as the sphere shifts, its dark surface pulsating with an eerie, colorless light. As the final seal is undone, the sphere shatters with a deafening explosion, and the fragments disperse like shattered glass. In the middle of the room, in the spot that previously housed the enormous, spherical prison, you distinguish a figure, wreathed in shadows and thick smoke. The creature is slender, more than you had expected. Her arms and hooved legs are thin, and her frame is lithe, covered in a layer of dark fur. Her most remarkable features have to be the bits of exposed skeleton that seem to come out of her body; a set of exposed ribs, and a long skull for a face. <img src="Images/Monsters/AzriFixed2.png" class="azri-image" alt="AzriImage"> Despite her somewhat brittle appearance, she is at least twice as tall as you are, with her horns only further increasing her imposing height, and the aura of power she exudes is undeniable. She scans her surroundings, an odd sense of confusion emanating from her as she is able to take a look at the real world for the first time in centuries. Her skull points first to the guards, then you, and two bright dots appear in her empty sockets. @@.azriSpeech;"But of course… she wouldn't even have the decency to face me herself"@@ She hisses with contempt, as a spot of pure darkness forms between her horns as she stands to her full height. You can see the muscles underneath her fur tensing as she prepares to attack, you willl have to act fast. <<if $azriExplosive is 1>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Get the explosives and use them against the impending attack |AzriUseExplosives]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedItem.png"></div>\ <</if>>\ <<if $azriAmulet is 1>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Use the amulet |AzriAmuletFight]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedItem.png"></div>\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Try to convince her that you mean no harm |AzriConvinceNoHarm]]<div class="numberStat">X</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedCharisma.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Get ready for the fight |AzriGetReadtFight]]<div class="numberStat">X</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\Why fight her in this room or wait for her to come to you when you can take the initiative and attack her directly inside her prison? These function in a similar way to the pocket dimensions your order is all too familiar with, so you know what to expect. Walk in, kick her ass, and walk out. Easy. You inform the guardians of your plans, and they simply give their silent approval. The idea is... unconventional, but you are the one tasked with dealing with her, so… They accompany you to the rune-etched sphere, and begin the procedure, manipulating the complex series of runes that keeps the demoness confined to seal you inside as well. It's significantly easier with you than it was with her; it helps that you are not struggling and actively trying to reduce the guards to piles of scrap metal. @@.playerSpeech;"...and once I have dealt with her, you <strong>will</strong> get me out, yes?"@@ you clarify, just as the sphere's surface begins to ripple, and the black stone begins to lose its consistency. The guardians remain silent, apparently too focused on the task at hand to give you any kind of verbal confirmation. @@.playerSpeech;"...right?"@@ you insist, not wanting to get locked up inside Azri'Elis' own, personal hell for god knows how long. Then, a blinding flash of white light engulfs your whole being, and when it's gone, so are the guardians and the rest of the room. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |FightOwnPrisionAzri2]]</div>\ <<set $theIndomitableHumanSpirit to true>> @@.title;PHANTOM WASTES@@ <<if $darkDevotion == 100>>\ The phantom wastes, an echo of reality itself. A desert of dust from the ages, where souls wither and fade. Endless and empty... But someone is there to receive you, to give your soul a form again. You gasp for air as your lungs come into existence, and you feel the soft sand below your feet. Your senses return to you, and before you, a towering figure looms, seated on a colossal throne of bones. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |ContinueDeath]]</div>\ <<else>>\ <<if $playerKills >=3 && $darkDevotion ==0>> <div class="passCheck">\ Death is an overwhelmingly empty expanse, darker than the void itself. A formless, shapeless wasteland. And yet... your genocidal efforts seem to have been noticed by a certain entity. Her voice, carrying over the void, reaches your mind. <img src="Images/Icons/Death.png"><img src="Images/Icons/FailEye.png"><img src="Images/Icons/FailItem.png"></div>\ <<set $exalted to 1>>\ @@.oblivionSpeech;"There is potential in you, mortal. Much blood has already been spilled, and more is yet to be shed." @@ <<else>> <div class="failCheck">\ Death is an overwhelmingly empty expanse, darker than the void itself. There is nothing for you here. <img src="Images/Icons/FailDeath.png"><img src="Images/Icons/FailEye.png"><img src="Images/Icons/FailItem.png"></div>\ <</if>> <<set $deaths +=1>> <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Play again">> <<goto "SetName">> <</link>>\ </div>\ <</if>>\ Hours pass, you are not sure how many as there is no way to track the time, but it's enough for your ass to grow sore and your limbs to grow tired and numb. You step up and walk around the room, looking to stretch your legs and hopefully relieve the dull, pulsating pain in your head. The guardians on their end seem to be doing just fine, standing in the exact same position without moving an inch. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |WaitAzri3]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTime.png"></div>\More time goes by, and you have to resort to all kinds of methods to keep yourself occupied and distracted. You know you are supposed to focus on the task at hand, but there is not much you can do right now. You try talking with the guards (Not like they reply much), pace around the room, and count the cracks on the walls, but you soon grow tired of it all. Besides, that cursed pain in your head keeps growing, and it's getting harder and harder to concentrate. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |WaitAzri4]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTime.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Maybe this is not a good idea... reconsider your plan |StopWaitAzri]]</div>\Nothing. Nothing happenes. Not even a single sound, a new crack, a flash. Nothing. Not that you notice, anyways. You are not sure how long you have been waiting, but you are fairly certain it's been longer than a couple of days. By this point, your headache is severe, and you can barely think straight. A burning sensation, as if someone had set up a torch inside your skull... <<if $eyeSeeing > 2>> <div class="passCheck">\ In a way… it feels similar to the revolting sensation you get when you travel through the void between reality, only much denser, as if it was aimed directly at you... <img src="Images/Icons/Eye.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ <div class="failCheck">\ <img src="Images/Icons/FailEye.png"></div>\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |WaitAzri5]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTime.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Maybe this is not a good idea... reconsider your plan |StopWaitAzri]]</div>\ Not even fully sure why, you decide to keep waiting. You close your eyes and clutch your head, and then, everything ends. The nauseating sensation and the increasing pain. You fall unconscious into a most pleasant sleep, never to wake up. <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Death embraces you...">> <<goto "Death">> <</link>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png" >\ </div>\ You are beginning to feel unwell, and there is no indicator the demoness will be coming out any time soon. Whatever foul magic is at play here, it's affecting you. Maybe you should try to think of another strategy. Something less... passive. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Fight her in her own prison |FightOwnPrisionAzri]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Break her containment and fight her head-on |HeadOnAzri]]</div>\ Feeling the immediate threat, you attempt to talk her out of whatever she is about to do. Your weapon remains sheathed, and you raise your hands in the air, trying to signal you mean no harm. The guardians don't strike yet, seemingly awaiting your command. @@.playerSpeech;"Easy, easy! I am not here to fight y-"@@ She, however, doesn't seem to have the same idea as you. Before you can even finish your sentence, she lunges at you, her body a blur as she speeds across the room, so fast that her body seems to disappear and reappear right in front of you. Before her lethal claws can even connect with your face, a shockwave of psychic energy reaches your mind, threatening to overwhelm and shatter your very being. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Try to resist the mental onslaught |AzriMentalOnslaught]]<div class="numberStat">X</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedMagic.png"></div>\ She's about to attack, and you are not going to sit there and do nothing. Your hands move fast, reaching for the blade in your hip, and you take a stance, preparing for whatever she is about to unleash. Almost immediately, she charges at you, her body a blur as she speeds across the room, so fast that her body seems to disappear and reappear. Before her lethal claws can even connect with your face, a shockwave of psychic energy reaches your mind, threatening to overwhelm and shatter your very being. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Try to resist the mental onslaught |AzriMentalOnslaught]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedMagic.png"></div>\Not that much a decision, as it is a reaction based on survival and instinct, you focus your will and concentrate, pushing back against the powerful force. <<if $magicSkill <= 3>> <div class="failCheck">\ The surge of psychic energy hits you like an oncoming tidal wave, and despite your best efforts to resist, it's simply overwhelming. Your thoughts are scattered, and your consciousness begins to unravel. <img src="Images/Icons/FailMagic.png"><img src="Images/Icons/FailMagic.png"></div>\ In that brief moment of struggle, you feel as if you're falling into an endless abyss, not unlike the feeling you get when traveling between worlds. Your vision blurs… and everything goes dark. At least you aren't awake to feel your flesh being sliced clean from the bone. <<else>>\ <div class="failCheck">\ You are no complete stranger to such powers, it… feels somehow similar to the nausea you feel traveling between worlds, so you try to suppress the sensation in the same way you have instinctively learned. <img src="Images/Icons/MindMagic.png"><img src="Images/Icons/FailMagic.png"> </div>\ You manage to resist the initial assault, if only barely. However, the massive effort that you need to put into it keeps you too distracted to focus on other matters. As the world spins around you, Azri'Elis's claws connect, cracking your plate and ribcage open and pulverizing the heart beating inside, dooming you to a certain, nearly instant death. You fall to the ground, and then, darkness takes you. <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Death embraces you...">> <<goto "Death">> <</link>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png" >\ </div>\@@.title;AZRI'ELIS PRISON@@ You are greeted with a sad expanse of sand and stone, a lifeless wasteland under a perpetually overcast sky. Azri'Elis is nowhere to be seen, and the place is much colder than you thought it would, its not unbearable, but the still air seems to bite your very skin.. It feels as if... You were naked... You suddenly realize that indeed, you are completely naked, your armor, clothes, and weapon have been left behind. Being vulnerable to the cold is far from the worst part, how are you supposed to deal with the demoness without your fucking weapons? Humans have many strengths… but deprived of their tools, they don't exactly make for good combatants. @@.playerSpeech;"Those fucking bastards..."@@ You curse under your breath, not wanting to yell too much in case the creature is close by. Has it really not occurred to them to inform you about this? What the fuck are you supposed to do now? This is going to be one great piece of shit of a day... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Indeed... |FightOwnPrisionAzri3]]</div>\You very much doubt any kind of exit can be found here, and even if it happened to exist, even if the demoness inside had been unaware of it for some unexplainable reason, you very much doubt you'd be able to use it, or even recognize it for that matter. Some creatures have an innate sense when it comes to traveling between realities, but you are obviously not one of them. You merely accept it and use the tools at your disposal, but without those... hell, even if you for some sleight of hand managed to stumble upon your very own portal room, you would still be unable to make it work. But then... what are you supposed to do? Wait for the inevitable? There is no way you are fighting her one-on-one like this, it wouldn't be an easy feat even fully armored and armed, but at least then, you'd have a chance. Right now, you are as defenseless as a newborn. You look around, your eyes scanning the lifeless landscape around you for any kind of suspicious or out-of-the-ordinary thing. Anything you can use, really. And it's then that you notice... a lot of very particular oddities. The rocks that litter the wasteland are all the same distance from each other, and all ordered from the largest ones to the smallest, describing a spiral pattern... the dead trees all have the exact same number of branches; with each tree showing clear marks where previous branches once existed, meticulously removed to ensure uniformity. The sand itself seems to have been organized, the lightest grains arranged in nearly perfect, geometric patterns. Such a futile, meaningless waste of time and effort.... though you suppose she has nothing but time. Still, and perhaps evidently, you find nothing of any real use, not even when you try making your way to the edge of the pocket dimension. This one, unlike most of the ones you've known, doesn't seem to have any. Instead, it simply seems to loop into itself once you walk in a certain direction for enough time. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Hide yourself under the sand and wait for a rescue |WaitRescue]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTime.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go find her. Get this over with |GetAzriOverWith]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\You are not sure why or how you have not stumbled upon Azri'Elis yet, but you don't want to risk it. If you have a chance of making it out alive and in one piece, it's by laying low and waiting for a rescue, hoping the guardians will see it fit to come and help you, or Lauren herself when she, hopefully, notices your absence. Surely they wouldn't just abandon you. And you have not really seen anyplace where you could hide. The trees are not big enough, and the rocks are far too scattered, the sand seems like the only possible option. So you dig, moving the sand and forming a shallow hole in which you can lay down and bury yourself, careful to keep your nose and a bit of your face sticking out so that you can breathe. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Time passes... |WaitRescue2]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTime.png"></div>\Time passes, and you wait patiently underneath the sand, your breathing steady and deep, the sand tickling your nostrils as you draw air in and out. This is really going to be awful. How long will you be able to stay in there? It's painfully boring, and without water or food... it won't be long until you succumb to thirst or starvation. You try not to think too hard about it, not now. To pass the time, you decide to take a nap. Time will go by much faster that way... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Time passes... |WaitRescueAzri]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTime.png"><img src="Images/Icons/RedSleep.png"></div>\You are jolted awake sometime later, when an ethereal hand grabs hold of your neck and yanks you out of the ground, lifting you and holding you up in the air. <img src="Images/Monsters/AzriFixed2.png" class="azri-image" alt="AzriImage"> @@.azriSpeech;"I have come to know every single individual grain of sand of this wretched place. Did you <i>really</i> think I wouldn't notice your ugly proboscis sticking out?"@@ She sneers, clenching her clawed hand and by doing so, strengthening the invisible grip you are subjected to, wanting to make clear she could end your life in an instant. @@.azriSpeech;"...A hunter, deprived of their utensils… and alone. Trapped in my very penitentiary... If I didn't know any better, I would say you were an apology gift from that wretch you serve. A sacrifice to appease me... I will give you a chance, though. Speak. What kind of elaborate trickery are you part of?"@@ She questions, her voice dangerous, her clasp threatening to crush your windpipe. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Try to break free |TryBreakFree]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Threaten her, try to make her believe more are on their way |TryLieAzri]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Tell the truth |TruthAzri]]</div>\<div class="failCheck">\ You struggle, trying your best to break free from the shadowy grasp that holds you, but to no avail. <img src="Images/Icons/FailGeneral.png"></div>\ @@.azriSpeech;"...Pity. Brainwashed halfwits, every last one of you. I'll just pull the truth out of your lifeless corpse, then."@@ Not a second later, her hand squeezes tighter, and your neck snaps with a wet crunch. <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Death embraces you...">> <<goto "Death">> <</link>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png" >\ </div>\@@.playerSpeech;"D-did… did you think this scape attempt would go unnoticed? If I'm not out of here soon, more will come, so you bette-"@@ Maybe she buys it, maybe she doesn't. Before you can even finish your sentence, the demoness closes her hand and snaps your neck. There is something clearly odd about a hunter suddenly popping up naked in her prison, and if indeed you know about her near escape, which you do… then time is a sensitive matter and she'd rather get the information straight of your brain, rather than through your deceiving mouth. <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Death embraces you...">> <<goto "Death">> <</link>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png" >\ </div>\@@.playerSpeech;"I...I'll talk..."@@ You manage to croak out. @@.playerSpeech;"...just... please, let me talk..."@@ @@.azriSpeech;"I am listening."@@ Her grasp loosens a just bit, you are still suspended in the air like a rag doll, but at least it no longer feels as if your larynx is about to become fine paste. Even so, it’s so tight it hurts to talk… So, you begin your rather sad tale. You explain how the order noticed her escape attempt, and how Lauren sent you to kill her. How you had the bright idea to 'fight' her in her own cell, unaware of the fact you would be transported without your weapons... or armor. She lets out a forced, mirthless laugh, and suddenly the pressure on your neck increases, causing you intense pain. @@.azriSpeech;"Of course. And I am supposed to believe such a blatant lie? Do you take me for that much of a fool? You really expect me to believe that a member of your <i>illustrious order</i>, with all their cunning and resources, would just be dumb enough to step here, unarmed and unprepared?"@@ She pauses for a moment, her voice turning darker. @@.azriSpeech;"I will pull the truth out of your skull, one way or the other..."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Beg for your life |BegAzri]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Reaffirm yourself; the truth is you are an idiot |ReaffirmAzri]]<div class="numberStat">1</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedCharisma.png"></div>\ @@.playerSpeech;"P-please, no! Don't kill me, I'm telling the truth, I swear! Ha-have mercy, please!"@@ You cry out, the pain in your neck and the fear for your life making your words come out in a pitiful whimper. @@.azriSpeech;"Mercy? Oh, how utterly pathetic"@@ she hisses, cutting your pitiful speech by gripping your neck even harder. @@.azriSpeech;"<i>This</i> is mercy. Far more than you deserve."@@ With that, she finally closes her hand, crushing your neck and effectively killing you. What will her surprise be, when she discovers that you were indeed telling the truth... <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Death embraces you...">> <<goto "Death">>\ <</link>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png" >\ </div>\<<if $charismaSkill <1>>\ <div class="failCheck">\ You know you have to reaffirm yourself, to make it clear you are not lying... but panic overtakes you, and what comes out of your mouth is not the eloquent speech that you needed, but rather a blubbering, panicked mess.<img src="Images/Icons/FailCharisma.png"></div>\ @@.playerSpeech;"P-please, no! Don't kill me, I'm telling the truth, I swear! Ha-have mercy, please!"@@ You cry out, the pain in your neck and the fear for your life making your words come out in a pitiful whimper. @@.azriSpeech;"Mercy? Oh, how utterly pathetic of you"@@ she hisses, cutting your pitiful speech by gripping your neck even harder. @@.azriSpeech;"This is mercy. Far more than you deserve."@@ With that, she finally closes her hand, crushing your neck and effectively killing you. What will her surprise be, when she discovers that you were indeed telling the truth... <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Death embraces you...">> <<goto "Death">> <</link>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png" >\ </div>\ <<else>>\ <div class="passCheck">\ You try to breathe deep, you know you have to stay calm. She has not killed you yet, so she is still not fully convinced of your intentions... <img src="Images/Icons/Charisma.png"></div>\ @@.playerSpeech;"It... It is the truth. I... I am not supposed to be a high hunter. There… there just was a vacant, and I was promoted. I'm just... an idiot"@@ You admit, your words coming out with great effort, both due to the pain of having your neck on the verge of being crushed, and also because admitting so hurts your ego just a little bit. @@.playerSpeech;"You... you can read my mind if you'd like. You'll see that I'm not lying. I just... made a mistake, there is no elaborate sheme."@@ Azri'Elis stares at you, her expression unreadable as her piercing gaze drills holes in your soul. She's looking deep into your eyes, her claws still digging into your neck, as if she is about to hypnotize and kill you at the same time. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Just stay silent and let her read your mind |SilentRead]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Take a good look of her in the meantime |LookMind]]</div>\ <</if>>You simply stay silent, waiting for her to come to her own conclusion. Nothing you can say will be better than her reading the truth straight from your mind, after all. <<if $karma <= -2>>\ <div class="failCheck">\ Azri’Elis may not be able to fully read minds without reducing the host to ash, but as she stares deep into your soul, she becomes aware of a deep darkness lurking inside it. You, like the ones that put her here, are just another vile, merciless creature, a slave to your basest instincts, a selfish brute. <img src="Images/Icons/FailKarma.png"></div>\ If she were to extract any information out of your brain directly, you would die. But then, is it so much of a loss, for you to die here and now? Her claws dig deeper into your flesh, and she squeezes, until, with a sickening crack, your body goes limp. <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Death embraces you...">> <<goto "Death">>\ <</link>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png" >\ </div>\ <<else>>\ <div class="passCheck">\ However... Nothing really happens, she continues looking at you with the same stern expression. You may not be a saint, but you don't carry the same aura of hatred some members of your order do. <img src="Images/Icons/Karma.png"></div>\ @@.playerSpeech;"...you cannot read minds, can you?"@@ You say after some seconds of uncomfortable silence, and neck pain. @@.azriSpeech;"Not quite. Not without causing irreversible damage, at least..."@@ She replies, before releasing the grip on you and letting you fall down on the sand again. @@.azriSpeech;"But I can tell you are being honest."@@ She chuckles, her toothy jaws somehow forming a wicked smile. @@.azriSpeech;"You truly are... an idiot."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["Thanks for letting me live..." |ThanksLetLive]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["I suppose I am..." |Isup]]</div>\ <</if>>\You can't help but let your eyes wander around, unable to fully focus them on hers. Something about her gaze makes you feel dizzy, as if you were being sucked into a void. You roll your eyes from your uncomfortable position, taking in your bleak surroundings, and then her body. You realize that she has no breasts at all, just some bones sticking out of her chest. Why in such a critical situation would you focus on that? Muscle memory you suppose. You try to keep those thoughts out of your mind, you don't want her to know you are ogling her body, even if it's definitely pleasant to the eye… Her legs are muscular, but also slender, and her waist is narrow, accentuating the curve of her hips... <<if $karma <= -2>>\ <div class="failCheck">\ Azri’Elis may not be able to fully read minds without reducing the host to ash, but as she stares deep into your soul, she becomes aware of a deep darkness lurking inside it. You, like the ones that put her here, are just another vile, merciless creature, a slave to your basest instincts, a selfish brute. <img src="Images/Icons/FailKarma.png"></div>\ If she were to extract any information out of your brain directly, you would die. But then, is it so much of a loss, for you to die here and now? Her claws dig deeper into your flesh, and she squeezes, until, with a sickening crack, your body goes limp. <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Death embraces you...">> <<goto "Death">>\ <</link>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png" >\ </div>\ <<else>>\ <div class="passCheck">\ However... Nothing really happens, she continues looking at you with the same stern expression. You may not be a saint, but you don't carry the same aura of hatred some members of your order do. <img src="Images/Icons/Karma.png"></div>\ @@.playerSpeech;"...you cannot read minds, can you?"@@ You say after some seconds of uncomfortable silence, and neck pain. @@.azriSpeech;"Not quite. Not without causing irreversible damage, at least..."@@ She replies, before releasing the grip on you and letting you fall down on the sand again. @@.azriSpeech;"But I can tell you are being honest."@@ She chuckles, her toothy jaws somehow forming a wicked smile. @@.azriSpeech;"You truly are... an idiot."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["Thanks for letting me live..." |ThanksLetLive]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["I suppose I am..." |Isup]]</div>\ <</if>>\@@.playerSpeech;"Thanks for letting me live,"@@ You tell her, incorporating and rubbing the aching parts of your neck, the pain slowly subsiding. @@.azriSpeech;"For now."@@ She is quick to remind you. @@.azriSpeech;"Now... why did you aspire to such a position? It is a job that requires a certain degree of... intelligence. At least, that is the impression I've gotten."@@ She asks, now clearly amused. <<if $magicSkill > 0>>\ <div class="passCheck">\ You may not be the most intelligent or skilled hunter to have ever lived, and yet you can tell she's dying to have a conversation with someone, even if that someone is... well, you. <img src="Images/Icons/MindMagic.png"></div>\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["... I wanted to see new worlds and learn new things..." |SeeWorlds]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["Because I wanted to meet some cute, nonhuman girls along the way" |MeetGirls]]</div>\ <<if $karma >=4>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["Because I wanted to protect the lives of the innocent" |AgentJustice]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedKarma.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ @@.fadedLink; <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedCross.png">[["Because I wanted to protect the lives of the innocent" |AgentJustice]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedKarma.png"></div>@@\ <</if>>\@@.playerSpeech;"I... suppose I am,"@@ You tell her, incorporating and rubbing the aching parts of your neck, the pain slowly subsiding. "I guess my stupid decisions have consequences..." @@.azriSpeech;"And praytell, why did you aspire to such a position? It is a job that requires a certain degree of... intelligence. You took this position so you could murder and hunt down 'cute, non-human females?'"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"No no, of course not! Let me explain!"@@ You hurry to say. @@.playerSpeech;"My Order has no immediate way of knowing if our targets are dead or not, and a lot of time, they are no real threats to anyone so I of course just... let them be. I just use that time to... well, to try and chat them up."@@ @@.azriSpeech;"A hunter that does not hunt..."@@ She pauses for a moment, @@.azriSpeech;"Do I fit into that category, then? Is that why you are here?"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Well... you actually are a real job you know? The way I usually do it is picking up unimportant contracts of creatures that can't rip my throat in the middle of a conversation if one of my many cheesy pick-up lines goes wrong. If I don't take care of some important work from time to time, I'd lose my position real quick. You... just happened to be female."@@ @@.azriSpeech;"I see, I see..."@@ she replies, and she sounds almost... disappointed. Maybe you are just imagining it, but regardless, you decide to act as the true gentleman you are. Letting your dick put your life in danger once more and you speak again. @@.playerSpeech;"...But I must admit, now that Im here, you are <i>definitely</i> one of the hottest creatures I've ever seen"@@ To your surprise, she doesn't seem offended or disgusted. Instead, she just chuckles, shaking her head. "Wise of you to pick your contracts carefully. I can now understand why you wouldn't want someone who could rip your throat." @@.playerSpeech;"That's... a fair assessment. Hehe. But even so, I must say I've always had a thing for dangerous-looking women."@@ @@.azriSpeech;"Oh, you can stop now. I got notified with a lot of urgency… but that should be enough for us to get to know each other~"@@ @@.azriSpeech;"Two days, you say?"@@ She laughs. @@.azriSpeech;"That may indeed be the case out there, but I'm afraid to inform you that time here operates on a different scale. This hellish limbo distorts the flow of time, and days outside are stretched into months. If that is true, even if they came to rescue you right now, it would still take a long time for them to do it. You take another look at her. @@.playerSpeech;"I…"@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["... can't even imagine what you have gone through…." |gonethorugh]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["...Had nothing to do with any of this…" |hadNothingToDo]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"Because I wanted to do some good, to be an agent of justice, and help protect the innocent..."@@ Perhaps it was not your only goal, but you have certainly helped others in your path. @@.azriSpeech;"An agent of justice? Please. Now that has to be a lie."@@ She snickers, tail swishing behind her as she speaks. @@.azriSpeech;"Do not take me for a fool. Your order is an agency of control, corrupt to the core, and a mockery of anything even resembling justice."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"I... can understand why you would say that about my order... "@@ @@.azriSpeech;"No, you can't. You can imagine why I would say that. But you cannot understand what it is like. To be imprisoned here, for years unknowable, for a crime I didn't commit."@@ She speaks, her clawed fingers digging into the palms of her hands. @@.playerSpeech;"That is exactly why I sought to take this position. My order has... gone astray, lost its way. Our work is still necessary, but lately... we receive more and more pointless genocidal orders, or we are sent after the wrong targets."@@ Azri'Elis looks at you, her piercing gaze softening just a bit. @@.azriSpeech;"You are not as naive as I expected. Not many of your kind are capable of admitting just how twisted your order has become. You'll get to experience some of it yourself firsthand now. Poetic justice huh?"@@ @@.azriSpeech;"What I want is not entirely important. I do not wish to punish what’s essentially a foot soldier. I wouldn't wish this experience... upon anyone. For just a moment, she looks defeated, tired, on the brink of collapse. It doesn't last long, though, as she quickly straightens herself. @@.azriSpeech;"It matters not. And while it hasn't been a day I haven't fantasized about crushing Lauren's head, I do not fault you for her actions. I'm not like your kind, after all. I'm able to distinguish between the individual and the whole."@@ She explains, using her magic to sweep some of the sand you have disarranged back in place. @@.playerSpeech;"I know that what we do is not always right, but you have to understand where we are coming from..."@@ @@.azriSpeech;"I am more understanding than you may think. I won't debate the morality of your actions... or mine. Our paths are intertwined now, and it seems we both have a vested interest in finding a way out of this place. Perhaps you'll even be able to help…"@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |azriTimeSkip1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTime.png"></div>\ <<set $temporaryDays to $days>><<set $days to $temporaryDays + 7>> Azri'Elis turns out to be a lot nicer than you had expected her to be. Despite her fearsome facade and first impression, she is quite pleasant to talk to. Even if she tries not to make it too obvious, it's clear just how desperate she was for a bit of company, and how grateful she is to not have to spend more time alone sorting rocks and sand. As she says, inactivity is the death of the mind, but you don't think moving grains of sand around is the definition of stimulating healthy activities, either. Maybe she still hates you to your core, but if that were the case, she at least does a remarkably good job at hiding it. <div class="passCheck">\ Some time passes, weeks perhaps, and though the monotony persists to some extent, you begin to adapt to the daily routine, if you can even call it that. <img src="Images/Icons/Time.png"></div>\ The primary source of entertainment, of course, lies in each other's company. When she's not immersed in her meditation, using her magic to debilitate the prison from within, the two of you engage in extensive conversations that span a wide range of topics... reminiscing about your past experiences, talking about your plans for the future, hopes and dreams… Azri'Elis on her end is quite open about... well, everything really. Eventually, she even casually drops the list of those who have wronged her, those she will see dead one way or another... and some of them are quite familiar to you. Not a big surprise. @@.playerSpeech;"...and aren't you worried that I might just alert my order about your intentions? "I mean, it's not unreasonable to think I might get in touch with my superiors once we are both out... they will want to know what happened here."@@ @@.azriSpeech;"It would be the logical... <i>humane</i> choice for you to make. It doesn't matter, they will know I'm coming for them...."@@ She pauses for a moment, lost in her own thoughts. @@.azriSpeech;"...humane... inhumane... mhm, I see what you did there..."@@ You shrug, not really wanting to engage in an argument over semantics, despite how much she loves those. Not again. @@.playerSpeech;"Hey, and have you thought maybe... not going through with your revenge?"@@ She sighs. @@.azriSpeech;"...I understand you want to protect your kin, but some of them are truly irredeemable. I will be doing the universe a favor, and I hope you will eventually come to realize that."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[You say so because indeed, you care about them |careOrder]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[You say so because you care about her |careAboutHer]]</div>\ <<if $elixirExertion == 2>> <<set $hunterSkill -=1>> <<set $charismaSkill -=1>> <<set $eyeSeeing -=1>> <<set $magicSkill -=1>> <<set $healSkill -=1>> <<set $cumSkill -=1>> <<set $elixirExertion to 3>> <</if>>You remain silent, not really knowing how to continue the conversation. Once we are out, we'll go our separate ways."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Yeah... I... sorry"@@ You apologize, not even sure what exactly you are apologizing for. @@.azriSpeech;"Why would you be sorry? I really do understand,"@@ she assures you, giving your shoulder a gentle tap with her claw. @@.azriSpeech;"While you came here as an enemy, I want you to know I appreciate your company."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Same. I appreciate you not... killing me."@@ @@.azriSpeech;"Couldn’t bring myself to destroy my only distraction in years..."@@ She chuckles. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |EpilogueNoSexAzri]]</div>\ @@.playerSpeech;"It's not that. But think about it. What's the point? I really do understand,"@@ she assures you, giving your shoulder a gentle tap with her claw. @@.azriSpeech;"While you came here as an enemy, I want you to know I appreciate your company."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Same. I appreciate you not... killing me."@@ @@.azriSpeech;"Couldn’t bring myself to destroy my only distraction in years..."@@ She chuckles. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |EpilogueNoSexAzri]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"Well..."@@ You begin, still not completely sure you want to risk your neck like this. @@.playerSpeech;"I could offer you some intel on the inner workings of the order... even if I'm the lowest of the low, I've had access to some... information that could maybe help you."@@ @@.azriSpeech;"Really? You know me for a couple of instants, and you would be willing to betray your kind for me?"@@ She asks, and you are not sure if she is amused or simply skeptical. @@.playerSpeech;"...well, you could say I've been having a change of heart. I'm not too convinced of our cause anymore… the markings of The Eye are increasingly more and more innacurate…"@@ @@.azriSpeech;"The Eye... that's how you call it?"@@ She muses, tapping her chin with a finger. @@.azriSpeech;"Xa'Rdrath... what remains of it, that is."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Used to be a reliable source, or so I have been told. But lately, at least half the creatures it identifies as dangers turn out to be perfectly harmless. You know, perhaps getting rid of them would be a good thing... a step in the right direction."@@ @@.azriSpeech;"I'm pleased to know you are willing to combine forces. I won't even have to shatter your mind to get all those details myself."@@ she says, letting her glistening, serpentine tongue slip out of her bony maw. It's a lighthearted effort to intimidate you, but all you can really focus on is just how wet and inviting her tongue looks, glistening with dark saliva. If you are to make a move, now is the time. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Make a move? |AzriMakeMove]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Say Nothing |EpilogueNoSexAzri]]</div>\<<set $days to $temporaryDays + 18>> More time goes by, the sunless days going by at a steady, slow pace. You are now completely used to what has become of your life, though one of the main reasons for that is the hope of an eventual change. Azri'Elis spends most of the time meditating to debilitate the seal, but she has to take breaks to recover from the effort from time to time... Her company is surely appreciated; you can't begin to imagine just how dull this would have been if you had been stuck here all alone. You may have started sorting sand and rocks yourself… or using the latter to bash your skull. But even then, you miss the comfort of your modest home, the bland taste of the highly nutritious sludge you used to eat... Luckily, one day you are greeted with a pleasant sight; it seems this imprisonment will not last as long as it could have. During one of Azri'Elis' meditations, you see it. A small bright dot in the firmament, through which a thin sliver of light pours into the desert. It wouldn't really stand out that much if it wasn't for the overwhelming emptiness of the sky above you. And as you keep looking, it appears to grow, as if the very fabric of this dimension was being torn open. Did she finally manage to break the seal? Her eyes ignite then, alerted of the situation by her preternatural senses, and soon finds herself staring at the same spot you are. @@.playerSpeech;"Guess that's it," You tell her. "You finally managed to do it."@@ @@.azriSpeech;"It's not me. This is something else. Someone's trying to break the seal from the outside..."@@ She explains, and then, as the sky continues cracking, an uncomfortable silence falls between the two of you. @@.playerSpeech;"So... what now?"@@ @@.azriSpeech;"You? I don't know. As for myself, I'd better prepare for the inevitable confrontation... I doubt your fellows have had a change of heart."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Remain silent |RemainSilentAzri]]</div>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedCross.png">[[Wish her luck |WishAzriLuck]]</div>@@\Not being able to keep it in anymore, your dick thinks faster than your brain and you surprise yourself by saying something along the lines of @@.playerSpeech;"Are you trying to frighten me, or turn me on?"@@ However, she doesn't seem to be upset or offended, and instead just chuckles at it. @@.azriSpeech;"Humans have always looked at me in a myriad of ways. I can easily feel and read emotions, and yours are... hard to misinterpret."@@ <<if $saidAzriHot is true>>\ Her statement is hard to challenge, but before you can even try to explain yourself, she continues @@.azriSpeech;"At first, I thought you were just complimenting me in an effort to reduce the chances of your premature demise… but it would seem like it was genuine. Don't I wear the face of death?"@@ She says, her fingers tracing her skull to help her make her point. <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["I don't know, I guess you are my type..." |YouAreMyType]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["I guess this place makes me a little crazy..." |placeLittleCrazy]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"I don't know, I guess you are my type..."@@ You concede. @@.azriSpeech;"Your type? Thanks for not killing me yet."@@ @@.azriSpeech;"You are welcome."@@ She smiles. @@.azriSpeech;"Yes, I've had better company, but as it stands, you are leagues above the alternative. Then, suddenly, the whole world shakes, the sky coming undone in a bright flash of light. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |AzriRazelComfrontation]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTeleport.png"></div>\ @@.title;DARTHAX@@ Vague hints of reality begin to tug at your consciousness, as the world begins to come back together around you. Soon you begin to perceive more and more, first gravity; the sensation of being solid and having mass, then touch, sound... and finally sight. You open your eyes, slowly, finding yourself face-first against the cold stone of Darthax's floor. It takes a moment for your dazed brain to process what's happened, and just why you'd be here. Azri'Elis! Of course, the seal has just been broken and you have been pulled back, along with her. You look around, taking in your surroundings, and there she is, standing amongst three dead prison guardians; plates broken and charred, and another High Hunter from your order. The battle must've been going on for a while... And from the looks of it, she is not winning. Black blood pours out of several wounds, her left arm is clearly broken, hanging limply on her side, and her posture is one of pure exhaustion. Her opponent turns out to be none other than Razel, his great hammer held with grim determination and clad in heavy armor. On his neck, a tear-shaped amulet shines with power, and every time the demoness tries to summon her magic, the trinket seems to glow, and the spell is snuffed before it even has a chance to fully manifest. He clearly is a lot more prepared than you were... Razel gives you a quick glance, but immediately turns his attention back to his prey. He has her cornered against the circular wall, and swing after swing, great hammer comes ever closer to the wounded demoness. Sensing her impending doom, Azri'Elis goes for a final, desperate maneuver. She lunges at Razel, ignoring the threat that his weapon represents and trying to grab the amulet with her clawed hands, in an attempt to pull it away from his neck. Surprised by her burst of speed, Raziel reacts just a second too late, as her left hand takes hold of the jewel. Smoke comes out; the mere touch burning her skin, but then, before she has a chance to seize it, a powerful blow to her chest sends her flying all the way across the room, breaking two of her outer ribs and leaving her lying on the floor, coughing black blood. You hear Raziel curse. The swing has thrown her away, and the amulet has come off as well. It's now laying on the ground... and as if by some cosmic joke, it has landed right in front of you. @@.hunterSpeech;"Quick! The amulet!"@@ Razel commands, already rushing towards you at a surprisingly fast pace, clearly unsure of your intentions. For what he knows, you could be under her influence. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[You stand with the order; give him the amulet |PassAmulet]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png"></div>\ <<if $azriSex is true>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[You stand with Azri'Elis; throw the amulet far away |ThrowAmulet]]<div class="numberStat">1</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[You stand with Azri'Elis; throw the amulet far away |ThrowAmulet]]<div class="numberStat">1</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>@@\ <</if>>\ You take the amulet and stand frozen in place for a split second. As the ironclad knight rushes towards you, you feel your life flashing before your eyes. He'll be on you in but a moment, and your body is not as though as Azri'Elis'. That hammer will shatter your bones like glass, and the only thing left of you will be a bloody stain on the ground. It doesn't matter what you do, it will feel like a betrayal to you one way or another. Yet... you cannot bring yourself to side with Razel. So, without thinking about it further, you throw the amulet away as far away as you can and then turn to face the enraged knight with an apologetic smile. @@.hunterSpeech;"You... bumbling imbecile!"@@ Razel screams, his eyes shining with fury. @@.hunterSpeech;"Traitorous scum, you've doomed us both!"@@ She must have mind-controlled you, somehow. But he doesn't have the time nor the patience for a proper interrogation, or exorcism. Right now, you are an obstacle in his way, and he doesn't hesitate to bring his demonic weapon down upon you. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Try to resist his onslaught |ResistRazel]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\A part of you wants to side with her, but you know you cannot. Even if your time with her has not been as awful as it could have surely been, this creature is far too dangerous to be left alive and free. Azri'Elis is a threat that, like many others, must be destroyed for the good of mankind. ...That is what you tell yourself, though. It sounds far more convincing than admitting that you just couldn't doublecross your order even if you really wanted to. The consequences would be terrible. So, not daring to look her in the eye, you reach for the amulet and quickly hand it over to Razel, who takes a deep breath. @@.hunterSpeech;"Well done, boy. For a moment, I was afraid you'd be under her spell. Now stand aside and let me finish this."@@ He commands, and you obey, walking back and staying there. The spell Azri'Elis was channeling fizzles out once more, and this time, she's too weak to try again. @@.hunterSpeech;"Lauren insisted on letting you live last time... but it seems her mercy is wasted in a creature such as you."@@ Razel tells her, raising his hammer and walking menacingly towards her as she struggles to stand back up on her hooves @@.hunterSpeech;"Your time has come, wretch"@@ The demoness doesn't reply, a hand clutching her chest tightly. She can barely stand, and just falls on her knees as the High Hunter approaches. She seems resigned to her fate, she doesn't have the strength to try to defend herself. It would only bring more crippling, nonlethal blows, it would only prolong her agony. He raises his hammer, crimson energy swirling around the head. @@.hunterSpeech;"Any last words, beast?"@@ @@.azriSpeech;"...Be done with it already..."@@ She growls, her voice trembling slightly from pain and exhaustion. She gives you one last glance before the hammer comes crashing down. Not one full of hatred and resentment, like you'd expect. Just a simple, understanding look. You had no real choice… <div class="passCheck">\ Then, a deafening blast. The ground shakes and the air vibrates as her skull cracks and a wave of fire engulfs her. And then, nothing. Her body simply ceases to be, not a single trace left behind but a few dark smudges on the floor where she once stood. At least, she is finally free, in a way. <img src="Images/Icons/Death.png"></div>\ Razel doesn't waste any time. He walks up to you and places a heavy gauntleted hand on your shoulder. @@.hunterSpeech;"You've done good, kid."@@ He tells you, his eyes shining with the satisfaction of a job well done. @@.hunterSpeech;"Take this and cover yourself. We are going back."@@ He throws his cloak at you, and you quickly put it on. It's better than walking around naked. @@.hunterSpeech;"You know... I should have been the one to do this job. Using what strength she has left, she lets out a blast of her dark that washes over him. He is quickly thrown back, his armor manages to keep him alive but without the amulet its still a pretty devastating blow. His attempts to call for help devolve into agonized wails, echoing through the space until they either fade into unconsciousness or, perhaps, meet a more absolute end.
