We're too close, they can still find us, I have to-"@@ She protests, but is interrupted by another violent coughing fit, more black blood pouring out of her mouth and spilling on the grass. @@.playerSpeech;"We will find somewhere to hide here, and you can shift us again when you're rested, but not now. I don't want you to die."@@ <div class="passCheck">\ She is just in no shape to try it again. The void can be quite hostile, and trying again could only end in disaster. <img src="Images/Icons/Heal.png"></div>\ @@.azriSpeech;"If they... get to us, death <strong>will</strong> be the preferable outcome"@@ She replies, still trying to stand up despite your insistence to keep her from doing so. She's not putting much energy into it though, deep down knowing that, despite how much she hates it, you are right. @@.playerSpeech;"My order is usually slow. Paperwork, bureaucracy, the whole thing. Even if they are aware of what happened, it should take them at least a day or two to come here, maybe more."@@ She doesn't look too convinced, but gives in, slowly putting more of her weight back on you. @@.azriSpeech;"We'll need... to hide."@@ You look around, trying to spot any sort of structure. There isn't much of anything nearby. Some trees, a couple of bushes, and far away, the vague shape of a mountain in the mist. @@.playerSpeech;"Maybe there's a cave we could hide in, somewhere in the mountains?"@@ She weakly nods, and that settles it. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Walk to the mountain |WalkAzriMountain]]</div>\You begin to walk towards the mountains, helping her up the entire way. Just what exactly has happened? Sure, the fight against Razel was a pretty close one, but even then, she was doing… fine. Now, walking by itself is a struggle. @@.playerSpeech;"Are you okay? Do you need to stop and rest a bit?"@@ She shakes her head slowly. @@.azriSpeech;"I'll be fine."@@ You won't argue with her, and just continue doing your best to reach the mountain as quickly as possible. It takes a while, but eventually, you reach the barren mountain, and thank The Eye, in between some sharp rocks you manage to find a small cave, which hopefully will allow you to hide and rest. You gently set her down on the floor, and then proceed to sit down beside her. Her pupils turn to black for a moment, but before you express your worry she takes a deep breath and sighs, calming down a bit. @@.azriSpeech;"Why."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Why... what?"@@ @@.azriSpeech;"Why did you save me? Why did you risk everything? You would have been able to continue living amongst your peers. Why throw that all away?"@@ You take a moment to ponder your answer... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[I just helped a friend in need, was the right thing to do |RightThingAzri]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Because I love you |LoveAzri]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"It... was the right thing to do. You are my friend, and I couldn't let you die like that."@@ @@.azriSpeech;"Wasn't that human your friend as well?"@@ She asks. @@.azriSpeech;"I... killed him."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"I... barely knew him. Being human alone doesn't make him my friend..."@@ She stays silent for a few moments, then. @@.azriSpeech;"I'm sorry I have done this to you."@@ The demoness mumbles at last, her voice filled with regret. @@.playerSpeech;"What? It was my choice, and I don't regret it. What is it to be sorry about? You didn't force me into anything"@@ She stays silent again. @@.azriSpeech;"What we did in there, I understand its importance for both your species... and mine."@@ She sighs, her eyes dimming for a moment. @@.azriSpeech;"I was bored, I... craved some entertainment. I never thought you would grow attached to me in such a way. How long do humans live, one, two centuries at most? Your whole life is no more than an instant to me!"@@ It's your turn to stay silent then, not sure what to say to that. @@.azriSpeech;"I like you. I do. But... I cannot..."@@ She pauses for a moment, sighing again. @@.azriSpeech;"I'm sorry."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"I... get it. I'm just happy you are alive."@@ Any kind of real relationship had always seemed like a daunting prospect, and despite the shitty circumstances, you've not had a bad time with her. She weakly smiles at that, and rests her head against your shoulder, careful not to take your eye out with one of the many ramifications her horns have. @@.azriSpeech;"I... thanks. Foolish and impulsive as you are, your kindness... I will not forget it. I don't know how I'll repay you, but I will. That I promise."@@ The two of you remain in silence, simply catching your breath. She's still cold, and her breathing is very heavy, as if she was struggling with the weight of her own body. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Offer her to lean on your lap |AzriLeanLap]]</div>\ @@.playerSpeech;"I would much rather die for someone I care about, than for the order. Maybe it didn't mean much to you, but I grew quite fond of you in that prison."@@ @@.azriSpeech;"I... feared that would be the answer."@@ She whispers, lost in her thoughts. @@.azriSpeech;"I'm sorry I have done this to you. I really am"@@ She adds, her voice filled with regret. @@.playerSpeech;"What? It was my choice, and I don't regret it. What is it to be sorry about? You didn't force me into anything"@@ She stays silent then. @@.azriSpeech;"What we did in there, I understand its importance for both your species... and mine."@@ She sighs, her eyes dimming for a moment. @@.azriSpeech;"I was bored, I... craved some entertainment. I... never thought you would grow such an attachment to me."@@ Fearing she is being too harsh, she is quick to add: @@.azriSpeech;"I'm not rejecting your feelings, just... how long do humans live, one, two centuries at most? Your whole life is but an instant to me!"@@ It's your turn to stay silent now, not sure of what to say to that. @@.azriSpeech;"I like you. I do. But I... won't be able to give you what you seek."@@ She pauses for a moment, sighing again. @@.azriSpeech;"I'm sorry."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"I... understand. I'm just happy you are alive."@@ You say. @@.playerSpeech;"I always knew any kind of real relationship would have been impossible, even with my gear, I cannot compare to you. And I wouldn't wish to. You deserve someone who can live just as long as you, someone who can match you both in power and intelligence."@@ @@.azriSpeech;"That's... that's not-"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"It's fine. But I will again reaffirm myself, I did what I did because I care about you. That doesn't change."@@ She weakly smiles at that and rests her head against your shoulder. @@.azriSpeech;"I... thanks. Foolish and impulsive as you are, your kindness... I will not forget it. I don't know how I'll repay you, but I will. That I promise."@@ The two of you remain in silence, simply catching your breath. She's still cold, and her breathing is very heavy, as if she was struggling with the weight of her own body. @@.azriSpeech;"You're different, you know?"@@ She whispers, her voice tired and weak. @@.playerSpeech;"Shh, I'll let you compliment me later. Now just... get some rest."@@ You lean in and plant a gentle kiss on her maxilla, and she hums softly in response. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Offer her to lean on your lap |AzriLeanLap]]</div>\ You stay quiet, allowing her to go on, but nearly as soon as she tries to summon her powers back, she lets out a choked cry and falls, almost as if she'd just been smitten by some unseen force. This time, she seems to be completely out, and were not for your support, she would have hit the ground quite hard. @@.playerSpeech;"Hey!"@@ You call her, patting her maxilla. @@.playerSpeech;"Hey! Wake up! Can you hear me? Come on, wake up!"@@ But she does not. The only response is the sound of her heavy, labored breathing, and her occasional pained whimpers. @@.playerSpeech;"Shit..."@@ You have to take her somewhere safe, you can't just stay right in the middle of this field, waiting for a hunter to come around and spot the two of you. You look around, trying to spot any sort of structure. There isn't much of anything nearby. Some trees, and a couple of bushes, but not that far away, the clear shape of a mountain in the mist. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Carry her to the mountain |WalkAzriMountain2]]</div>\ You summon your strength and grab the demoness by her armpits, trying to lift her and placing her back against yours. She is big and quite heavy, but the adrenaline helps, and you manage to lift her just enough so that you can drag her without causing her any damage. Her hooves are the only part of her body that touches the ground. The walk to the mountain is a slow and difficult one. Your legs feel like jelly, and carrying the demoness is quite tiring. Still, you do your best, and eventually, you find a big enough cave to hide in. With one last effort, you place her inside, and finally, let yourself rest, leaning right against her. Thankfully, after a few minutes, her eyes seem to flicker, her ethereal gaze unfocused as she comes back. @@.playerSpeech;"Shit, are you alright? You got me worried there for a second"@@ @@.azriSpeech;"I... have been better..."@@ She mutters and then coughs up a little. She stays silent for a while, and you don't push her any further. It seems as if talking alone is a difficult task for her right now. Eventually, she manages to regain her bearings enough to talk again, but she asks a simple @@.azriSpeech;"Why?"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"What do you mean why?"@@ @@.azriSpeech;"Why... did you save me? Why did you risk everything? You would have been able to continue living amongst your peers. Why throw that all away?"@@ You take a moment to ponder your answer... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[I just helped a friend in need, was the right thing to do |RightThingAzri]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Because I love you |LoveAzri]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"Hey, if you want to be more comfortable, you can lay your head on my lap. These rocks aren't exactly the most comfortable..."@@ You know that well, your naked flesh is way more sensitive than her leathery hide, and sitting on them hurts a bit. But you are not dying from god knows what, so she most likely deserves better treatment. @@.azriSpeech;"Mmm..."@@ She mumbles, hesitating for a moment, but eventually moving. Careful not to impale you with her long horns, she rests her head on your lap, and her body seems to relax a bit. You wipe some of the blood staining her skull, and once she is clean, you gently run a hand over her forehead and hair in a soothing motion. She doesn't say a thing, simply resting there as her breathing slowly steadies. She never falls truly asleep, but she stays so still, so quiet, that were it not for the faint light in her eyes and the occasional sigh, you could easily have mistaken her for a corpse. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Keep soothing her |AzriSoothing]]</div>\ You keep rubbing her head in comforting motions, running your fingers over her skull and horns. As time goes on, you get more daring, moving a little lower and rubbing her neck as well. You know for a fact that despite the lack of reaction, the loving touch is definitely appreciated, and so you keep gently massaging her neck and head. Eventually, you lower your head onto hers, and place a soothing little kiss on her forehead. @@.azriSpeech;"Mhm. You are... a good human."@@ She whispers slowly. @@.playerSpeech;"Shh... just rest."@@ She does not say another word, but you can almost see a faint smile on her face. <<if $healSkill >= 1>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Use your healing magic on her |HealingMagicAzri][$azriHealth +=1]]<div class="numberStat">1</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedHeal.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedCross.png">[[Use your healing magic on her |HealingMagicAzri]]<div class="numberStat">1</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedHeal.png"></div>@@\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Try to hunt something for her to eat |TryToHuntAzri]]<div class="numberStat">2</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\ You decide to use some of your healing magic on her, doubting it will be nearly enough to make her recover, but hopefully, it'll give her a small boost. You place your hand on her chest, right in the middle of her protruding bones, and after taking a moment to focus, let the magic flow out of you and into her. The faint yellow light that emanates from your hand washes over her, and she lets out a soft sigh of relief that warms your heart. <div class="passCheck">\ Some cuts disappear under your palm, and the bleeding from her mouth seems to slow down, but it's clear that this is not a true fix. She's still badly wounded, and your magic can only do so much. <img src="Images/Icons/Heal.png"></div>\ Nonetheless, the demoness appreciates every bit of the attention you are giving her. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Try to hunt something for her to eat |TryToHuntAzri]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\@@.playerSpeech;"Hey um... I'm still not completely sure what you eat. But I could try to hunt or gather something in this area."@@ @@.azriSpeech;"Some of my... relatives can gain physical nourishment from consuming raw emotions alone. Yours would surely qualify, given the intensity and depth of the feelings they elicit..."@@ She muses, a weird compliment but a compliment nontheless @@.azriSpeech;"I however am not one of them, I suppose evolution didn't see fit to give me that more... refined diet."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"What do you need, then?"@@ @@.azriSpeech;"Souls."@@ She states matter of factly. @@.playerSpeech;"Any soul?"@@ You reply, not surprised by the answer in the slightest. @@.azriSpeech;"Any living creature. The more complex, the better."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Let her rest for a bit, you will hunt later |AzriLetRest][$azriHealth +=1]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Try to find a creature for her |HuntAzriSegment0]]<div class="numberStat">2</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\You decide to let her rest for a little bit. She seems way too comfortable in your lap, with your hand caressing her skull... and you could use some rest yourself. @@.playerSpeech;"I will see what I can hunt, then."@@ @@.azriSpeech;"We must not stay idle for too long... the longer we linger, the more likely they will find us."@@ She says, though she does not move from her position in the slightest. She is quite comfortable lying on your lap, after all... @@.playerSpeech;"Just a little bit. The sun is still high"@@ @@.azriSpeech;"Mhm. Well, you are the hunter..."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Rest, then"@@ <div class="passCheck">\ She nods, her eyes flickering out as she goes back into that semi-meditative state you have seen her so many times inside her prison. <img src="Images/Icons/Heal.png"></div>\ She stays completely still for at least a couple of hours, and once you are positive she is completely relaxed, you carefully pull yourself from under her head, and go outside, hoping to find some kind of animal or prey to hunt. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Lets see... |HuntAzriSegment]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\<<if $hunterSkill >= 2>>\ You spend hours wandering through the forest, searching for some animal to hunt. The terrain is treacherous, full of sharp rocks, thorny bushes, and all kinds of garbage you wouldn't have to deal with, had you just some meager boots. The place seems to be mostly devoid of any kind of animal life. You nearly give up a couple of times, your feet burn, and your skin is full of scratches, but eventually, a sound reaches your ears. A couple of branches breaking, and some steps, not too far from you. Careful not to make any sound, you walk towards the noise, until you find some kind of four-legged, green, fluffy creature, not bigger than an ordinary dog, eating some berries. Its long, slender tail stays constantly in movement, and the thing is completely oblivious to your presence. <div class="passCheck">\ You crouch and wait, making sure that it will not hear you, and once you are confident, you pounce. You manage to pin the animal on the ground, and after a brief struggle, you manage to immobilize it. The creature squeaks and hisses, desperately trying to escape, but no matter how much it squirms and wiggles, it is no match for you. <img src="Images/Icons/General.png"></div>\ It's not much, but at least you'll be able to return with something. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back to the cave |ReturnCave][$azriHunt = true]]</div>\ <<else>>\ You spend hours wandering through the forest, searching for some animal to hunt. The terrain is treacherous, full of sharp rocks, thorny bushes, and all kinds of garbage you wouldn't have to deal with, had you just some meager boots on your feet. However and much to your anger, despite walking for what seems like forever, you aren't able to find a single damn creature. No birds, no rodents, nothing. There are a couple of bugs and some other insects, but that's it. <div class="failCheck">\ Eventually, the sun sets completely, and as the cold begins to creep through the land, you make your way back to the cave, empty-handed and tired. <img src="Images/Icons/FailGeneral.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back to the cave |ReturnCave][$azriHunt = false]]</div>\ <</if>>\ @@.playerSpeech;"Alright. Let me see what I can do"@@ You say, carefully placing her head on the ground to avoid hurting her, and getting up. You are not sure what you will be able to get naked and unarmed, but hey, you are a fucking hunter of the Veilwalkers. You can certainly get something with your bare hands. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Lets see... |HuntAzriSegment]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\<<if $azriHealth >=3 >>\ You make your way back to the cave, and Azri'Elis is still lying down just as you left her, but it's looking just a tiny bit better. As you step into view, she seems to perk up, and she sits down to greet you. <<else>>\ You make your way back to the cave, and Azri'Elis is still lying down, and still looking as bad as when you first arrived. Her fingers softly probe her chest, and she winces in pain at the touch. <</if>>\ <<if $azriHunt is true>>\ @@.azriSpeech;"Your feet..."@@ She observes from the ground, and you glance down at the state of your soles. They're scratched and covered in blood, the only result of walking barefoot for hours. @@.playerSpeech;"The terrain isn't very friendly"@@ You shrug. @@.playerSpeech;"I would have appreciated some shoes... anyways."@@ You present the demoness your catch, not impressive by any means but the best you could manage, and the creature's hissing gets louder. @@.playerSpeech;"This was all I could find. This fucking place is barren."@@ <<if $azriHealth >=3 >>\ She nods, quite grateful still, and extends a hand to receive the creature as it squeaks and tries its best to chomp your arm. The moment her claws close around the animal's body, the hissing and thrashing dies down. The dark dot in the center of her horns makes itself visible once more, and from her mouth, a thick black fog begins to pour out, seeping through her teeth and enveloping the creature. The poor animal stops moving, and soon its body begins to dry and decay, turning into a grey, desiccated husk. <div class="passCheck">\ Her eyes light up a little, and you can feel some warmth returning to her body. <img src="Images/Icons/Heal.png"></div>\ @@.azriSpeech;"Thank you,"@@ She tells you, as the creature crumbles into dust and blows away in what little wind makes its way inside the cave. @@.azriSpeech;"I had forgotten how eating felt like..."@@ Indeed, this must be the first piece of... soul? she's gotten to taste since her imprisonment. @@.playerSpeech;"Shame it was... well, that."@@ You reply, jokingly. @@.playerSpeech;"So... do you feel any better?"@@ She nods. @@.azriSpeech;"A little. I think I have figured out why the void was so hostile to me. The weapon the other human wielded was imbued with some sort of curse, a powerful one. I'm sure they knew about my... ability. Whatever it was, it is keeping me from using my magic. They wanted to make sure I stayed pinned to one world, helpless and ready to die. It's a miracle I managed to shift us before the curse buried its roots."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"I.. see"@@ Not a big surprise, every last one of the weapons handcrafted by Vael uses the power of some kind of bound entity, giving them all kinds of special properties. @@.azriSpeech;"Still, I should recover eventually, hopefully soon. Enough to take us somewhere else. Somewhere safe."@@ You nod, sitting down beside her. Now it's your time to catch your breath a little. @@.azriSpeech;"...Aren't you cold?"@@ She suddenly asks, @@.azriSpeech;"Night here is going to be more frigid than it was in our prison"@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["A little" |AzriLittleCold]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["Im fine" |AzriFineCold]]</div>\ <<else>>\ She nods, quite grateful still, and extends a hand to receive the creature as it squeaks and tries its best to chomp your arm. <div class="failCheck">\ However, she is too weak to even consume the small animal, her grip loosening as she tries to focus her energy on draining the life of the little creature, and it ends up slipping out of her hands and bolting back into the woods. <img src="Images/Icons/FailHeal.png"></div>\ @@.playerSpeech;"Fuck! Come back here you piece of shit"@@ You curse, trying to catch it before it escapes. But it's too late. The animal has run away, and it's way too dark outside for you to try and track it down. @@.azriSpeech;"I... am sorry. I... I can't believe I have lost it."@@ She mumbles, shame washing over her. @@.playerSpeech;"It's alright. Not your fault. We can try again, I'll see if I can catch another one tomorrow"@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Sleep |SleepAzri]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedSleep.png"></div>\ <</if>>\ <<else>>\ @@.azriSpeech;"Your feet..."@@ She observes, and you glance down at the state of your soles. They're scratched and covered in blood, a result of walking barefoot for hours. @@.playerSpeech;"The terrain isn't very friendly"@@ You shrug. @@.playerSpeech;"I would have appreciated some shoes... anyways. And sadly it was all for nothing. This place is completely barren, I didn't spot a single animal."@@ @@.azriSpeech;"I indeed couldn't have shifted into a worse world.."@@ She admits, looking a little apologetic. @@.playerSpeech;"How are you feeling?"@@ @@.azriSpeech;"Bad. I think I have figured out why the void was so hostile to me. The weapon the other human wielded was imbued with some sort of curse, a powerful one. I'm sure they knew about my... ability. Whatever it was, it is keeping me from using my magic. They wanted to make sure I stayed pinned to one world, helpless and ready to die. It's a miracle I managed to shift us before the curse buried its roots."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"I.. see"@@ Not a big surprise, every last one of the weapons your order uses uses the power of some kind of bound entity, giving them all kinds of special properties. @@.azriSpeech;"Still, I should recover eventually, hopefully soon. Enough to take us somewhere else. Somewhere safe."@@ You nod, sitting down beside her. Now it's your time to catch your breath a little. @@.azriSpeech;"Sleep now. The night is fast approaching. We'll find something tomorrow"@@ She whispers, her tone soothing and almost motherly. You lay down and she moves closer, holding you against her body. She is a bit cold, but compared to the stone, her embrace is incredibly warm. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Sleep |SleepAzri]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedSleep.png"></div>\ <</if>>\ @@.playerSpeech;"A little"@@ You admit, and she scoots closer, the faint glow of her eyes intensifying. @@.azriSpeech;"Perhaps I could help you warm up, then."@@ With that, she pulls you in for a hug and holds you against her body. She has regained her body temperature, and her black fur is once again quite pleasant to the touch. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Sleep |SleepAzri]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedSleep.png"></div>\ <<if $healSkill >=3 >>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Try to remove the curse overnight |RemoveCurse]]<div class="numberStat">3</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedHeal.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Try to remove the curse overnight |RemoveCurse]]<div class="numberStat">3</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedHeal.png"></div>@@\ <</if>>\@@.playerSpeech;"I'm fine,"@@ You tell her, not too sure of it yourself. @@.azriSpeech;"Mhm. Then come here, I could use some warmth myself"@@ She lets out a small chuckle, before pulling you in for a hug and holding you against her body. She has regained her body temperature, and her black fur is once again quite pleasant to the touch. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Sleep |SleepAzri]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedSleep.png"></div>\ <<if $healSkill >=3 >>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Try to remove the curse overnight |RemoveCurse]]<div class="numberStat">3</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedHeal.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Try to remove the curse overnight |RemoveCurse]]<div class="numberStat">3</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedHeal.png"></div>@@\ <</if>>\You decide get some rest yourself. You'll need to be in a decent shape if you are to catch something else for her to eat, and right now... you are fairly exhausted. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Next morning... |NextMorningAzri]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedSleep.png"></div>\You decide to push yourself a little more and stay awake, emboldened by her small improvement. Sure, it may not have been much, but with what little you have been able to do for her, she already looks so much better than when you first arrived. Ignoring the rocks on your skin, you cuddle close to her, one hand on her back and the other on her chest. After taking a deep breath, you start focusing again, channeling the magic into her. Another pleased sigh escapes her, and the sound alone is enough to make you smile. @@.azriSpeech;"Come on... don't you need to rest? I'm exhausted…"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"I can sleep tomorrow."@@ She doesn't complain any further. By now, she knows just how stubborn you can be, and so instead, she just tucks you under her big tail, as if she were some kind of makeshift blanket. You manage to stay like that for hours, simply focused on pouring as much of your limited magic into her as you can, and the results are visible. As you grow more tired, she seems to grow warmer, and some of the more grievous wounds and cracks on her skin start healing over as well. To your surprise, you hear her snore lightly at some point. You have never seen her truly sleeping, only in that meditative trance of hers, and so it's quite a curious sight. She looks... so peaceful, and her expression is so serene. It almost makes you forget she could snap you in half in a second... almost like she did when you first met. She surely had her reasons. However, you know that healing her physical wounds is not enough. You need to remove the curse that Razel's weapon inflicted on her. You've never tried to work with curses before, but you have some knowledge of the subject. The process can be tiresomely long, but it can be approached with... simpler methods. It's not a carefully tailored curse, so it won't take much to disrupt its hold on her, hopefully. Just a great amount of effort. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Next morning... |nextMorningHealed]]</div>\A decently sound night's sleep is suddenly interrupted by Azri'Elis, who shakes your shoulder and whispers to you, her tone urgent. @@.azriSpeech;"Wake up."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Mhm...?"@@ @@.azriSpeech;"They are here. You need to go."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Wha- who?"@@ As you blink the sleep away, you become fully aware of who exactly. @@.playerSpeech;"Already?"@@ @@.azriSpeech;"I can feel the disturbance in the veil. We don't have much time. They're coming, and they're coming fast. You need to go. If you leave now, maybe they won't find you"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"And what about you?"@@ @@.azriSpeech;"I'm easier to find, even more so already cursed and marked as I am. I don't think I'd get far with the state I'm in."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Shit..."@@ You curse. Your order sometimes takes years to finally deal with some individuals. Did it really have to be now, the time they decided to move in just hours? @@.playerSpeech;"...Can't you try and shift again? Maybe to a nearby plane"@@ @@.azriSpeech;"..."@@ Her silence speaks for itself. She looks just a tiny bit better than she was when you first arrived here, but she still can barely stand upright without wobbling. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Leave while you still can |LeaveAzri]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["I'm not leaving you here" |DontLeaveAzri]]</div>\The first rays of the sun filter through the cave's opening, and only then does Azri wake up, slightly alarmed. She's not too used to fully giving herself to sleep. @@.azriSpeech;"What... what happened?"@@ She asks, looking around and slowly taking in the details. It's morning already, and... you are still awake and channeling your magic into her. @@.azriSpeech;"You... haven't slept yet?"@@ "No." You reply, a bit drily. You are feeling quite fatigued by now... It's your turn now to feel like shit. "But... I think I've managed to lift the curse on you." @@.azriSpeech;"I... feel much better."@@ She replies, and she sounds genuinely surprised. @@.azriSpeech;"You are a much more capable caster than I thought. I... thank you. Again."@@ "Good. I... am glad. Yes..." You mumble, laying down and closing your eyes, unable to hold back the yawn. Under your eyes, two heavy eyebags have formed. @@.azriSpeech;"Oh. I see. Come here"@@ She says, picking you up and cradling you against her chest, right in the middle of her outer set of bones. @@.azriSpeech;"We won't waste any more time here, we are too easy to find. I will take us to somewhere safer."@@ "Mhm, glad to hear" She chuckles at the sight, amused by the sudden role reversal. @@.azriSpeech;"I'll make sure to <strong>properly</strong> reward you once I have fully recovered~"@@ She says, her voice teasing even if she's still struggling to fully control her body. That catches your attention. @@.playerSpeech;"Properly?"@@ You repeat, raising an eyebrow and smirking. @@.playerSpeech;"I hope there are some teeth involved in this reward..."@@ @@.azriSpeech;"Plenty~"@@ <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Continue...">> <<goto "Ending1">> <</link>>\ </div>\ Even if it pains you to admit it, Azri'Elis is right. She is an easy target, and even if you probably are as well, you at the very least have a chance to run. To disappear. Korvaax has been missing for years, maybe they'll think the same of you. Just another Veilwalker who went rogue. @@.playerSpeech;"F-fine. I will leave."@@ You say, and the demoness nods at that. @@.azriSpeech;"Run, and don't look back. I will hold them off for as long as I can"@@ "I'm... sorry" You tell her as you slowly make your way to the mouth of the cave. @@.azriSpeech;"You have nothing to apologize for. I couldn't take us far away. And you saved me one time already. If you stay this time you would be giving your valuable life for nothing."@@ You wave goodbye to her as you step out. @@.azriSpeech;"Farewell, human. May our paths cross once more"@@ You highly doubt that, but you appreciate the sentiment. @@.playerSpeech;"May they do."@@ ... <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Continue...">> <<goto "DeathAzriHuntersLeave">> <</link>>\ </div>\ @@.playerSpeech;"I'm not leaving you here,"@@ You tell her, firmly. "Don't be foolish! You already saved my life once. If you stay, they'll just kill you. Your death would acomplish nothing." @@.playerSpeech;"I know it might not make any sense... but… look. I'm not abandoning you, and that's final"@@ She stays silent for a few seconds, concern in her expression. Then, rage. @@.azriSpeech;"If you don't leave <strong>RIGHT</strong> now, I'll kill you myself"@@ No doubt she is bluffing, but... are you really willing to die for her? Someone is dying here, that is for sure. But maybe... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Leave while you still can |LeaveAzri]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Offer her your soul so that she can hopefully shift |OfferSoulAzri]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png"></div>\ @@.playerSpeech;"Kill me, then. Maybe with my soul as a source of energy, you'll be able to shift again. Or at least, stand a chance in the fight to come."@@ That seems to shock her to her core. Clearly not expecting such a response, she stares at you, and the dim lights of her eyes seem to flicker. @@.azriSpeech;"Are... are you insane?"@@ She seems at a loss for words; anger She seems at a loss for words; anger and sadness overwhelming her usual neutral tone. @@.azriSpeech;"Why would you be willing to pay the ultimate price for me? What did I ever do to deserve it? Sharing my flesh with you back there? For the love of all that is, just leave at once you idiot!"@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Try to convince her with logic; the order will kill you regardless |ConvinceLogic]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Try to convince her through emotion; She is worth it |ConvinceEmotion]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Leave while you still can |LeaveAzri]]</div>\ @@.playerSpeech;"Lauren never forgives... and never forgets. They will catch me, and they will kill me or even worse. You, on the other hand, have the chance to outrun them, to defend yourself once you've recovered enough. I'm a dead man walking."@@ She shakes her head, still having a hard time believing it. @@.playerSpeech;"Why are you hesitating? You told me before, my life is but an instant compared to yours! Why would you hesitate to sacrifice me? You wouldn't hesitate to consume an animal, why are you doing so now?"@@ @@.azriSpeech;"That's different!"@@ She mumbles, realizing that perhaps, the right, logical choice would be taking your offer, and saving her skin. @@.playerSpeech;"I... appreciate your feelings, and I wish it could be any other way. The fact is, we either both die here, or you take my soul, and have a chance at survival"@@ She remains silent, struggling with her emotions. After a moment, she nods slowly. @@.azriSpeech;"You're a fool. Irredeemable. But a brave one. If you're truly set on this, then take a step forward."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Take a step forward... |GiveSoulAzri]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png"></div>\@@.playerSpeech;"You deserve it. You have suffered at our hands for far too long, and you have at least earned a chance at survival, even if it means I die. It's the least I can do."@@ @@.azriSpeech;"No, no, no"@@ She shakes her head, a mixture of sadness and frustration in her voice. @@.azriSpeech;"How can you be such a, such a...? How can you have so much compassion for me? You know what I am. What I will do! How can you say that?"@@ You shrug. @@.playerSpeech;"Your revenge? I suppose it's fair after all. My order... we have done some awful things. I have done some awful things."@@ She lets out a long, sad sigh. @@.azriSpeech;"I am not going to let you die for me. You have done more than enough for me already, and I refuse to exploit you for my own benefit. Now LEAVE before it's too late."@@ You open your mouth to protest, but another voice interrupts you. Razel. @@.hunterSpeech;"The creature can't be helped, it's in her evil nature... but you? How DARE you betray the Order. How DARE you turn your back on the will of The Eye, and the mission we all swore to fulfill?"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Razel, listen..."@@ @@.hunterSpeech;"Silence."@@ He cuts you off. Behind him, two more hunters appear. Your former comrades. "You are a disgrace to our ranks, and your sentence can only be death." Azri'Elis speaks then, her tone surprisingly calm and composed. @@.azriSpeech;"Fools, all of you. He's been dancing to my tune for days now. You were stupid enough to trap him with me, did you think I wouldn't seize the opportunity? Rage has always blinded your judgment, mortals." She then turns at you, slowly. "On your knees, slave."@@ A wave of what little power she can muster crashes into you, trying to force you down to keep the act. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Play along |PlayAlongAzri]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Stand with her |StandAzri]]<div class="numberStat">X</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\The choice is already made, isn't it? You know it's the only way to save her. With a heavy heart, you take a step towards her. @@.azriSpeech;"It will be painful."@@ She warns you, maybe hoping that would change your mind. You nod, and close your eyes, bracing for what's to come. @@.azriSpeech;"Then... then..."@@ She takes a deep breath, and a single dark tear runs down her skull. @@.azriSpeech;"Goodbye."@@ You can hear her whispering an apology in guilt, sorrow, and self-hatred, and then, the pain begins. It feels like a thousand burning needles piercing your skin at the same time, and you grit your teeth, fighting the urge to scream, but luckily, it doesn't last long. Death comes for you soon, and your last thought is for the demoness, hoping that she will have a better fate than yours. <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Death embraces you...">> <<goto "Death">> <</link>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png" >\ </div>\Playing along feels like a betrayal, but at the same time... perhaps it is the only chance you’ll have for one of you to come back out alive from this. Trying to get ahold of your emotions, you do as told, falling on your knees and lowering your head, showing deference and submissiveness. Razel arches an eyebrow, not quite convinced and still furious. @@.hunterSpeech;"...Lauren shall decide your fate. Ron, tie him up and take him back. Ema, with me. This wretched abomination dies now."@@ As you feign obedience, Azri'Elis meets your gaze one last time, a silent understanding passing between you. She can barely muster her strength to fight but still tries to make it seem like she has some amount of power left in her. <div class="passCheck">\ As a result, the hunters make quick work of her, one heavy blow is enough to shatter her ribcage, and another one crushes her head, causing her whole form to banish into thin smoke. <img src="Images/Icons/Death.png"></div>\ After making sure she is indeed dead, the hunters escort you out and take you back to Lauren, barely a word is exchanged the whole time. <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Continue...">> <<goto "PlayAlongAzriEnding">> <</link>>\ </div>\ However, you do not play along. Instead, you stand firm and look at Razel right in the eye. @@.playerSpeech;"I did it on my own, I was not tricked, or brainwashed. And I will stand for Azri'Elis again."@@ You announce, raising your voice and your fists, ready to fight even when you know the battle is completely lost. The demoness hisses in anger. @@.azriSpeech;"Why? WHY can't you just..."@@ Another sprout of black blood comes out of her mouth, as a result of the little magic she tried to conjure. Razel's face remains emotionless as he slowly draws his greathammer, the other two hunters readying their weapons as well. @@.hunterSpeech;"Very well."@@ ... The battle is not that much a battle but rather, a one-sided slaughter. Without your weapons, you are completely defenseless, and Azri'Elis is too weakened to be much of a help. In mere moments, you're lying on the ground, barely alive, as Razel raises his hammer, ready to smash your head into a paste. @@.hunterSpeech;"You are not a true Veilwalker. You never were."@@ <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Death embraces you...">> <<goto "Death">> <</link>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png" >\ </div>\Lauren isn't pleased when you return. She's understandably upset that you failed your assigned mission in such a spectacular fashion, and even more so when they dig a bit more into it and find no real trace of Azri'Elis magic. Soon enough, the truth is out; the sentence, death. And Razel happily volunteers for the task, eager to get payback... <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Death embraces you...">> <<goto "Death">> <</link>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png" >\ </div>\@@.title;ENDING 1@@ @@.titleSmall;Traitor in hiding@@ You had to make a decision, and you chose. Your short days as a High Hunter are evidently over, but Azri'Elis is alive because of you, and that's all that really matters. And she didn't forget about it. You couldn't stay in her world; it would be the first place they'd look. But from there, it's easier for her to navigate the other planes, and you keep on the move to avoid your former masters. This time, you actually get to see many new worlds, unconstrained by your former duties. Many, many interesting sights, from the beautiful and idyllic to the terrible and desolate... The only constant that remains between all these lands is, well, the demoness that carries you through them. It may be true that your life is but an instant to her, but Azri'Elis is eager to repay your kindness, sharing her time with you, and even teaching you to draw power from the same dark source she does. You are no match for her in your sparring sessions, of course, but she is impressed with how quickly you are picking up magic. And then there are the more... intimate lessons. Maybe your blood can't channel magic as effectively as hers can, but when it comes to sexual gratification, you take the lead, much to the demoness' delight. You make her moan and squirm, and she eventually just gives up trying to understand your odd ministrations and weird human ways, allowing you to explore her body and indulge your kinks at will. <<if $cumSkill >=5>>\ <div class="passCheck">\ <<set $impregnations to $temporaryImpregnations +1 >>\ One day however, you both face an unexpected turn of events. It's a bit of a shock for the both of you, hers especially... but it comes to a point where denying the truth before your eyes its simply impossible. She is carrying your child, and while neither of you can be certain of what that will bring, there is a spark of happiness within the two of you. Your nomad life has been fun, but now, there is a purpose to it. A goal to work towards. You settle down in a plane far, far away from any known paths, and there, you make a home. There is a lot of work to do, and the threat of your order will always be a distant shadow, but... it feels right. <img src="Images/Icons/PregIcon.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ <div class="failCheck">\ A new life, harsher, but in a way, better. You have no regrets about the decision you made that day. <img src="Images/Icons/FailPreg.png"></div>\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Play again">> <<goto "SetName">> <</link>>\ </div>\ <<set $ending1 to 1>>You leave, and the hunters arrive just minutes later, three of them with Razel leading them. Azri'Elis doesn't stand a chance, but at the very least, her death is a quick one. Unfortunately for you, the hunters are equally quick to catch up to you. You move way too slowly naked as you are, and your bloody footprints are easy to follow. <div class="passCheck">\ A precisely shot arrow piercing your lungs and the harsh ground are the last you ever feel. <img src="Images/Icons/Death.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Death embraces you...">> <<goto "Death">> <</link>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png" >\ </div>\@@.title;ENDING 2@@ @@.titleSmall;Demoted@@ Lauren isn't pleased when you return. She's understandably upset that you failed your assigned mission in such a spectacular fashion, and even if she is glad you are back and relatively unharmed, she's quick to revoke your status as a High Hunter and assign you to other duties. Putting you there was a rushed decision, and a mistake. The Essence Feaster is never returned to you, and your personal quarters are given to a newer member of the Order. Still, you have the 'chance' to be of use to the order, even if your role is much less prestigious.... and a lot less interesting. But hey, you are alive, right? <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Play again">> <<goto "SetName">> <</link>>\ </div>\ <<set $ending2 to 1>>You decide that the best course of action is to find her. Let's face it, no one is coming to rescue you right now, and by the time they decide to even come looking, You will either be dead from thirst, or by the fucking demon trapped with you. So why would you even bother in hiding? If you are to die, you prefer dying a warrior, and not cowering in the dirt... So you set off, in the hopes of seeing the demoness, and you do. It does not take long... she has always been quite visible in fact. Makes you wonder just how could you have missed her. She's sitting on the flat surface of the biggest boulder, in what you assume to be the center of the space, legs crossed and arms folded in her lap. She seems to be in deep meditation, her thin form outlined against the dimly glowing sky. She doesn't seem to notice you though, and if she does, she's not showing it. <img src="Images/Monsters/AzriFixed2.png" class="azri-image" alt="AzriImage"> Maybe she is inactive while she is inside her own prison? You don't remember reading or being told anything along those lines, but if it were to be that way... then it would certainly facilitate things a whole lot. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Strike now |StrikeAzriNow]]<div class="numberStat">X</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Check if she is indeed, inert |CheckIfInert]]</div>\However it may be, you are simply not going to let such an opportunity go to waste. If she is meditating, in a coma, asleep, or whatever it is that she is doing, you have to act as soon as possible. You stare at her for a moment, trying to figure out the best course of action. Even if she is not conscious, you are not really sure how you are supposed to take her down without your tools, she is slightly taller than you and even if she is thin, she looks awfully strong. Her neck seems particularly vulnerable... maybe you could sneak up on her and snap it? Or choke her, she is quite skinny, after all. You are not really sure she needs to breathe, so just in case you would rather avoid doing that. Just in case. @@.playerSpeech;"Here goes nothing..."@@ You whisper, before silently making your way towards her. You move as stealthily as possible, climbing the boulder with ease. She doesn't stir, doesn't seem to sense you in the slightest. You can do this. You creep up behind her, cautious, trembling hands, reaching out around her neck and skull, and with all the strength you can muster, you attempt to snap her neck in one swift motion. However, there is no crack, no pop as her spine shatters... and the next instant, she is a blur of motion. The only thing that ends up snapping is your own neck, and your corpse tumbles down the boulder, lifeless eyes staring into the sky as your head rests at an unnatural angle. <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Death embraces you...">> <<goto "Death">> <</link>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png" >\ </div>\Before you do anything stupid, you first want to make sure she is not going to wake up the moment you lay your hands on her. Even if you've got the element of surprise, you are pretty much useless without your weapons... @@.playerSpeech;"Hello? I am $playerName, I'm not sure how, but I have ended up here..."@@ You begin, not wanting to immediately antagonize her. She does not move, nor does she answer. @@.playerSpeech;"Can you hear me, Azri'Elis?"@@ You ask, a bit louder this time. No response. You climb the boulder then, and approach her, your movements cautious and careful. Even if she has a lithe build, her arms and legs thin and delicate, the sharp talons and horns are a dead giveaway of just how lethal she can be. And now that you are barely a couple of feet away from her, you can't help but notice her odd beauty. Her animal skull for a face is haunting, with empty eye sockets that make you feel dizzy just by looking at them, and her form is as dark as night, covered in a patchwork of fur, but the most striking feature has to be the set of ribs that protrude from her chest. They jut out like a macabre chest-plate, creating quite an unsettling image. Despite it all, and despite the initial fear she evokes, you can't deny that there's a strange and captivating beauty in her terrifying form. Something must be really wrong with your brain because as you stare at her more and more, other ideas begin to pop in your mind. For now, you still need to verify she is truly unconscious... so, your hand reaches for her shoulder, and your fingers graze her short fur. Her warmth comes as a pleasant surprise. Creatures born of the void are not supposed to be warm, are they? Her eyes remain unlit, and you now rest your hand on her shoulder, the sensation of her soft, sleek fur tickling your palm. @@.playerSpeech;"Azri'Elis...?"@@ you repeat. No response. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Strike now |StrikeAzriNow]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Have some... fun, since you are at it|HaveFunAzri]]</div>\ You stay silent for a moment then, your hand still on her shoulder, now caressing her slowly. You know it doesn't really make much sense, but... it's all you can think of right now. The argument for doing this is there, kind of; even if you wanted to, even if she really would stay inert as you tried to kill her, you doubt you could ever accomplish such a feat naked and unarmed. These creatures don't die easily. It's a flawed explanation, and you know it's only an excuse for the real motivator behind your actions. If you are going to die, or if you are going to kill her, you'd at least have some fun before that. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Feel her up |FeelAzriUp]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Jerk off to her |JerkOffAzri]]</div>\Your fingers now trail over her fur, along her shoulder blades, and then up the side of her neck, until they reach her jawline. She's really quite pretty, despite the danger her sharp fangs pose, and the eerie emptiness of her vacant eye sockets. Your eyes fall to the protruding ribs that decorate her flat chest, and your curious hand wanders there, right between them. You can feel another set of ribs beneath her skin, so you figure the ones at both sides, not actual ribs. A shiver goes down your spine as you imagine those closing over your arm like a bear trap, but it's not enough to make your hard dick go down. @@.playerSpeech;"I shouldn't be doing this..."@@ You mumble, but you simply can't stop. She may have no breasts, but the odd bones sticking out of her chest manage to captivate your attention the same or even more. They look so pure, so polished... You reach out, your fingers trembling slightly, and you gently touch the exposed 'ribs', feeling their cold, smooth surface. Your touch is delicate as if you're handling something both fragile and dangerous, and your fingers trail up and down, tracing their contours and exploring each one individually. The skin surrounding the base of each bone is melded perfectly to them, and the ring of smooth, dark flesh surrounding them is devoid of fur. It's something imperceptible, as the nearby hair covers it entirely, but you can feel it with your fingertips as they explore her. @@.playerSpeech;"Such a pretty thing... It's a shame you're a murderous monster... we could've had a lot of fun..."@@ You are too focused on her chest to notice how your touch is actually affecting her, how she has started to twitch ever so slightly from your relentless fondling on her quite sensitive chest. @@.playerSpeech;"Oh shi-"@@ By the time you notice it, it's already too late. Two burning dots become visible in her eye sockets, and a dark sphere materializes between her two impressive horns. What comes next is so quick you barely have time to register it. Understandably startled by the sight and proximity, she lashes out at you. The orb formed above her head pulses and extends itself in your direction, her normally controlled magical prowess spiraling into an unbridled maelstrom of raw power. A shockwave of burning dark energy crashes upon you, instantly reducing your body to a charred, smoking husk. Azri'Elis remains unmoving for a few seconds, coming out of her stupor and trying to understand what the hell just happened. Just why a naked hunter was... feeling up her external bones. But your body has been so utterly incinerated that she has no hope of retrieving your brain and extracting any satisfactory answer from there. The rather simple truth dies with you... <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Death embraces you...">> <<goto "Death">> <</link>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png" >\ </div>\ What are you gonna do? Stupid as it is, the only thing on your mind right now is how good it would be to mark her dark fur with a thick load of cum. And you are going to do just that. You take a step forward, your dick pointing straight at her as you prepare to unleash a torrent of seed all over her chest. She's for sure not what many would hold as pretty, her sharp fangs and eerie, empty eyes would be more than enough to give most people nightmares, but something about her just gets to you. You are not sure if it's her toned body, her angular, almost predatory skull, or the exposed bones that stick out of her chest, but whatever it is, it has gotten to you. @@.playerSpeech;"Such a pretty thing..."@@ You say, your hand stroking your length rapidly. "It's a shame you're a murderous monster... I would have loved to take you for a ride..." <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Cum over her chest |CumAzriChest]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCum.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Cum over her skull |CumAzriSkull]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCum.png"></div>\@@.playerSpeech;"I'm gonna paint your fur white..."@@ You grunt, your eyes glued to her body as your orgasm builds. It comes quickly, but the amount of jizz you expel is nothing short of impressive. Thick ropes of spunk shoot from your tip, going through her exposed bones and landing right in the middle of her chest, a lonely spurt even landing on her neck. @@.playerSpeech;"Holy fuck..."@@ You moan, still coming down from the high, and a bit surprised at the volume. You have always cum quite a bit, but it's always a nice sight... A nice, messy sight... But then, to no one's surprise, the inevitable happens. Perhaps finally alerted of your presence because of the sudden warmth of your cum, perhaps awoken by your grunting, two bright dots of burning bright suddenly flare up in her empty sockets, and a dark sphere materializes between her two impressive horns. What comes next is so quick you barely have time to register it. Understandably startled by the sight, she lashes out at you, the orb formed above her head pulses and extends itself in your direction, and her normally controlled magical prowess spirals into an unbridled maelstrom of raw power. A shockwave of burning dark energy crashes upon you, instantly reducing your body to a charred, smoking husk. Azri'Elis remains unmoving for a few seconds, now realizing the puddle of cum on her chest, and snaps her mandibles in frustration. What the hell would a naked hunter be doing on her prison, jerking off, and cumming all over her? Just a madman, a sexual deviant?... or something more? But your corpse has been so utterly incinerated, that there is no chance she can take a look into your brain to understand what the hell is going on. The rather simple truth dies with you. <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Death embraces you...">> <<goto "Death">> <</link>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png" >\ </div>\@@.playerSpeech;"I'm gonna paint that pretty skull of yours..."@@ You grunt, your hand pumping furiously as you imagine just how good it would be to have your thick load running down the edges of her bony skull. It comes quickly, but the amount of jizz you let off is nothing short of impressive. Thick, sticky strands fly through the air, coating her skull all the way from her maxilla to her forehead, a stray spurt even landing directly into her eye socket. @@.playerSpeech;"Holy fuck..."@@ You moan, still coming down from the high, and a bit surprised at the volume. You have always cum a lot, but it's always a nice sight... A nice, sticky sight... But then, to no one's surprise, the inevitable happens. Finally alerted of your presence; probably because of your intrusive cum plastered all over her face, and even inside her left eyes, two bright dots of burning bright suddenly flare up in her empty sockets, and a dark sphere materializes between her two impressive horns. What comes next is so quick you barely have time to register it. Understandably startled by the sight, she lashes out at you, the orb formed above her head pulses and extends itself in your direction, and her normally controlled magical prowess spirals into an unbridled maelstrom of raw power. A shockwave of burning dark energy crashes upon you, instantly reducing your body to a charred, smoking husk. Why would I need to run away from you rangers?"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"We…Listen, I'm not a ranger from Pendervale, whatever that may be. I'm from somewhere far, far away, though that doesn't matter."@@ The people from my order are a threat. We hunt creatures like you. The reason I appeared by your side is precisely that I was supposed to end you, The Eye had marked you as a threat. But... I couldn't bring myself to do it." @@.wolfSpeech;"You...you were going to kill me?"@@ She asks, perplexed. You nod @@.playerSpeech;"Yes, that's what I was sent here to do. I'm an oddity within my order... But take my word when I tell you the others won't hesitate to carry out a job, however gruesome or wrong it may be."@@ Lyra stays silent for a while, before asking a simple @@.wolfSpeech;"But...why?"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"I... I must admit, I don't know how it works myself."@@ @@.wolfSpeech;"W-will they come for me? Or my dad?"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"I don't think so. Seeing her face of pure panic, you decide to try and make it a bit better. @@.playerSpeech;"Look, it was probably a mistake. The Eye sometimes marks innocent creatures, such as yourself. She takes the bow, and stays silent for a moment, looking at the ground. @@.wolfSpeech;"I… well, thank you for everything, really."@@ You smile at her. @@.playerSpeech;"It was my pleasure. Despite your eagerness to get down to business, you take a step back and adopt a more cautious stance, trying to decide how to better approach the fight. <<else>> <<if $wantedFightWolyrn is true>> The fight seems inevitable, even if it's not what you originally wanted. It's understandable that she doesn't trust you. You’ll have to reduce her first, one way or the other. <<else>>\ You get ready for the fight, and try to decide how to better approach it... <</if>>\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Use your hopefully superior martial skill to disarm and immobilize her |DisarmWolyrn]]<div class="numberStat">2</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\ <<if $magicSkill >=0>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Try to use your mental magic to debilitate her |MentalWolyrn]]<div class="numberStat">2</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedMagic.png"></div>\ <</if>>\You want her in one piece, for now at least... So you focus your full attention on her movements, trying to anticipate her attacks as you find an opening to strike. And again, it seems that she had the same idea. You both engage in a cautious dance, circling each other warily as you wait for an opportunity to strike. She is clearly afraid of what you are capable of, and you can sense the hesitation in her movements, her grip on her sharp kamas tight, and a defensive position with her hands on both sides of her head. Your blade offers more protection than her weapons, but she has both greater reach than you, and two vectors she can use to attack simultaneously, so once the assault begins, it quickly becomes apparent that her fighting style is a bit too unpredictable for your liking. As if they were an extension of her dangerous claws, her kamas slash at you from multiple directions, sometimes from up close, others the blades slice through the air with deadly precision. You manage to deflect and evade most of her attacks, relying on your honed martial skills to keep yourself unscathed, but even then a couple of weak strikes manage to hit home, bouncing off your own armor. If you are to defeat her, you cannot let her be the one on the offensive; you must exploit her lackluster defense. <<if $hunterSkill >=2>>\ You trade some blows back now, bringing your sword down to her and shooting crackling beams from it, forcing her to relent her brutal onslaught and instead adopt a more cautious approach. Impressively enough, she is able to dodge and parry your attacks, dancing around you as she avoids being struck, but now her attacks come less frequently, and her blows are weaker. You press the advantage, keeping up the pressure, and after a while, she starts to slow down. You can see the exhaustion creeping into her movements, and you know that it won't be long before she is too tired to continue the fight. <div class="passCheck">\ Finally, you manage to spot an opening, and right as she is about to use her chain to deflect your blade, you let out a point-blank blast that lands square on her chest. <img src="Images/Icons/General.png"></div>\ As if the very crackling beam has a sentience of its own, which, in a way, it has, it extends from your blade and wraps tightly around her body, arms, and then wrists, forcing her to release her grip on her weapons, and from there immobilizing her is a rather simple task, more of the ethereal chains crawling outwards from the point of impact until she is completely at your mercy. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |ContinueWolyrn]]</div>\ <<else>>\ In a sudden burst of speed, you launch yourself at her, hoping to catch her off guard. However, she gracefully sidesteps your advance, expertly twirling one kama to parry your attack while the other darts towards your exposed side, and this one manages to find the gap between your plates and sink into your skin, cutting deep and drawing blood. <div class="failCheck">\ The wound isn't lethal, but as the searing pain reaches your brain, your focus is momentarily broken, and a quick succession of blows hits you, one on the back of your knee, other on the forearm that holds your weapon, and one last on your chest, right below your neck… and you find yourself on the ground. <img src="Images/Icons/FailGeneral.png"></div>\ As you gasp for breath, she simply stands there, visibly shaken as well at the sight of blood, having a hard time believing that she has actually won. Somehow, the wine of victory doesn't taste nearly as good as she hoped it would. @@.wolyrnSpeech;"Why, why did you make me do this?! She doesn't even dodge the oncoming attack, barely stopping the blade with her chain, but not realizing the crackling bolt that quickly follows. It extends from your blade and wraps tightly around her body, arms, and then wrists, forcing her to release her grip on her weapons, and from there immobilizing her is a rather simple task, more of the ethereal chains crawling outwards from the point of impact until she is completely at your mercy. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |ContinueWolyrn]]</div>\ <<else>>\ It's easy to guess just what her weakness is, she has gone through a lot of pain, and you decide to use her fears against her, bringing the visions of her family, slain before her very eyes to the forefront of her consciousness as she fights. <div class="failCheck">\ However, with your limited mental powers, you fail to foresee the outcome of this action, because rather than breaking her spirit, the memories of her family's demise only serve to fuel her rage. <img src="Images/Icons/FailMagic.png"></div>\ Her eyes burn with an intense fury as she channels her grief and anger directly into her strikes, attacking with increasing ferocity with her kamas. Despite the plates and chainmail on your body, your movements are nearly soundless... and guided by the mark, its not long until you spot her; a cute demi-deer collecting fruits from a nearby tree. <img src="Images/Monsters/DeerFixed.png" class="resizable-image" alt="DeerImage"> Her lower body is that of a deer, a graceful creature with delicate hooves and a puffy little tail flicking behind her, but her upper torso is that of a petite teenage girl, with some more animalistic features; Her skin is completely covered by a thin layer of fur, her two antlers stand out amidst her short brunette hair. You watch her for a moment, studying her as you decide how to proceed. It seems like you have been able to reach her unnoticed, but again, these creatures can be really elusive. Even if she looks like a weak enough creature, the last thing you want is having to chase after her through an unfamiliar forest. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Present yourself, try to win her trust to get closer and then strike |PresentToDeer]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCharisma.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Use your Essence Feaster to bind her |AttackDeer]]<div class="numberStat">1-4</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\ <<if $deerPheromones is true>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Use the concentrated pheromones |DeerPheromones]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedItem.png"></div>\ <</if>>Without hesitation, you step out from your hiding spot and present yourself to her, hands up in the air to let her know you mean no harm. Still, you are not precisely a calming sight, and she immediately freezes, her blue eyes widening in fear. @@.playerSpeech;"Don't be scared, I..."@@ you begin, a gentle smile and your softest voice, hoping that she won’t run off. But it's too late. Before your words even have a chance to reach her big ears, she drops her basket and turns around in but a split second, bolting away into the woods as if her life depended on it. You sigh, realizing that your only options now are either chasing her like a maniacal dog in heat or returning blueballed to your world. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Return to your world |ReturnWorldDeer]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTeleport.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Chase after her |ChaseDeer]]<div class="numberStat">3</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\You really have nothing to lose, and so, even if you are not 100% confident you’ll be able to catch a fucking deer, you sprint after her like a madman. You are not completely delusional, though, you don't mean to outrun her, just outlast her. It's the way humans used to hunt faster animals, just wear them down until they collapsed from exhaustion. Its broken but I thought you could be interested in it. If you repair it you could maybe..."@@ The Forgemaster just shakes his head, and sighs. @@.vaelSpeech;"Is this a joke? It's expecting payment. <<set $temporaryCost to $playerLevel * 5>>\ <<set $temporaryCost +=15>>\ <<if $money < $temporaryCost>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedCross.png"> <<link "Level up ($temporaryCost)">> <<goto "LevelUp">> <</link>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedLevelUp.png">\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png">\ </div>@@\ <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"> <<link "Level up ($temporaryCost)">> <<set $money -= $temporaryCost>> <<set $playerLevel += 1>> <<goto "LevelUp">> <</link>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedLevelUp.png">\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png">\ </div>\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["Why do we need to pay you to get stronger?"|AskLuminarch]]</div>\ <<if $fromPortal is false>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |Hunters Hall]]</div>\ <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |PortalRoom]]</div>\ <</if>>\ You pay the amount that is due and watch the construct carefully. Its metallic fingers twitch and flex, taking the offered gold coins and depositing them somewhere within its dark, inner workings. The machine is quiet and still for a while, before it speaks. @@.luminarchSpeech;"Sit. Close thine eyes. Contemplate all thou hast achieved. Reflect upon all thou hast learned..."@@ Following its instructions, you sit before The Luminarch, closing your eyes and letting your mind both wander and focus. As you settle into your seat, thoughts drifting through the corridors of your past achievements and experiences, you feel a subtle shift in the atmosphere. The air around you begins to hum with a gentle, almost imperceptible energy... the creature above you channeling a subtle flow of mana into you, its influence reaching deep into you... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedLevelUp.png">[[Reflect upon your battles |LevelUpStrenght][$hunterSkill +=1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedLevelUp.png">[[Reflect upon your lust|LevelUpCum][$cumSkill +=1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedPreg.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedLevelUp.png">[[Reflect upon those you have met...|LevelUpCharisma][$charismaSkill +=1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCharisma.png"></div>\ <<if $eyeSeeing <= 0>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedLevelUp.png">[[You would like to forget...|LevelUp0][$eyeSeeing -=1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedEye.png"></div>@@\ <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedLevelUp.png">[[You would like to forget... |LevelDownEye][$eyeSeeing -=1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedEye.png"></div>\ <</if>>\ <<if $awakenedMagic is false>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedLevelUp.png">[[Awaken more of your magic powers |LevelUpMagic][$magicSkill +=1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedMagic.png"></div>@@\ <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedLevelUp.png">[[Awaken more of your magic powers |LevelUpMagic][$magicSkill +=1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedMagic.png"></div>\ <</if>>\ <<if $awakenedHeal is false>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedLevelUp.png">[[Awaken more of your magic powers |LevelUpMagic][$healSkill +=1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedHeal.png"></div>@@\ <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedLevelUp.png">[[Awaken more of your magic powers |LevelUpMagic][$healSkill +=1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedHeal.png"></div>\ <</if>> @@.playerSpeech;"Sorry, pal, but not this time. You know the drill. Good luck, and... be safe, will ya?"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Yeah, yeah, I will..."@@ <div class="passCheck">\ Haider hands you a bag of gold, and the travelstone. You take the items and leave him be. <img src="Images/Icons/Item.png"><img src="Images/Icons/Coins.png"></div>\ <<set $haiderIntro to false>>\ <<set $furyJobState to 1>>\ <<set $furyStone to true>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |Hunters Hall][$reputation += 1]]</div>\ No sun can be found in this dimension, so the usual warmness and coziness of a living room are completely absent as well. Regardless, the place has a certain… charm to it. The nearly ancient furniture and the dusty, barely used surfaces give off an old-school feel, somehow. It would certainly improve if the fireplace was lit up, wood burning with a satisfying crackling sound and bright orange light. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue.. |LivingRoom]]</div>\<div class="failCheck">\ You take a closer look at the basement. The stone walls seem almost too well-preserved for a place like this, a shocking contrast with the rest of the house. There is something more as well, something you can't quite describe. An unsettling aura, a lingering sensation of dread... probably just your imagination. <img src="Images/Icons/FailEye.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |Basement]]</div>\ <div class="failCheck">\ You look around, basking in the unknowable darkness that surrounds you. Some people find the void unsettling and frightening, and while you share their sentiment, there is something almost... hypnotic about the sight. Something that draws your eyes and won't let them go, like the crashing waves of a vast ocean, or the flickering of the flames. <img src="Images/Icons/FailEye.png"></div>\ It's then, as your eyes move from piece of debris to piece of debris, that you spot something in the distance. Another small piece of land, with another small house resting atop it. It's not close enough to be seen clearly, but you can tell it's there. Has it always been there? Probably. Maybe it was some sort of storage house, built on another chunk of land that eventually broke off from the main one. It doesn't really matter. Not like you have any way of reaching it... right? <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |Outside]]</div>\ Just a fountain. Water no longer flows out of it, and the basin is full of mud and sand. You notice a vacant spot on the pedestal where something once stood, likely a statue. Maybe you could find one to put there, for the sake of aesthetics... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |Courtyard]]</div>\You take a closer look at the workbench. It's covered in dust, but the surface is still perfectly usable, despite a few stains here and there. <<if $foundWorkshop is false>> <div class="passCheck">Then, you notice something else. Lodged between the workbench and the wall, seems to be a small, crumpled envelop of paper. It seems to have gone unnoticed for quite some time....</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Take the envelop |Envelop]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedItem.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |Workshop]]</div>\ <</if>>\ The bedroom is sparsely decorated, with plain walls and simple furniture. It's clear that the previous owner didn't put much effort into personalizing this place. <<linkreplace "Inspect the nightstand">>\ You open the nightstand drawer, but it's empty. There's nothing inside except for a layer of dust. <</linkreplace>> <<linkreplace "Inspect the wardrobe">>\ No trace of Elisa's belongings, it’s as if she has never existed. <</linkreplace>> <<linkreplace "Inspect the bed">>\ You run your hand over the white bedsheets, feeling their smooth texture. They feel nice against your skin, and they're probably a good deal softer than anything else you've slept on in a while. <</linkreplace>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |Bedroom]]</div>\You are not going to lose any more time. I… I will remain still, y-yes, very still!"@@ She had heard many stories of just how scary humans were, and even those didnt make it justice. I-I'm a virgin, and the Eldarani Ceremony was this spring!"@@ she cries. @@.deerSpeech;"Isn't there another way? I promise I can… I can maybe sing for you! Or or… or I can-”@@ You put a finger to her lips. @@.playerSpeech;"You think telling me you are a virgin is going to make me change my mind? You should have kept that to yourself cutie"@@ You reply, grinning wickedly at her and giving her rump a gentle squeeze. @@.playerSpeech;"I've always been curious, where do you things have your fun parts?
