His carefully calibrated shift leaves you right in the middle of the large valley he had described, with mountains of dark stone surrounding the land on all sides. Unlike with regular travelstones, your time in this world isn't necessarily short. Regardless, you decide to waste no time in deciding where to start looking for the creature he has tasked you to bring back. You have no interest in being here longer than needed. @@.playerSpeech;"These creatures usually reside in either a cave underground caverns, or other kinds of artificial structures… so let's see…"@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Search the feet of the mountain for any cave or crevice |SearchFeet]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Search inside the forest |SeatchForest]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Climb to the mountain peaks |SearchMountainPeaks]]</div>\You search the feet of the mountain for caves or crevices, just in case the creature decided to nest there. The search is long and tedious, and even if these mountains seem prone to fissures and holes, there's nothing around you that would seem suitable for what is essentially a drake that's at least similar in size to you. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Search inside the forest |SeatchForest]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Climb to the mountain peaks |SearchMountainPeaks]]</div>\Feragons aren't usually too fond of forests, but it's not completely off the table that one of them has found a tree or other large piece of plant that they found comfortable to nest on. You walk into the forest, the foliage is not too dense so you move rather quickly. You search for a little while, but you find no sign of any drake or any large animal for that matter. No bones, no tracks, no burned patches of grass... <<if $flowerItem is false>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Pick up a bunch of flowers |PickUpFlowers][$flowerItem = true]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedItem.png"></div>\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Search the feet of the mountain for any cave or crevice |SearchFeet]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Climb to the mountain peaks |SearchMountainPeaks]]</div>\You decide to reach for the mountain peaks, even though you doubt the chances of finding a cave that high up. The temperature drops a little as you begin your thinking trip, but you soon find something that makes a confident smile spread through your lips. Claw marks embedded in the stone, pretty noticeable ones. These creatures are decently heavy and can reach extremely high body temperatures, and their claws are sharp and hot enough to leave their marks on anything short of pure diamond. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Follow the marks |FollowFeragonMarks]]</div>\You change your path and focus on the marks, they're still clear enough for you to follow. The marks are deep but distant, as if the creature was running. Maybe chasing some prey? You chuckle at the thought, what could it be chasing, some kind of particularly swift chunk of steel trying to escape? Quick enough, you spot an opening in the mountainside. This must be the place... And as you focus your ear, you can hear a faint noise coming from within. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Step inside the cave |StepInsideFeragon]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Take a look first |TakeLookFeragon]]</div>\You don't know exactly what you are dealing with, so before barging on like a complete moron, you sneak your way to the cave entrance, slowly peeking inside to take a glimpse of the creature. <img src="Images/Monsters/FeragonFixed.png" class="resizable-image" alt="FeragonFixed"> Luckily, the feragon inside hasn't noticed you, as she is way too busy with her current task. The silver drake is sitting in the middle of the room, her left claw clutching a small piece of semi-molten gold, and you watch as she carries the golden nugget into her open maw. You watch her swallow it and let out a soft, satisfied groan, leaning back on her two hindlegs and dragging her right claw along her long neck, as if accompanying the golden treasure into her belly. She whispers something then, in a tongue you cannot understand, and immediately goes back to business, spewing a short-ranged beam of green fire into the remaining pile of gold of what once could have been a small statue. She melts another chunk, plays with it in her mouth a bit, as if tasting some kind of expensive wine, and then gulps it down with another contented sigh. Quite the performance for what's, essentially just eating. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Step inside the cave |StepInsideFeragon2]]</div>\You step inside, but the feragon doesn't notice your presence. She seems to have all her senses focused on the feast she is having. From a puddle of semi-molten gold, she takes another clawful and lets it drip into her open, waiting jaws, purring softly as the delicious metal makes its way to her stomach. <img src="Images/Monsters/FeragonFixed.png" class="resizable-image" alt="FeragonFixed"> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Grab her attention by saying something |SaySomething]]</div>\You step inside, but still, she doesn't notice your presence. Still too busy nearly having an orgasm by swallowing more gold, eyes closed and tail lazily swishing from side to side. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Grab her attention by saying something |SaySomething]]</div>\<<set $wantedFightFeragon to true>>\ You sigh, finding the situation amusing and stupid at the same time. You clear your throat loudly, making the young feragon's eyes suddenly shoot open. Her head darts towards you, and her tail quickly straightens up. In her pretty face, a wide range of emotions pass. Surprise, fear, embarrassment, and finally fear again. She opens her mouth to say something, but nothing more than a few unintelligible syllables spill out from her lips. Simultaneously, she makes an effort to conceal the puddle of gold by putting her slender frame between you and the evidence. @@.playerSpeech;"Calm down,"@@ you tell her, your voice low and smooth. @@.playerSpeech;"You understand my language?"@@ @@.feragonSpeech;"Y-yes! Yes, I… I do."@@ She looks around, as if looking for a way to escape. You are blocking the main exit, so going around you is the only option. But she'd have to run past you... So, after a moment of contemplation, she finishes with a panicked: @@.feragonSpeech;"I didn't eat your gold!"@@ You simply raise an eyebrow. She still has traces of it clinging to her chin, and the remaining puddle isn't even close to being hidden. @@.playerSpeech;"Look, I'm not here for the gold. I don't care about it in the slightest-"@@ @@.feragonSpeech;"Oh, thank goodness, because, you know, that gold... I don't even know how it got here, or who put it there. Not that I'm complaining! I mean, whoever brought it is very generous, I'd hate for them to think that I was ungrateful..."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Really, I don't care about the gold, won't even comment on the nearly obscene noises you were making every time you swallowed it."@@ She blushes a little, before going back to trying to keep up her best poker face. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Tell her the absolute truth, you are not leaving here without her |TellFeragonTruth]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\ <<if $karma > $karmaToEvil>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Warn her about Vael's threat |WarnFeragon]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedKarma.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Warn her about Vael's threat |WarnFeragon]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedKarma.png"></div>@@\ <</if>>\ <<if $charismaSkill >0>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Perhaps you could convince her to come willingly with some lies… |LieFeragon]]<div class="numberStat">1</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedCharisma.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Perhaps you could convince her to come willingly with some lies… |LieFeragon]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCharisma.png"></div>@@\ <</if>>\@@.playerSpeech;"I have come here for you. The Forgemaster has asked me to retrieve you. You can either surrender yourself and come willingly, or I will take you by force. This is the only choice I am offering."@@ The young feragon is speechless. At least, she now knows the reason for your visit, now. No beating around the bush at all. @@.feragonSpeech;"L-look, if it's about something I have stolen, I can surely find a way to compensate-"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"No compensation to be made. Just you."@@ You take a step towards her, and she instinctively backs away. @@.feragonSpeech;"I... I am not coming with you, you... you madman. If… if you want to take me, then I will fight!"@@ Her tail ignites with green fire, and the cavern suddenly begins to glow a deep, emerald green. @@.playerSpeech;"So be it."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Get ready to fight |FightFeragon]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\@@.playerSpeech;"I'm just here to talk"@@ @@.feragonSpeech;"Talk?"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Yes, just talk."@@ You sit down on a nearby boulder, and make a gesture, inviting her to join you. She's not risking coming much closer, having heard a million times just how dangerous humans are, so a couple steps forth is all she takes, her posture defensive, and tail frozen in place. With her every step, her metallic scales clang softly against each other, the sound like tiny wind chimes. @@.playerSpeech;"...I was tasked with bringing you to my contractor, Vael. He likes to call himself The Forgemaster"@@ you begin, and you don't beat around the bush. @@.playerSpeech;"He intends on harvesting your scales, for they hold valuable properties. This can only mean a pretty painful existence, and a brutal death. He is not exactly a merciful man..."@@ You are not even sure he can be classified as a man anymore. She lets out a soft gasp, but you continue. @@.playerSpeech;"I will not turn you in. I'm just here to inform you, so that you have the time to prepare. I will be gone in no time, but The Forgemaster will send other people, eventually."@@ @@.feragonSpeech;"I... Don't even know what to make of this. I have never heard of this man."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"I know, but he has been keeping a close eye on both you and your mother. Isolated from other feragons as you are, it makes you both easy targets. Never wondered how your father disappeared, all those years ago?"@@ @@.feragonSpeech;"W-what?!"@@ She mumbles something in her own language, her mind going a ten hundred miles an hour. @@.feragonSpeech;"Y-you cannot... You can't possibly..."@@ But then, her eyes fall on your armor. It has been more than a decade, but still, her memory is sharp and clear. The plates you are wearing... The glint of the metal, the scent, the feel of it... It's awfully similar to the metal her father's scales were made of. She begins to hyperventilate, not able to even keep her gaze fixed on the grotesque visage in front of her. It's then that you realize what is going on. You quickly stand up, raising your hands, palms outward. @@.playerSpeech;"Wait, this is a misunderstanding! I am not-"@@ @@.feragonSpeech;"A-a misunderstanding?! You are wearing the remains of my father! H-how dare you?!"@@ "He forged this armor, but I swear on The Eye, I'm just here to-" @@.feragonSpeech;"E-enough! If you really wanted to talk, you wouldn't be wearing… Y-you..."@@ Her voice breaks. She's shaking like a leaf. Then, suddenly, her tail ignites in emerald fire, so hot that the whole cave seems to shimmer with heat. @@.feragonSpeech;"I will end you, and then I will end him."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Shit..."@@ <<set $wantedFightFeragon to false>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Get ready to fight |FightFeragon]]<div class="numberStat">3</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\ @@.playerSpeech;"I'm just here to talk"@@ @@.feragonSpeech;"Talk?"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Yes, talk. The Forgemaster happens to be in need of your services, and I, his... messenger have been tasked with finding you."@@ @@.feragonSpeech;"M-my services?"@@ She replies, quite clearly suspicious. @@.feragonSpeech;"Who is this Forgemaster you are speaking of? I have never met a man going by that title, how could he know me? And what kind of services could he possibly want from me?"@@ You sigh, as if it was something totally obvious. @@.playerSpeech;"Your species has a reputation of being pretty valuable companions for metalworkers. You just effortlessly melted a whole bunch of gold, and from what I know, your fire can burn much, much hotter than that."@@ She squishes her mandibles together, deep in thought and she looks at the remaining puddle. @@.playerSpeech;"...you would of course receive a handsome compensation for your help. A feragon such as yourself deserves only the best, and he has promised a good price in exchange for your time and effort. Food and shelter would, of course, be provided as well."@@ Even in someone as naive as her, the offer raises some alarm bells, but if indeed some kind of master of the forge is actively looking for her services… that is a huge compliment. @@.feragonSpeech;"I... have never worked with humans before. I would need to consult with my mother... if that is okay. I could give you a definitive answer in a couple of days at most."@@ It's not okay. Maybe she is gullible enough to buy your story, but her mother certainly will not be. Moreover, Vael mentioned she had contact with your order… surely she remembers you after you took her mate. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Try to use your mental powers to convince her |MindMagicFeragon]]<div class="numberStat">3</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedMagic.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["If you are not coming willingly, then I am taking you with me" |TakeFeragon]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["Time is a sensitive matter..." |TimeSensitiveFeragon]]</div>\ <<set $feragonAskedJob to true>><<if $magicSkill >=3>>\ You decide to try and use your mental powers. She seems a remarkably smart creature, capable of fluent speech in at least two languages, so it will not be an easy task. While you could try to make her believe her mother has already agreed with your deal, implanting such an idea in her mind would be next to impossible, too many modifications to actually make it work. The simplest approach to this particular problem turns out to dig into her desire to impress and make her mother proud, of being able to deliver the stable life she always wanted. And that... is not something <i>that</i> complicated. Without breaking the flow of the conversation, you focus on those particular areas, reinforcing the belief of how her mother has always wanted this, to see her daughter thrive. The Forgemaster, even if she is unable to put a face behind the title, you make her believe she has heard of him somewhere, a renowned master craftsman among humans. Makes sense she isn't entirely familiar with him…. <div class="passCheck">\ And she would be a fool to pass such an opportunity... <img src="Images/Icons/MindMagic.png"></div>\ @@.playerSpeech;"... I'm afraid time is a sensitive matter. While The Forgemaster would deeply appreciate your collaboration, he has other options at his disposal... thank you for your time, Miss"@@ You stand up and begin making your way back outside, and almost immediately she jumps into action. @@.feragonSpeech;"Wait! Wait!"@@ You turn around, raising an eyebrow, and she looks at you with a desperate look. @@.feragonSpeech;"I... I will go. I... can't let such an opportunity just pass me by, I know I would regret it. Could I speak with my mother after, maybe?"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"But of course. I'll take you to The Forgemaster so that you can discuss terms, and then you'll be able to go back and talk to your mother."@@ @@.feragonSpeech;"S-sure! Right now?"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Yes. He resides in a place called… the Citadel. Have you ever been there?"@@ @@.feragonSpeech;"I... think I haven't."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Well, then you will need to use this"@@ You present her the magical collar, and you see a flash of hesitation and disgust on her face, before she manages to get ahold of her emotions. @@.feragonSpeech;"Is... this really necessary?"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"It is"@@ You nod. @@.playerSpeech;"Just for the travel. We are moving to somewhere very far away, planeshifting is the only viable method, and the collar will help you do it without getting sick. It is a powerful magic relic, crafted by his very hands."@@ @@.feragonSpeech;"Alright then"@@ She mumbles, walking beside you and offering you her long, beautiful neck to be adorned with the collar. You snap the collar in place, and she shudders a bit, feeling a small tingle as her body suddenly ceases to respond to her. @@.feragonSpeech;"Don't worry, what you are experiencing right now is completely normal. The effects will wear off as soon as we reach our destination."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask Vael to recall you|RecallAfterFeragonMind][$reputation +=2]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTeleport.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ You decide to try and use your mental powers. She seems a remarkably smart creature, capable of fluent speech in at least two languages, so it will not be an easy task. You could try to make her believe her mother has already agreed or something along those lines… <div class="failCheck">\ You are quick to try and plunge into her mind, but almost as soon as you try to establish a connection you meet a wall, hard as steel. <img src="Images/Icons/FailMagic.png"></div>\ It makes you wince a little, and she takes a step back, feeling your intrusion, or your attempt at it. @@.feragonSpeech;"Agh, what... What did you just try to do?!"@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["If you are not coming willingly, then I am taking you with me" |TakeFeragon]]<div class="numberStat">3</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\ <</if>>\ @@.playerSpeech;"Look. If you are not coming willingly, then I'm just taking you with me"@@ You tell her as you unseathe your blade, tired of playing games with her. @@.feragonSpeech;"W-what?"@@ She is caught off guard by the sudden threat, and instinctively takes a step back. @@.feragonSpeech;"Wait, I thought we were negotiating, why the sword?!"@@ You take a step closer, and the silver feragon backs away. @@.feragonSpeech;"I-I told you I was thinking about it, you don't have to get aggressive! My mother is really protective, I can’t just disappear for who knows how long!"@@ When you don't relax, she hisses, and her tail flares up in green flames. @@.feragonSpeech;"F-fine, have it your way then! Mother was right, you are all crazy and unpredictable!"@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Get ready to fight |FightFeragon]]<div class="numberStat">3</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\@@.playerSpeech;"Time is a sensitive matter here. If you are to accept his offer, it has to be right now."@@ She blinks, confused. @@.feragonSpeech;"R-right now? That's... I have a lot to consider here...."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"If you can't give me a straight answer, I'll have to assume your refusal,"@@ You tell her, trying to pressure her into a decision. @@.feragonSpeech;"I... I mean... I can't... I would need to talk about this with my mother, it seems like quite a big decision... and... and it's a decision I cannot afford to make hastily. She is quite protective of me, if I just disappear for who knows how long…"@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["If you are not coming willingly, then I am taking you with me" |TakeFeragon2]]<div class="numberStat">3</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\@@.playerSpeech;"Look. I'm tired of playing games. The Forgemaster requires your services, and if you are not coming willingly, then I'm just taking you with me"@@ You tell her as you unseathe your blade. @@.feragonSpeech;"W-what?"@@ She is caught off guard by the sudden threat, and instinctively takes a step back. @@.feragonSpeech;"Wait, I thought we were negotiating, why the sword?!"@@ You take a step closer, and the silver drake backs away. @@.feragonSpeech;"I-I just told you I had to think about it, how can you expect me to make such a decision in a matter of seconds?! I don't know any of you!"@@ When you don't relax, she hisses, and her tail flares up in green flames. @@.feragonSpeech;"F-fine, have it your way then! Mother was right, you are all crazy and unpredictable!"@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Get ready to fight |FightFeragon]]<div class="numberStat">3</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\She doesn't wait for you to make the first move. On all fours, she rushes at you, jaws wide open and sharp claws leaving deep marks in the stone. Soon, the feragon becomes a blur of metal and emerald fire, and in the blink of an eye, she has crossed the distance between you. Right before she lunges at you, you have a split second to cast a powerful blast of crimson energy, and it turns out into a square hit on her back, but her powerful scales easily deflect the impact. The blast is not enough to even slow her down, in a split second, the ferocious creature is on top of you. <<if $hunterSkill >= 3>> You manage to sidestep <strong>just</strong> in time, and the burning feragon rushes past you. As she flies through the air, you land a quick blow with your blade, but exactly as with your previous attack, her scales are more than a match, and the impact is nothing but a tiny scratch. She is quick to turn around and lunge at you again, a pillar of short, hyperconcentrated flame emerging from her maw. The heat is immense, but this time you are ready to act. Blocking the brunt of the impact with your blade, which trembles and shrieks in protest, you shove the blade's edge into her open mouth, and let another burst of energy explode as she bites down on her blade. <div class="passCheck">\ The interior of her mouth is still quite resistant to damage, but the blast still manages to find some purchase, enough to make her yelp in pain and stumble backward as a puff of smoke and red sparks come out of her mouth. <img src="Images/Icons/General.png"></div>\ <div class="passCheck">\ The ferocious creature is quick to recover, but before she does, you press the advantage and jump into her, placing the cursed collar around her neck with a satisfying 'click'. <img src="Images/Icons/Item.png"></div>\ Then, a sudden wave of calm and silence washes over the cave. Her whole body relaxes, her tail extinguishes, and she collapses onto the ground. Panicking, she tries to reach for the collar with one of her clawed hands, but her body doesn't answer her commands anymore. She mumbles something you don't understand, and then her eyes meet yours. @@.feragonSpeech;"What is this thing? Why can't I move!"@@ <<if $wantedFightFeragon is false>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Talk to her, you dont mean her harm |TalkFeragon]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedKarma.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Take her back to Vael |ForgemasterRecallHaveFeragon1][$reputation +=1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTeleport.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Have some fun with her first |HaveFunFeragon]]</div>\ <</if>>\ <<else>>\ <div class="failCheck">\ You are not quick enough to react, and the next thing you feel is her powerful jaws on your chest. Her teeth don't find purchase, but her short-ranged beam of fire does… The unbearable heat of her green fire is like a blowtorch against your armor. It's only thanks to your chestplate that you manage to survive the attack, but even then, the blow is hard enough to send you flying against the wall, as part of your flesh sizzles and melts away. <img src="Images/Icons/FailGeneral.png"></div>\ Your vision becomes blurry and your grip on your sword loosens, but the final strike is delayed by some excruciating instants. Adrenaline still flooding her system, she just stands there, a puff of smoke emanating from her jaws, as she carefully considers what to do. She can't bring herself to kill you now that you are already helpless, and so her only real option is fleeing and leaving you lying there, barely conscious. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask Vael to recall you |ForgemasterRecall]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTeleport.png"></div>\ <</if>>\@@.playerSpeech;"Sh..it..."@@ You are lying on the ground, unable to get up. The more time it passes, the more it hurts. You must have a rib or two broken on top of the severe burns. Trembling, you reach into your pocket and retrieve the collar, feeding it a spark of magic to let The Forgemaster know you are ready to be recalled, and almost instantaneously, the world around you becomes a blur. ... His raspy, metallic voice is the first thing you hear as you materialize in the room. @@.vaelSpeech;"My, my, my... Now this is quite the disappointment."@@ He says, a note of amusement in his flat, mechanical tone. @@.vaelSpeech;"Ugh, and the stench of burned flesh... It makes my gears rust. Disgusting."@@ You clench your teeth, trying to summon your strength to speak. @@.vaelSpeech;"To think a feragon hatchling could prove a match for a High Hunter... Inconceivable. How utterly shameful, how far has this once great order fallen…"@@ You can hear his joints creak as he leans in closer. @@.vaelSpeech;"What happened, exactly? I think you at least owe me an explanation."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"T-take... me... to the infirmary..."@@ You tell him, resisting the urge to scream in frustration. It takes a moment for him to reply, giving you a few more seconds to wallow in the humiliation and agony of your defeat. @@.vaelSpeech;"Ah, but of course. That must be quite painful... your chest plate is still red-hot. The metal must feel like molten plastic on your fragile skin."@@ He retrieves the collar, making an effort not to come in contact with you, as if you were infected by some kind of incurable disease. Once he has secured his artifact, and with noticeable thriftiness, Vael calls a pair of lesser hunters to carry you over to Nyxa's quarters. @@.vaelSpeech;"It's my mistake, anyways. Next time, I will trust these matters to more… competent hunters."@@ <<set $feragonJobState to 4>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[To the infirmary |InfirmaryVael]]</div>\This job is... more delicate than your usual ones, for a change, you are tasked with delivering this one back alive. Still, you have this cute murder machine completely helpless in front of you, and you are in no real rush to bring her back to Vael... why not take a look a the goods before that? <img src="Images/Monsters/Feragon2.png" class="resizable-image" alt="FeragonFixed"> @@.playerSpeech;"Mhm, be good, now. The Forgemaster wouldn't want his prize damaged, after all"@@ More confident now that you know there is no chance for retaliation, you lean down beside her, and run a hand across her loin, enjoying her silvery, metallic scales and the bigger plates. Individually, they are sharp enough to cut flesh… but scruffed the right way, they are quite smooth. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Be at least moderately nice to her, see if you can make her cum |NiceFeragon]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCharisma.png"></div>\ <<if $karma > $karmaToEvil>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Why even pretend to be nice? Rape her here and now |RapeFeragon]]</div>\ <<else>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedCross.png">[[Why even pretend to be nice? Rape her here and now |RapeFeragon]]</div>@@\ <</if>>\ Emboldened by her cute reactions, you figure it can't hurt to explore her a bit further. @@.playerSpeech;"Since you seem to be enjoying my saliva that much, then why not have a bit more?"@@ You tell her, and before she can protest, you bring your lips against her muzzle and slip your tongue inside. Due to the shape of her triangular snout, it's not as much of a kiss as it is just another lick, but this time, you are letting her get a good, rich taste of your drool. @@.feragonSpeech;"S-stop it! Don't! I swear, I'll bite your face off the first chance I get, so stop licking me! E-even mother doesn't lick me! It's GROSS!"@@ She protests, but her words are quickly lost in a sloppy, wet mess of smooches. You can't take your tongue too deep, though. Despite being relatively pacified, her mouth is at very high temperatures, and her own tongue burns hot like a piece of glowing metal. You learn to avoid it after the first couple of tries, and focus on the roof of her mouth, which is just a tiny bit more bearable. @@.feragonSpeech;"So it was all a lie. You just wanted to... ugh... use me!"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Mhm?"@@ You sneer at her, flicking your tongue over her snout. @@.playerSpeech;"Oh no, I am still taking you there. I'm just curious how a feragon tastes like, you know?"@@ @@.feragonSpeech;"You are the worst. I don't go around tasting humans because I'm curious what their smooth skins could taste like. That is disgusting."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"And yet, you are getting a good taste of me."@@ @@.feragonSpeech;"I'm getting curious how human flesh would sizzle under my fire..."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Move over to her slit |HaveFunFeragon3]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Take her back to Vael |ForgemasterRecallHaveFeragon][$reputation +=1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTeleport.png"></div>\@@.playerSpeech;"Really? What a coincidence, now I want to taste out another part of your anatomy."@@ @@.feragonSpeech;"H-hey, no! You aren't touching my... that's not for your human mouth! It's..."@@ Her eyes drift away, embarrassed, but still, she is unable to move a muscle. @@.playerSpeech;"It's what, dear?"@@ @@.feragonSpeech;"I-its for... for mating purposes!"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"So, you want me to mate with you, then?"@@ @@.feragonSpeech;"N-no! Of course not, you perverted, evil human, I just want you to stay away from me!"@@ You give her a smirk, and a couple more smooches that threaten to burn your lips, and then, finally, you decide to move on. You place yourself in between her legs, and carefully spread them apart, revealing a fairly unassuming vertical slit, barely different from the rest of her scales. @@.feragonSpeech;"A-ah! W-wait, please... This is..."@@ You run a finger along her slit, and then press in, enjoying the contrast between her smooth vulva, and the thousands of little spiky scales all over her body. @@.feragonSpeech;"Ahn! G-get your claw out!"@@ Her voice comes out so high-pitched you can't help but find it adorable. You place one index on each side and pull it open, revealing a small opening of grey flesh, dripping with some kind of clear, slick fluid. @@.playerSpeech;"That is a lot of lubrication for a feragon that isn't interested in being bred, you know?"@@ @@.feragonSpeech;"T-that is a lot of stupid comments from a human who is going to be a pile of cinders the moment I am free!"@@ You chuckle at her comment and lower your head. You can't wait to have a taste of her. @@.feragonSpeech;"I'm serious! Don't you dare put your tongue theeAHhh"@@ You pay no mind to her words, and slide the tip of your tongue right in between her spread lips, coating it with her fluids. As with the rest of her body, they are warm, almost to the point of being too hot for you to pass, but they taste good enough for you to not care. Surprisingly thick and salty, and they carry out a taste of metal. You drive your tongue deep into her slit, one time and another, savoring her unique flavor, and getting closer to a burned tongue with each lick. If her mouth was hot, the interior of her pussy is almost scalding. Makes sense considering she can swallow molten metal as if it were nothing, but the fact is that her juices keep coming out more and more heated as you keep working on her. By this point she isn't saying anything, her silver face red as a furnace, and her breathing ragged and uneven. You hesitatingly let go, fearing that, upon reaching her climax, she may cause you real injury. Your tongue hurts already. @@.feragonSpeech;"A-are you happy now? H-have you gotten your fill, you depraved monster?"@@ She says, but in her voice, you can sense a note of... disappointment? <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Give her a mouthful of human spunk |FeragonCumMouth]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCum.png"></div>\ <<if $firePotion ==1>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Use the fire resistence potion to go all the way |FuckFeragon]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedItem.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedCross.png">[[Use the fire resistence potion to go all the way |FuckFeragon]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedItem.png"></div>@@\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Take her back to Vael |ForgemasterRecallHaveFeragon][$reputation +=1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTeleport.png"></div>\@@.playerSpeech;"Not quite. You've been having your fun, but I also want some release, you know?"@@ @@.feragonSpeech;"Wha- oh.... oh."@@ You stand up and remove your pants, giving her a clear view of your fully erect shaft. At least, she is relieved when you begin to jack off near her face, instead of trying to put your tool in more... delicate areas. @@.feragonSpeech;"J-just looking at your pink, ugly, irregular… <i>organ</i> is making me feel nauseous."@@ She complains, but she never stops looking. You just grin, and keep pumping. You don't need too much, since you have been getting worked up ever since you started playing with her, and you soon reach your own climax, which you carefully aim between her jaws. Rope after rope of your sticky, viscous semen flies directly into her maw, forming a puddle on the back of her mouth that gets bigger and bigger until she has no choice but to swallow, freeing up her windpipe, but passing all that vile, smelly goop right into her stomach. Your saliva was bad enough, but at least there wasn't much of it. Now your cum on the other hand... it manages to erase every last trace of the delicious gold she had eaten earlier. You place a hand on either side of her head, straightening it up and "helping" her swallow what she has left in her mouth. You scoop what little has managed to escape, making sure no traces are left behind, and then, give her a final, affectionate smooch, right on top of her snout. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Take her back to Vael |ForgemasterRecallHaveFeragon2][$reputation +=1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTeleport.png"></div>\ <<if $feragonAskedJob is false>> @@.playerSpeech;"You are tougher than you look, I'll give you that..."@@ @@.feragonSpeech;"Get off me! Get this thing off me!"@@ She hisses back at you. You feed the collar a tiny spark of magic, letting Vael know you are ready for the return, and he s fast to materialize the both of you in his chamber. The Forgemaster is looming over his work desk, tinkering with one of his toys, his attention mostly focused on it. It is as if he wasn't expecting anything from you, so when you return with the young feragon in tow, he turns around with a look of mild surprise. @@.vaelSpeech;"Well, isn't this a... pleasant surprise..."@@ He mumbles, his mechanical voice betraying a hint of amusement. He quickly makes his way towards you both, metallic insect-like legs scurrying across the floor. As he comes closer, you can see the feragon's eyes widen with terror. This is the man you have told her about, but she hadn't imagined him to be this hideous biomechanical abomination. @@.vaelSpeech;"And what a fine specimen... so young and fresh..."@@ One of his metal hands reaches for the feragon, who tries to pull back, but her body still does not respond. His metal fingers run all over her chin and neck, but his touch is not a gentle one. It is inquisitive and precise... as if he were looking for the right spot to make a cut. Without a warning, his sharp fingers dig into her back, making her squeal in pain as he plucks a single scale of considerable size. @@.vaelSpeech;"At first glance, the composition seems... adequate."@@ The young feragon is still a bit disoriented from her first planar shift, but her senses are sharp enough to recognize the very real danger she is in. Not being able to defend herself with her body, talking seems like the only reasonable option. @@.feragonSpeech;"H-hey, I... already told your human that I have done n-nothing wrong....I am not a criminal, I... agh"@@ She grunts as The Forgemaster presses his finger deeper into her back again, this time drawing a little sample of blood. @@.vaelSpeech;"Would you look at that? Such fluidity in her speech. Not sure it will do you much good..."@@ He sneers, a metallic rasp escaping from the depths of his metallic skull. @@.vaelSpeech;"But I like your voice, reptile. Those vocal cords will make for a great replacement."@@ @@.feragonSpeech;"W-what? N-no! I-I've done nothing, I swear!"@@ Vael ignores her now, focusing on the droplet of blood on his finger. His seven eyes examine it carefully, as if trying to decipher secrets unknowable. @@.feragonSpeech;"Yes, yes..."@@ He mumbles absent-mindedly. @@.feragonSpeech;"Good job, hunter. You may take your leave now... "@@ You nod, stand up, and dust yourself off. You've done the job, and you have no reason to stay here and ruin the good deed by bothering the easily irritable Forgemaster. As you are leaving, you can hear the feragon beg one last time. @@.feragonSpeech;"S-sir I... I don't want to die, I've done nothing wrong, please-"@@ Vael chuckles darkly at that. @@.vaelSpeech;"Oh, fret not. Every last one of your biological necessities will be taken care of... you will live a long life..."@@ <<set $feragonJobState to 3>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Take your payment and leave |LeaveFeragon][$money += $paymentVael]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\ <<set $VaelIntro to "afterFeragon">>\ <<else>>\ @@.playerSpeech;"You are tougher than you look, I'll give you that..."@@ @@.feragonSpeech;"Nghn, I just told you I needed time! Why are you doing this!"@@ You feed the collar a tiny spark of magic, letting Vael know you are ready for the return, and he s fast to materialize the both of you in his chamber. The Forgemaster is looming over his work desk, tinkering with one of his toys, his attention mostly focused on it. It is as if he wasn't expecting anything from you, so when you return with the young feragon in tow, he turns around with a look of mild surprise. @@.vaelSpeech;"Well, isn't this a... pleasant surprise..."@@ He mumbles, his mechanical voice betraying a hint of amusement. He quickly makes his way towards you both, metallic insect-like legs scurrying across the floor. As he comes closer, you can see the feragon's eyes widen with terror. This is the man you have told her about, but she hadn't imagined him to be this hideous biomechanical abomination. @@.vaelSpeech;"And what a fine specimen... so young and fresh..."@@ One of his metal hands reaches for the feragon, who tries to pull back, but her body still does not respond. His metal fingers run all over her chin and neck, but his touch is not a gentle one. It is inquisitive and precise... as if he were looking for the right spot to make a cut. Without a warning, his sharp fingers dig into her back, making her squeal in pain as he plucks a single scale of considerable size. @@.vaelSpeech;"At first glance, the composition seems... adequate."@@ The young feragon is still a bit disoriented from her first planar shift, but her senses are sharp enough to recognize the very real danger she is in. Not being able to defend herself with her body, talking seems like the only reasonable option. @@.feragonSpeech;"H-hey, I... already told your human... I just needed more time! I... am not opposed to the idea of working with you, sir... I just... agh!"@@ She grunts as The Forgemaster presses his finger deeper into her back again, this time drawing a little sample of blood. @@.vaelSpeech;"Would you look at that? Such fluidity in her speech. Not sure it will do you much good..."@@ He sneers, a metallic rasp escaping from the depths of his metallic skull. @@.vaelSpeech;"But I like your voice, reptile. Those vocal cords will make for a great replacement."@@ @@.feragonSpeech;"W-what? N-no! I-I've done nothing, I swear!"@@ Vael ignores her now, focusing on the droplet of blood on his finger. His seven eyes examine it carefully, as if trying to decipher secrets unknowable. @@.feragonSpeech;"Yes, yes..."@@ He mumbles absent-mindedly. @@.feragonSpeech;"Good job, hunter. You may take your leave now... "@@ You nod, stand up, and dust yourself off. You've done the job, and you have no reason to stay here and ruin the good deed by bothering the easily irritable Forgemaster. As you are leaving, you can hear the feragon beg one last time. @@.feragonSpeech;"S-sir I... I don't want to die, I've done nothing wrong, please-"@@ Vael chuckles darkly at that. @@.vaelSpeech;"Oh, fret not. Every last one of your biological necessities will be taken care of... you will live a long life..."@@ Her protests fade into whispers as you close the door behind you. You'd better stay ignorant about how exactly The Forgemaster goes about turning these creatures' harvestable parts into pieces of armor and equipment. <<set $feragonJobState to 3>>\ <<set $VaelIntro to "afterFeragon">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Take your payment and leave |LeaveFeragon][$money += $paymentVael]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\ <</if>>\ @@.playerSpeech;"Well, as much as I would like to have more fun with you, I would rather deliver you completely unscathed. Can't have that Forgemaster complain, can I?"@@ @@.feragonSpeech;"W-what?"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Oh yes, what I told you earlier wasn't a lie. I am supposed to bring you back to my contractor..."@@ You feed the collar a tiny spark of magic, letting Vael know you are ready for the return, and he s fast to materialize the both of you in his chamber. The Forgemaster is looming over his work desk, tinkering with one of his toys, his attention mostly focused on it. It is as if he wasn't expecting anything from you, so when you return with the young feragon in tow, he turns around with a look of mild surprise. @@.vaelSpeech;"Well, isn't this a... pleasant surprise..."@@ He mumbles, his mechanical voice betraying a hint of amusement. He quickly makes his way towards you both, metallic insect-like legs scurrying across the floor. As he comes closer, you can see the feragon's eyes widen with terror. This is the man you have told her about, but she hadn't imagined him to be this hideous biomechanical abomination. @@.vaelSpeech;"And what a fine specimen... so young and fresh..."@@ One of his metal hands reaches for the feragon, who tries to pull back, but her body still does not respond. His metal fingers run all over her chin and neck, but his touch is not a gentle one. It is inquisitive and precise... as if he were looking for the right spot to make a cut. Without a warning, his sharp fingers dig into her back, making her squeal in pain as he plucks a single scale of considerable size. @@.vaelSpeech;"At first glance, the composition seems... adequate."@@ The young feragon is still a bit disoriented from her first planar shift, but her senses are sharp enough to recognize the very real danger she is in. Not being able to defend herself with her body, talking seems like the only reasonable option. @@.feragonSpeech;"H-hey, I... already told your human that I have done n-nothing wrong....I am not a criminal, I... agh"@@ She grunts as The Forgemaster presses his finger deeper into her back again, this time drawing a little sample of blood. @@.vaelSpeech;"Would you look at that? Such fluidity in her speech. Not sure it will do you much good..."@@ He sneers, a metallic rasp escaping from the depths of his metallic skull. @@.vaelSpeech;"But I like your voice, reptile. Those vocal cords will make for a great replacement."@@ @@.feragonSpeech;"W-what? N-no! I-I've done nothing, I swear!"@@ Vael ignores her now, focusing on the droplet of blood on his finger. His seven eyes examine it carefully, as if trying to decipher secrets unknowable. @@.feragonSpeech;"Yes, yes..."@@ He mumbles absent-mindedly. @@.feragonSpeech;"Good job, hunter. You may take your leave now... "@@ You nod, stand up, and dust yourself off. You've done the job, and you have no reason to stay here and ruin the good deed by bothering the easily irritable Forgemaster. As you are leaving, you can hear the feragon beg one last time. @@.feragonSpeech;"S-sir I... I don't want to die, I've done nothing wrong, please-"@@ Vael chuckles darkly at that. @@.vaelSpeech;"Oh, fret not. Every last one of your biological necessities will be taken care of... you will live a long life..."@@ <<set $feragonJobState to 3>>\ <<set $VaelIntro to "afterFeragon">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Take your payment and leave |LeaveFeragon][$money += $paymentVael]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\@@.playerSpeech;"Well, that was great. Ready to go, my beautiful feragon?"@@ @@.feragonSpeech;"...go?"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Oh yes, what I told you earlier wasn't a lie. I am supposed to deliver you to my contractor..."@@ You feed the collar a tiny spark of magic, letting Vael know you are ready for the return, and he s fast to materialize the both of you in his chamber. The Forgemaster is looming over his work desk, tinkering with one of his toys, his attention mostly focused on it. It is as if he wasn't expecting anything from you, so when you return with the young feragon in tow, he turns around with a look of mild surprise. @@.vaelSpeech;"Well, isn't this a... pleasant surprise..."@@ He mumbles, his mechanical voice betraying a hint of amusement. He quickly makes his way towards you both, metallic insect-like legs scurrying across the floor. As he comes closer, you can see the feragon's eyes widen with terror. This is the man you have told her about, but she hadn't imagined him to be this hideous biomechanical abomination. @@.vaelSpeech;"And what a fine specimen... so young and fresh..."@@ One of his metal hands reaches for the feragon, who tries to pull back, but her body still does not respond. His metal fingers run all over her chin and neck, but his touch is not a gentle one. It is inquisitive and precise... as if he were looking for the right spot to make a cut. Without a warning, his sharp fingers dig into her back, making her squeal in pain as he plucks a single scale of considerable size. @@.vaelSpeech;"At first glance, the composition seems... adequate."@@ The young feragon is still a bit disoriented from her first planar shift, but her senses are sharp enough to recognize the very real danger she is in. Not being able to defend herself with her body, talking seems like the only reasonable option. @@.feragonSpeech;"H-hey, I... already told your human that I have done n-nothing wrong....I am not a criminal, I... agh"@@ She grunts as The Forgemaster presses his finger deeper into her back again, this time drawing a little sample of blood. @@.vaelSpeech;"Would you look at that? Such fluidity in her speech. Not sure it will do you much good..."@@ He sneers, a metallic rasp escaping from the depths of his metallic skull. @@.vaelSpeech;"But I like your voice, reptile. Those vocal cords will make for a great replacement."@@ @@.feragonSpeech;"W-what? N-no! I-I've done nothing, I swear!"@@ Vael ignores her now, focusing on the droplet of blood on his finger. His seven eyes examine it carefully, as if trying to decipher secrets unknowable. @@.vaelSpeech;"Hmh, would you look at this,"@@ he mumbles, turning to you. @@.vaelSpeech;"Her body reacted to the aetherial phase-shifting by producing a whole cocktail of hormones. Either that, or she was already in estrus."@@ You laugh a little at that, knowing exactly what he is talking about. @@.playerSpeech;"Oh, yes... well creatures react in all manner of unpredictable ways when planeshifting, right?"@@ @@.vaelSpeech;"Quite so. I have... plenty of stored genetic material from the previous specimen. This just means I can start right away. I know how bothersome it can be to catch these, after all. Until inbreeding becomes a real issue, of course."@@ He turns to face the poor creature, who looks downright mortified by his words. @@.feragonSpeech;"Good job, hunter. You may take your leave now... "@@ You nod, stand up, and dust yourself off. You've done the job, and you have no reason to stay here and ruin the good deed by bothering the easily irritable Forgemaster. As you are leaving, you can hear the feragon beg one last time. @@.feragonSpeech;"S-sir I... I don't want to die, I've done nothing wrong, please-"@@ Vael chuckles darkly at that. @@.vaelSpeech;"Oh, fret not. Every last one of your biological necessities will be taken care of... you will live a long life..."@@ <<set $feragonJobState to 3>>\ <<set $VaelIntro to "afterFeragon">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Take your payment and leave |LeaveFeragon][$money += $paymentVael]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\@@.playerSpeech;"Shit, I didn't come here to fight you, y'know?"@@ She lets out a long hiss as she tries her best to fight the effects of the collar, but to no avail. @@.playerSpeech;"If you can quit that and just listen... I will free you the moment I am done talking..."@@ She continues fighting the collar, weak spasms and growls being the only response you get… but she gets tired of it rather quickly, and finally gives up. She glares at you, her eyes wet with tears. @@.feragonSpeech;"Well... I am listening... I would like to know just why you are wearing the remains of my father"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"I didn't know how this thing was even made, trust me on that. I am....sorry it happened to your father."@@ @@.feragonSpeech;"Oh, sure you are! That's why you work for the monster that did it!"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"I have no choice but to work for these people. The Forgemaster personally chose me for this... Grim task. You can get mad at me, and I understand it, but when I come back empty-handed, someone else will come for you."@@ You sigh again and reach for her collar. @@.playerSpeech;"I know nothing I say will make you trust me, so just tell this to your mother and run away, hide. So that hopefully, you'll never see any of us again."@@ You remove the collar from her neck, and the fire returns to her tail. She doesn't attack you, however. Now that it has become clear that she doesn't really have a reason to be mad at you, no way to vent her frustrations, she just looks awfully fragile, as if she was made of porcelain instead of unbreakable scales. @@.feragonSpeech;"My dad... I... Loved him so much. Did he... Did he suffer?"@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Yes |YesFeragon]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[No |NoFeragon]]</div>\You figure she doesn't need more bad news right now. A comforting lie, perhaps. @@.playerSpeech;"His death was a quick one. He didn't suffer."@@ @@.feragonSpeech;"I see... well... "@@ She takes a deep breath, trying her best to quiet her shaking. @@.feragonSpeech;"I... am sorry for attacking you... I... It was stupid. I thank you for your kindness. Now... now I should go."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Sure thing. Take care of yourself, alright?"@@ She nods as she stands up, a bit unsteadily, and walks away slowly towards the exit. Hopefully, this will be the last time you ever see her. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask Vael to recall you|RecallAfterFeragonEscape][$reputation -=1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTeleport.png"></div>\ @@.playerSpeech;"I... Would like to give you a comforting lie, but... knowing The Forgemaster, I would assume his death was anything but merciful."@@ She takes a deep breath and holds back her tears, whatever words she is about to say are lost in the turmoil of her mind. @@.playerSpeech;"Even more reason for you to take my advice. What happened back then wasn't a separate instance, and I am not sure if there will be another chance for me to try and help you. He will not just kill you, he will subject you to unspeakable suffering."@@ @@.feragonSpeech;"I see... well... "@@ She takes a deep breath, trying her best to quiet her shaking. @@.feragonSpeech;"I... am sorry for attacking you... I... It was stupid. I thank you for your kindness. Now... now I should go."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Sure thing. Take care of yourself, alright?"@@ She nods as she stands up, a bit unsteadily, and walks away slowly towards the exit. Hopefully, this will be the last time you ever see her. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask Vael to recall you|RecallAfterFeragonEscape][$reputation -=1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTeleport.png"></div>\Nothing more to do in this plane, you suppose. You feed the collar a tiny spark of magic, letting Vael know you are ready for the return, and he doesn't hesitate for a second to materialize you in his chambers. You find him looming over his work desk, tinkering with one of his toys, his attention mostly focused on it. He wasn't expecting much, and you didn't fail his expectations. @@.vaelSpeech;"I am... deeply disappointed to see you return with nothing to show."@@ You nod silently. @@.vaelSpeech;"Even more disappointing perhaps to find you returning without a single new scar."@@ He adds, gesturing at your body with one of his four hands. @@.playerSpeech;"The feragon was too scared to fight back. Its metallic scales reflected my attacks, and it ran away before I could pin it down."@@ He hums and continues tinkering with the tool in his hands. @@.vaelSpeech;"And why didn't you follow it? Not a single day has gone by since I sent you"@@ You hesitate for a moment. @@.playerSpeech;"I am not a tracker, I wouldn't have caught up to it."@@ @@.vaelSpeech;"You are not a fighter, you are not a tracker... you shouldn't have inherited this fine role, hunter. Take your leave now, I have no interest in your excuses. I’ll just find someone more competent for this job."@@ You nod silently and walk out of his room. If anything, you are relieved the young feragon has been warned, and that Vael is too convinced of your incompetence to suspect any sort of treason... <<set $feragonJobState to 4>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Leave |Hunters Hall]]</div>\You feed the collar a spark of magic, letting The Forgemaster know you are ready to be recalled, and almost immediately, the process begins. @@.playerSpeech;"Oh, his name is Vael. The Forgemaster"@@ @@.feragonSpeech;"Vael..."@@ She tries the foreign word on her tongue before you are both transported away. ... The Forgemaster is looming over his work desk, tinkering with one of his toys, his attention mostly focused on it. It is as if he wasn't expecting anything from you, so when you return with the young feragon in tow, he turns around with a look of mild surprise. @@.vaelSpeech;"Well, isn't this a... pleasant surprise..."@@ He mumbles, his mechanical voice betraying a hint of amusement. He quickly makes his way towards you both, tiny insect-like legs scurrying across the floor. As he comes closer, you can see the feragon's eyes widen with terror. This surely is the man you have told her about, but she hasn't imagined him to be this hideous biomechanical abomination. Still, the young feragon swallows her fear and speaks, her voice surprisingly clear despite the fear, and strong nausea she is feeling after the planeshift. @@.feragonSpeech;"M-master Vael, is it?"@@ The Forgemaster looks directly at you before speaking, his cogs turning and clicking rapidly. @@.vaelSpeech;"How dare, share my name with this disgusting reptile"@@ He hisses, red eyes glowing with fury. His fist clenches, and you decide to speak up before he lashes out. @@.playerSpeech;"Sorry about that..."@@ It was an honest mistake. @@.playerSpeech;"I brought the creature here willingly. The feragon believes she is here to assist you, and so I didn’t think much of it and gave her your name"@@ Then, he laughs out loud, a terrible, ear-splitting sound. @@.vaelSpeech;"Oh, is that so?"@@ He cackles, arching his back until his face is inches away from the feragon’s. @@.vaelSpeech;"Did he tell you that nonsense?"@@ @@.feragonSpeech;"W-what?"@@ Fear and confusion are now mixed on the young dragon's pretty face. What's the meaning of all this? Vael laughs again, realizing that indeed, the feragon does believe she has come here to provide a service. @@.vaelSpeech;"Marvelous! I knew you weren't much of a fighter, $playerName, but this? Oh, this is just priceless"@@ His seven unblinking eyes turn back towards the feragon. @@.vaelSpeech;"Did he mention what service you would provide?"@@ @@.feragonSpeech;"Um... n-no... I... he mentioned... um... discuss terms w-with you d-directly a-a-and..." @@ The poor creature is beginning to hyperventilate, realizing that everything has been a ruse. What kind of delusion possessed her to take such a stupid decision? Only now she realizes just how utterly defenseless she is. No one knows where she is, not even herself. Why did she accept this madness? What got in her head exactly? @@.vaelSpeech;"Discuss terms?"@@ He laughs out loud again. As with the previous two times, it is horrible, fake laughter only produced to further startle the poor creature, but even then it is clear that the situation is quite hilarious to him. @@.vaelSpeech;"Fine. Let me tell you what your life will be about from now on, creature."@@ He leans even closer to her, the stench of old metal, decayed skin and muscular tissue invading her nostrils, and whispers with a tone of mockery. @@.vaelSpeech;"You will spend the rest of your days chained, and collared. Your scales will be harvested, and the flesh underneath will be laid bare. New scales will take their place, and the cycle shall continue, over and over."@@ The look on her face is one of absolute terror. Tears begin streaming down her cheeks as she struggles to even find words. @@.vaelSpeech;"Fret not. I have plenty more uses for you. Your horns, and your claws… even your vocal cords will make a fine replacement for mine. Rejoice, for your life <i>will</i> have a purpose..."@@ @@.feragonSpeech;"N-no!"@@ She tries to back away, but with the collar still around her neck, she can't move a muscle. @@.feragonSpeech;"Let me go! Let me go!"@@ @@.vaelSpeech;"Silence now, animal"@@ Vael hisses, and then turns towards you. @@.vaelSpeech;"A great job, hunter. Not only did you accomplish to bring this fine specimen to me completely unblemished, but you also made me laugh. You may take your leave now... I'll let Lauren know of your efforts, and I will reward you with a little tip."@@ You nod, stand up, and dust yourself off. You've done the job, and you have no reason to stay here and ruin the good deed by bothering the easily irritable Forgemaster. And as you close the door behind you, you can hear the feragon's heartbreaking wails, begging for her mother to save her. <<set $feragonJobState to 3>>\ <<set $VaelIntro to "afterFeragon">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Take your payment and leave (150)|Hunters Hall][$money += 150]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\<div class="passCheck">\ Work in progress… for now, let's say you get all patched up. <img src="Images/Icons/Heal.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |Hunters Hall]]</div>\Her protests fade into whispers as you close the door behind you. You'd better stay ignorant about how exactly The Forgemaster goes about turning these creatures' harvestable parts into pieces of armor and equipment. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue|Hunters Hall]]</div>\@@.title;THE FORGE@@ You don't feel like talking to him. He was pretty clear in his message, and after the crushing defeat you suffered in the duel, you are not really keen on showing yourself in front of him anytime soon. <<if $fromPortal is false>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |Hunters Hall]]</div>\ <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |PortalRoom]]</div>\ <</if>>\@@.title;THE FORGE@@ <<if $sixEightJobState == "">> Vael salutes you when you make your way downstairs, seemingly a bit less grumpy than usual. And his voice sounds... better. Less raspy. You suppose he ended up using the feragon's vocal cords for himself, after all... <<elseif $sixEightJobState != "" && $sixEightJobState != "completed">> @@.vaelSpeech;"The sample... did you bring the sample?"@@ <</if>> <img src="Images/Hunters/VaelFixed.png" class="resizable-image" alt="Vael Image"> <<if $feragonJobState != 1 && $sixEightJobState == "">> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["Do you have a job for me?" |AskJob]]</div>\<</if>>\ <<if $feragonJobState == 3>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask Vael to see the Feragon |AskSeeFeragon]]</div>\<</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["I would like to purchase some wares from you" |PurchaseWares]] <img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["I would like to sell you something" |SellWares]] <img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\ <<if $sixEightJobState == "dead">> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Tell Vael that the creature was dead |682Died]]</div>\<</if>>\ <<if $sixEightJobState == "escaped">> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Tell Vael that you had to flee |Flee682]]</div>\<</if>>\ <<if $sixEightJobState == "big" || $sixEightJobState == "medium" || $sixEightJobState == "small">> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Give Vael the sample |giveSample682]]</div>\<</if>>\ <<if $fromPortal is false>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |LeaveVael]]</div>\ <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |PortalRoom]]</div>\ <</if>>\@@.title;THE FORGE@@ You descend to Vael's lair, but he is not there. You suppose he is still busy with the feragon's "accommodations". <<if $fromPortal is false>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |Hunters Hall]]</div>\ <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |PortalRoom]]</div>\ <</if>>\Vael simply continues his never ending work, not giving you much notice. <<if $feragonJobState != 1>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["Do you have a job for me?" |AskJob]]</div>\<</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["I would like to purchase some wares from you" |PurchaseWares]] <img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\ <<if $feragonJobState is 1>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["Im ready to retrieve that feragon" |FeragonStart]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTeleport.png"></div>\<</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["I would like to sell you something" |SellWares]] <img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\ <<if $fromPortal is false>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |LeaveVael]]</div>\ <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |PortalRoom]]</div>\ <</if>>\You step into the round chamber, ready to do some business, when a voice calls out to you. @@.haiderSpeech;"Ah, $playerName!" Heider says, approaching you with a smile. You can tell he wants something by the tone, and his next words confirm that theory. "I've heard you are accepting jobs, and I have something for you if you'd like. I've been meaning to do it myself but there have been some... complications, you know?"@@ You simply raise an eyebrow. @@.haiderSpeech;"Hey, I'll pay you for it. Really, its not the reward Im after. Just help a friend out, will you?"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"What kind of job, Haider?"@@ @@.haiderSpeech;"A dragon. Wouldn't be the end of the world, really, but turns out that it's a she. After having tasted some gold not that long ago, the sudden change is so shocking, that she can't help but gag, feeling her stomach turn. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Force her to swallow every last drop |FeragonCumMouth3]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"Not so cocky now, are we?"@@ You hold her mouth closed, preventing her from spitting out the load, and keeping her jaws firmly shut with both hands, you watch her expression shift from disgust to anger, to finally, reluctant acceptance, as she realizes that there is no way out of this. The longer she keeps your load in her mouth, the longer she'll get to enjoy its awful taste. Slowly, and with an expression of revulsion, the feragon swallows, grimacing at the foul substance going down her throat, and feeling it pool in her stomach. @@.feragonSpeech;"I... hope you are satisfied."@@ She grumbles, as soon as you let go of her maw. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Clean her up and take her to Vael |TakeVaelAfterFuck]]</div>\You are jolted awake by the sound of a distorted, metallic voice, a most unpleasant surprise considering that you are supposed to be in your own house, away from any work-related thing. Before you, stands one of Vael's servants, and that makes you fear the worst has happened. Not because the figure is an intimidating sight to wake up to, you have seen and dealt with far worse things. <img src="Images/Hunters/VaelSentinel.png" class="resizable-image" alt="FeragonFixed"> No, what has you worried is how lazy Vael comes to moving his metallic ass out of his forge, and even if (of course), he hasn't come himself, just the fact he has sent one of his rusty constructs to call you is enough to send a cold shiver down your spine. The construct tells you, with its strange, raspy voice, to go back to the forge. Apparently, Vael wishes to talk to you. Immediately. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Get dressed and hurry back to the forge |BadEndVael2]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTeleport.png"></div>\With no other real option, you dress yourself and follow the golem, fearing the worst. The travel there is short, and you soon get your answers. Vael is standing in the middle of his workshop, and beside him, still collared and with a face of pure terror, is the feragon. Not even a day has gone by since you left her in Vael's clutches, and already, she looks completely broken. Her metallic body is covered in marks and incisions, and her eyes are swollen from crying. Vael looks up at you. @@.vaelSpeech;"There you are. I wanted to discuss this delicate matter with you first. I would like to hear your opinion on this."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"My opinion on what?"@@ You mumble, trying to play it safe for now. @@.vaelSpeech;"Upon arrival, I noticed a certain... irregularity. I thought it to be more than the shift, but upon closer inspection, I realized just why exactly this feragon was oozing hormones. Why exactly she reeked of burnt... Ejaculate."@@ You stay silent, and he continues. @@.vaelSpeech;"The genetic material in her was clearly human. Which led me to assume the two of you had a... certain encounter before arriving here. And sure enough, the creature admitted it herself."@@ Your mind races, thinking about a thousand different excuses, but Vael speaks first, as if he could read your thoughts. @@.vaelSpeech;"Do not bother. I just want to know what possessed you to have intercourse with this disgusting creature, before escalating it any further."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[You were curious |BadEndVaelCurious]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[You owe him no explanation |BadEndVaelExplanation]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[She is just a tool, an animal. You could do anything to her |BadEndVaelTool]]</div>\ @@.playerSpeech;"I... was just curious. It was my first time with a feragon, and I was wondering how the experience would be."@@ @@.vaelSpeech;"Curious you say?"@@ He repeats. He takes another look at the creature, who immediately tries to make herself as small as possible. He fails to understand, just what you could possibly find attractive in such a creature. He then looks at you, and he doesn't even look particularly mad, just... Disappointed. As if he was looking at a gravelly ill man. @@.vaelSpeech;"The inner workings of the mind are an enigma, so vexing I know I will never be able to unravel. But this... Degeneracy of yours could endanger the whole order, and so, cannot be tolerated. Lauren will cast her judgment on this matter."@@ <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Continue...">> <<goto "Ending3">> <</link>>\ </div>\@@.playerSpeech;"I don't owe you an explanation, Vael."@@ Vael's expression remains neutral. @@.vaelSpeech;"No. No you do not. Lauren will cast her judgment on this matter."@@ <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Continue...">> <<goto "Ending3">> <</link>>\ </div>\ @@.playerSpeech;"She is an animal, an object. A tool. I could do whatever I wanted with her."@@ Vael looks at you for a second, before turning his gaze towards the feragon. @@.vaelSpeech;"I do not disagree. However, the fact you could not resist the temptation makes you an unfit servant of the order. I understand the cravings for carnal pleasure, but... this? Makes me wonder. Just what kind of deep-rooted perversions could be hiding in your mind."@@ You try to respond, but before you can say a word, he cuts you off. @@.vaelSpeech;"I have heard enough. Lauren will decide your fate..."@@ <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Continue...">> <<goto "Ending3">> <</link>>\ </div>\@@.title;ENDING 3@@ @@.titleSmall;Exposed Monster-fucker@@ Lauren's judgement is surprisingly, not the death penalty. However, you are immediately demoted and put through a series of tests and examinations to ensure that you are still of some use. You will never run another mission on your own, which means that your days of being a high hunter, short as they have been, are already over. <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Play again">> <<goto "SetName">> <</link>>\ </div>\ <<set $ending3 to 1>>It only takes a couple more minutes for you to finish, pressing your cock tight against her cervix and injecting a thick load right into her womb. Almost all the semen you inject into her womb boils and evaporates instantly, but some few of them, empowered by the drive to survive, push deep into her, desperate to find an ovum in which to hide from the inferno outside. One manages to reach its target, and buries itself into the safe embrace of one of her eggs, ensuring the feragon will bear your young. <img src="Images/Icons/PregIcon.png"></div>\ <img src="Images/Other/PregImg.png" class="resizable-image" alt="PregImg"> <<else>>\ <div class="failCheck">\ Your cum splashes against her deepest parts, but every single sperm cell meets a rather unpleasant fate, the heat of her body quickly killing all of them. Quite the strong defense indeed... <img src="Images/Icons/FailPreg.png"></div>\ <</if>>\ She remains silent for a while, holding her tears as she comes to terms with what just happened, and you use the moment of respite to catch your breath. @@.feragonSpeech;"S-satisfied now? You monster..."@@ <<set $fuckedFeragon to true>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Clean her up and take her to Vael |TakeVaelAfterFuck]]</div>\Surely a wise purchase. Your armor is pretty much immune to normal flames, but your dick is not... and you'd rather not have to suffer third-degree burns because you were stupid enough to stick it somewhere hot without the proper protection. <<set $firePotion to 1>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |PurchaseWares]]</div>\<<if $elixirExertion == 0>> @@.playerSpeech;"I was looking for a vial of that famous elixir of yours. Could come in handy."@@ Vael lets out a small chuckle, as if he had just heard the punchline to a joke. "Oh, of course, you could use some of it. You are in desperate need of some." Vael gives you a small vial of the elixir, full of bright green liquid that fizzles and pops. @@.vaelSpeech;"It's no substitute for actual skill, but... It should give you a nice boost. For a while."@@ <<if $money >= 10>><div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Buy the item (10) |BuyExertion][$money -=10]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\<<else>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedCross.png">[[Buy the item (10) |BuyExertion][$money -=10]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>@@\<</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |PurchaseWares]]</div>\ <<else>>\ @@.playerSpeech;"I was looking for another vial of that Elixir of Exertion."@@ This time, surprisingly perhaps, Vael doesn't chuckle. His lenses narrow, and his expression grows more serious. @@.vaelSpeech;"This substance is useful, but highly toxic. I'm not sure you'd want to use it on a regular basis."@@ He pauses for a second, then shakes his head, @@.vaelSpeech;"Regardless of my perception of you, I will not endanger your life with another dose."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |PurchaseWares]]</div>\ <</if>>\ You pay him and take the small bottle, stowing it away in your bag. <<set $elixirExertion to 1>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |PurchaseWares]]</div>\A vial filled with some ungodly concoction. Despite its many secondary effects, this powerful drug will give you a powerful boost to your attributes, until the next time you sleep. [[Go back |Inventory]]You take out the small vial, and take off the small cork plugging its opening. The substance inside has a powerful scent, of chemicals and herbs that are not entirely pleasant. @@.playerSpeech;"Oh well. This is no time for a moral dilemma,"@@ you think out loud. @@.playerSpeech;"This stuff may be dangerous, but if I die to these beasts, then I'll die anyway."@@ You gulp down the elixir, feeling it burn down your throat. You feel like you're about to pass out. Your muscles are burning, and you can barely stand up... but then, as the nauseating sensation passes, a wave of strength washes over you. It feels like your veins are filled with fire, and your muscles with steel. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |PortalRoom]]<img
