End</div> <</if>> </div> <br> <div class = "close-Inventory"> <<link "Go Back">> <div class="stat-container"> Go back </div> <<goto Inventory>> <</link>> </div> <</nobr>>@@.title;RUNETERRA; THE GREAT SAI@@ <<set $naafiriJobState to 2>>\ <<set $playerEnergy to "tired">>\ <<set $naafiriStone to false>>\ <<set $temporaryImpregnations to $impregnations >>\ <<set $temporaryKills to $playerKills >>\ <<set $survivedTenSeconds to false>>\ <<set $runNaafiri to false>>\ After a grueling instant traveling through the void, you arrive at a large, sand-covered, sunlit plain. The heat of the Shuriman desert hits you like a hammer, but even worse is the sudden brightness... used to the darkness of your home and the void, the burning sun is far too much for your sensitive eyes. Your vision takes a few moments to recover from the blinding light, but when it does, you realize you are surrounded by nothing but a vast expanse of sand. Nothing but endless desert. You cannot feel the mark of your prey, but there is a strong magical presence nearby, like a pulsing heart beating in the air.... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Walk in a random direcion |WalkRandomNaafiri]]</div>\ <<if $eyeSeeing >=1>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Focus on the eerie presence, and make your way to your prey |FocusNaafiri]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedEye.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedCross.png">[[Focus on the eerie presence, and make your way to your prey |FocusNaafiri]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedEye.png"></div>@@\ <</if>>\Not the brightest idea, but what else can you do? Without a clear destination in mind, all you can do is walk in a straight line and hope that somehow you will come across your prey. And so you walk, the sun heats your plate more and more, making you sweat beneath it, and sand gets into your boots to the point where just walking is making your feet ache... But you don't have to bask in the miseries of the desert for long; before the hour is over, barking can be heard from afar, triumphant and hungry, like a pack of wolves closing in on a deer. You turn around, and there, in the distance, three figures are trotting towards you, panting and drooling, snapping their jaws excitedly. They look like regular hounds, but with some grisly details that set them apart from their non-supernatural counterpart. They are at least twice the size of a normal dog, and they seem to have disproportionally larger jaws than the rest of their body, full of long, sharp teeth. A long spike juts out from their forehead, each of them pointing straight at you. @@.playerSpeech;"Shit..."@@ You take out your sword and get ready for the inevitable fight. They don't seem particularly strong, but three at the same time... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Get ready to repel them |RepelFirstWaveNaafiri]]<div class="numberStat">1</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\You take a defensive stance, your back straight and your legs steady, and hold the hilt of your blade with both hands, its tip pointing towards the beasts. The pack closes in, and they immediately set out to surround you, as if they had a single mind, and from there, a quick, savage attack. <<if $hunterSkill >=1>>\ You duck and dodge, avoiding the vicious bites and retaliating with quick strikes, demonic blade sinking in their hides and drawing blood. <div class="passCheck">\ The creatures are not particularly skilled, and your blade is a wall they have trouble breaking through... One by one, they fall dead at your feet, and you stand victorious over thir corpses. <img src="Images/Icons/General.png"></div>\ You barely have time to bask in the glory of your victory; more are coming. Down the dunes, you see another four rushing towards you, just as big, just as hungry, and just as eager for the kill. Behind them, three more, and behind them, more still. The desert ignites with the barking and yelping of the beasts, a chorus of hunger and anger. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Get ready for a long fight... |RepelSecondWave]]<div class="numberStat">3</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ You duck and dodge, avoiding the vicious bites of the hounds, but soon, the fight turns against you. While they are not too formidable on their own, the three hounds are smart, and they coordinate their attacks to make sure at least one of them always has your back, and it doesn't take long before the dogs start to break through your guard. <div class="failCheck">\ Their jaws latch onto your arms, legs, and torso, and eventually, one of them manages to jump on you and bring you down, its enormous jaws crushing your neck. <img src="Images/Icons/FailGeneral.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Death embraces you...">> <<goto "Death">> <</link>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png" >\ </div>\ <</if>>\There's no way you can repel this army of bloodthirsty beasts other than with the same amount of savagery. <<if $hunterSkill >=3>>\ The beasts close in, and you meet them halfway, sword first, cutting into the flesh and tearing into the skin, bursts of ultra-concentrated lightning striking them and sending them flying backwards. Corpse after corpse falls, but they seem endless. For each beast you slay, two more arrive, and soon enough you're surrounded by a sea of teeth and steel. <div class="passCheck">\ However, it comes a point where they simply... cease to attack. They stand there, circling you, and watch as you stand there, bloody and bruised, waiting. <img src="Images/Icons/General.png"></div>\ @@.playerSpeech;"What the fuck are they doing...?"@@ You wonder, confused. They are certainly not going to retreat, no matter how much blood you spill, they are fearless, fully focused on their prey, and yet, they do nothing but circle you, salivating. And then, a loud growl echoes throughout the desert, a thunderous, mindsplitting roar that shakes the very ground itself. A hound, noticeably bigger than the rest, comes from the dunes. Even on all fours, she stands nearly as tall as you, and her jaw is full of fangs the size of knives. Its not the only knife-resembling thing about her, in fact, her whole body seems to be covered in the sharpest blades imaginable, a mane of steel needles. <img src="Images/Monsters/NaafiriFixed.png" class="resizable-image" alt="NaafiriImage"> @@.naafiriSpeech;"Cease. Stand down, my children."@@ The hound snarls, and the smaller hounds immediately move away, giving her a wide berth. She steps closer to you, and you instinctively brandish your sword, trying to hide your shaking hand. What kind of death sentence did you accept by taking this contract? This creature is no ordinary hound, and you can feel her raw, unbridled power, even before she starts to speak. @@.naafiriSpeech;"A human trespasses into our hunting grounds, one that doesn't even bother to hide, stench and arrogance emanating from its skin... Foolish, no doubt. And yet, you manage to spill my blood, to fell my children. An impressive display."@@ You say nothing, watching her intently. She circles you, her bright yellow eyes fixed on your sword, then, she looks at your armor, her gaze lingering on your chestplate, on the symbol of your order. @@.naafiriSpeech;"You are far from your nest, little bird. Your flock must miss you dearly, so far away from your kin. Why come here, alone and weak, so far from your protection?"@@ The creature may be fearsome, but beneath her many <i>many</i> spikes, there is a beautiful predator that you absolutely wouldn't mind spending some intimate time with... perhaps she'd be willing as well <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Propose to her; You came here to mate with her |MateNafiriPropose]]</div>\ <<if $darkDevotion <= 0>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[You have come here to end her |EndNaafiri]]</div>\ <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[You have come here to end her, in the name of the dark lady |EndNaafiriDarkLady]]<div class="numberStat">5</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedDevotion.png"></div>\ <</if>>\ <<else>>\ You duck and dodge, avoiding the vicious bites of the hounds, but soon, the fight turns against you. While they are not too formidable on their own, the relentless fight turns into an attrition war, and, you soon begin to feel more and more tired. <div class="failCheck">\ Their jaws latch onto your arms, legs, and torso, and eventually, one of them manages to jump on you and bring you down, its enormous jaws crushing your neck. <img src="Images/Icons/FailGeneral.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Death embraces you...">> <<goto "Death">> <</link>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png" >\ </div>\ <</if>>\You sheathe your blade, trying to appear as confident as possible. @@.playerSpeech;"I trudged through the desert to mate with you, my lady."@@ Naafiri raises an eyebrow. @@.naafiriSpeech;"Oh?"@@ She asks, amused. @@.naafiriSpeech;"Mate with me? My my... Such confidence. Perhaps the arrogance was deserved, then. A lowly human, wishing to rut with a divine being..."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"A beautiful, majestic divine being, yes."@@ She smiles, baring her sharp, deadly fangs. Her eyes fixate on your crotch, then on the rest of your body, and her smile grows. @@.naafiriSpeech;"Many would find this form repulsive, and yet..."@@ She steps closer, and you can't help but feel a shiver run down your spine. In one second, she could crush your whole skull in her powerful jaws. @@.naafiriSpeech;"You slaughtered many of my children, human. I will offer you a deal, then. Prove your worth, fight against me. If you can survive my onslaught for five seconds, you shall have my body. Show me you can give me a strong litter."@@ You take a deep breath, and ponder your answer... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Oh, you are so making her your breeding bitch… make it ten seconds. |TenSecondsNaafiri]]<div class="numberStat">7</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"><img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Accept the deal |Accept the deal]]<div class="numberStat">5</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["What happens if I say no?" |AskNoNaafiri]]</div>\You smile back at her. @@.playerSpeech;"Five seconds? Make it ten. If I win, I'm fucking you until you are fat with my pups."@@ Her lips curl, her mouth opens, and an amused laugh escapes her. @@.naafiriSpeech;"If."@@ With no further warning, Naafiri charges, claws digging into the sand, and leaps forward, jaws wide open, ready to rip you apart. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Try to resist the onslaught |ResistOnslaughtTen]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\ @@.playerSpeech;"Deal. Five seconds."@@ She chuckles. @@.naafiriSpeech;"Very well, mortal. Prove that you are truly worthy."@@ With no further warning, Naafiri charges forward, claws digging into the sand, and leaps forward, jaws wide open, ready to rip you apart. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Try to resist the onslaught |ResistOnslaught]]<div class="numberStat">5</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"><div class="numberStat">3</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedHeal.png"></div>\You manage to dodge the initial charge, albeit barely. Even then, she is quick to turn around and lunge back at you, daggers flashing, teeth glinting, claws ready to tear flesh. She is fast, agile, and relentless. Even with your extensive training, you can barely keep up with her attacks, she is only one but they seem to come from everywhere all at once. <<if $hunterSkill <5>>\ <<if $hunterSkill >=3 && $healSkill >=3>>\ Despite all that, the fight goes on. Sure thing, she is holding back, not using the full extent of her magic and her strength, but at the same time, not pulling any punches either. She is truly testing you, and the five seconds that the combat lasts seem endless. You are not entirely able to fend her off, and occasionally, a dagger meets your flesh, or her teeth grace your soft skin. Your sword may not be able to move as fast as she does, but you know you just have to outlast her for five seconds, and your curative powers are enough to heal most of your wounds as you fight. <div class="passCheck">\ As promised, when the count reaches five, Naafiri steps back, not even winded. Contrary to her, you are panting and bleeding slightly from a few cuts, but your healing magic is enough to patch the more severe ones. You made it, you managed to survive against a Darkin... quite an impressive feat. <img src="Images/Icons/General.png"><img src="Images/Icons/Heal.png"></div>\ You sheathe your sword, a confident smile on your face despite the fading pain on your whole body. You've won, after all. @@.naafiriSpeech;"Not a bad show of strength for a human. Your claim is earned, little bird. Now, submit to me, and take your prize~"@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[You are not submitting to her, she is submitting to you |DomNaafiri]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Submit to her, have her take the lead |SubNaafiri]]</div>\ <<else>>\ Even if you just have to focus on defending yourself, never opening your guard to strike, it is not enough. Naafiri is relentless, and she does not give you the chance to breathe or plan. <div class="failCheck">\ With a final, savage attack, she breaks through your guard, and with one swift, brutal swipe, she slashes your throat, your blood gushing out and splashing on the sand. The world starts spinning, and your legs wobble. You still reek of dog."@@ You roll your eyes and turn to leave, making a mental note not to ever accept a contract will be easy just because Damlon says so. <<else>>\ You decide to not make any comments, you barely even engaged the hounds in the first place, so you just take your reward and leave. Hopefully, Naafiri will not be marked again. Would be bad having to explain just why you took the gold without dealing with her. <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |DamlonIntro][$continueDamlon= true]]</div>\ <<set $reputation to $temporaryReputation +1>> <<set $naafiriJobState to 3>> <<set $activeDamlonContract to "">>\<<nobr>> <<if $currentContract != "">> <div class="item-panel"> You are currently taking care of a contract. </div>\<br> <<else>> <div class="item-panel"> [[Azri Elis |AzriElisContractInventory]] </div><br> <div class="item-panel"> [[Deer Girl |DeerGirlContractInventory]] </div><br> <div class="item-panel"> [[Wolyrn |WolyrnContractInventory]] </div><br> <div class="item-panel"> [[Feragon |FeragonContract]] </div><br> <div class="item-panel"> [[Wyvern |WyvernContract]] </div><br> <div class="item-panel"> [[Yuumi |YuumiContract]] </div><br> <div class="item-panel"> [[Light Fury |LightFuryContract]] </div><br> <div class="item-panel"> [[Wolf Girl|WolfGirlContract]] </div><br> <div class="item-panel"> [[Naafiri |NaafiriContract]] </div><br> <div class="item-panel"> [[Deathclaw |DeathclawContract]] </div><br> <div class="item-panel"> [[SCP-682 |682Contract]] </div><br> <div class="item-panel"> [[Oblivion |DeathContract]] </div><br> <</if>> <br> <div class = "close-Inventory"> <<link "Go Back">> <div class="stat-container"> Go back </div>\ <<goto Inventory>> <</link>> </div>\ <</nobr>><<nobr>> <div class="header-container"> <div class="middle-section">AZRI'ELIS</div> </div> <div class="long-container"> <div class="left-long-container-2"> <img src="Images/Monsters/AzriFixed.png" alt="IMG"> </div> <div class="right-long-container-2"> @@.titleSmall;STATUS@@ <br><br> Lauren herself asked you to go and take care of Azri'Elis. You have the travelstone and can warp to the world she is currently trapped in. </div> </div> <div class = "close-Inventory"> <<link "Go Back">> <div class="stat-container"> Go back </div> <<goto Contracts>> <</link>> </div> <</nobr>><<nobr>> <div class="header-container"> <div class="middle-section">Meyla</div> </div> <div class="long-container"> <div class="left-long-container-2"> <<if $deerJobState != 0>> <img src="Images/Monsters/DeerFixed.png" alt="IMG"> <<else>> <img src="Images/Monsters/DeerDark.png" alt="IMG"> <</if>> </div> <div class="right-long-container-2"> @@.titleSmall;STATUS@@ <br><br> <<switch $deerJobState >> <<case 0>> <<if $eyeSeeing >=2>> <div class="passCheck"> You can get this contract by inspecting the workshop, in your house. Then you can warp to her world by going to the portal room and using the travelstone. <img src="Images/Icons/Eye.png"></div> <<else>> <div class="failCheck"> You have not yet found this creature, or anything related to her. <img src="Images/Icons/FailEye.png"></div> <</if>> <<case 1>> You have found this contract, and you have the travelstone to make the trip. You may warp to her world from the portal room in your home. <<case 2>> <<if $killedDeer is false>> You have been near her enough, the mission has been counted as a success, despite her still being alive. You can claim your reward for this contract from Damlon, and as long as no one looks into it, you should be fine. <<else>> You have killed this creature, and you can claim the reward from Damlon. <<case 3>> You have already claimed the reward for this creature. <<case 4>> You have not even gotten near her, and the mission has not been considered a success. Won't probably matter, since this contract has been long forgotten, but you won't be able to claim the reward. <<default>> <</switch>> </div> </div> <div class = "close-Inventory"> <<link "Go Back">> <div class="stat-container"> Go back </div> <<goto Contracts>> <</link>> </div> <</nobr>><<nobr>> <div class="header-container"> <div class="middle-section">Nelia</div> </div> <div class="long-container"> <div class="left-long-container-2"> <<if $wolyrnJobState != 0>> <img src="Images/Monsters/WolyrnFixed.png" alt="IMG"> <<else>> <img src="Images/Monsters/WolyrnDark.png" alt="IMG"> <</if>> </div> <div class="right-long-container-2"> @@.titleSmall;STATUS@@ <br><br> <<switch $wolyrnJobState >> <<case 0>> <<if $eyeSeeing >=2>> <div class="passCheck"> You can get this contract by talking to Damlon, and then warping from your home. <img src="Images/Icons/Eye.png"></div> <<else>> <div class="failCheck"> You have not yet found this creature, or anything related to her. <img src="Images/Icons/FailEye.png"></div> <</if>> <<case 1>> You have accepted this contract, and you have the travelstone to make the trip. You may warp to her world from the portal room in your home. <<case 2>> <<if $killedWolyrn is false>> You have been near her enough, the mission has been counted as a success, even if she survived your encounter. You can claim your reward for this contract from Damlon, and as long as no one looks into it, you should be fine. <<else>> You have killed this creature, and you can claim the reward from Damlon. <</if>> <<case 3>> You have already claimed the reward for this creature. <<case 4>> This should be impossible, what kind of disgusting spagetti code brought us here... <<default>> <</switch>> </div> </div> <div class = "close-Inventory"> <<link "Go Back">> <div class="stat-container"> Go back </div> <<goto Contracts>> <</link>> </div> <</nobr>><<nobr>> <div class="header-container"> <div class="middle-section">Yuumi</div> </div> <div class="long-container"> <div class="left-long-container-2"> <<if $foundYuumi is true >> <img src="Images/Monsters/YuumiFixed.png" alt="IMG"> <<else>> <img src="Images/Monsters/YuumiDark.png" alt="IMG"> <</if>> </div> <div class="right-long-container-2"> @@.titleSmall;STATUS@@ <br><br> <<if $foundYuumi is false>> <<if $eyeSeeing >=2>> <div class="passCheck"> You can randomly encounter Yuumi during your sleep. <img src="Images/Icons/Eye.png"></div> <<else>> <div class="failCheck"> You have not yet found this creature, or anything related to her. <img src="Images/Icons/FailEye.png"></div> <</if>> <<else>> The eye has not marked this creature, so you won't get a reward for dealing with her. <</if>> </div> </div> <div class = "close-Inventory"> <<link "Go Back">> <div class="stat-container"> Go back </div> <<goto Contracts>> <</link>> </div> <</nobr>><<nobr>> <div class="header-container"> <div class="middle-section">Naafiri</div> </div> <div class="long-container"> <div class="left-long-container-2"> <<if $naafiriJobState != 0>> <img src="Images/Monsters/NaafiriFixed.png" alt="IMG"> <<else>> <img src="Images/Monsters/NaafiriDark.png" alt="IMG"> <</if>> </div> <div class="right-long-container-2"> @@.titleSmall;STATUS@@ <br><br> <<switch $naafiriJobState >> <<case 0>> <<if $eyeSeeing >=2>> <div class="passCheck"> You can get this contract by talking to Damlon, and then warping from your home. <img src="Images/Icons/Eye.png"></div> <<else>> <div class="failCheck"> You have not yet found this creature, or anything related to her. <img src="Images/Icons/FailEye.png"></div> <</if>> <<case 1>> You have accepted this contract, and you have the travelstone to make the trip. You may warp to her world from the portal room in your home. <<case 2>> <<if $killedNaafiri is false>> You have been near her enough, the mission has been counted as a success, even if she survived your encounter. You can claim your reward for this contract from Damlon, and as long as no one looks into it, you should be fine. <<else>> You have killed this creature, and you can claim the reward from Damlon. <</if>> <<case 3>> You have already claimed the reward for this creature. <<case 4>> This should be impossible, what kind of disgusting spagetti code brought us here... <<default>> <</switch>> </div> </div> <div class = "close-Inventory"> <<link "Go Back">> <div class="stat-container"> Go back </div> <<goto Contracts>> <</link>> </div> <</nobr>><<nobr>> <div class="header-container"> <div class="middle-section">Light Fury</div> </div> <div class="long-container"> <div class="left-long-container-2"> <<if $furyJobState != 0>> <img src="Images/Monsters/FuryFixed.png" alt="IMG"> <<else>> <img src="Images/Monsters/FuryDark.png" alt="IMG"> <</if>> </div> <div class="right-long-container-2"> @@.titleSmall;STATUS@@ <br><br> <<switch $furyJobState >> <<case 0>> <<if $eyeSeeing >=2>> <div class="passCheck"> You can get this contract by coming into the Hunter's hall with at least 1 point in reputation. <img src="Images/Icons/Eye.png"></div> <<else>> <div class="failCheck"> You have not yet found this creature, or anything related to her. <img src="Images/Icons/FailEye.png"></div> <</if>> <<case 1>> You have accepted this contract, and Heider paid for it upfront. <<case 2>> You have accepted this contract, and Heider paid for it upfront. <<case 3>> You have accepted this contract, and Heider paid for it upfront. <<case 4>> This should be impossible, what kind of disgusting spagetti code brought us here... <<default>> <</switch>> </div> </div> <div class = "close-Inventory"> <<link "Go Back">> <div class="stat-container"> Go back </div> <<goto Contracts>> <</link>> </div> <br> <div class = "credits">This little encounter was commissioned by @@.highlighted;Dargon@@, a most generous and patient patron. Thank you for supporting my work!</div> <</nobr>> <<nobr>> <div class="header-container"> <div class="middle-section">Lyra</div> </div> <div class="long-container"> <div class="left-long-container-2"> <<if $wolfJobState != 0>> <img src="Images/Monsters/WolfFixed.png" alt="IMG"> <<else>> <img src="Images/Monsters/WolfDark.png" alt="IMG"> <</if>> </div> <div class="right-long-container-2"> @@.titleSmall;STATUS@@ <br><br> <<switch $wolfJobState >> <<case 0>> <<if $eyeSeeing >=2>> <div class="passCheck"> You can get this contract by talking to Damlon, and then warping from your home. <img src="Images/Icons/Eye.png"></div> <<else>> <div class="failCheck"> You have not yet found this creature, or anything related to her. <img src="Images/Icons/FailEye.png"></div> <</if>> <<case 1>> You have accepted this contract, and you have the travelstone to make the trip. You may warp to her world from the portal room in your home. <<case 2>> <<if $killedWolf is false>> You have been near her enough, the mission has been counted as a success, even if she survived your encounter. You can claim your reward for this contract from Damlon, and as long as no one looks into it, you should be fine. <<else>> You have killed this creature, and you can claim the reward from Damlon. <</if>> <<case 3>> You have already claimed the reward for this creature. <<case 4>> This should be impossible, what kind of disgusting spagetti code brought us here... <<default>> <</switch>> </div> </div> <div class = "close-Inventory"> <<link "Go Back">> <div class="stat-container"> Go back </div> <<goto Contracts>> <</link>> </div> <</nobr>><<nobr>> <div class="header-container"> <div class="middle-section">Sxath</div> </div> <div class="long-container"> <div class="left-long-container-2"> <<if $wyvernJobState != 0>> <img src="Images/Monsters/WyvernFixed.png" alt="IMG"> <<else>> <img src="Images/Monsters/WyvernDark.png" alt="IMG"> <</if>> </div> <div class="right-long-container-2"> @@.titleSmall;STATUS@@ <br><br> <<switch $wyvernJobState >> <<case 0>> <<if $eyeSeeing >=2>> <div class="passCheck"> You can get this contract by talking to Damlon, and then warping from your home. <img src="Images/Icons/Eye.png"></div> <<else>> <div class="failCheck"> You have not yet found this creature, or anything related to her. <img src="Images/Icons/FailEye.png"></div> <</if>> <<case 1>> You have accepted this contract, and you have the travelstone to make the trip. You may warp to her world from the portal room in your home. <<case 2>> <<if $killedWyvern is false>> You have been near her enough, the mission has been counted as a success, even if she survived your encounter. You can claim your reward for this contract from Damlon, and as long as no one looks into it, you should be fine. <<else>> You have killed this creature, and you can claim the reward from Damlon. <</if>> <<case 3>> You have already claimed the reward for this creature. <<case 4>> This should be impossible, what kind of disgusting spagetti code brought us here... <<default>> <</switch>> </div> </div> <div class = "close-Inventory"> <<link "Go Back">> <div class="stat-container"> Go back </div> <<goto Contracts>> <</link>> </div> <</nobr>><<nobr>> <div class="header-container"> <div class="middle-section">Kirla</div> </div> <div class="long-container"> <div class="left-long-container-2"> <<if $feragonJobState != 0>> <img src="Images/Monsters/FeragonFixed.png" alt="IMG"> <<else>> <img src="Images/Monsters/FeragonDark.png" alt="IMG"> <</if>> </div> <div class="right-long-container-2"> @@.titleSmall;STATUS@@ <br><br> <<switch $feragonJobState >> <<case 0>> <<if $eyeSeeing >=2>> <div class="passCheck"> You can get this contract from the Forgemaster. Then, you can ask him to take you to her world. <img src="Images/Icons/Eye.png"></div> <<else>> <div class="failCheck"> You have not yet found this creature, or anything related to her. <img src="Images/Icons/FailEye.png"></div> <</if>> <<case 1>> You have accepted this contract, and can now ask Vael to bring you there. <<case 2>> You have successfully completed this contract, and Vael has paid you for the service. <<case 3>> You have successfully completed this contract, and Vael has paid you for the service. <<case 4>> You failed to retrieve the Feragon to Vael, and, as a result, you doubt he'll be willing to pay you. <<case 5>> The feragon is succesfully restrained in the depths of the forge. <<default>> <</switch>> </div> </div> <div class = "close-Inventory"> <<link "Go Back">> <div class="stat-container"> Go back </div> <<goto Contracts>> <</link>> </div> <</nobr>>@@.playerSpeech;"How about..."@@ Your attention falls on a contract depicting some kind of hound, the reward is not astronomical so you figure it should be an easy task. Naafiri is her name, and, despite the spiky exterior, she seems moderately cute. You think you should be able to deal with her without too much difficulty... @@.damlonSpeech;"The Eye recently marked this one, we don't have much information about this creature, but it's pack has been wreaking havoc. Better to take care of it now, before the danger it presents can increase any further."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"How many are in the pack?"@@ Damlon lets out a soft chuckle. @@.damlonSpeech;"Come on, $playerName, they are just a pack of dogs. I'm sure you can handle them, no matter their number."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Take this contract |TakeContract][ $activeDamlonContract = "naafiri"]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedItem.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Check one of the other contracts |AskDamlonJob][$returnDamlonContract = true]]</div>\ <<set $feragonJobState to 5>> @@.playerSpeech;"So... how is the feragon holding up?"@@ Vael turns towards you, eyes gleaming with a hint of malice... and something else. It's as if he wanted to share it with someone, but wouldn't just out of pride. @@.vaelSpeech;"Oh, I finished its accommodations already... such a fine specimen. Its scales are quite a sight to behold, they reflect light so beautifully. And its vocal cords... ah, a most wonderful addition. It feels so much more natural to speak with them..."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"I had noticed the change in your voice, yes."@@ @@.vaelSpeech;"But you are not here to talk about that, are you, hunter? You are here to see the creature..."@@ You don't even try to deny it, but he seems eager to show you his work, like a kid that just finished his science project. He leads you down the corridor, his many tiny insect-like legs working in harmony to carry his monstrous form, passing over various rooms where his creations are stored, or in some cases, being built. After going down some sets of stairs, you arrive at a pitch-black room, cold, stale air wafting through the cracks of the metal plates that form it. @@.vaelSpeech;"Here we are. Tell me, what do you think?"@@ Noticing your lack of response, and remembering you can't see in the dark, he snaps his fingers. A brazier nearby springs to life, lighting up the chamber and revealing the horrific sight hidden inside. Hanging inside a steel disk, held by chains and cables, the feragon is still alive, if only barely. Most of her beautiful scales are gone, her naked flesh a testament to the tormentous procedure that Vael put her through. Her horns, claws, and teeth have been removed, the holes left behind masterfully sutured and sealed. A tube is stuck inside her mouth, held in place with a clamp, to ensure nutrients are properly fed to her stomach. The feragon is momentarily blinded by the light, and as her eyes adjust to the brightness and focus on you, a fresh wave of tears rolls down her cheeks. <<if $karma <= $karmaToEvil>>\ <div class="passCheck">\ And yet, at the sight of her broken, tortured form, you can't help but feel... a twisted sense of pride, knowing that the one who caused her such agony is, in a way, yourself. <img src="Images/Icons/EvilKarma.png"></div>\ @@.playerSpeech;"A magnificent sight, truly. You did a good job."@@ Vael chuckles, looking at the suffering feragon, and then back at you. @@.vaelSpeech;"I'm glad we agree. Now, however, I must ask you to leave... I have more work to do."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask him if you could stay a little with the feragon |StayFeragon1]]</div>\ <<else>>\ <div class="passCheck">\ The sight is so pitiful, so horrible, that it actually makes you flinch. You expected Vael to use her for parts, but not this extreme... <img src="Images/Icons/Karma.png"></div>\ Vael seems to notice your reaction, and turns towards you. @@.vaelSpeech;"This creature is but an animal, devoid of deeper thought, or feeling. Do not pity this feragon, for it would happily rip your fragile throat out given the chance..."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask him if you could stay a little with the feragon |StayFeragon2]]</div>\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Leave |LeaveWithVael]]</div>\Both you and Vael take your leave, and the young feragon is once again left in the darkness. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |Hunters Hall]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"Do you mind if I stay here a little, Vael? I would like to talk with the creature, seeing that it remembers me..."@@ Vael considers the request for a moment, his metallic body creaking softly as his brain works the possibilities. @@.vaelSpeech;"Fine. You brought her to me after all... try not to get too attached to the creature. It's not worth the effort."@@ And with that, the Forgemaster leaves the two of you alone, and you can finally have a closer look at her. Her whole body is shaking, and her eyes are swollen and red from all the crying. She can't talk, even if the huge tube stuck inside her mouth wasn't there, she would still be unable to produce any coherent sound. <<if $karma >= 0>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Reach out and touch her cheek, tell her you are sorry... |SorryFeragon]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedKarma.png"></div>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Torment her a little; Tell her just how miserable the rest of her life will be |SorryFeragon]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedEvilKarma.png"></div>@@\ <<else>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Reach out and touch her cheek, tell her you are sorry... |SorryFeragon]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedKarma.png"></div>@@\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Torment her a little; Tell her just how miserable the rest of her life will be |TormentFeragon][$karma -=1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedEvilKarma.png"></div>\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Just give her a kiss and leave |KissFeragon]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Leave |LeaveWithoutVael]]</div>\ @@.playerSpeech;"Do you mind if I stay here a little, Vael? I would like to talk with the creature, seeing that it remembers me..."@@ Vael considers the request for a moment, his metallic body creaking softly as his brain works the possibilities. @@.vaelSpeech;"Very well. You brought her to me, after all. I cannot linger here, but I trust you will not tamper with her."@@ And with that, the Forgemaster leaves the two of you alone, and you can finally have a closer look at her. Her whole body is shaking, and her eyes are swollen and red from all the crying. She can't talk, even if the huge tube stuck inside her mouth wasn't there, she would still be unable to produce any coherent sound. <<if $karma >= 0>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Reach out and touch her cheek, tell her you are sorry... |SorryFeragon]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedKarma.png"></div>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Torment her a little; Tell her just how miserable the rest of her life will be |SorryFeragon]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedEvilKarma.png"></div>@@\ <<else>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Reach out and touch her cheek, tell her you are sorry... |SorryFeragon]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedKarma.png"></div>@@\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Torment her a little; Tell her just how miserable the rest of her life will be |TormentFeragon][$karma -=1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedEvilKarma.png"></div>\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Just give her a kiss and leave |KissFeragon]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Leave |LeaveWithoutVael]]</div>\ With nothing else to do here, you take your leave, the young feragon once again left alone in the darkness. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |Hunters Hall]]</div>\You reach out and gently caress her cheek, her soft, exposed flesh trembling under your fingertips. @@.playerSpeech;"I'm sorry. I really am..."@@ You tell her, and mean it. You didn't think she would end up like this. Still, your words mean nothing, not when she is trapped here, doomed to be cut and harvested until her body simply can't take anymore. Knowing you can't do much for her, you just leave, the young feragon once again left alone in the darkness. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |Hunters Hall]]</div>\You look at her directly into her eyes, and smile. @@.playerSpeech;"It's not going to get any better, you know?"@@ You tell her. @@.playerSpeech;"Vael will cut and carve away at your flesh, over and over... oh but he is a most skilled surgeon, he will make sure you live a long, miserable life."@@ The young feragon's eyes widen even further as she hears the words, but restrained and voiceless as she is, her reaction is nothing but a silent shudder. @@.playerSpeech;"Your mother must be already looking for you, but we are not even on the same plane anymore. She'll never find you... no one will. We can just hope she forgets about you eventually, for her own good..."@@ She begins sobbing again, the idea that she'll never get to see her mother again somehow even more painful than her current predicament. You stand up and begin making your way out, leaving the broken, helpless creature behind, and close the heavy steel door behind you... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |Hunters Hall]]</div>\You lean closer and press a kiss on her forehead. She just stays there, trembling, her eyes wide open. Vael is keeping a close eye on her, so you know you can't really do much for her... or to her. All you can do is share a tiny moment with her before leaving, the young feragon once again left alone in the darkness. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |Hunters Hall]]</div>\You approach the girl, and she simply stays there, her big eyes drifting nervously from your body to your blade and then to your body again. @@.wolfSpeech;"T-thank you,"@@ She mumbles shyly, clearly a bit intimidated by your attire. @@.wolfSpeech;"I... I really thought I was going to die for a moment."@@ Oh, if only she knew what you had in mind for her... she wouldn't be thanking you so eagerly. Your mental powers are usually only used to plant small ideas in the minds of people, subtly manipulating them into doing what you want, but with a creature as weak and vulnerable as her, you figure you could try using it in a more... direct approach. Humans may not be the best at wielding these kinds of abilities, but your powers have grown much, and with a mind as fragile as hers, you know you will be able to get some fun out of it. You feel your mind heating up as you focus on her. Injured as she is, she would already have trouble moving, but even if she wasn't, all she can do is lie there, dumbfounded, unable to take her eyes off you. You kneel beside her, your sparkling gaze locking onto hers, and then you take hold of her head, keeping the by now terrified girl in place. @@.playerSpeech;"I can sense your fear, little wolf... you do not have to be. Just surrender yourself to me... and everything will be okay..."@@ You speak slowly and carefully as you blast her innocent mind to the full extent of your ability. The girl whimpers and tries to look away, but it's a futile struggle. She is powerless against your might. @@.wolfSpeech;"W-what... what are you... doing?"@@ <div class="passCheck">\ She many not know exactly what's happening, but she sure as hell knows it can't be good. Something is prodding into her mind, rummaging through her brain in a way that is as invasive and violating as it is terrifying. You learn quite a bit about her as you go deeper into her memories, Lyra, the adoptive daughter of a human hunter who recently fell gravelly ill... the reason she felt the need to come into the woods alone, to hunt by herself. Not like you care much, though... you are just glad to find out no one will randomly sneak in on you while you work on her... <img src="Images/Icons/MindMagic.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Erase her mind, turn her into a sex doll |EraseMindWolf][$karma -=2]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedMagic.png"><img src="Images/Icons/RedEvilKarma.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Push her mind aside and gain control of her body |GainControlWolf]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedMagic.png"></div>\Soon, all of that will be irrelevant, anyway. You like your bitches as compliant and obedient as they can possibly be, and that's exactly what you'll make out of her, a living, breathing sex doll. You will leave her behind in no time, never to see her again, so there's no point in doing a delicate job on her mind when you can just scour it all clean. @@.playerSpeech;"Shhh, everything will be better soon... you don't need any of those thoughts, any of that fear... you will feel better, I promise."@@ Lyra's eyes widen and tears run down her cheeks, pain spreading through her body as her mind is forcefully rewired. She tries to beg with you, but much to her surprise, she is unable to formulate any coherent sentence, words becoming more and more foreign to her as the seconds pass. That makes her realize what's going on, and a primal instinct of self-preservation takes over. These have all been written by Orion himself, and it makes you realize just how long he has been here. "Mankind has always suffered the threat of creatures far beyond their capabilities, and in the darkest of times, we rose, the first hunters..." You keep reading, flickering through the pages. "Mankind has always relied on tools to survive, and when confronted with a foe too mighty, we sought a greater power..." You continue, and continue, hours going by as you read. "...the gods shall see us humbled for our hubris. Even now, as they rot beneath our very feet, the echoes of their wrath linger. Or pretending he was reading , at least. @@.OrionSpeech;"Talk? Of course. What is it that you want?"@@ <<set $fastOrion to false>>\ <<else>>\ @@.OrionSpeech;"Is there anything else you would like to discuss?"@@ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["Don't you get bored here?" |AskBoredOrion]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["What's your opinion on Vael?" |AskVaelOrion]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["What's your opinion on Damlon?" |AskDamlonOrion]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["What's your opinion on Lauren?" |AskLaurenOrion]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["What's your opinion on Nyxa?" |AskNyxaOrion]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[["What's your opinion on Korvaax?" |AskKorvaaxOrion]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[On second thought... |LibraryIntro]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"This is a pretty boring place, isn't it? Don't you get bored here?"@@ Orion thinks about the question for a moment, before replying. @@.OrionSpeech;"Sometimes, perhaps. But we all made a promise, and have a duty to uphold. Mine is to guard this library and make sure the knowledge we have gathered doesn't fade."@@ @@.OrionSpeech;"So, here shall I remain, until the end of days..."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |TalkOrion][$fastOrion = true]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"What are your thoughts on Vael? They say he is almost as old as you are."@@ The old librarian chuckles. @@.OrionSpeech;"Yes, Vael was one of the first ones. Time hasn't been kind to him, his steel body may not age, but his mind is certainly not the same it used to be... Reserved as he is, he is still one of the few I can call a friend."@@ He sets his empty eye sockets on a flickering candle for a moment, seemingly lost in his memories. @@.OrionSpeech;"Time is cruel to us all. No matter how we may struggle, it will always have the final say."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |TalkOrion][$fastOrion = true]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"What are your thoughts on Damlon?"@@ @@.OrionSpeech;"He is worthy of respect, having been able to live through most of his human life as a High Hunter, without meeting his untimely demise. Perhaps he will be worthy of immortality when the time comes."@@ He sighs. @@.OrionSpeech;"I wouldn't wish such burden on anyone, but he has always been bound by duty, and I do not think he'll let entropy take him away without a fight."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |TalkOrion][$fastOrion = true]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"What are your opinions on Lauren? She is rarely seen here"@@ @@.OrionSpeech;"As the head of our order, she has many responsibilities to attend to. She barely has enough free time to indulge in her passion, so I can't blame her for not visiting often."@@ Orion' skull contorts into something somewhat resembling a smile. @@.OrionSpeech;"She'll never admit it, but she's quite the skilled pianist. Music is a good outlet, wouldn't you agree?"@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |TalkOrion][$fastOrion = true]]</div>\<<if $nyxaState == "partner" ||$nyxaState == "readyForNight1" ||$nyxaState == "readyForNight2" ||$nyxaState == "readyForNight3" ||$nyxaState == "romanced">>\ @@.playerSpeech;"What's your thoughts on Nyxa? A rather unusual addition to the Order, wouldn't you say?"@@ The old, skeletal librarian glances at you. Though his lack of properly moving facial muscles limits his expressions, his posture reveals a subtle... hint of a smirk. @@.OrionSpeech;"Treat her well. I'm most pleased to see her happy, at last."@@ <<else>> @@.playerSpeech;"What's your thoughts on Nyxa? A rather unusual addition to the Order, wouldn't you say?"@@ @@.OrionSpeech;"A most unusual recruitment forsooth. I harbor no ill will towards non-humans, for I myself can hardly be deemed one myself anymore. Yet, to have a vixen serve as the healer of our esteemed order is without precedent..."@@ He shakes his skull slowly, as if to dismiss the thought. @@.OrionSpeech;"Her loyalty is certain, and her skill as a healer cannot be disputed. I only hope the rest of our members will, in due time, warm to her presence."@@ <</if>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |TalkOrion][$fastOrion = true]]</div>\ @@.playerSpeech;"What's your thoughts on Korvaax? Now that he isn't with us..."@@ @@.OrionSpeech;"He was a most skilled member of our order... its a shame his mind was not nearly as resilient as his body..."@@ Orion remains silent for a moment, seemingly deep in thought. @@.OrionSpeech;"Darkness began to seep into his mind, every monster felled by his blade adding to the ever-growing weight that threatened to crush him, every shift between the planes eroding his will, bit by bit. Death is a poison, and those who are tasked with its delivery are never exempt from its toxicity..."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Is that why he turned?"@@ Orion nods, slowly. @@.OrionSpeech;"The weight of guilt twisted his mind, and the whispers of the shadows broke his sanity. He began to find pleasure in the kill, and what once was a duty became a vice. Eventually, we had to confront him, for his own sake and ours. He was becoming more and more erratic, and the aura of malevolence that seeped from his very core was poisoning the minds of all those around him as well."@@ He points at your sheathed sword, which now you can feel is humming, almost imperceptibly. @@.OrionSpeech;"The blade you carry was his, once. I wonder if Lauren was right in giving it to you."@@ The skeleton's eye sockets are dark and cold, and in their black depths, you see your reflection. You shiver, and the moment passes. @@.OrionSpeech;"If you ever find yourself feeling overwhelmed, and fear you may succumb to the darkness, talk to me. We will find a way."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |TalkOrion][$fastOrion = true]]</div>\You decide to check if the library has anything regarding the contracts you are supposed to deal with. Preparation is the key to success after all. Orion gives you a free berth to his books and other materials and you make your way through the maze of a library.... <<if $eyeSeeing <=0>>\ <div class="failCheck">\ However, you don't find anything too useful. Sure, the library has an entry for pretty much every monster in existence, but most of it you already know. And what little additional information there is doesn't seem like something that will help you out of this situation. <img src="Images/Icons/FailEye.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |LibraryIntro]]</div>\ <<else>>\ <div class="passCheck">\ And you are positively surprised when you find extremely accurate descriptions of the contracts you have. Sure, the library has an entry for pretty much every monster in existence, but what you find seems tailor-made just for you... <img src="Images/Icons/Eye.png"></div>\ <<if $feragonJobState >=1>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Recommended stats for the feragon |FeragonLibrary]]</div>\ <</if>>\ <<if $furyJobState >=1>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Recommended stats for the Light Fury |FuryLibrary]]</div>\ <</if>>\ <<if $naafiriJobState >=1>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Recommended stats for Naafiri |NaafiriLibrary]]</div>\ <</if>>\ <<if $wolfJobState >=1>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Recommended stats for the wolf girl |WolfLibrary]]</div>\ <</if>>\ <<if $wolyrnJobState >=1>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Recommended stats for the wolyrn |WolyrnLibrary]]</div>\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Recommended stats for Azri'Elis |AzriLibrary]]</div>\ <<if $wyvernJobState >=1>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Recommended stats for the wyvern |WyvernLibrary]]</div>\ <</if>>\ <<if $deerJobState >=1>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Recommended stats for the deer girl |DeerLibrary]]</div>\ <</if>>\ <<if $deathclawJobState >=1>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Recommended stats for the deathclaw |DeathClawLibrary]]</div>\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Enough books for now |LibraryIntro]]</div>\ <</if>>\ The recommended minimum stats for this encounter are: <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/General.png"> 3<img src="Images/Icons/Heal.png"> ? <img src="Images/Icons/MindMagic.png"> ?<img src="Images/Icons/PregIcon.png"> 4<img src="Images/Icons/Charisma.png"> 4</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |BooksLibrary]]</div>\The recommended minimum stats for this encounter are: <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/General.png"> 2<img src="Images/Icons/Heal.png"> ? <img src="Images/Icons/MindMagic.png"> 3<img src="Images/Icons/PregIcon.png"> 2<img src="Images/Icons/Charisma.png"> ?</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |BooksLibrary]]</div>\The recommended minimum stats for this encounter are: <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/General.png"> 5<img src="Images/Icons/Heal.png"> 3 <img src="Images/Icons/MindMagic.png"> ?<img src="Images/Icons/PregIcon.png"> ?<img src="Images/Icons/Charisma.png"> 3</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |BooksLibrary]]</div>\The recommended minimum stats for this encounter are: <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/General.png"> 0<img src="Images/Icons/Heal.png"> 1<img src="Images/Icons/MindMagic.png"> 1<img src="Images/Icons/PregIcon.png"> 1<img src="Images/Icons/Charisma.png"> ?</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |BooksLibrary]]</div>\The recommended minimum stats for this encounter are: <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/General.png"> 2<img src="Images/Icons/Heal.png"> ? <img src="Images/Icons/MindMagic.png"> 2<img src="Images/Icons/PregIcon.png"> 3<img src="Images/Icons/Charisma.png"> 2</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |BooksLibrary]]</div>\The recommended minimum stats for this encounter are: <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/General.png"> 3<img src="Images/Icons/Heal.png"> 3 <img src="Images/Icons/MindMagic.png"> ?<img src="Images/Icons/PregIcon.png"> 5<img src="Images/Icons/Charisma.png"> 1</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |BooksLibrary]]</div>\The recommended minimum stats for this encounter are: <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/General.png"> 0<img src="Images/Icons/Heal.png"> 1 <img src="Images/Icons/MindMagic.png"> 3<img src="Images/Icons/PregIcon.png"> 3<img src="Images/Icons/Charisma.png"> ?</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |BooksLibrary]]</div>\The recommended minimum stats for this encounter are: <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/General.png"> 3<img src="Images/Icons/Heal.png"> ? <img src="Images/Icons/MindMagic.png"> ?<img src="Images/Icons/PregIcon.png"> 2<img src="Images/Icons/Charisma.png"> 1</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |BooksLibrary]]</div>\<<set $temporaryCode to "">> <<textbox "$temporaryCode" $temporaryCode>> [[Continue |CodesRead]]<<nobr>> <<switch $temporaryCode>> <<case "possibledreams">>\ <<set $temporaryCode to "">>\ <<if $possibleDreams.length > 0>> Possible Dreams: <ul> <<for _dream range $possibleDreams>> <li><<print _dream>></li> <</for>> </ul> <<else>> No dreams available. <</if>> <<if $possibleNightmares.length > 0>> Possible Nightmares: <ul> <<for _nightmare range $possibleNightmares>> <li><<print _nightmare>></li> <</for>> </ul> <<else>> No nightmares available. <</if>> <<case "vaelItems">>\ <<set $temporaryCode to "">>\ Granted. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/Item.png"> </div>\ <<set $deerPheromones to true>> <<set $firePotion to 1>> <<set $elixirExertion to 1>> <<case "lotsofcoins">>\ <<set $temporaryCode to "">> Granted. <<set $money +=200>> <<case "day">>\ <<set $temporaryCode to "">> Granted. <<set $playerEnergy to "tired">> <<case "travelingstones">>\ <<set $temporaryCode to "">> Granted. <<set $deerStone to true>> <<set $azriStone to true>> <<set $wolfStone to true>> <<set $wolyrnStone to true>> <<set $wyvernStone to true>> <<set $furyStone to true>> <<set $naafiriStone to true>> <<set $deathclawStone to true>> <<set $orionNyxaStone to 1>> <<set $vaelNyxaStone to 1>> <<set $damlonNyxaStone to 1>> <<case "grantmeeyes">>\ <<set $temporaryCode to "">> Granted. <<set $eyeSeeing +=5>> <<case "veryfighty">>\ <<set $temporaryCode to "">>\ Granted. <<set $hunterSkill +=5>> <<case "impregnator">>\ <<set $temporaryCode to "">> Granted. <<set $cumSkill +=5>> <<case "charismatic">>\ <<set $temporaryCode to "">> Granted. <<set $charismaSkill +=5>> <<case "magicmind">>\ <<set $temporaryCode to "">> Granted. <<set $magicSkill +=5>> <<case "healer">>\ <<set $temporaryCode to "">> Granted. <<set $healSkill +=5>> <<case "venerable">>\ <<set $temporaryCode to "">> Granted. <<set $reputation +=5>> <<case "stats">>\ <<set $temporaryCode to "">> Granted. <<set $healSkill +=10>> <<set $reputation +=10>> <<set $magicSkill +=10>> <<set $charismaSkill +=10>> <<set $cumSkill +=10>> <<set $hunterSkill +=10>> <<default>>\ <<set $temporaryCode to "">> Wrong code. <</switch>>\ <</nobr>>\ [[Continue |Inventory]]@@.title;SOUTH BOSTON RUINS@@ <<set $deathclawJobState to 2>>\ <<set $playerEnergy to "tired">>\ <<set $deathclawStone to false>>\ <<set $temporaryImpregnations to $impregnations >>\ <<set $temporaryKills to $playerKills >>\ The smothering darkness around you finally recedes, and when it does, you find yourself standing on a piece of corroded debris, so utterly mangled that you are not even sure what purpose it once served. Around you, the ruins of what you can only assume to be an immense city stretch as far as the eye can see. The remains of tall, elegant buildings jut up towards the sky, while other, more modest constructions litter the landscape. The only commonality between them seems to be their state of decay. You can only wonder, just how many people used to live here... and where did they all go? Perhaps the creature you are about to confront should be taken a lot more seriously if she is the one responsible for this devastation. Then, a voice catches your attention. It is deep, metallic, and a bit distorted as if spoken through some sort of filter. @@.steelSpeech;"Citizen! This is a restricted area. I don't know how on hell you got here, but I suggest you turn around and go back the way you came."@@ There are two of them, clad in some sort of extremely heavy armor, tubes and plates covering their entire body, and a helmet obscuring their faces as well. The other figure speaks, and her softer, feminine voice is distorted by the same device to the point it nearly sounds the exact same as the male one. @@.steelSpeech;"We have a deathclaw cornered in one of the nearby buildings. You don't want to be here when we open fire."@@ It seems like they have the same goal you supposedly should have... but if they just kill the creature without you even coming near it, you doubt your quest would count as complete. Besides, you are not sure you want it to come to that. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Talk to them |TalkBrotherhoodSteel]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCharisma.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Attack them|AttackBrotherhoodSteel]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\You pull out your sword, and get ready for the fight. Even beneath their suits, the two figures seem quite surprised at your reaction. Why would the cosplaying madman be so eager to die? One of them snorts under his helmet. @@.steelSpeech;"Whats that sword for, big man? Planning to bash us with it? Go home, idiot."@@ The second one seems a bit more wary, as she readies her laser rifle. @@.steelSpeech; "You heard him, MOVE! We won't ask again."@@ It's clear they don't take you very seriously. How could they, for them, you indeed look like a fool playing pretend, and a piece of sharp metal is completely useless against T-60 power armor. You will show them the error of their ways... <<if $magicSkill >= 3>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Use your mind powers |MindPowersSteel]]<div class="numberStat">3</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedMagic.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedCross.png">[[Use your mind powers |MindPowersSteel]]<div class="numberStat">3</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedMagic.png"></div>@@\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Your sword should be enough to deal with them |SwordBrotherhoodSteel]]<div class="numberStat">2</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\Using your blade against them would perhaps not be the brightest idea, so you decide to use another, more subtle means. Beneath their armors, they are still just humans, and thus vulnerable to your mental attacks. You run for cover before they open fire, you are not sure what kind of high-tech crossbow thing they are carrying but you're not in a hurry to find out. There, you concentrate on the two armored paladins, quickly shifting through their thoughts, and soon enough, they find themselves unable to move a muscle, their bodies completely paralyzed by your will. @@.steelSpeech;"The fu..."@@ Their fear grows by the second as they try to figure out what is even going on, just what kind of trickery you might have used on them... You leave the safety of your hiding spot, and step in front of the pair, who are now unable to do anything as you approach. @@.steelSpeech;"What is happening? What did you do??"@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Make them collapse |CollapseBrotherhood]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedMagic.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Order them to kill themselves |ExecuteBrotherhood1][$karma -=1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedMagic.png"><img src="Images/Icons/RedEvilKarma.png"><img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png"></div>\ You know full well that a strike of your sword wouldn't do anything, their armors seem way too sturdy, but your weapon has more in it than meets the eye... <<if $hunterSkill >= 2>>\ Your sword should be enough to deal with the two paladins, but you are not dumb enough to charge in a straight line towards them. Whatever weapon they carry, you don't want to find out firsthand. So you duck and dodge, running from one piece of rubble to another, slowly closing the distance between the two of you as their lasers shoot through the air, barely missing you... Their shots grow more and more erratic as you draw closer, getting more and more frustrated as they keep missing, and soon, you manage to get within striking distance. <div class="passCheck">\ Unleashing the demon trapped within your blade, an arcing lightning of red-red energy shoots out, enveloping their forms and frying their power armor. It is not enough to destroy it completely, but their rifles have been disabled, and their suits are smoking and sparking. <img src="Images/Icons/General.png"></div>\ Now it's a fair fight... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Knock them out |KnockOutBrotherhood]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Kill them |ExecuteBrotherhood2]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedEvilKarma.png"><img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ You charge towards the two paladins, holding your sword at the ready, red lightning coursing through the blade... However, much, <i>much</i> before you can even get withing striking distance, the two of them have already unloaded their guns on you. The shots pack noticeably more punch than crossbows, and you are blasted backwards, hitting the ground a few feet away. Your armor is strong enough to protect you, but that's the only good news. The paladin on the right takes aim at your unprotected head before you can fully recover, and pulls the trigger. <div class="passCheck">\ You don't even have time to regret your mistakes as your skull is blasted away under a hyper-powered laser blast. <img src="Images/Icons/FailGeneral.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Death embraces you...">> <<goto "Death">> <</link>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png" >\ </div>\ <</if>>\ You have no intention of killing them, they did nothing to deserve such a fate, but you will have to knock them unconscious. Besides, killing a human would be the complete antithesis of your order's ideals. You send one more command into their heads, and they are completely out. Interestingly enough, their power armors still hold them in place, so they stand still even as they are sleeping. @@.playerSpeech;"There, there... now lets see where this 'deathclaw' is, shall we?"@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Take one of their weapons first |TakeWeaponBrotherhood]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedItem.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |ContinueNoWeapon]]</div>\ A sadistic smile creeps onto your face, right before you send an order to the two soldier now standing frozen in front of you. <div class="passCheck">\ They are helpless to watch as their own bodies turn against them, and their guns are turned towards their own helmets, and soon, they are both firing, blasting away through the visor of the armor, burning through it, and turning their brain to mush. <img src="Images/Icons/Death.png"></div>\ They are no more, and you didn't even have to get your hands dirty. <<set $playerKills to $temporaryKills +2 >>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Take one of their weapons first |TakeWeaponBrotherhood]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedItem.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |ContinueNoWeapon]]</div>\Before you move on, following the presence of your target, you decide to take one of their weapons with you. Perhaps Vael will be interested in the strange design... <<set $lasRifle to true>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |ContinueNoWeapon]]</div>\You make your way deeper into the ruins, slowly trudging through the debris, careful not to alert the beast lurking around. You are still not sure what you are dealing with... hey, if those two useless chumps behind you managed to corner it, then it can't be THAT strong. Finally, you reach a building, one of the few that are still standing somewhat. Even with the enormous holes in the walls, the main structure remains mostly intact. Inside, you are greeted by a scene you were not expecting... The creature is in a bad shape. Lying against a brick, peeled off wall, the reptilian beast has her eyes fixated on your every move, hissing threateningly but not immediately attacking you. If for no other reason, she hardly seems to be able to stand on her back two claws; her body is covered in burns and wounds, and one of her horns is broken. <img src="Images/Monsters/DeathclawFixed.png" class="resizable-image" alt="DeathclawImage"> You suppose the paladins managed to get a few shots in before she managed to run and hide. You stop in the doorway, or rather, the blasted-off remains of a doorway, looking over the scene and considering what to do... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Talk to her |TalkDeathclaw]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Quickly subdue her using your blade |BladeDeathclaw]]<div class="numberStat">2</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\Bloodlust takes hold of you, and soon you are on top of the closest paladin, dodging his clumsy blows and fitting your blade through the weak spots of his armor. <div class="passCheck">\ Seeing her comrade falling, the other paladin jumps at you, or rather, slooowly trudges her way towards you, in a hopeless attempt to help her companion, and soon she falls victim to your blade as well. <img src="Images/Icons/Death.png"></div>\ <<set $playerKills to $temporaryKills +2 >>\ Oh well... that was easy. The lightning from your blade has sadly rendered their rifles unusable, so no point in taking them... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |ContinueNoWeapon]]</div>\With them unable to use their weapons, you have an easy time knocking the two paladins unconscious, lightning from your blade coursing through their suits and bodies and sending them into a world of dream. Killing two humans would be against the principles of your order, so this is the best solution... you just hope nothing deals the killing blow while they are still out. The lightning from your blade has sadly rendered their rifles unusable, so no point in taking them... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |ContinueNoWeapon]]</div>\The recommended minimum stats for this encounter are: <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/General.png"> 0<img src="Images/Icons/Heal.png"> 2 <img src="Images/Icons/MindMagic.png"> 3<img src="Images/Icons/PregIcon.png"> 4<img src="Images/Icons/General.png"> 2</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |BooksLibrary]]</div>\You take a step into the nearly dilapidated room, your hand reaching for the hilt of your sword, just in case. @@.playerSpeech;"You must be the deathclaw they were talking about... the one I came here for. Are you the one responsible for this... destruction?" @@ The creature hisses menacingly at you, warning you to stay back... If she is capable of speech, she is not showing it. @@.playerSpeech;"Calm down, I'm not going to hurt you. I just want to have a chat, if you don't mind." @@ She bares her teeth at you, and this time, she doesn't just hiss. With noticeable effort, the deathclaw manages to stand up, and charges towards you, clawed paws digging into the concrete floor, tail swaying behind her. Cornered as she is, the creature is not about to go down without a fight. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |BladeDeathclawAttacking]]<div class="numberStat">2</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\ Perhaps you can just get them to leave, you figure. Standing tall to them, despite their impressive size, you speak up. @@.playerSpeech;" I am well aware of that, and I would ask you to stand down. This creature is mine to deal with, not yours. I have a task to complete... and you are interfering with it." @@ The two hulking steel suits glance at each other, and then back at you. @@.steelSpeech;"And I suppose you'll use that sword buckled to your hip? Don't waste our time, stranger. "@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Threaten them |ThreatenBrotherhood]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Offer to assist them, you can fight the deathclaw together |OfferAssistance]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Attack them|AttackBrotherhoodSteel]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\The creature has not jumped onto you yet, but you can see it's not for lack of want. She's wounded, and cornered, and any second now, she could just lash out, so you decide you should make sure she is not going to be a threat. Without breaking eye contact, you slowly reach for your weapon, and as soon as you touch the hilt, the creature charges towards you, feeling the immediate threat. <<if $hunterSkill >= 2>>\ <div class="passCheck">\ Thankfully, the wounded deathclaw cant put up much of a fight, and in no time the red lightning of your sword envelops her, draining what little strength she had left and sending her into the cold, hard floor, barely conscious. <img src="Images/Icons/General.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Rape her|RapeDeathclaw]]</div>\ <<if $darkDevotion >=1>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Kill her, in the name of the dark lady |KillDeathclawLady][$darkDevotion +=1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedDevotion.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Execute her |KillDeathclaw]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png"></div>\ <</if>>\ <<if $healSkill >=2>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Heal her? |HealDeathclaw1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedHeal.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedCross.png">[[Heal her?|RapeDeathclaw]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedHeal.png"></div>@@\ <</if>>\ <<else>>\ <div class="passCheck">\ And for a creature in her condition, she is remarkably fast. You clumsily try to get your weapon out, but before you can, her sharp claws are already in your face. They are more than enough to slice through your weak skin and pierce your skull... a quick and messy death. <img src="Images/Icons/FailGeneral.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Death embraces you...">> <<goto "Death">> <</link>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png" >\ </div>\ <</if>>\ Whatever the cause of the damage in the city is, this creature doesn't look exactly peaceful, and it's your duty to protect the people, and to eliminate the creatures The Eye deems as threats. <div class="passCheck">\ Without giving her proper time to realize what's about to happen, you sink your sword deep into her hardened skull and end her life. <img src="Images/Icons/Death.png"></div>\ <<set $playerKills to $temporaryKills +1 >>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Get ready to return |ReturnDeathclaw3]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTeleport.png"></div>\Having her somewhat docile now that her body is tightly restrained, you have the perfect opportunity to satisfy your urges... and she should be strong enough to take it, even hunched down she is quite a bit bigger than you. We are good, alright?" @@ Slowly, trying to appear as non-threatening as possible, you reach towards the deathclaw's snout, the beast watching with interest as your hand gets closer and closer to her menacing chomping jaws. She has no idea what you are trying to do, hell, not even you know. But by now, she can make the link between your hand and her pain going away, so she lets you do it, trusting her ability to react quickly if needed. Your fingers come in contact with her snout, the scales feeling rough and coarse on your fingers, this creature wasn't made for affectionate pets. Still, you rub your hand against her nose, and then, lower, rubbing the side of her maw and finally, under her chin. @@.playerSpeech;"Aw, who is a good girl?" @@ No response, not like you were expecting any, but she is not protesting either. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Keep petting her|CaressDeathclaw2]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Pull her face closer and give her a kiss |KissDeathclaw]]</div>\Your hand stays under her chin, and gently, tries to guide her head lower, but this doesn't seem to go entirely well, as the deathclaw refuses to cooperate, clearly not understanding what it is that you want.
