Perhaps the only reason she is letting you touch her is that she is expecting you to keep doing the healing magic thing, which certainly felt great. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Use your healing magic to warm her up |HealDeathclaw3]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedHeal.png"></div>\ <<if $magicSkill >=2>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Use your mind powers now that she is... docile |MindPowersDeathclaw]]<div class="numberStat">2</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedMagic.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedCross.png">[[Use your mind powers now that she is... docile|MindPowersDeathclaw]]<div class="numberStat">2</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedMagic.png"></div>@@\ <</if>>\@@.playerSpeech;"Can't be helped... Come here..." @@ This time, she doesn't shy away from your touch, and you run your palm across her lower jaw and over her neck, a trail of warmth and soothing magic emanating from your hands. She is in pretty good shape so wasting your energy in this act is rather unnecessary, but the sensation must be a welcome one, seeing how she leans in to allow you to better reach her. With a rumble, the deathclaw reaches a squatting position, with her two muscular arms tucked between her legs, helping support her weight and allowing her to get closer to your level, so that you can properly reach her. Your left hand joins the right one, cupping her neck and massaging your way down, the creature purring at your touch. Its tiresome for sure, but seeing her enjoying the massage, you can't bring yourself to stop, and soon your hands are traveling along her collarbone, showering her in magical warmth. Then a bit lower, feeling her chest, and the strong muscles underneath the scales. She of course has no breasts, but the way her torso tapers down to her waist gives her a feminine silhouette, the curve of her waist being very evident from the front. The deathclaw has her eyes closed now, a soft purr escaping her maw and her tail swaying, she looks pretty much like a giant cat... with scales and claws the size of your forearms, that is. Your hands run across her stomach and her sides, but mostly her stomach. If the scales there are already a bit rough, the ones that begin to form the closer you get to her back are like stone. It is only after a long time, and after a significant amount of magical energy spent, that you are unable to keep the heat coming, and you find yourself needing a break. @@.playerSpeech;"A-alright... that should be enough for now..." @@ You take a step back, your butt finding a spot to rest against a particularly big chunk of debris, and try to catch your breath. Channeling any kind of magic is truly a draining affair, and it will take you a while before you can resume the petting session... in fact you doubt you'll have time before your time here runs out. But the deathclaw follows you, a low growl escaping her maw as she pokes your shoulder with her snout, urging you to resume. You can't help but chuckle at the sight of this gigantic, imposing beast, acting like a needy animal... and she is indeed very needy. And you'd have no trouble keeping this up for hours, had you not a pressing concern: your time in this world is limited, and eventually, you find the familiar pull dragging you back. You try to communicate the idea of your leaving, but the deathclaw doesn't seem to understand. You'd like to stay longer, but you really don't have a choice... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue|LeaveDeathclaw1]]</div>\Your time eventually comes to an end, and you have no choice but to leave the needy deathclaw behind... <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png" ><<link "Continue">> <<set $enterFromVoid = true>>\ <<goto "PortalRoom">>\ <</link>>\ </div>\Now that she is somewhat docile, and not feeling like a wounded, cornered animal, you figure you could try to use some of your mind powers on the deathclaw. You doubt she has a complex mind or is very smart, but you'd like to see if you can make her understand your intentions, or at least, that you are not a threat. So, you focus on her mind, and the first thing that greets you is a wall of primal, animal instinct. You are not surprised, it's only natural, given the creature's bestial nature. You try to push past it, and you find a more complex web of thoughts and memories, feelings and impulses. It's not nearly close to the level of thought, say, a human would have, but it's a far cry from the mindless animal most would assume her to be. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Try to convince her of your good intentions and just keep petting her |MindPowersDeathclaw2]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedMagic.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Use your mental prowess to suggest something a bit more... Intimate |MindPowersDeathclawChoose]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedMagic.png"></div>\Most of her thoughts are hard to understand to you, but making her understand your good intentions is not a complicated thing, she trusts you already (somewhat) With little effort, you manage to impress on her mind the simple idea of not wanting to hurt her, and that you are not a threat, and that your touch is meant to soothe her. Maybe she can no longer feel your healing magic working on her previously battered body, but you convince her that you are not intending any harm. After that, it's just a matter of using a little bit of mental persuasion to get her to lie down on the ground, allowing you to resume petting her without having to crane your neck and stretch your arm. @@.playerSpeech;"Much better, isn't it?" @@ You scratch the deathclaw's head, running your hand over the back of her elongated skull and her neck, the beast purring in response. You are not even sure how she is able to feel your touch there, the scales are so thick and rough, it must be like trying to pet a brick. But hey, just having this terrifying creature lying on the ground in front of you, not moving a muscle as you pet her, is a reward in itself. @@.playerSpeech;"What is this? The big, scary, and deadly deathclaw... likes being scratched under her chin?~" @@ She may be a merciless killing machine, but damn it, the deathclaw has a lot of charm. Your hands now roam all across her body as she lies flat on the ground, a bit like a deflated balloon. You trace the hefty ridge of her spine, your fingers following each and every scale, and then you follow her tail. A giant, spiked serpent, ending in a wicked, dagger-like tip that you have no doubt is more than capable of piercing through you. You'd be scared if not for the fact that very tail was now lazily resting next to your leg, the creature too relaxed and content to worry about anything else. And you'd have no trouble keeping this up for hours, had you not a pressing concern: your time in this world is limited, and eventually, you find the familiar pull dragging you back. You try to communicate the idea of your leaving, but the deathclaw doesn't seem to understand. See? I am just like you." @@ As the creature begins to understand, a deep rumble escapes her maw, and she jumps backwards with surprising speed given the fact she was resting only a few seconds ago. You are confused for a moment, until you read between the lines of her messy thoughts and realize what is going on. If you are a deathclaw, then why are you so... tiny, soft, and weak? You may have had her chained up just minutes ago, but she just assumes it to be because of the element of surprise and her weakened state. You try to think fast and explain yourself, casting your thoughts into her mind but the deathclaw seems to have none of it, and takes another step back, as if scared whatever condition made you look like that is contagious. And even if it was not, she wants nothing to do with a pathetic creature such as you. What kind of sickly children would she produce if she bred with someone like you? It seems there is no convincing her, and before you know it, the deathclaw is taking off, leaping over the debris, and quickly disappearing from sight. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue|LeaveDeathclaw2]]</div>\Well, if the deathclaw doesn't have the most complex of minds, perhaps a direct approach is the best course of action. It shouldn't be much of a challenge for a High Hunter like you." @@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Take this contract |TakeContract][ $activeDamlonContract = "deathclaw"]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedItem.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Check one of the other contracts |AskDamlonJob][$returnDamlonContract = true]]</div>\You take the odd-looking weapon you got from the paladins and present it to Vael. His lenses flicker briefly before he speaks. @@.vaelSpeech;"A primitive energy weapon. I doubt its effective in it's current condition, but some components should be salvageable. I can offer you fifty pieces of gold." @@ <<set $lasRifle to false>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |SellWares][$money += 50]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;" Perhaps we can join forces, and deal with the deathclaw together. You seem more than capable, but a deathclaw is... a dangerous foe. I'd be more than willing to lend a hand, should you accept it." @@ You don't even know what a deathclaw is exactly but you try to keep a confident façade. The two paladins however, seem... unsure about the proposition. Just why would this random idiot be offering them help, without even a gun to use? One thing is certain, keeping you near them is more a liability than an asset, and it's clear their mind is set. @@.steelSpeech;"The creature is already wounded, and we won't risk our lives by letting... whatever you are into the fight. I suggest you turn tail, stranger, before we put a hole in your chest." @@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Stand aside and let them proceed|LetbrotherhoodProceed]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Attack them|AttackBrotherhoodSteel]]<div class="numberStat">3</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\ @@.playerSpeech;"Listen, I am here on an important mission. I must deal with this creature myself, and if you stand in my way, then I assure you, you won't be completing yours either." @@ The two armored figures exchange glances again, before one of them lets a dry chuckle. Then, a laser rifle gets pointed right at you. @@.steelSpeech;"Are you threatening us? Stand aside, or we'll blast you to pieces. Don't say we didn't warn you." @@ They seem adamant about getting rid of the creature, and perhaps the most sensible choice would be to let them do the work. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Stand aside and let them proceed|LetbrotherhoodProceed]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Attack them|AttackBrotherhoodSteel]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\You are not sure what kind of weapon they are carrying, so you decide to err on the side of caution. With a shrug, you raise your hands and take a step back, letting them know you won't interfere. The steel-clad soldiers give a firm nod, and then, once you are outside of their range, they begin to advance slowly, rifles raised and their steps calculated. You follow them at a safe distance, curious to see how they will tackle the situation, and soon enough, you start hearing the unmistakable sounds of their gunfire. The shots are not particularly loud, but they break through the eerie silence of the dead city, and you know the fight has already begun. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Wait for them to engage the creature and see what happens|WaitEngage]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Engage them now; try to make sure the deathclaw survives|EngageBrotherhood]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\You decide to wait, not to get caught in the crossfire. You'd rather not deal with whatever that deahtclaw is, along with two angry knights with heavy weapons. The sound of their gunfire soon ceases, replaced by metallic, victorious cheers, and you hear the steel soldiers' voices. @@.steelSpeech;"Ha! Eat that, you fuckin' lizard!" @@ The female soldier's voice can be heard as well, though it's not quite as loud as her partner's. @@.steelSpeech;"Good job, good job. We should keep moving, we still have to deal with that nest of radscorpions, and I don't want to get stuck in this damn place when the night falls." @@ @@.steelSpeech;"Right. Let's get a move on then, the faster we get the job done, the faster we can go back home." @@ The loud footsteps of the paladins fade away as the two begin to march in another direction, leaving you to your own devices. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Check on the deathclaw |CheckDeathclaw]]</div>\You waste no more time, and immediately barge into the ongoing conflict... more like an execution than a real fight. The creature is cornered in a dilapidated building, and the armored soldiers are taking advantage of their position, peppering her with their long-range weapons, quickly chipping away at her already damaged hide. And you are not letting them finish their job. You rush to her aid, sword in hand and ready for combat, and one of them seems to catch on to your movements. @@.steelSpeech;"Its the idiot from before! Watch out, to your left!" @@ The one not currently focused on the deathclaw whips around and starts to take aim, the rifle's barrel aimed squarely at your head... <<if $hunterSkill >= 3>>\ Your training kicks in, and you move with a fluid grace, stepping sideways just in time to avoid the instantaneous discharge of overheated energy, and you retaliate in kind. Their weapons may be powered by their own magic, but yours is powered by a literal bound demon, eagerly waiting to be unleashed. <div class="passCheck">\ You raise your sword high and, with a quick motion, a burst of lightning shoots out from the blade and chains its way to the enemy's armor, jumping from one to the other as if with life of its own, frying their rifles and making them scream inside the steel coffins that now completely cease to respond to their control<img src="Images/Icons/General.png"></div>\ The fight is quickly over, and the two incapacitated soldiers lay on the ground. Smoke and steam hisses from the damaged parts of their armors, and no further movement comes from the defeated paladins. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Execute them |ExecuteBrotherhood3]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Turn your attention to the deathclaw |TurnAttentionDeathclaw]]</div>\ <<else>>\ <div class="passCheck">\ And that is the last thing you ever see. The weapon's projectile travels instantaneously from the muzzle to your skull, and you drop lifelessly to the ground. <img src="Images/Icons/FailGeneral.png"></div>\ And it was only a 14% chance shot... <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Death embraces you...">> <<goto "Death">> <</link>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png" >\ </div>\ <</if>>\ You don't have to wander too far, and soon enough, you stumble across the site of the skirmish. A dilapidated building, in which the creature lays to rest, charred and bleeding, her broken body right at home amidst the bricks and debris. <img src="Images/Monsters/DeathclawFixed.png" class="resizable-image" alt="DeathclawImage"> Her gaze is empty, devoid of life, and as her blood flows onto the ground, it becomes clear that there is not much for you to do here. The creature has been dealt with and you should be able to claim the bounty for her, but if you were hoping to have a friendly chat, perhaps even something more, that is not going to happen. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Get ready to return |ReturnDeathclaw2]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTeleport.png"></div>\ You are not taking any chances, and you quickly close the distance, aiming the tip of the blade between the exposed areas between their helmets and chestplates, and plunge the sharp tip right in their necks. <div class="passCheck">\ The protective, flexible layer of rubber-like material offers no resistance against the magical steel, and the blades cut through with ease, slitting one throat and then the other. <img src="Images/Icons/Death.png"></div>\ <<set $playerKills to $temporaryKills +2 >>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Turn your attention to the deathclaw |TurnAttentionDeathclaw]]</div>\You turn your attention to the deathclaw, still lying on the floor and groaning in pain. She... is not looking too hot. Burnt scales, dents, and scrapes litter her body, and blood pours from countless wounds... the damage done to her is quite significant. You stepped in as soon as you could but even that seems to haven't been enough, and the creature is probably not going to last much longer. <img src="Images/Monsters/DeathclawFixed.png" class="resizable-image" alt="DeathclawImage"> You either try to tend to her injuries, and hope she'll pull through, or you can end her suffering now and save her the agony of dying a slow death. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Execute her |ExecuteDeathclaw]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png"></div>\ <<if $healSkill >= 1>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Try your best to heal her |HealDeathclaw4]]<div class="numberStat">4</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedHeal.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedCross.png">[[Try your best to heal her |HealDeathclaw4]]<div class="numberStat">4</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedHeal.png"></div>@@\ <</if>>\You approach the deathclaw, figuring that there is no way you'd be able to heal her with your abilities. This would be a tough job even for Nyxa, and this is the only mercy you can offer her. Her breathing is ragged, and her eyes are half-closed as she struggles to stay conscious. @@.playerSpeech;"Sorry... I was a bit late. It seems like those two were determined to put you down..." @@ <div class="passCheck">\ The creature can barely manage a grunt, and you raise your sword, ready to deliver her to the final embrace of death. <img src="Images/Icons/Death.png"></div>\ <<set $playerKills to $temporaryKills +1 >>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Get ready to return |ReturnDeathclaw1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTeleport.png"></div>\You are well aware this may be way beyond your capabilities, but you are willing to give it a try. You kneel next to the wounded deathclaw, and reach out with a hand, focusing on the gaping, bleeding wounds that mar her flesh. The rivulets of blood are concerning, but more concerning is the damage that surely lies underneath. As you begin to channel some healing magic, you realize that the situation is worse than you expected; the wounds are deeper than they seem... recalling the fight against the paladins, you shudder to think what would have happened to you if that fateful shot had actually hit you. But there is no time for such thoughts, and you focus all your energy into healing the creature's damaged body, trying to seal the worst of the bleeding and mend the flesh back together, the process painfully slow, but hopefully effective... @@.playerSpeech;"Come on, come on..." @@ <<if $healSkill < 3>>\ <div class="passCheck">\ It... is not. Your healing magic is mostly a trick, a small amount of regeneration coupled with the pain-blocking ability. Sure, you can stop a few superficial cuts from bleeding, but something this severe? This would be something the head healer of your order would take a moment dealing with, and the only thing you can really do is buy the creature some time. <img src="Images/Icons/failHeal.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Execute her |ExecuteDeathclaw2]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ <div class="passCheck">\ It... suffices, somewhat at least. Your healing magic is more powerful than that of most other hunters, but still not nearly enough to deal with such serious injuries, or to return the ungodly amount of blood the deathclaw has lost back into her veins. <img src="Images/Icons/Heal.png"></div>\ However, you do manage to patch up the more severe injuries, and while she is not out of the woods yet, she is not in danger of dying, from this at least. @@.playerSpeech;"Alright, that's a bit better. Can you... can you understand me?" @@ The deathclaw doesn't seem capable of speech, but even if she happened to be, she would be way too out of it to be able to formulate a coherent response. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Keep pouring your healing magic on her |KeepPouringDeathclaw]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedHeal.png"></div>\ <</if>>\After a while, you can feel the effort starting to become too much, and you can no longer ignore the exhaustion taking hold of you. <div class="passCheck">\ The creature has fallen unconscious, her breathing still weak, and she will not wake up again... A merciful death is all you can give her, and you raise your blade, ready to deliver the final blow.<img src="Images/Icons/Death.png"></div>\ <<set $playerKills to $temporaryKills +1 >>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Get ready to return |ReturnDeathclaw1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTeleport.png"></div>\You decide to keep up your efforts. There is plenty of damage on her body, and seeing the creature is too far gone for any kind of meaningful lewd interaction, it would just be best to focus on the task at hand. Your hands traverse all through the beast's scaly body, tracing the contours of her frame as you focus the warm magical energy to mend her torn flesh, and as the seconds turn into minutes, the creature's breathing grows more steady, a soft rumble coming from her throat at the pleasant sensations. Just why would a human do this? She has no idea, but she is not complaining. @@.playerSpeech;"There we go... that's better. I don't think I can do anything about that broken horn of yours but hopefully that one will grow back on its own."@@ No response, other than a quiet, pleased groan, and her tail gently swishing. Still, her eyes are closed and she doesn't look particularly active, so you decide to leave her be. If only you had managed to arrive a bit sooner... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Get ready to return |ReturnDeathclaw7]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTeleport.png"></div>\@@.title;THE VOID@@ <<if $travelsVoid is 0>>\ You insert the travelstone into the device, and soon enough, the disk below your feet ignites. The gloomy portal swallows you whole, and the world around you immediately twists and turns around as if you were stepping into a kaleidoscope. Your stomach churns and your mind spins as your very body dissolves into nothingness, deeper and deeper you go. All that remains of you is an empty shell of your consciousness, floating through the empty void, unable to even scream out for help. Luckily, before you can be completely devoured by this alien realm, this terrifying absence of everything, you are brought back into reality, seemingly unchanged. Your stomach felt like it was turning inside out, and as a result, you couldn't help but puke the moment you felt the stable ground under your boots again. You cursed under your breath as you wiped your mouth with the back of your hand. Hopefully, this will be better the second time. <</if>><<if $travelsVoid is 1>>\ You stand atop the disk with a cold sense of determination. The memories of your first trip flood back, and a sense of unease washes over you. Maybe it was just a rough experience the first time, you try to reassure yourself. With some self-persuasion, you manage to muster the courage to insert the travelstone and let the shifting gateway swallow you again. Immediately, you regret your choice. It's exactly as shitty as the first time, the only difference being that you were somewhat expecting it this time. To your dismay, it's exactly as shitty as the very first time, the only difference this time is that you were somewhat expecting it. <</if>><<if $travelsVoid > 1>>\ By now, you already know exactly what to expect out of it. It's going to suck ass, but it has to be done… …. <</if>>\ <<switch $currentContract>> <<case "wyvern">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue|WyvernIntro][$travelsVoid +=1]]</div>\ <<case "wolf">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue|WolfIntro][$travelsVoid +=1]]</div>\ <<case "wolyrn">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue|WolyrnIntro][$travelsVoid +=1]]</div>\ <<case "lightfury">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue|furyIntro][$travelsVoid +=1]]</div>\ <<case "deer">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue|DeerIntro][$travelsVoid +=1]]</div>\ <<case "naafiri">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue|NaafiriStart][$travelsVoid +=1]]</div>\ <<case "deathclaw">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue|DeathclawIntro][$travelsVoid +=1]]</div>\ <<case "reptile">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue|682Intro][$travelsVoid +=1]]</div>\ <<default>>\ <</switch>>\ With nothing else to do, you figure you may as well get ready to return. If anything, the creature is dead and you'll be able to claim her bounty. If only you had managed to reach her a bit earlier... then perhaps things would have been different. <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png" ><<link "Continue">> <<set $enterFromVoid = true>>\ <<goto "PortalRoom">>\ <</link>>\ </div>\With nothing else to do, you figure you may as well get ready to return. If anything, the creature is dead and you'll be able to claim her bounty. <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png" ><<link "Continue">> <<set $enterFromVoid = true>>\ <<goto "PortalRoom">>\ <</link>>\ </div>\With the deathclaw dead, there is nothing more for you to do here. You will be able to claim the reward placed on her head, and sleep well knowing you actually did your job. <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png" ><<link "Continue">> <<set $enterFromVoid = true>>\ <<goto "PortalRoom">>\ <</link>>\ </div>\With the task successfully failed and your balls nice and empty, its time to return home, where you are sure to get a good night sleep. You would have perhaps liked to get a bit more of a reaction out of her, but hey, you got your rocks off and there is no point for you to stay there anymore. <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png" ><<link "Continue">> <<set $enterFromVoid = true>>\ <<goto "PortalRoom">>\ <</link>>\ </div>\You usually avoid killing the creatures you've fucked, unless you have to, so you decide that leaving is the best course of action, and get ready to return home. The chains will fade into nothingness soon enough, and she will be free to go. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Get ready to return |ReturnDeathclaw5]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTeleport.png"></div>\With the task successfully failed and your balls nice and empty, its time to return home, where you are sure to get a good night sleep. This creature probably wasn't dangerous, <i>probably</i> another of The Eye's missed targets... but you hope she will remember your encounter for long after you are gone. <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png" ><<link "Continue">> <<set $enterFromVoid = true>>\ <<goto "PortalRoom">>\ <</link>>\ </div>\ You give her a gentle pat on the shoulder and get up. @@.playerSpeech;"I'll let you rest. Don't get yourself killed, alright?" @@ Even if that was, precisely, what you should have done. <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png" ><<link "Continue">> <<set $enterFromVoid = true>>\ <<goto "PortalRoom">>\ <</link>>\ </div>\A laser rifle, both alien and useless to your hands. What need would a high hunter have for an energy-based weapon when the sword he wields is so perfectly suited to his needs? [[Go back |Inventory]]<<nobr>> <div class="header-container"> <div class="middle-section">Deathclaw</div> </div> <div class="long-container"> <div class="left-long-container-2"> <<if $deathclawJobState != 0>> <img src="Images/Monsters/DeathclawFixed.png" alt="IMG"> <<else>> <img src="Images/Monsters/DeathclawDark.png" alt="IMG"> <</if>> </div> <div class="right-long-container-2"> @@.titleSmall;STATUS@@ <br><br> <<switch $deathclawJobState >> <<case 0>> <<if $eyeSeeing >=2>> <div class="passCheck"> You can get this contract by talking to Damlon, and then warping from your home. <img src="Images/Icons/Eye.png"></div> <<else>> <div class="failCheck"> You have not yet found this creature, or anything related to her. <img src="Images/Icons/FailEye.png"></div> <</if>> <<case 1>> You have accepted this contract, and you have the travelstone to make the trip. You may warp to her world from the portal room in your home. <<case 2>> You have been close enough to the creature, and you can claim the reward from Damlon. <<case 3>> You have already claimed the reward for this creature. <<case 4>> This should be impossible, what kind of disgusting spagetti code brought us here... <<default>> <</switch>> </div> </div> <div class = "close-Inventory"> <<link "Go Back">> <div class="stat-container"> Go back </div> <<goto Contracts>> <</link>> </div> <</nobr>><<if $metNyxa is false>>\ You make your way to the infirmary, consciously located away from the main hub. Of course, it's still decently close, being part of the same megalithic building, but you still have to go through some sets of stairs, and corridors. It does make sense, somewhat. Nyxa is one of the first ever non-human members of your order... and yeah, while most of them can barely be recognized as anymore, with them having transcended their frail, biological bodies... that's not true for Nyxa. Nyxa was never born as one, and even if her skills as a healer are unrivaled, most of your fellows will never see her the same. How could they, trained to deal with all sorts of creatures and monsters during years? This is why it was considered that for the best interests of everyone really, her little workstation would be placed a bit further. Just enough for it to be easily accessible in case of a serious injury, but isolated enough not to be door-to-door with everyone. <<set $metNyxa to true>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Barge in |BargeInNyxa]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Knock on the door |KnockDoorNyxa]]</div>\ <<else>>\ <<if $angeredNyxa is 2>>\ You don't have any medical emergencies, and you very much doubt Nyxa is interested in having idle chats with you, so you figure it will be better to just not bother her and instead, stay away. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |Hunters Hall]]</div>\ <<else>>\ You yet again make your way to the infirmary and find Nyxa busy as usual, preparing some kind of concoction. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Back so soon? Did you break something?"@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |ContinueNyxaGeneric]]</div>\ <</if>>\ <</if>>\ You take the steel door knocker and hit the metallic door with it twice, waiting for the vixen to acknowledge your presence. You... wait for a moment, and there's no answer. You can't even hear anything... maybe she is not here right now? <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Wait patiently |WaitNyxa][$nyxaPoints +=1]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Barge inside |BargeInNyxa]]</div>\You don't think you would ask for permission to enter Vael's Forge or Orion's workshop. So you simply stride in, the heavy metallic door giving way to your push. It's a nice room. Clean, organized, despite the countless jars, boxes and tools that are neatly stored on the walls, the tables, and pretty much every flat surface... and then, you are also immediately assaulted by the smell of herbs. <<if $eyeSeeing >=1>>\ <div class="passCheck">\ It's so intense and complex you should be pretty much unable to make them out, and yet you manage to identify some. The sharp, slightly sour tang of mint, a faint hint of thyme, and the sweet, flowery scent of lavender... makes you wonder just how Nyxa manages to not be constantly overwhelmed by it. <img src="Images/Icons/Eye.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ <div class="failCheck">\ Its so intense and complex you are pretty much unable to tell anything other than a general smell, pervasive and strong. <img src="Images/Icons/FailEye.png"></div>\ <</if>>\ In the middle of it, you find the vixen herself, leaning over her table, grinding something in a mortar with a pestle, a few books open before her, and a small pot with steaming water next to her. With a nearly imperceptible sigh, the vixen places the bowl down and turns her head towards you, her two fluffy tails twitching behind the armor. <img src="Images/Hunters/NyxaArmored.png" class="resizable-image" alt="NyxaArmored"> You've never had the chance to see how she looks like, not really. Her three tails and long mane are the only physical parts of her you've seen, the rest of her body always hidden behind her suit, as if to make her inhuman appearance less... obvious. It's still obvious, but... you get what she tries. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Well then, welcome... How can I help you?"@@ <<if $awakenedHeal is false>>\ <<if $healSkill >=1>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask for her to teach you some of her skills |TeachMagicNyxa]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedHeal.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask for her to teach you some of her skills |TeachMagicNyxa]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedHeal.png"></div>@@\ <</if>>\ <</if>>\ <<if $askedJobNyxa is 0>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask if she has any job for you |AskJobNyxa][$askedJobNyxa = 1]]</div>\ <<else>>\ <<if $askedJobNyxa is 1>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask if she needs help with anything |AskJobNyxaHelp][$askedJobNyxa = 2]]</div>\ <</if>>\ <</if>>\ <<if $injured is true>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[You were seeking medical attention |TreatInjuryNyxa]]</div>\ <<else>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedCross.png">[[You were seeking medical attention |TreatInjuryNyxa]]</div>@@\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[You were looking to have a chat |ChatNyxaDay1]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Leave |LeaveNyxaWithout]]</div>\You keep waiting, your hand slooowly rising to reach for the knocker again. Before you can grab it, the door is opened from the inside, Nyxa's helmeted face coming to view as she tilts her head from behind the door. Her eyes, hidden beneath the visor, still seem to pierce into yours. <img src="Images/Hunters/NyxaArmored.png" class="resizable-image" alt="NyxaArmored"> @@.nyxaSpeech;"Oh, there is actually someone here. I was just preparing something. Please, do come in."@@ You nod and take a step inside, looking around. It's a nice room. Clean, organized, despite the countless jars, boxes and tools that are neatly stored on the walls, the tables, and pretty much every flat surface... and then, you are also immediately assaulted by the smell of herbs. <<if $eyeSeeing >=1>>\ <div class="passCheck">\ It's so intense and complex you should be pretty much unable to make them out, and yet you manage to identify some. The sharp, slightly sour tang of mint, a faint hint of thyme, and the sweet, flowery scent of lavender... makes you wonder just how Nyxa manages to not be constantly overwhelmed by it. <img src="Images/Icons/Eye.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ <div class="failCheck">\ Its so intense and complex you are pretty much unable to tell anything other than a general smell, pervasive and strong. <img src="Images/Icons/FailEye.png"></div>\ <</if>>\ @@.nyxaSpeech;"You can just enter when you need anything, though I appreciate the courtesy. I didn't even know the door had a knocker."@@ Nyxa's voice carries a gentle humor, though her smirk is lost behind her helmet. You've never had the chance to see how she looks like, not really. Her three tails and long mane are the only physical parts of her you've seen, the rest of her body always hidden behind her suit, as if to make her inhuman appearance less... obvious. Its still obvious, but... you get what she tries. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Now... you needed my help? What is the issue?"@@ <<if $awakenedHeal is false>>\ <<if $healSkill >=1>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask for her to teach you some of her skills |TeachMagicNyxa]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedHeal.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask for her to teach you some of her skills |TeachMagicNyxa]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedHeal.png"></div>@@\ <</if>>\ <</if>>\ <<if $askedJobNyxa is 0>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask if she has any job for you |AskJobNyxa][$askedJobNyxa = 1]]</div>\ <<else>>\ <<if $askedJobNyxa is 1>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask if she needs help with anything |AskJobNyxaHelp][$askedJobNyxa = 2]]</div>\ <</if>>\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[You were looking to have a chat |ChatNyxaDay1]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Leave |LeaveNyxaWithout]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"Actually, I am good. Don't bother, I was just curious about what you were doing."@@ Nyxa tries her best not to look annoyed. She just nods and returns to her task, not saying another word. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |Hunters Hall]]</div>\<<if $nyxaPoints <=1>>\ @@.playerSpeech;"I should get going, thanks for your time."@@ The vixen simply nods, and goes back to her tasks. <<else>>\ <<if $nyxaPoints <= 3>>\ @@.playerSpeech;"I should get going, see you around."@@ @@.nyxaSpeech;"Take care out there, okay?"@@ Seeing your flattered smile, the vixen chuckles a bit and gives you a small, polite wave with her hand. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Not that I particularly care, but you'd be giving me extra work if you get injured."@@ <<else>>\ @@.playerSpeech;"See you soon! I should go, but thanks for your time again"@@ Nyxa seems almost disappointed by your hasty exit, her ears twitching beneath her helmet... or maybe you are just imagining things. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Well, you know where to find me. Try not to get killed."@@ <</if>>\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |Hunters Hall]]</div>\<<if $angeredNyxa is 2>>\ @@.playerSpeech;"I would like to learn your healing techniques, if possible."@@ She tilts her head and gives you a confused, suspicious look. When she sees you are serious, she just lets out a dry chuckle. @@.nyxaSpeech;"My healing techniques? Sure thing. Head into the library, and you will find plenty of books on the topic. You are a bright one, you'll pick up on them quickly." Ignoring the jab, you continue. Nyxa can be quite unpleasant when she wants to... no wonder half the order despises her. @@.playerSpeech;"I'm just looking for something quick, not necessarily deep or complex. Something I can apply in battle, so I can patch myself up and continue the fight."@@ Nyxa shakes her head, still refusing to cooperate. Teaching you to awaken your passive magic potential wouldn't be such a huge deal, but then again... why bother? Such a favor is beyond her assigned duties, and in her eyes, you are just another brutish hunter. @@.nyxaSpeech;"I think I've been quite clear. You can learn on your own, but Im not going to waste my time teaching someone who clearly doesn't respect me."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Seems like her mind is set... |Hunters Hall]]</div>\ <<else>> @@.playerSpeech;" I am quite impressed with the way you treat injuries, and I was hoping I could learn from you. Could you spare some of your time, teach a fellow hunter a few things?"@@ Nyxa eyes you for a moment, considering your request. Not used to being approached for anything other than immediate medical attention, the idea of her teaching a fellow member of the order her skills and knowledge sounds a bit bizarre, if... perhaps, mildly flattering. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Well... it's a first time. The art of healing is a rather complex and time-consuming one, but I think I can give you a good selection of books about the subject, so you can start reading up on the basics."@@ The vixen directs her gaze to the small mountain of books in her cabin, but before she can get and grab one, you interrupt her. @@.playerSpeech;"I'd actually prefer the hands-on approach. I've seen you use your hands to treat injuries, channeling energy through them and mending the skin and muscles, and I'd like to be taught in a similar manner. Something I can use in battle and such."@@ Nyxa lets out a sarcastic chuckle, figuring you wouldn't be willing to sink the thousands of hours of research and practice required to properly learn the skill. @@.nyxaSpeech;"What I do is a form of magic that taps into the natural energy flows of the world to facilitate healing, but as much as I'd like to help... magic is not something that you humans can easily tap into. You seem capable... but I feel the risks outweighs the benefits. Take my word for it, I was born with this... 'gift'."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask about those risks |AskRisksNyxa]]</div>\ <</if>>@@.nyxaSpeech;"I... don't think Im the right person to ask. I just treat the injured and the sick. If you are looking for jobs, then Damlon is probably the right person to talk with."@@ You figured she wouldn't have any jobs to give out, but it couldn't hurt to ask. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |ContinueNyxaGeneric]]</div>\ @@.playerSpeech;"Well, and is... there anything else I can help you with?"@@ Nyxa ponders your question, somewhat surprised and skeptical of your willingness to lend a hand. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Not really... if anything, I should be helping you, <i>High Hunter</i>"@@ She tells you, and even if the sarcasm in her voice is evident, it doesn't sound malicious. @@.playerSpeech;"Just wondering if there was anything I could help with, since I am here and all. Us High Hunters like to take care of the little guys..."@@ Nyxa chuckles a bit. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Little guys huh? You are shorter than me, and our rank is basically the same."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Come on, I didn't mean it like that..."@@ @@.nyxaSpeech;"You know what? Maybe there is something this <i>courageous</i> High Hunter can do for the little guys... I've been meaning to grab a plate of that warm goop they serve for lunch, but I haven't had the time to. Would you mind, o brave and daring one, fetching one for me?"@@ Her tone makes it obvious she is just playing with you, and almost as soon as she is done talking, she turns back to the mortar on her hands, and continues grinding whatever is inside. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Actually go fetch her a plate |GoFetchAPlate][$nyxaPoints +=2]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Kindly deny the request, ask to eat something with her instead |AskNyxaGoEat][$nyxaPoints +=1]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"Oh I dont think we've properly met yet, so I thought I could come over to say hi."@@ <<set $talkedNyxa to true>>\ Nyxa stares at you for a second, her eyes hidden by her visor, and nods slowly. She may think this is some sort of odd trick. @@.nyxaSpeech;"I don't think we did, though I remember having to stitch your leg and back after you got ambushed by that giant centipede. Still, it's... nice meeting you."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"I'd rather forget about it... that was not a pleasant experience."@@ @@.nyxaSpeech;"Who could have guessed. I've heard you've been promoted to a higher rank, so... congratulations. Now you can enjoy the privilege of having your own squire and watching them nearly get devoured by centipedes. Lucky you."@@ You chuckle at that, but by her serious tone, you are not even sure she was joking. @@.playerSpeech;"I prefer working alone, so I doubt it. But it's nice being to work on my own, if I get ambushed by giant centipedes again, this time I'll have no one to blame for getting hurt."@@ The vixen hums, as if the idea of you getting fucked up by giant, venomous centipedes is somehow amusing... <<if $ask1 is false>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask about how she joined the order, being a vixen and all |VixenOrder][$ask1 = true]]</div>\ <</if>>\ <<if $ask2 is false>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask how she is doing with the others |AskDealingOthersNyxa][$ask2 = true]]</div>\ <</if>>\ <<if $ask3 is false>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask about her tails |AskTails][$ask3 = true]]</div>\ <</if>>\ <<if $ask4 is false>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask about the armor she is wearing |AskAboutArmor][$ask4 = true]]</div>\ <</if>>\ <<if $ask5 is false>>\ <<if $eyeSeeing >=1>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask about the scent in the room |AskAboutScent][$ask5 = true]]<div class="numberStat">1</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedEye.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedCross.png">[[Ask about the scent in the room |AskAboutScent]]<div class="numberStat">1</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedEye.png"></div>@@\ <</if>>\ <</if>>\ @@.playerSpeech;"The dining hall is barely a minute away. I don't think you need my assistance for that... but how about you let me accompany you, and we both have a hot plate of goop together?"@@ Nyxa seems slightly surprised by the request, having been the only person to ask such a thing from her, probably. But even if she were to accept; her stomach is rumbling, she'd need to get her helmet off in front of the other hunters, and she isn't particularly pleased by the thought. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Thank you for the offer, but... I think I will pass. I'm not hungry anyways."@@ You shrug, and decide not to press further. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |ContinueNyxaGeneric]]</div>\ Surely she's just kidding; she is certainly not expecting you to play along and do her silly request, but you figure that complying with her rather stupid demand would be a nice way to keep the mood light and playful. Without another word, you storm out of her cabin and head to the dining hall, ignoring the surprised, confused look the vixen gives you. One quick visit later, you are back with her meal, a plate of a homogeneous, extremely nutritive grayish goop that you have eaten for breakfast, lunch, and dinner ever since you joined the order. @@.playerSpeech;"As promised, one plate of the finest, warmest goop. I hope it pleases the... high lady."@@ The vixen looks at the plate, then at you, and then back at the plate, some embarrassment creeping up her cheeks as she realizes you actually took her request seriously. @@.nyxaSpeech;"I... uhm. Well you know I was joking, right? I don't need you to go fetch me food... doesn't a High Hunter have anything more important to do?"@@ @@.playerSpeech; "What is more important than ensuring the health of our members? That you should know."@@ Nyxa rolls her eyes, takes the plate from your hands, and places it on her crowded table. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Right. The health of the order is of the utmost importance.... Thank you I suppose"@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |ContinueNyxaGeneric]]</div>\ @@.playerSpeech;"How come a vixen like you ended up in our order? Not the usual sight around here."@@ You are not sure what kind of reaction you expected, at such a question, but Nyxa seems to take it personally, her tone shifting <i>dramatically</i>. @@.nyxaSpeech;"But of course... why would I be here, instead of in a pen or a zoo? What is the story behind me, the vixen that is allowed to walk the halls of the great Order of the Veilwalkers..."@@ <<if $charismaSkill >=1 && $angeredNyxa is 0>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Tell her that you are sorry; You didn't mean to offend |NoOffenseVixen][$angeredNyxa =1]]<div class="numberStat">1</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedCharisma.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedCross.png">[[Tell her that you are sorry; You didn't mean to offend |NoOffenseVixen]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCharisma.png"></div>@@\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Say nothing |SayNothingVixen][$angeredNyxa = 2]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"That was not at all what I wanted to say! I apologize if my words were rude. I... imagine you get asked questions like these a lot."@@ The vixen shakes her head, her tails twitching behind her. Your apology seems to have a bit of a soothing effect, as Nyxa takes a deep breath, and lets it out in a long, tired sigh. Still, you can tell her perception of you has taken a hit already. How were you supposed to know she was so very sensible to the topic? @@.nyxaSpeech;"Yes... you would be right. I would rather not talk about it."@@ <<set $fuckedUpNyxa = true>>\ <<set $angeredNyxa to 1>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |NyxaBetweenScenes]]</div>\Nyxa pauses for a moment, before continuing. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Now, I'd appreciate it if you'd leave. I've got work to do, so if there is nothing you need, please, do be on your way."@@ <<set $angeredNyxa to 2>> <<set $nyxaState to "angered">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[You would still like to have a chat with her |StillWantChatNyxa]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Leave |LeaveNyxaAfterRude]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"I'd still like to chat with you, if that is alright."@@ The vixen returns her attention to the mortar, and grinds the content with more strength than necessary. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Why don't you go bother someone else? Plenty of fellow humans to annoy, I'm sure."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Leave |LeaveNyxaAfterRude]]</div>\Seeing that you may have struck a nerve, you decide to leave, and bid the vixen goodbye, her only response a non-committal grunt as she wonders why every last one of you humans is so damn insufferable. <<set $nyxaPoints to 0>> <<set $angeredNyxa to 2>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |Hunters Hall]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech; "So, how is life treating you here, among... us?"@@ The vixen stops grinding, and turns her head slightly towards you. You can almost feel the glare she is giving you, as she tries to decipher the true intent behind your words. They don't seem to come out of a place of malice, so she sighs and responds. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Life is tolerable if that is what you mean. Orion has been kind, though I rarely see him. If only the rest had the same courtesy... but that's the price of being unique, isn't it?"@@ She muses, trying to add some humor to her otherwise bleak answer. <<if $nyxaPoints >=2>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Offer her your sympathies |OfferSympathiesNyxa][$nyxaPoints +=2]]</div>\ <<else>> @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedCross.png">[[Offer her your sympathies |OfferSympathiesNyxa]]</div>@@\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Nod along |NodAlong][$nyxaPoints +=1]]</div>\ @@.playerSpeech;"So those tails of yours are real? They seem pretty impressive. All vixen's have them?"@@ Her two tails seem to hide behind her back, and Nyxa's voice drops way lower, almost like a warning growl. You can't see her eyes, but you are sure she is glaring at you with a dangerous intensity. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Oh, I see. My tails are not a topic for your curiosity, and I'd appreciate it if you'd show me the same courtesy as I have shown you."@@ <<if $charismaSkill >=1 && $angeredNyxa is 0>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Tell her that you are sorry; You didn't mean to offend |NoOffenseVixen][$nyxaPoints -=1]]<div class="numberStat">1</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedCharisma.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedCross.png">[[Tell her that you are sorry; You didn't mean to offend |NoOffenseVixen]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCharisma.png"></div>@@\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Say nothing |SayNothingVixen][$nyxaPoints -=3]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"Isn't wearing that armor at all times a bit uncomfortable? It doesn't seem practical to... well you know, there is no danger inside these walls so why bother."@@ Nyxa takes a moment to respond, clearly not too pleased by your curiosity. Still, she keeps her composture and replies, her voice even. @@.nyxaSpeech;"It is a bit uncomfortable, yes. But trust me, not being covered is even more uncomfortable."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Push for an explanation |PushExplanation]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Say that you think her armor is cool |DontPushNyxa][$nyxaPoints +=1]]</div>\You can tell she is getting more and more annoyed, so you decide not to push anymore. It seems she is really sensible to some topics, so better tread carefully. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |NyxaBetweenScenes]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"Come on, I am curious! Is that like... a cultural thing, or?"@@ If Nyxa wasn't particularly amused by the question before, now her displeasure is quite obvious. @@.nyxaSpeech;"It is a cultural thing, you could say. You humans can't seem to avoid gawking at things that don't conform with your ideals. I'd rather wear this armor than getting asked dumb questions each and every time I happen to cross paths with anyone. Though some still manage to find a way..."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |NyxaBetweenScenes][$nyxaPoints -=1]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"That's the impression I've gotten. I'm sorry it is like that, we hunters have been trained to deal with... well, the unusual. It's not an excuse, but, you know. I just hope people get used to you eventually. They'll realize you are a wonderful addition to the team, I'm sure of it!"@@ Nyxa doesn't answer for a moment, but you can tell her posture relaxes a little, even beneath her armor. @@.nyxaSpeech;"I appreciate the kind words. Hopefully your optimism is well-founded. What about you? You don't seem as... cautious or judgmental, compared to the rest of your peers."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[You are more open minded than the rest of your peers |MoreOpenMinded]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[You trust Lauren's judgment |TrustLaurenJudgement]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"Don't get me wrong, I have undergone the same training as everyone else... But, I do believe you should judge people based on their actions, and not their appearances."@@ @@.nyxaSpeech;"...I can understand where they come from. You don't always get the chance to learn the true intentions of the 'unusual'. Is that why you got caught in those centipedes? Wondering if they were the nice, friendly kind?"@@ She chuckles at that, and you appreciate the small jab. It's perhaps a bit too close to the truth, though. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |NyxaBetweenScenes]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"Lauren vouched for you, so to me, you are just another member of the order. And besides... it's not as if the older hunters look anything like humans anymore. You are a lot less weird than them... Vael still gives me nightmares sometimes."@@ Nyxa laughs at that, a soft, gentle sound. @@.nyxaSpeech; "True! I could hardly believe my eyes the first time I saw him. He forged my armor though, and for that I am grateful. Surprisingly light and flexible, makes it less of a pain to move around in."@@ She raises her arms, flexing and stretching them as if to emphasize her point. @@.nyxaSpeech; "Orion... it makes more sense, but Vael? Why did he choose such a dreadsome appearance, with her talent he should be able to forge his body in any shape he wants..."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Honestly, I'm sure he just like messing with us. He has a strange sense of humor."@@ Nyxa laughs again, her tone amused, but also a bit appreciative. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |NyxaBetweenScenes]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"Is that lavender I smell? Quite the fragrance you have around here..."@@ Nyxa nods slowly, and continues grinding whatever is inside her mortar. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Indeed, my own little touches. I think it makes the whole room a more pleasant place to be in... the mixture of scents. Soothing and relaxing, or at least they should be. You must have a decently sensitive nose if you can pick up the lavender from all the others."@@ You are not even sure how you are capable of noticing the faint floral smell amongst the other stronger, muskier ones, but you suppose that's a topic for another time. @@.playerSpeech;"It's perhaps... a bit too much? It's borderline overwhelming for me, isn't it a bit too much for you? Sensitive nose or not, Im sure mine can't compare to yours..."@@ @@.nyxaSpeech;"You would be right... I had to amp it up quite a bit as no one seemed to notice, but now I'm regretting my decision. You are the first one to point it out, so... I think its not worth the effort, or the headaches."@@ She pauses for a moment, a hand on her covered muzzle as she ponders if it's alright to ask you for your opinion. The main point of the strong, floral fragrance was to create a more appealing environment for her patients, and so far she's had no luck with it. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Mind if I ask... what scents do you humans usually find pleasant?"@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Say lavender is fine |LavenderFine]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Sulphur and brimstone |SulphurBrimstone]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Her scent alone would be enough |ScentNyxa]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"Oh, nothing like that. Lavender is quite the lovely scent, the problem I'd say comes with the overwhelming quantity, and the other many different scents competing for the spotlight."@@ @@.nyxaSpeech;"I'll go back to something simpler then... if no one notices anyways, then at least I'll preserve my sense of smell. And yours, I suppose."@@ The vixen seems satisfied with your answer, and grinds some of the contents of her mortar with idle thoughts. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |NyxaBetweenScenes][$nyxaPoints +=1]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;" There is nothing wrong with the current combination, but perhaps something more suited to the dark nature of the order would suit us better. Something like sulphur, or maybe brimstone."@@ The vixen pauses her movements and considers your odd request. How in the world did you even get that idea? @@.nyxaSpeech;"...sulphur and brimstone, you say? I... well, I will keep it mind. May be too much for me but if it's more suitable for your kind, I might have a go at it."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |NyxaBetweenScenes][$nyxaPoints +=1]]</div>\You could have answered with something reasonable. The horny little shit you are, instead you tell the vixen that her scent alone would suffice, which catches her off-guard. She blinks twice, not even sure how to react to such a sudden, unabashedly honest and perverted comment. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Wha... what did you just say?"@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Double Down |DoubleDownScent][$angeredNyxa =2]]</div>\ <<if $charismaSkill >=1>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Try to save it by saying you thought she was using some kind of perfume |SavePerfume]]<div class="numberStat">1</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedCharisma.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedCross.png">[Try to save it by saying you thought she was using some kind of perfume |NoOffenseVixen]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCharisma.png"></div>@@\ <</if>>\ You are quick to respond, and you try to dig yourself out of the hole by telling the shocked vixen you thought she was using some sort of perfume or cologne, even feigning surprise when she denies it. @@.nyxaSpeech;"I... I'm not using any kind of scented product. Do I really..."@@ Now, you can tell she is getting a bit embarrassed, thinking that for you to have noticed, it must mean her scent is quite noticeable. Is it because of the armour? @@.playerSpeech;"Oh, I really thought... so sorry, didn't mean to come off like that. I was just trying to pay you a compliment."@@ You manage to salvage the situation, and after a moment of awkward silence, the vixen clears her throat and tries to move the conversation along. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Let's... just forget about this, shall we?"@@ One thing is certain, she is making sure her armor stays well ventilated from now on... altough her scent is barely noticeable even up close. The master of gaslighting, you are. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |NyxaBetweenScenes]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"No need to be shy about it, your natural musk is very... pleasing to the nose. If only you didn't cover yourself so much, I'm sure everyone-"@@ Nyxa doesn't let you continue, interrupting you with an angry growl. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Shut up. Just shut up. I can't belive I heard it right the first time... get out. Get out before I punch your face in."@@ She's... angry, and you have the feeling she means her words, so you back away and quickly walk out the door, her threatening, menacing stare following you as you leave her cabin. And here you thought you were giving her a compliment... don't vixens like to hear about their natural, alluring scents? <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Leave |LeaveNyxaAfterRude]]</div>\You nod along... you can imagine what she says isn't exactly a lie, so you decide not to push it any further in case she find the topic unpleasant. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |NyxaBetweenScenes]]</div>\<<if $nyxaPoints >= 4>>\ Getting gradually more comfortable with your presence, the vixen begins to relax and let her guard down a little, somewhat happy to get someone half decent to have a conversation with. She is not exactly the most social creature, but being surrounded by mostly humans who kinda despise her, the chance to have a conversation with someone is a nice change. You keep rambling for a while, not about anything in particular. Just sharing stories about your time in the order and occasionally asking her something, until Nyxa realizes you've been there for almost an hour and has to gently tell you she has a lot of work to do. @@.playerSpeech;"Of course, I don't want to be a bother. Nice meeting you, Nyxa, I'll be seeing you around."@@ @@.nyxaSpeech;"You are not a bother, but I have to get this done... and, likewise. Perhaps we can talk another time?"@@ You smile and nod, before bidding her goodbye and leaving her to her own devices. Even if your conversation didn't amount to much; mostly just exchanging pleasantries and a couple of stories, you feel like you made a good impression on the vixen. <<set $nyxaState to "niceDay1">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |Hunters Hall]]</div>\ <<else>>\ <<if $nyxaPoints <= 0>>\ There is a moment of silence after the little exchange, and even if she doesn't seem particularly thrilled to keep talking, Nyxa doesn't make any attempt to shoo you away, so you could hang around just a bit longer... <<else>>\ <<if $fuckedUpNyxa is true>> <<set $fuckedUpNyxa = false>>\ Despite the little fuckup, it would seem Nyxa is willing to somewhat tolerate your presence... you figure you'll have to be more careful as you had heard she had very short temper, it seems some seemingly innocent topics are completely off-limits. <<else>>\ You can tell that the vixen is somewhat warming up to your presence, and although her face remains concealed beneath her helmet, there is a certain lightness to her posture that makes her seem more relaxed, less tense. A casual, friendly chat is not something she gets to have too often. <</if>>\ <</if>>\ <<if $ask1 is false>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask about how she joined the order, being a vixen and all |VixenOrder][$ask1 = true]]</div>\ <</if>>\ <<if $ask2 is false>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask how she is doing with the others |AskDealingOthersNyxa][$ask2 = true]]</div>\ <</if>>\ <<if $ask3 is false>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask about her tails |AskTails][$ask3 = true]]</div>\ <</if>>\ <<if $ask4 is false>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask about the armor she is wearing |AskAboutArmor][$ask4 = true]]</div>\ <</if>>\ <<if $ask5 is false>>\ <<if $eyeSeeing >=1>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask about the scent in the room |AskAboutScent][$ask5 = true]]<div class="numberStat">1</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedEye.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedCross.png">[[Ask about the scent in the room |AskAboutScent]]<div class="numberStat">1</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedEye.png"></div>@@\ <</if>>\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Back |ContinueNyxaGeneric]]</div>\ <</if>>\<img src="Images/Hunters/NyxaArmored.png" class="resizable-image" alt="NyxaArmored"> <<if $awakenedHeal is false>>\ <<if $healSkill >=1>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask for her to teach you some of her skills |TeachMagicNyxa]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedHeal.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask for her to teach you some of her skills |TeachMagicNyxa]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedHeal.png"></div>@@\ <</if>>\ <</if>>\ <<if $askedJobNyxa is 0>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask if she has any job for you |AskJobNyxa][$askedJobNyxa = 1]]</div>\ <<else>>\ <<if $askedJobNyxa is 1>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask if she needs help with anything |AskJobNyxaHelp][$askedJobNyxa = 2]]</div>\ <</if>>\ <</if>>\ <<if $talkedNyxa is false>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[You were looking to have a chat |ChatNyxaDay1]]</div>\ <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[You were looking to have a chat |NyxaBetweenScenes]]</div>\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Leave |LeaveNyxa]]</div>\<<if $nyxaDay2State == "nothing">> You make your way to the infirmary, looking forward to seeing the vixen once more. You aren't sure what's the deal with her, but... she's a bit of a breath of fresh air. This time however, as you make your way through the hallway that connects the infirmary, you hear Nyxa's somewhat familiar voice, as well as two other ones and when you round the corner, you spot her on the doorframe, having what appears to be a heated conversation with a couple of fellow hunters. You stay out of sight and listen in before deciding whether you should get involved or not. @@.nyxaSpeech;"For the last time, I'm <i>not</i> doing this again. It would be dangerous at this point. So take whatever complaint you have to Lauren, I'm sure she will tell you to go fuck yourselves in a much more polite manner than myself."@@ One of the hunter snorts, and looks at the vixen with disdain. @@.hunterSpeech;"What we need is a proper healer. Someone who knows how to heal and isn't going to put us at risk. This is a clear example of what happens when a fucking animal is given the task of healing our kind."@@ He points at his face, and you can see a scar that encompasses a large portion of his cheek, stretching all the way down his neck. It looks properly treated, but it is still a rather ugly scar. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Call me that again and I'll leave a much bigger scar next time, you disgusting, ungrateful sack of shit. I saved your life, do not forget yourself."@@ The hunter laughs before taking a step forward, invading her already disturbed personal space and then doubling down with an accusatory finger to the chest. @@.hunterSpeech;"You expect me to lick your boots for doing your fucking job? I risk my life everyday to deal with abominations such as yourself, the least you can do is treat my wounds, and instead of that, I get an amateur trying to play doctor."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Step in |StepInNyxa2]]</div>\ <<else>> <<if $nyxaDay2State == "hunter">> You are not exactly welcome in Nyxa's room, and you don't really have any medical emergencies or issues to bother her with... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |Hunters Hall]]</div>\ <<else>> Nyxa should be quite busy right now... you could come visit her again tomorrow, as it would seem she wouldn't mind the company. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |Hunters Hall]]</div>\ <</if>> <</if>> Having seen enough to understand what is going on, you step in and intervene before the situation escalates any further. If they reach for their weapons, there will be blood, and that's something you'd rather avoid. You try to play it somewhat casually, so that your intervention doesn't come across as hostile, and you don't seem like you are taking sides yet. @@.playerSpeech;"Hey, hey. What is going on here?"@@ The hunter that was previously accosting Nyxa, steps away from her and gives you a sideways look. @@.hunterSpeech;"This animal here... Look at the scar she left on my face!"@@ @@.nyxaSpeech;"You were brought to me with your neck torn open. You are lucky that scar is all you have. Besides, you can hardly get anymore hideous, so what's the problem?"@@ @@.hunterSpeech;"Listen you fucking-"@@ He quickly turns back to her, using all his self-restraint to avoid slamming her against the wall. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Side with Nyxa |SideNyxa]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Side with the hunters |SideHunters]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"Alright, I've heard enough. This is no place for a dispute, and less one about something as petty as cosmetic damage. You are still breathing, aren't you? Then get over it, and be thankful Nyxa was there to patch you up."@@ The hunter is about to argue, but your harsh look and the hand gripping his shoulder stops him. He is no doubt angered, but not stupid enough to try and challenge the healer of the order and a High Hunter at the same time. It's easy to get away with bullying the vixen, but you are a different matter... He huffs, and shakes his head in frustration. @@.hunterSpeech;"I can't belive you'd defend an animal over one of your own. Do you think our previous healer would have done such a shitty job? We need to start looking for a proper replacement, and soon. I can't trust a... beast to fix my wounds."@@ The hunter spits on the ground before leaving, not before bumping into you as he makes his way out. The other one follows closely behind, and you watch as they leave, both of them still bickering and arguing in hushed tones. You turn towards Nyxa now, and even underneath the armor, you can tell she is somewhat shaken; her breathing a bit heavy and the fur on her tails bristling. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Unvelibeable... those bastards. You have no idea in what state he was when I received him... It's a miracle he isn't five feet underground."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Don't mind them, Nyxa. I've had the displeasure of working with them in the past, they are just a couple of assholes. Don't let them get to you."@@ You pat her on the shoulder pad and offer her a reassuring smile, which seems to help, but only a little. The vixen sighs and shakes her head. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Yes... I just wish it was the exception and not the norm."@@ She sounds somewhat defeated, despite having handled herself decently well. She walks back to her work table and waits for you to follow before closing the door behind her. @@.nyxaSpeech;"I... suppose you were here for a reason. Or did you just overhear the argument and decide to get involved?"@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[The truth; You just came here to see her |CameSeeNyxa]]</div>\You don't know much about the situation, but the scar on the hunter's face and neck and Nyxa's attitude is enough to paint a pretty clear picture. Your previous healer wouldn't have made such a shoddy job, that is a fact. @@.playerSpeech;"Calm down, calm down. There is no point in reaching for your blades. The scar however is unacceptable for what's supposed to be the most skilled healer of the order, so you should at least quit the attitude and apologize for such a poor performance."@@ The hunter nods in agreement and points towards his scar. @@.hunterSpeech;"See? See how the wound closed so unevenly? How my neck is crooked now? This is a disgrace!"@@ It takes a moment for Nyxa to process your words, and she looks at you in surprise and disbelief. @@.nyxaSpeech;"W-what? You... you are aware of the miracle I had to perform here, right? This is the best this human could have asked for!"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Your best isn't good enough, then. I would be upset too if this is the result I got after paying with my own blood. I think a formal apology is the least you can do."@@ The armored vulpine looks at you for a moment, then back at the hunter, and then back at you again, her tails stiff behind her back. She looks quite crushed, even beneath all that concealing metal. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Get lost... you two"@@ She takes a step back, ready to close the door in your faces and shut herself inside. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Hold the door open; You are not letting go until she apologizes |ApologizeNyxa]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Just let go |LetGo]]</div>\ <<set $nyxaState to "angered">>\ <<set $angeredNyxa to 2>>@@.playerSpeech;"Ah... Well, I just came to visit, see how you were doing."@@ Nyxa is taken aback, but her tone remains mostly unchanged, her surprise only barely seeping through. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Oh... Well, I appreciate the gesture. Things have been mostly the same... Besides... Well, besides the encounter with the two gentlemen earlier."@@ She sighs and looks at the floor, clearly not over what happened. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Offer her a drink to cheer her up |CheerUpDrink]]</div>\ <<if $flowerItem is true>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Give her a little gift; The bunch of flowers you picked up before |CheerUpFlowers]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedItem.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Give her a little gift; The bunch of flowers you picked up before |CheerUpFlowers]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedItem.png"></div>@@\ <</if>> <<set $nyxaState to "niceDay2">>\@@.playerSpeech;"I... uh... I'm not sure if you are the type, but, do you drink? Perhaps a glass of something would help you get your mind off of things."@@ The vixen takes a moment to reply, clearly considering your offer, before politely declining it. @@.nyxaSpeech;"I shouldn't... I have trouble metabolizing alcohol, and getting drunk would be a recipe for disaster. But I... thanks."@@ Feeling a bit awkward now, Nyxa continues, trying not to seem ungrateful of the offer. @@.nyxaSpeech;"If you'd like... I could offer you some herbal tea. I usually make it just for me, but I don't mind sharing some. It's relaxing, helps me stay calm."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Accept her offer instead |AcceptNyxaOffer]]</div>\Herbal tea doesn't sound as immediately appealing as a nice pint, but you figure you could use the chance to get to know the vixen better, and accept her offer, hoping she doesn't think you are trying to force the issue. But the vixen seems happy to be able to share her brew, and sets a couple of cups on her table before starting to boil some water and get the ingredients ready. You sit and wait patiently, chatting a bit about whatever comes to mind, not particularly focusing on anything. She must have prepared this thing more than a couple times, as you watch her prepare the mix with practiced ease, almost without even paying attention to what she is doing. A few minutes later, the vixen hands you a steaming cup of some kind of brown beverage, the aroma strong and pungent. @@.nyxaSpeech;"I hope you like it. It's a bit of an acquired taste, so if you don't like it, don't feel pressured to drink it..."@@ You chuckle, finding her sudden timidity quite endearing, and bring the cup to your lips, sipping at the hot beverage. Meanwhile, Nyxa stays with her cup firmly grasped in both hands, watching intently for your reaction. And... you can confidently say is the worst beverage you've ever had. The taste is bitter, and a bit sour. It tastes like grass, and not in the good, earthy way. Just getting that sip down is a struggle, and you have to swallow a couple times and force yourself not to gag.
