Nyxa looks at you expectantly, waiting for you to say something. @@.nyxaSpeech;"D-do you like it?"@@ <<if $charismaSkill >=3>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Tell her that you like it, and force yourself to take another sip |LikeNyxaDrink][$nyxaPoints +=1]]<div class="numberStat">3</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedCharisma.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedCross.png">[[Tell her that you like it, and force yourself to take another sip |LikeNyxaDrink]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCharisma.png"></div>@@\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[You can't lie, the beverage isn't exactly enjoyable |DontLikeNyxaDrink]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"Its... a bit strong, but I think I like it."@@ You take another sip, cursing internally as your tastebuds are once again assaulted by the foul beverage. @@.playerSpeech;"What... is it exactly?"@@ It can't only be a mix of herbs, not all of them could possibly make the concoction taste like this. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Im glad you like it! It's a recipe passed down from my mother, it reminds me of home every time I have it. The recipe... perhaps I'll tell you someday. Its a bit of a family secret."@@ You don't think you need to know the recipe. In fact, the world may be a better place if no one ever tried to recreate it again. @@.playerSpeech;"Ahhh I see. That is a shame, but I hope one day I prove worthy of the secret recipe."@@ You smile, concealing the pain from the third sip that soon disappears down your gullet. The glass isn't particularly large, and yet its being such an uphill battle chore to drink it. Nyxa chuckles in return, and removes a small steel panel from her helmet, creating up the tiniest opening for her to be able to drink without exposing her face. She drinks it up in one go, not making a single sound of discomfort as the no doubt burning beverage goes down her throat. @@.nyxaSpeech;"I think I needed this... Thank you for sticking up for me, by the way. I... don't usually handle situations like that too well."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Don't mention it. Those two deserve a kick to the face, hopefully the next time they get badly injured they don't make it back here."@@ @@.nyxaSpeech;" It would serve them right, yes."@@ With the last of your drink downed, you leave the empty glass on the table and nod, feeling quite warm and relaxed. Nyxa was right, this brew has a rather calming effect, and you find yourself in a much better mood after finishing it... even if the process of getting there does NOT feel worth it. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |ContinueChattingNyxa1]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"I... um... Wow, I can really see how this would be an acquired taste."@@ The vixen let's ot a nervous chuckle, if your words aren't enough to give her the answer, your grimace certainly does. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Y-you don't need to finish it if you don't like it, it's okay! I just figured it's a traditional recipe, and you are the guest, and I wanted to share, but if it's not good..."@@ You try and calm her, not wanting her to feel bad for her efforts. @@.playerSpeech;"Hey, hey, it's okay, it's okay! I can appreciate the effort, and I'm grateful you offered. If I am honest, my taste is... pretty basic, and this is a bit too strong for me. Maybe it'll get better the second time..."@@ A bit hesitatingly, you bring the cup to your lips and take the smallest sip possible, wincing in disgust as the brew touches your tongue. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Come on, cut it off! No need to torture yourself, I'll get us something else."@@ The vixen is quick to retrieve the cup from your hand, and she sets it aside along with hers. She looks a bit embarrassed at the failed attempt at being a good host, and wonders why she didn't just offer you a regular old drink instead, like a normal person. Her eyes dart quickly between the cabinets, looking for something to offer you... everything that she has could prove as disgusting to you if not even more, so, to avoid the embarrassment of brewing another drink that you'll hate, she comes up with a glass of water. She hands you the glass, and then looks down, not meeting your eyes. Seeing she is a bit dejected, you take the glass and sip at it, letting the cool liquid run down your throat, cleansing the previous taste away. @@.nyxaSpeech;"There's nothing fancy about it, but... it's cold, at least."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Hey, don't worry. I'm here for the company, the drinks were just an added bonus."@@ Nyxa's tails move just a tiny bit, before she gets ahold of them and freezes them in place. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Well... Thanks. Haven't had many guests, and half the people that I've met don't consume any kind of food or drink, so... I'm not very knowledgeable on the subject."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Yeah, poor Vael... I'm sure he is dying to have a taste of that sweet, sweet tea."@@ @@.nyxaSpeech;"Hey! Come on, it's not that bad!" You both laugh, the vixen finally relaxing after the failed drink attempt.... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |ContinueChattingNyxa2]]</div>\ Wanting to cheer her up, you figure that a tiny present may be exactly what she needs to get her spirits back... and what better than the bouquet of flowers you picked up earlier? Some of them are already wilting, others have been a bit damaged by your lack of care, but they still look quite nice, and will probably perk up a bit after some water and a nice place to rest. @@.playerSpeech;"Oh, I also brought you a little gift... They aren't much, but I thought they looked nice, and they reminded me of you and your cabin."@@ They are indeed not much, and they'll likely wilt in a few days, but, the gesture alone is enough to put a smile on her face.... even if you can't see it. The smallest gestures of appreciation can sometimes mean a lot, and for someone as lonely and mistreated as Nyxa, this simple offering means a lot more than the sum of its parts. If for no other reason, this is the first time she has gotten a gift from anyone since she was brought here. Trying her best not to appear too overly emotional, and grateful for the helmet covering her face, she stammers a reply. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Th-this is... Um... Thanks, they are very beautiful. I'll have to find a nice place to put them..."@@ Unable to meet your eyes, the vixen takes the bouquet in one of her clawed gauntlets and looks around for a suitable bottle to serve as a makeshift vase. There are plenty of those; the infirmary is full of various containers, but the search provides a suitable excuse for her to turn around and hide her embarrassment. She knows she shouldn't feel so overwhelmed by something so small, but... As Nyxa turns and chooses a bottle that seems fitting, you catch a glimpse of her tails, wagging slightly, betraying her emotions. @@.nyxaSpeech;"This should... suffice."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"It should <i>suffice</i> as well. Glad you like them. I'm sorry they aren't in better condition, but things get a bit... messy when traveling between worlds."@@ Nyxa waves it off, unconcerned about the minor damage and flaws. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Not an issue, don't worry. I think they'll be fine with a bit of care... plants can be healed as well."@@ She then hovers her palm over the bouquet, and a dim light emerges from her gauntlet, enveloping the flowers and giving them a healthy, lively appearance. In the blink of an eye, the plants are completely repaired, looking as fresh and beautiful as when you found them. @@.playerSpeech;"Whoa. Now I can see why Lauren chose you"@@ @@.nyxaSpeech;"Come on now, don't be silly. Anyone could do this..."@@ Still, you can tell the compliment was well received, and the vixen seems to stand a bit taller, a hint of pride in her tone. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |ContinueChattingNyxa3][$nyxaPoints +=1]]</div>\You chat a bit more, and the conversation about plants and healing spells eventually leads to more chaotic, various topics. Time seems to fly as the two of you enjoy the company of each other, and before long, it's quite late... still, neither of you is particularly interested in calling it a night. Suddenly though, the peaceful conversation comes to an end. The door to the infirmary is kicked open, and a badly hurt hunter is carried by two of his companions, who rush him in, desperate and out of breath. @@.nyxaSpeech;"By the stars, what happened?"@@ Nyxa immediately snaps into a professional attitude, rushing over to tend to the patient, whose wounds look quite severe. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Get him to the table, I'll see what I can do..."@@ As the two hunters gently put their friend down on the operating table, Nyxa turns to you for a moment, her eyes apologetic. @@.nyxaSpeech;"We'll... um, have to cut the conversation short. Work calls... but thanks for the visit. Really."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Its okay. Good luck and take care."@@ She nods and focuses on her task, turning to her new patient. Even with what has happened just hours before, she'll certainly try her best to do a good job. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |Hunters Hall]]</div>\Even with the cups empty, you keep chatting for quite a while, Nyxa really needs to let out some steam, and you are happy to lend an ear, and some comforting words where needed. It's a shame the vixen feels she has to cover her face all the time, you can only imagine how much nicer she looks under all the metal. Suddenly though, the peaceful conversation comes to an end. The door to the infirmary is kicked open, and a badly hurt hunter is carried by two of his companions, who rush him in, desperate and out of breath. @@.nyxaSpeech;"By the stars, what happened?"@@ Nyxa immediately snaps into a professional attitude, rushing over to tend to the patient, whose wounds look quite severe. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Get him to the table, I'll see what I can do..."@@ As the two hunters gently put their friend down on the operating table, Nyxa turns to you for a moment, her eyes apologetic. @@.nyxaSpeech;"We'll... um, have to cut the conversation short. Work calls... but thanks for the visit. Really."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Its okay. Good luck and take care."@@ She nods and focuses on her task, turning to her new patient. Even with what has happened just hours before, she'll certainly try her best to do a good job. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Perhaps we can... have another drink someday? I... can make more tea, if you want. I don't mind."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Agree to meet her for drinks another day and leave |LeaveNyxaDrinksNice]]</div>\You keep chatting for quite a while, Nyxa really needs to let out some steam, and you are happy to lend an ear, and some comforting words where needed. It's a shame the vixen feels she has to cover her face all the time, you can only imagine how much nicer she looks under all the metal. Suddenly though, the peaceful conversation comes to an end. The door to the infirmary is kicked open, and a badly hurt hunter is carried by two of his companions, who rush him in, desperate and out of breath. @@.nyxaSpeech;"By the stars, what happened?"@@ Nyxa immediately snaps into a professional attitude, rushing over to tend to the patient, whose wounds look quite severe. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Get him to the table, I'll see what I can do..."@@ As the two hunters gently put their friend down on the operating table, Nyxa turns to you for a moment, her eyes apologetic. @@.nyxaSpeech;"We'll... um, have to cut the conversation short. Work calls... but thanks for the visit. Really."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Its okay. Good luck and take care."@@ She nods and focuses on her task, turning to her new patient. Even with what has happened just hours before, she'll certainly try her best to do a good job. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |Hunters Hall]]</div>\You smile, and nod, screaming internally at the prospect of more of this disgusting brew, but willing to go through it again to see her. @@.playerSpeech;"I'll hold you up to that."@@ Nyxa smiles and nods, bidding you farewell and getting back to her work, her mind a lot more calm and composed now despite the coming storm. <<set $nyxaDay2State to "happy">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |Hunters Hall]]</div>\Before Nyxa has a chance to retreat, you catch the door with your hand, preventing her from shutting it and then easily pushing it all the way open. You confront her again, and this time, you are not so gentle. @@.playerSpeech;"No. This man <i>deserves</i> an apology, and you are not getting away with this catastrophic mistake before you give him one. Apologize."@@ You say firmly, standing your ground. @@.nyxaSpeech;"A-are you serious? I saved his life! I..."@@ You are having none of it, and Nyxa realizes that you are not leaving her alone until she does as she is told. She may have the knowledge and experience, but her strength is a joke compared to yours, and she is not closing the door until you let her. @@.hunterSpeech;"You heard $playerName. Apologize, you fucking animal."@@ Defeated, Nyxa finally gives in and lets out a shaky sigh, tails stiff and low behind her. @@.nyxaSpeech;"I'm sorry, human. My... performance wasn't the best, and I will try and do a better job next time. I apologize for my incompetence."@@ The hunter is content with her reply and seems happy to leave it at that. @@.hunterSpeech;"Your performance was shit, that's what it was. Hopefully, you won't last much longer, and we'll get a real healer to take your place."@@ He spits at her feet before giving her his back. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |ApologizeNyxa2][$reputation +=1]]</div>\You don't bother stopping her, your main objective was to avoid things getting physical. @@.hunterSpeech;"Yeah that's right, scamper with your tails between your legs."@@ He spits at the floor, clearly still upset, and then walks off, patting you on the shoulder as he passes by. @@.playerSpeech;"Glad you didn't kill her."@@ The man scoffs and shakes his head. @@.hunterSpeech;"I know I can't. But you have no idea how much I wanted to when I found out the mess she left me with. I hope Lauren realizes that this creature is not worthy of her title and puts her down for good..."@@ You shrug, not really agreeing or disagreeing. With that, the three of you walk away, and continue with your respective paths. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |Hunters Hall]]</div>\Nyxa finally closes the door and retreats inside, and you and the other two hunters make your way back to the main hall. @@.hunterSpeech;"An apology was the least she could do. Glad you were here."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Hopefully that scar won't get in the way of your hunts."@@ The hunter nods, and the three of you walk away... <div class="passCheck">\ +1 <img src="Images/Icons/Reputation.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |Hunters Hall]]</div>\<<switch $nyxaState>>\ <<case "angered">>\ You make your way to the infirmary, and sure enough, Nyxa is there to receive you. She does so quite coldly, with nothing more than a polite, almost mechanical greeting. She knows she has no choice but to comply with your presence, but she doesn't have to like it, and her displeasure is obvious in her body language. @@.nyxaSpeech;"So. What can I help you with, hunter?"@@ <img src="Images/Hunters/NyxaArmored.png" class="resizable-image" alt="NyxaArmored"> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[You were looking to have a chat |ChatNyxaGenericAngered]]</div>\ <<case "neutral">>\ You make your way to the infirmary, and sure enough, Nyxa is there to receive you. <img src="Images/Hunters/NyxaArmored.png" class="resizable-image" alt="NyxaArmored"> @@.nyxaSpeech;"Oh, hello. What is it this time?" <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[You were looking to have a chat |ChatNyxaGenericNeutral]]</div>\ <<default>>\ You yet again make your way to the infirmary and find Nyxa busy as usual, preparing some kind of concoction. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Back so soon? Did you break something?"@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[You were looking to have a chat |ChatNyxaGenericNeutral]]</div>\ <</switch>>\ <<if $awakenedHeal is false>>\ <<if $healSkill >=1>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask for her to teach you some of her skills |TeachMagicNyxa]]<div class="numberStat">1</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedHeal.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask for her to teach you some of her skills |TeachMagicNyxa]]<div class="numberStat">1</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedHeal.png"></div>@@\ <</if>>\ <</if>>\ <<if $fromPortal is false>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Leave |Hunters Hall]]</div> <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Leave |PortalRoom]]</div> <</if>>\ @@.playerSpeech;"Why is that so? What are those risks?"@@ @@.nyxaSpeech;"Humans are naturally born without an affinity to magic. You hunters have a sliver of it, but it is a small, weak spark."@@ Seeing your look of confused disappointment, she goes on, trying to find the right words. @@.nyxaSpeech;"It is not necessarily a bad thing. Those that 'enjoy' a better connection with the weave are more prone to be corrupted by it... Traveling between worlds as much as you do with a bright, pure spark would surely attract unwanted attention..."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go through |GoThroughNyxaMagic]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[On second thought... |ReconsiderNyxa]]</div>\Despite her advice, you decide to try your luck anyway and ask her once again if she could teach you. @@.playerSpeech;"Even with those risks, I would still like to try and learn something. Whatever lies in the cold void between the worlds won't have the chance to come for me if I bleed out first. I would still like it if you could teach me something."@@ She eyes you for a moment, considering her options. Eventually, she nods, seeing that her attempts at dissuading you from the idea are falling on deaf ears. @@.nyxaSpech;"Very well. But don't say I didn't warn you."@@ Nyxa turns around, eyeing the shelves in her cabin, and starts rummaging through them. @@.nyxaSpeech;"As I said, you hunters are capable of tapping into your magic, even if it's weak. I'll try to concoct a potion that will allow you to better access it."@@ For the next hour, you watch the armored vulpine as she moves about, picking up roots and plants, crushing and boiling them, and mixing them into an odd brew. You just let her work, not daring to interrupt. Finally, after much grinding and boiling, Nyxa produces a single, tiny vial. Something so insignificant seems a bit too small and inadequate for such an extensive preparation. She holds the glass container up and examines it against the light. @@.nyxaSpech;"I can't promise results, but if you drink this, you should be able to access some of your natural healing abilities. Or die."@@ You chuckle at that. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Oh it's not entirely a joke. The side effects of such a brew can be quite severe... You'll be feeling them quite soon."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Side effects? Like what?"@@ Nyxa's lips form into a smirk beneath her helmet. @@.nyxaSpeech;"I'm not sure, I haven't done this before. Ever. But if you are still willing to take the risk... Then drink it here. If something goes wrong, I'll be able to help."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Drink the potion |DrinkMagicPotionHeal]]</div>\She has a point, you don't want to get the attention of anything... eldritch. So you quickly push away the idea, not wanting to put yourself at risk. No point in trying to awaken some of your latent power if it means you won't be able to use it regardless... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |Hunters Hall]]</div>\Nothing Nyxa could have said would have convinced you to reconsider your choice, for that choice was already made. You take the vial from Nyxa's clawed gauntlet with a speed that surprises the fox, and without hesitation, you gulp down its contents, feeling the strange, bitter taste spread all over your mouth. She watches you curiously, ready to intervene at any sign of trouble. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Sure, sure..."@@ At first, nothing happens. The small concoction tastes terribly and feels heavy and thick on your tongue, but other than that, it seems harmless enough. After a minute, however, the effects start to show, and you start feeling... different. First, you begin to sweat, profusely. @@.playerSpeech;"Ugh. Feeling... kinda hot here."@@ The heat continues to increase, and a few seconds later, the cold air of the infirmary suddenly feels scorching and unbearable. You fall unconscious then, a quick, sudden event... as if a candle had just been blown out. Nyxa was ready for that, and quickly catches you before your head hits the hard stone floor... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |DrinkMagicPotionHeal2]]</div>\You dream of a bright light, a light that blinds and burns, but a light that also comforts and soothes. It swirls and shifts, forming a series of undefined, blurry shapes, shapes that make no sense and yet somehow mean so much. Your heart beats fast and loud, and with each thump, the shapes become sharper and clearer, and the light becomes brighter, until you find yourself staring into a white void, a void that speaks. @@.cosmicSpeech;"You call, and I answer."@@ A complete dichotomy with the warm light surrounding you, the voice is cold as ice, and it makes you shiver, goosebumps running across your skin. @@.cosmicSpeech;"And yet... this is not your place, human. You have no claim, no right... and yet you seek. What sets you apart? What makes you believe this... artificial bond, this false link, makes you worthy of the power you desire?"@@ The voice falls silent then, and you realize that its somehow waiting for an answer. All of this is probably just a fever dream of yours, but... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Stay silent |StaySilentAwakenMagic]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[You desire this power to be able to better defend yourself in your hunts |ProtectYourselfAwaken]]</div>\ <<if $karma >= 2>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[You desire this power to protect those around you |KarmaAwakenMagic]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedKarma.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedCross.png">[[You desire this power to protect those around you |KarmaAwakenMagic]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedKarma.png"></div>@@\ <</if>>\You stay silent, having no idea of how to respond, or if you should. The silence persists, and the light slowly fades away, becoming a small, white dot, and then a speck of dust, and then it is gone. When it is, you feel the ground beneath you again, and Nyxa frantically channeling her magic into your body, trying to keep you alive. @@.nyxaSpeech;"$playerName! Wake up! Shit..."@@ You snap awake, and the armored vulpine sighs in relief. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Good. I was starting to think you were done for. I didn't think it would actually be that bad... your heart stopped beating for a moment. Shit, I knew it was a bad idea. Humans and magic don't mix."@@ You rub your face, feeling decently well, if... empty. This little experiment has not worked, and you will need to find other ways to patch yourself up during battle. @@.playerSpeech;"Well... it was worth a try."@@ @@.nyxaSpeech;"It absolutely wasn't, you nearly died on me! I'm sorry I ever agreed to this..."@@ You calm her down... it was your idea after all. She doesn't need to feel bad about it. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |Hunters Hall]]</div>\The voice is quiet for a moment, and you can tell it is studying you, trying to gauge your intentions. Eventually, it speaks, and its tone is softer now, warmer. @@.cosmicSpeech;"You wish to protect. A noble desire. A noble intent."@@ You nod, even if you are not certain it is watching, and it continues. @@.cosmicSpeech;"You desire, and I give."@@ With that, the light grows stronger, and you lose consciousness, the warmth enveloping you. You wake up back in the infirmary, with Nyxa desperately channeling her magic into your body, trying to keep you alive. @@.nyxaSpeech;"$playerName! Wake up! Shit..."@@ You snap awake, and the armored vulpine sighs in relief. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Good. I was starting to think you were done for. I didn't think it would actually be that bad... your heart stopped beating for a moment. Shit, I knew it was a bad idea. Humans and magic don't mix."@@ You rub your face, feeling surprisingly refreshed and well-rested. This little experiment seems to have worked... <<set $awakenedHeal to true>>\ <div class="important">You can now level up the heal skill.</div>\ @@.playerSpeech;"I... actually think it worked. I feel... different. Good."@@ Nyxa tilts her head, considering your claim and examining you carefully. @@.nyxaSpeech;"If you say so... I am certainly never doing this again."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |Hunters Hall]]</div>\The voice is quiet for a moment, and you can tell it is studying you, trying to gauge your intentions. Eventually, it speaks as cold as before. @@.cosmicSpeech;"You crave something that was not meant for you. What will you give in return?"@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Nothing. This power should be yours. |PowerShouldBeYours]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[You are ready to give some of your strength in return |GiveStrenght][$hunterSkill -=1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedLevelUp.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[You can pay some gold... |PayGold]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\@@.playerSpeech;"This power should be mine. And I will not be denied it."@@ The light seems to dim a little, and the chill enveloping your body is increased tenfold. @@.cosmicSpeech;"Arrogant. Selfish. Unworthy."@@ As the last words are uttered, the light disappears, and your consciousness ceases to be. Nyxa will try her best to revive you, but what remains of you is little more than an empty husk, a braindead corpse.... <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Death embraces you...">> <<goto "Death">> <</link>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png" >\ </div>\You speak then, offering the voice gold. Unsurprisingly, the voice doesn't seem particularly interested in such goods, and it a reply never comes. The silence persists, and the light slowly fades away, becoming a small, white dot, and then a speck of dust, and then it is gone. When it is, you feel the ground beneath you again, and Nyxa frantically channeling her magic into your body, trying to keep you alive. @@.nyxaSpeech;"$playerName! Wake up! Shit..."@@ You snap awake, and the armored vulpine sighs in relief. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Good. I was starting to think you were done for. I didn't think it would actually be that bad... your heart stopped beating for a moment. Shit, I knew it was a bad idea. Humans and magic don't mix."@@ You rub your face, feeling decently well, if... empty. This little experiment has not worked, and you will need to find other ways to patch yourself up during battle. @@.playerSpeech;"Well... it was worth a try."@@ @@.nyxaSpeech;"It absolutely wasn't, you nearly died on me! I'm sorry I ever agreed to this..."@@ You calm her down... it was your idea after all. She doesn't need to feel bad about it. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |Hunters Hall]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"I can give some of my strength. Will that be enough?"@@ The light is still cold and distant, but not as hostile as before. @@.cosmicSpeech;"It shall have to be. A trade, then."@@ Before you can ask what sort of trade, the light grows impossibly brighter, and the cold envelops your very soul. You wake up back in the infirmary, with Nyxa desperately channeling her magic into your body, trying to keep you alive. @@.nyxaSpeech;"$playerName! Wake up! Shit..."@@ You snap awake, and the armored vulpine sighs in relief. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Good. I was starting to think you were done for. I didn't think it would actually be that bad... your heart stopped beating for a moment. Shit, I knew it was a bad idea. Humans and magic don't mix."@@ You rub your face, feeling surprisingly refreshed and well-rested. This little experiment seems to have worked... even if with a few unexpected drawbacks. <<set $awakenedHeal to true>>\ <div class="important">You can now level up the heal skill.</div>\ @@.playerSpeech;"I... actually think it worked. I feel... different. Good."@@ Nyxa tilts her head, considering your claim and examining you carefully. @@.nyxaSpeech;"If you say so... I am certainly never doing this again."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |Hunters Hall]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"Nothing urgent. Just thought I'd drop by, have a chat."@@ Nyxa takes a moment to respond, not quite sure how to dismiss you without sounding outright offensive. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Hey, I'm sorry but I'm a little busy here. Maybe later? I'm supposed to be the healer of the order, not some sort of bartender."@@ <<if $fromPortal is false>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |Hunters Hall]]</div> <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |PortalRoom]]</div> <</if>>\@@.playerSpeech;"Nothing urgent. Just thought I'd drop by, have a chat."@@ Nyxa rolls her eyes inside her helmet, and crosses her arms over her chest. @@.nyxaSpeech;"I have no time for small talk. I have work to do. If you have nothing serious to discuss, then kindly leave."@@ Her tone is cold and distant... not much you can do here. <<if $fromPortal is false>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |Hunters Hall]]</div> <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |PortalRoom]]</div> <</if>>\<<if $metLuminarch is false>>\ <<set $metLuminarch to true>>\ You make your way to the ascension room, dimly lit and isolated from the rest of the fort. Its soundproofed walls reflect your every step as you walk inside and stand before the hulking dark metal construct that hangs from the ceiling. <img src="Images/Hunters/LuminarchFixed.png" class="resizable-image" alt="Luminarch Image"> The Luminarch; no trace of its former light remaining in its macabre form. An ancient and powerful entity, perhaps as old as Lauren herself... although unlike her, The Luminarch is not a living being, not anymore. Out of all the freaks your order has to offer, this has to be the most bizarre, a thing of nightmares... The few interactions it is capable of having are brief and limited by his... or its, condition. Not much more than a hollow puppet, its mind has long been reduced to a collection of basic instructions and responses, a mindless drone used to channel its vast magical spark onto deserving hunters. @@.luminarchSpeech;"$playerName."@@ The greeting is cold, and the entity is not expecting an answer. It is merely a statement, the luminarch's way of letting you know it has recognized you through its singular lens. <<else>>\ The Luminarch stands idle, its cold heart barely enough to keep the dim lights within it burning at a constant, low intensity. <img src="Images/Hunters/LuminarchFixed.png" class="resizable-image" alt="Luminarch Image"> <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[You came here to level up |LevelUp0]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedLevelUp.png"></div>\ <<if $fromPortal is false>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |Hunters Hall]]</div>\ <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |PortalRoom]]</div>\ <</if>>\ Under the oddly soothing light of the construct, you close your eyes and allow yourself to think back to all of your previous battles. You've been trained all your life to be a hunter, and yet, every encounter has taught you something new. You will never stop learning, and the knowledge you've gathered thus far will only make you stronger. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |LevelUp1]]</div>\ Under the oddly soothing light of the construct, you close your eyes and allow yourself to think back to the true motivation that drives your actions. Not the glory, nor the hunt, but the desire... Like an animal seeking to procreate, you can't help but to give in to that urge to spread your seed, to claim the creatures you encounter. Your cum is meant for their bodies, their wombs... and so it grows in power, your virility growing with it. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |LevelUp1]]</div>\ Under the oddly soothing light of the construct, you close your eyes and allow yourself to think back to all the encounters you've had, humans and otherwise. So much conflict could be avoided with the right words... and you wish to learn how to say the right things, to understand other creatures better. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |LevelUp1]]</div>\ You have seen much already, some of it still gnaws at the back of your mind... the touch of the void is cold, and its tendrils grip deep. You would rather quieten your mind, lest them become too loud, overwhelming... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |LevelUp1]]</div>\ Under the oddly soothing light of the construct, you close your eyes and focus on your latent spark. You have heard it time and time again; humans are not meant to wield magic. Their bodies and minds are too weak, too fragile... sparks are all they can muster. But you don't care about such limitations. Some humans have managed to transcend them, and so can you. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |LevelUp1]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"Always seemed a bit odd to me. Just why do we need to pay you in order to get stronger? Shouldn't such power be available for free?"@@ @@.luminarchSpeech;"The breath of life is not to be bestowed without due cause. By offering tribute, you affirm your worthiness to the order and your rightful place within its ranks."@@ The voice echoes around the room, a deep, reverberating sound. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |LevelUpLuminarch]]</div>\<<set $temporaryCost to $playerLevel * 5>>\ <<set $temporaryCost +=15>>\ <<if $money < $temporaryCost>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedCross.png"> <<link "Level up ($temporaryCost)">> <<goto "LevelUp">> <</link>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedLevelUp.png">\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png">\ </div>@@\ <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"> <<link "Level up ($temporaryCost)">> <<set $money -= $temporaryCost>> <<set $playerLevel += 1>> <<goto "LevelUp">> <</link>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedLevelUp.png">\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png">\ </div>\ <</if>>\ <<if $fromPortal is false>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |Hunters Hall]]</div>\ <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |PortalRoom]]</div>\ <</if>>\You make your way to the infirmary, hoping to check on Nyxa yet again. If luck is on your side, today there won't be a pair of hunters bleeding out on her tables, or expressing their dissatisfaction with her treatments, and she will actually have some time to spare. You gently open the door, and peek inside. And indeed, Nyxa is alone, back to you and working at her desk, writing down notes in a large leather-bound journal. <img src="Images/Hunters/NyxaArmored.png" class="resizable-image" alt="NyxaArmored"> She seems to be lost in her thoughts and does not notice you enter... <<if $hunterSkill < 2>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedCross.png">[[Be sneaky and try to surprise her |SneakUpNyxa][$nyxaPoints +=1]]<div class="numberStat">2</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>@@\ <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Be sneaky and try to surprise her |SneakUpNyxa][$nyxaPoints +=1]]<div class="numberStat">2</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Make her aware of your presence |MakeNyxaAwarePresence]]</div>\ A devious idea forms in your mind, and you quietly enter the infirmary, carefully approaching the busy vulpine. It's a little odd that she hasn't caught on to you from the moment you opened the door, the steel slab was quite noisy... but that only makes it easier for you to get close. You creep up behind her, trying your best not to make a single sound, and when you are just a couple steps away, you lunge at her, yelling loudly and grabbing her shoulders. The result is a very cute and amusing reaction, as she yelps in surprise and jumps in her seat, tails ruffling up and ears perking straight. She is quick to turn back, her expression concealed by her helmet but clearly irritated and slightly embarrassed. @@.nyxaSpeech;"$playerName! What was that for?!"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Saw you with your back turned to me and couldn't resist. Thought you would notice me, you know? The door was quite noisy."@@ Nyxa takes a moment to reply, busy feigning anger at your joke. Beneath that steel plate, she is likely smiling, glad you decided to pay her a visit at all. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Ha, ha. Very funny. Next time you are unconscious under my care... perhaps you'll wake with a misplaced limb or two."@@ You chuckle, but she just crosses her arms and stares at you, tails slowly wagging behind her. @@.nyxaSpeech;"I wouldn't laugh if I were you. It is a real possibility... I have... never been very good at reattaching limbs."@@ She quickly straightens herself, and gives you a once over, checking to see if you are injured or something, before relaxing a bit. @@.nyxaSpeech;"So... what brings you here?"@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[You just wanted to check in on her |CheckOnNyxaDay3]]</div>\Despite how appealing the thought is, you decide against trying anything funny. For now, anyway. Nyxa is not paying much attention to you, so you move up closer, and try to speak up, to make her notice you. @@.playerSpeech;"Nyxa? Are you working?"@@ She nearly jumps at your words, the fur in her tails bristling. She clumsily turns around, nearly knocking a glass flask over in the process. @@.nyxaSpeech;"I..uh.. $playerName! I didn't hear you come in!"@@ She quickly straightens herself, and gives you a once over, checking to see if you are injured or something, before relaxing a bit. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Did you... need anything?"@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[You just wanted to check in on her |CheckOnNyxaDay3]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"Well, as it's becoming custom already, I just wanted to check on you. Make sure you are not working too hard, and all that."@@ She lets out a soft, muffled chuckle, her hands resting over her armor's chestplate... it seems like see is about to say something, but stops herself, looking aside for a second. She seems unsure... conflicted? Hard to be sure just by her body language. @@.nyxaSpeech;"I... well, I appreciate it, you know that. But today... Im not sure I'll be able to spend any time with you... I... uh... have a lot of things to do today, and it's best you leave me to it. Sorry, really... but I cannot afford to get distracted. I should not... give them reasons to be mad at me..."@@ Something seems to be wrong, and as you hear the armored vulpine speak, you realize that her speech sounds a bit slurred, as if she was a bit drunk? Or just overly tired? <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask her about it |AskAboutIt]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Tell her that you understand and leave her to her own devices |TellNyxaUnderstand]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask if there is anything you can do to help |AskHelpNyxa][$nyxaPoints +=1]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"Hey, is everything alright? You sound a bit off."@@ Nyxa hesitates for a moment, in her silence, you notice a certain stiffness and tension in her stance. She seems to be contemplating something, her thoughts drifting in another direction. Eventually, she shakes her head, snapping out of whatever thought had occupied her. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Oh? I... yeah, I am alright. Just... overburdened, that's all. I really appreciate you checking on me, and I know you just want to make sure I am fine and... doing alright, but today isn't the best of days… r-really…"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Don't worry, I understand. A lot of things to take care of and all that. I will go if you want."@@ Nyxa nods, not really sure what else to say. You are a bit disappointed, and can tell she feels the same, but it can't be helped. You leave her, and she returns to her work, slumping onto the desk and burying her head in her arms as soon as she thinks you are out of sight. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |Hunters Hall]]</div>\ <<set $nyxaState to "niceDay3">>\@@.playerSpeech;"Can I help you out with anything? Not sure I can do much, but maybe I can take care of something for you, relieve some of your burden?"@@ Nyxa seems at a loss of words for a moment, a bit taken aback by your proposal. Underneath her helmet, many emotions pass her mind, but in the end, she just sighs and shakes her head. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Thank you... for the offer, really. But... this is not a burden I can share with others."@@ You nod. Yeah, something is clearly not right, but you doubt she will talk to you about it. @@.playerSpeech;"I won't press it any further. If you need anything, let me know, ok?"@@ Nyxa nods, not really sure what else to say. You are a bit disappointed, and can tell she feels the same, but it can't be helped. You leave her, and she returns to her work, slumping onto the desk and burying her head in her arms as soon as she thinks you are out of sight. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |Hunters Hall]]</div>\ <<set $nyxaState to "niceDay3">>\Something is clearly off, but you are not going to push the matter if she doesn't want to talk about it. @@.playerSpeech;"I understand, Nyxa. I will leave you to your own devices, then."@@ The armored vulpine nods slowly, perhaps a bit disappointed at your lack of interest... or maybe glad you are not prying any further. Either way, you have no business being here. You leave her, and she returns to her work, slumping onto the desk and burying her head in her arms as soon as she thinks you are out of sight. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |Hunters Hall]]</div>\ <<set $nyxaState to "failedRomanceDay3">>\The thought remains on your mind for a moment, as you consider just how uncomfortable it must be to work in full plate armor, for Nyxa day after day. Will she sleep with that steel covering her entire body? Or will she take it off, as you do now, and feel the fresh every once in a while? You can only imagine how liberating that must be… Your train of thought continues as you keep thinking about the armored vulpine. She seemed to be in a pretty bad shape today, and now that you can finally reflect upon it, the memory of her slightly slurred voice, her movements... a bit more awkward than usual... and there was a certain aura of melancholy around her. There is something troubling her, and with how harsh her daily life is, it is easy to guess what it is. You wonder... perhaps you should check up on her? It is late, and your bed is beckoning, but... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Sleep through the night |SleepNyxaThrough]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedSleep.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go check in on her |GoCheckNyxa]]</div>\It's indeed late, but Nyxa may be awake and could use a friendly face. If she's not in her usual spot, you can just return to bed. You get dressed, put on your usual attire, and make your way to the infirmary, hoping this little visit will not annoy her. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |GoCheckNyxa2]]</div>\ It's indeed late, and she already told you kindly to leave her be. You decide to just lie back and let the tiredness take over... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |SleepDreams]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedSleep.png"></div>\ <<set $nyxaState to "failedRomanceDay3">>\The door to the infirmary is firmly shut, but when you try it, it yields, the lock isn't engaged properly; but there has been an attempt to block the door from opening. Unusual for Nyxa. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Open the door |GoCheckNyxa3]]</div>\ You push the door open carefully, squeezing through the small gap created by the failed lock and whatever attempt was made to block it. The infirmary is dimly lit, and the faint glow of a single lantern reveals a disarray of medical supplies scattered across the floor. In the middle of the room, you see Nyxa, slumped on the ground, her helmet discarded nearby. Bottle in hand, her shoulders shake with silent sobs, tears streaming down her furry face... <img src="Images/Hunters/NyxaDrunk.png" class="resizable-image" alt="NyxaArmored"> @@.playerSpeech;"Nyxa?"@@ you call softly, stepping closer. She looks up, horrified to have been found like this and immediately reaches for her helmet, fumbling clumsily and failing to grab it. She just manages to kick it even further away, and when she tries to reach out for it again, she just falls face-first onto the floor. To say she is drunk would be an understatement. It's a miracle she manages to put a coherent sentence together at all, or even stay conscious. You remember her mentioning she couldn't metabolize alcohol very well, and apparently... She wasn't joking. @@.nyxaSpeech;"No... no no, why are you here! Getout! D-dont look at me..."@@ Unable to get a hold of her helmet, she instead buries her head between her arms, refusing to meet your gaze. It's such a stark contrast with the generally composed, faceless Nyxa you'd have come to know that it leaves you in stunned silence, not sure how to even react. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go comfort her |GoComfortNyxa][$nyxaPoints +=1]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Apologize and leave |LeaveNyxaDrunk]]</div>\She seems to be having a hard time, and despite her protests, she could use someone to talk to. You close the door, actually locking it this time and step closer, kneeling next to the drunk and tearful vulpine, careful to respect her personal space. @@.nyxaSpeech;"I... I said no-! Go... go away!"@@ She quickly loses steam, her protests dying down as she just gives up and keeps her head buried between her arms, crying quietly. @@.playerSpeech;"Come on... what happened? You can talk to me, Nyxa. I promise I'm here to help"@@ She just shakes her head, sniffling. @@.nyxaSpeech;"N-nothing. Nothing happ... nothing happened..."@@ She sniffles, reaching for the bottle lying next to her... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Take the bottle away, she's had enough |TakeBottleNyxa]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Just let her do as she pleases, you don't want to be obnoxious |LetBottleNyxa][$nyxaPoints +=1]]</div>\There's not much else you can do, Nyxa looks miserable right now, and you are certain the last thing she wants is you making it worse. @@.playerSpeech;"Sorry, I didn't mean to intrude..."@@ You turn away, closing the steel door behind you and returning to your room, getting into the bed. In the morning, Nyxa will be back to her usual self. Perhaps, just a little more quiet than usual, but otherwise, nothing will be mentioned about last night's incident. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Sleep through the night |SleepDreams]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedSleep.png"></div>\ <<set $nyxaState to "failedRomanceDay3">>\You are quick to intervene, the last thing she needs is more alcohol, and before she can grab the bottle, you pick it up, holding it far from her reach. @@.playerSpeech;"Come on, I think that is more than enough."@@ Nyxa glares at you for a moment, before deflating, sighing loudly, and letting her arm flop down on the floor, with her body following shortly after. She curls into a ball and mumbles something, though the words are barely audible. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |NyxaDrunk]]</div>\You figure its better not to intervene, not wanting to make yourself anymore unwelcome, and allow her to have the bottle. Her shaky hand closes around the neck of the bottle, and with great effort, she lifts it to her mouth. But in her drunken state, she misjudges the angle, and the liquid spills down her face and onto the floor, drenching her cheek. She lets out a frustrated sob, the bottle slipping from her grasp and rolling away. @@.nyxaSpeech;"S-shit... shit... can't do anything right... can't even..."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Ok, ok. Here, let me help."@@ She remains as still as before, trying to hide her face from you as you lean in closer, grabbing a nearby piece of paper. She's a bit uneasy, but lets you clean her face and wipe the sticky, stinking drink from her fur. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |NyxaDrunk]]</div>\You decide not to push her any further, so you just sit on the floor next to her and wait for her to calm down a little. Slowly, as she comes to accept that you are not leaving, she relaxes just a bit, her sobs becoming somewhat quieter... the new silence stretches on, filled only by her occasional sniffles and the soft rustling of your armors as either of you shift position. After a long while, she finally says something. @@.nyxaSpeech;"I hate it here. I hate this place, these people..."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"It's okay, Nyxa. You can tell me. What's been going on?"@@ She forces herself to chuckle, a hollow and humorless sound that's still wet with tears. @@.nyxaSpeech;"P-please... as if you didn't know."@@ She takes a shaky breath, staring at the floor. @@.nyxaSpeech;"I'm so... so tired of having fights every day, of being seen as a monster and a failure by everyone... But worst part is, they are r-right. I don't belong here, I..."@@ She trails off, her voice breaking as another wave of tears threatens to overcome her, and you reach out with a hand, gently placing it on her shoulder. @@.playerSpeech;"That's not true, Nyxa..."@@ You pause, trying to gather your thoughts and find the right words. Seeing how delicate she is right now, you'd rather not just blurt out the first thing that comes to mind. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[You think she is a fantastic healer, a valuable member of the order |NyxaOrder][$nyxaPoints +=1]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[You think she is great person |NyxaOrder2][$nyxaPoints +=2]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"You are a fantastic healer, that's a fact. You have helped us time and time again, and your presence has kept many of us alive... The fact that Lauren chose you over any human, is a testament to your abilities. With how close-minded people are around here, were you not the best of the best, she wouldn't have picked you."@@ That's indeed the truth, and hearing it from another person is bound to help a lot more than just from her own thoughts. @@.playerSpeech;"I know how hard it can be, believe me, I've had my fair share of problems with some of my... fellows. But the point is, we need you. Everyone here does, even if they don't say it or act like it."@@ She is silent for a moment, staring at the ground, contemplating your words. @@.nyxaSpeech;"You... are just trying to m-make me feel better."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Come on, now. Give yourself more credit, you are too smart not to realize how valuable your presence here is."@@ @@.nyxaSpeech;"I... I... I just... had a bad day today. It all caught up with me."@@ She then pauses again, a considerable effort going on in her drunken mind to try and form a proper response. @@.nyxaSpeech;"I'm sorry... I'm sorry you had to see me like this. I-I..."@@ You sigh and squeeze her shoulder reassuringly. @@.playerSpeech;"We all have bad days. It's nothing to be sorry about. Come on, let's get you to bed. You need to rest."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Carry the drunken vixen to her bed |CarryBedNyxa]]</div>\<<set $rapedNyxa to false>>\ She nods, allowing you to pull her up and lead her towards her room. It's just a few stairways away from her main office, a plain and somewhat depressing space even compared to the rest of the order's simple facilities. The room is sparsely furnished with only the essentials—a simple bed, a small desk cluttered with papers, and a worn-out chair. Practicality over style, just like everything else in the fortress... but you can't help but wonder how distressing it must be to live trapped in this dark and lifeless place. Your little pocket dimension isn't much better, but at the very least you can visit the many outside worlds whenever you wish... @@.nyxaSpeech;"My helmet... I left it... forgot."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Come on, you don't need it. You don't sleep in full armor, do you?"@@ Nyxa doesn't seem too convinced and is clearly uncomfortable with her face being exposed like this, but realizing it would be silly to insist on going all the way back, she just keeps walking, following your guidance. And thank god she's not heavy, even with the steel plates covering her body, the way she stumbles and sways, unable to keep her balance, would make it a complete nightmare to carry her otherwise. She's barely able to stand on her own, so you guide her to the bed, and gently lay her down. There, she just collapses, back flopping on the mattress and limbs spread haphazardly. The spikes threaten to pierce the filmly sheet and poke the mattress, but they thankfully hold... @@.nyxaSpeech;"Ghh... I don't know why I..."@@ She trails off, struggling to get the words out, her eyelids growing heavier by the second. It looks like she is about to fall asleep, but not without a last-minute surge of energy. She tries to sit up, swaying in her drunkenness, and fumbling for her armor. She grunts in frustration when she can't undo the fastenings, her clumsy hands refusing to cooperate, and you decide to intervene, helping her take her armor off. @@.playerSpeech;"Here, let me help..."@@ She just accepts, too tired to even protest. You remove her gauntlets, her tasset, and then get hands to work with the endless rows of buckles keeping the whole thing together. Underneath, she's wearing a simple, loose shirt and pants, both stained and wrinkled from the long day, but those look comfortable enough to sleep in. And you doubt she'd like to get naked in front of you... with how very protective she was of her helmet earlier. Its then that she seems to realize how much of her fur is now showing, nothing out of the ordinary for many but for Nyxa, that's practically indecent. She fumbles to grab the covers, pulling them up and covering herself as she curls on the bed. Chaotic and clumsy, its more as if she was having a fight with the sheets and they were winning.The gesture is so uncharacteristically childlike that you can't help but chuckle softly, endeared. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[ You don't find her ugly |NotFindUglyNyxa]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"I can't wrap my head around you being so self-conscious about your body... can't blame you for it, with how others treat you. But really, you've got nothing to be ashamed of, Nyxa."@@ She blinks twice at the unexpected compliment, tails swishing nervously. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Whu- huh?..."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Nothing. Come on, get some sleep."@@ You take it upon yourself to take the covers and evenly distribute them over her, making sure she's well tucked in instead of the utter mess she was doing moments earlier. With that done, there is nothing left for you to do, so you just nod and wish her goodnight, getting ready to leave. You make your way towards the stairway leading out, but before you can step foot into it, Nyxa's hesitant voice stops you. @@.nyxaSpeech;"W-wait..."@@ Its as if she's not sure what she's saying or why. Perhaps the alcohol is talking, or perhaps, its just helping her say what's really on her mind. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Could you... stay? Just u-until I... fall asleep?"@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Stay |StayNyxa]]</div>\You continue, your tone gentle. @@.playerSpeech;"I haven't known you for long, but I can already tell you are an incredible person, Nyxa. I am here for you, Nyxa."@@ The vulpine seems a bit taken aback, surprised at the sincerity in your words. Her booze-addled mind needs a few moments to process them, and a few more to come up with a reply. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Y-you really mean that?"@@ With tenderness, you help her sit upright and wrap your arms around her, pulling her into a weird hug. Both your chestplates collide, producing an awkward metallic clang, but despite the barriers keeping you apart, the closeness and intimacy of the gesture is still conveyed. You nod, letting go of her. @@.playerSpeech;"Of course."@@ She sniffles a few times, rubbing her eyes. @@.nyxaSpeech;"T-thanks for being here... I'm sorry... I'm sorry you had to see me like this. You sit, and Nyxa seems a bit calmer, a nervous smile spreading across her muzzle. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Thank you again... I wouldn't usually ask something like this, I just... hmmm.... I really don't know anymore."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"I don't mind. In small quantities, booze can help lower your inhibitions, and in some cases even increase your focus, but there is no real use for a drunken hunter. @@.playerSpeech;"A... healthy amount of times, yeah. She's silent for a while, the only sound coming from the fortress's inner workings, the faint, almost imperceptible hum of machinery, the low, distant echoes of activity somewhere below, and the quiet, deep breaths coming from the fox, each one longer and slower than the previous. In no time, she's asleep, a rather loud snore escaping her throat and echoing throughout the room. It's so sudden that it startles you, and you can't help but laugh. While her quarters are decently large and spacious, it's still a small, a nightmare of stone and dust and cobwebs. The whole place looks like a dungeon... @@.playerSpeech;"It's not much better, but when you are ready... why don't you come over to my place? I should... get back to work now, I have more than a few potions to finish, but I will see you around."@@ There's not much more to say. You don't care, you want her to know you are there for her, and that you genuinely care about her wellbeing. You open your arms and move in to embrace Nyxa, the clinking and shifting of metal echoing softly in the room as your armored forms meet. The embrace feels somewhat rigid and unconventional; the hard surfaces of your armor pressing against each other. Nyxa hesitates for a moment, clearly surprised by the gesture, so foreign and alien to her that she does not quite know how to react. So she just stands there, unmoving and with her arms glued to her sides, as if she were a statue for the few seconds that it lasts. The swishing of her tails is all you need to know that it's not in vain, though, the gesture has touched her. @@.nyxaSpeech;"I... um... thanks... I made that weird, didn't I? You just... caught me off guard."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"I'm the one who jumped at you, it's fine. I just want you to know I'm here for you."@@ A short goodbye later, and you are on your way. Nyxa stays there, still a bit overwhelmed... and even if you can't see her face, you are pretty sure she is smiling underneath the helmet. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |Hunters Hall]]</div>\You nod, and walk away, and so does she. Who is there?"@@ Before you can get worried enough to reach for your sword, Nyxa's voice answers from the other room. @@.nyxaSpeech;"It's me! Couldn't find a way to tell you I was coming, so I just hopped through."@@ You make your way to the portal room and receive her there, the armored fox girl resting against a wall as the nausea from the teleportation fades. It seems she took you up on your offer, after all! @@.playerSpeech;"Well, welcome to my humble abode, then... I was about to call it a day, but I'm glad you decided to come."@@ @@.nyxaSpeech;"Yeah...
