I thought this place was supposed to be... hmm. Different than my place, but it is still the same. All stone and cobwebs."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Let me give you the grand tour, then!"@@ Your home is indeed not the greatest thing in the world, a bleak, lifeless little pocket of space surrounded by the endless void, but it serves its purpose. You guide the vixen through the rooms, who tries her best to make herself comfortable in the unfamiliar place. Its quite literally more of the same, the architecture, the style of the furniture, the overall feeling of the place... it could all be part of the main building. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |NyxaNight4]]</div>\You can't help but chuckle. The fox girl is cute when she's flustered. @@.nyxaSpeech;"So... what did you have in mind? I thought this place was supposed to be... hmm. Different than my place, but it is still the same. All stone and cobwebs."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Let me give you the grand tour, then!"@@ Your home is indeed not the greatest thing in the world, a bleak, lifeless little pocket of space surrounded by the endless void, but it serves its purpose. You guide the vixen through the rooms, who tries her best to make herself comfortable in the unfamiliar place. Its quite literally more of the same, the architecture, the style of the furniture, the overall feeling of the place... it could all be part of the main building. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |NyxaNight4]]</div>\Even then, she seems to be genuinely interested in the rather boring interior, and bleak little wasteland outside. Perhaps it's not the place, but the company that matters... Or perhaps it's just that Nyxa is happy to have a change of pace, even if it's the tiniest of changes. The visit goes fairly smoothly, if a bit awkwardly. It's as if you were both afraid of crossing a line and getting a bit too familiar with the other, too soon for that. Eventually, you end up in your living room, sitting on the time-worn couch and talking about this and that, while sipping away at some ungodly beverage that the vixen brewed in your kitchen. Despite the... The simpleness of it all, the vixen seems to be fairly relaxed and at ease, tails swishing gently. The only thing kinda bothering you is the fact that she is still wearing her helmet, even as you enjoy your drinks… she keeps sipping at it from that retractile plate, instead of simply removing her mask. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask her to take off the helmet; she is in a private place |NyxaNight4-1][$nyxaPoints +=1]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Tell her to take it off, you think it's rude |NyxaNight4-2]]</div>\ You feel like this is the perfect time to ask if you want her to start opening up to you. @@.playerSpeech;"Could I, um, see you without your helmet? If you don't mind, of course."@@ She shifts in her seat, clearly a little uncomfortable. She knew this moment was coming, and drinking from the cup through the little opening in her helmet is indeed ridiculous. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Are... You sure?"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"I won't push if you are not comfortable. But you are in a private place."@@ @@.nyxaSpeech;"Mhm... Fine."@@ Nyxa's armor creaks softly as she reaches for the helm, removing it carefully and placing it on the table. Her features are exactly how you remember them; a pair of big, beautiful golden eyes, and a soft-looking, furry muzzle with a cute, upturned nose. Her ears are folded against her head, and her cheeks have a rosy color to them, but she tries to ignore it and appear cool and collected. No matter how badly she would like to, she cannot bring herself to stare at you. It's really as if she was a different person, without the anonymity of her armor. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Well, that's that, I guess."@@ She forces a small, nervous chuckle. @@.nyxaSpeech;"I don't know, I always feel weird without it, but you've already seen me before... "@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Tell her she is between friends, and that she doesn't have to hide |NyxaNight4-3]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Compliment her, tell her she looks cute |NyxaNight4-4][$nyxaPoints +=1]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"Why are you still wearing that? I get that it makes you feel safe, but... We are alone here, don't you think it's a bit rude?"@@ It is rude, and she seems to realize that. She shrinks a little, the last thing she wants is for you to be mad at her. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Y-yeah sorry. I guess there is no need for it here, just a habit. I didn't mean to be impolite."@@ No getting out of it, huh? It's such an innocuous act, but still one she seems so reluctant to commit. Eventually, she figures that it's best to just get it over with and remove it before the situation gets any more awkwardNyxa's armor creaks softly as she reaches for the helm, removing it carefully and placing it on the table. Her features are exactly how you remember them; a pair of big, beautiful golden eyes, and a soft-looking, furry muzzle with a cute, upturned nose. Her ears are folded against her head, and her cheeks have a rosy color to them, but she tries to ignore it and appear cool and collected. No matter how badly she would like to, she cannot bring herself to stare at you. It's really as if she was a different person, without the anonymity of her armor. @@.nyxaSpeech;"W-well, there."@@ She forces a small, nervous chuckle. @@.nyxaSpeech;"I don't know, I always feel weird without it, but you've already seen me before... "@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Tell her she is between friends, and that she doesn't have to hide |NyxaNight4-3]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"You are among friends, you don't have to remain hidden in that thing."@@ She seems to still be a bit anxious about it, so you try to shift the conversation and keep her distracted from her own thoughts. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Offer her to accompany you in some of your hunts |NyxaOfferHunts]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"I just think it's a shame that you hide yourself beneath that bucket all the time. You are really pretty, you know."@@ As expected, the compliment gets to her in a rather... explosive way. She tries her best to keep herself composed, grabbing her tails and squeezing them against the sofa to prevent them from flailing around like crazy. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Come on, you don't have to make stuff up. I'll keep it off around here..."@@ She tries her best to deflect the comment and ignore it, but it is painfully obvious she is happy to hear that. @@.playerSpeech;"Why would you think I'm making stuff up?"@@ @@.nyxaSpeech;"Puh-lease... Im a mess of fur and pointy teeth. And a muzzle... its not exactly what you'd call attractive."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Sure thing, you are not human, but I can't see how that's a problem. You are gorgeous, AS a vixen."@@ The rosy color on her white, fluffy cheeks has intensified. She opens her mouth to let a rebuttal, but no words come out, just an adorable, incoherent, mumbling. Seeing what an absolute mess she is right now, she tries to hide her face beneath her hands; in turn letting her tails free to flail around wildly. You'd compliment her a bit further, but it seems you have reached her limit, so you just give her some space, allowing her to collect herself a bit, and try to shift the conversation somewhere she can feel more comfortable. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Offer her to accompany you in some of your hunts |NyxaOfferHunts]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"I was thinking... it's not that this little abode has a lot to offer. Not the breath of fresh air I was hoping you'd get, so how about you accompany me to one of my hunting trips sometime? I have seen some truly beautiful places." @@ Nyxa perks up at the suggestion, glad to have a way to move on and leave her embarrassment behind. @@.nyxaSpeech;"That sounds...!"@@ She takes a moment to actually consider the suggestion. Maybe its not that a great idea. @@.nyxaSpeech;"There are a couple of issues, though. I can hardly lift a sword, Lauren tried to teach me but I'm not that great a fighter... and besides, I don't think I could enjoy the hunts. I just... don't like watching things die."@@ Of course... not like every hunt must end with death, you have your own way of doing things. The way you had thought about it would be basically just using travelstones to get the two of you to various different locations and spending a little while exploring the area together, not fighting the creatures that you are supposed to hunt. Most of them are innocuous regardless, so no real need for killing. However... can you trust her with such a confession? You are a High Hunter, and your duties are not to be taken lightly. If your odd methods of "hunting" became known to your superiors, they might not take kindly to it. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Tell her the truth, you don't always kill your prey |TruthNyxa]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Maybe its not a good idea |NotGoodIdeaNyxa]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"Can I entrust you with a secret?"@@ The sudden shift in tone makes her ears perk up. She's not sure where this is going, but it's clearly important. @@.nyxaSpeech;"O-of course."@@ She is not exactly the kind to blab, either. You proceed to tell her your methods. How you believe The Eye's markings to be wrong lots of the times, and how you have been avoiding killing when possible, simply leaving the creatures be or even helping them. How you just wanted to take her to the different regions and show her some beautiful landscapes, not so much fight and kill. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Shit... that is a pretty big deal." @@ She seems thoughtful, taking the new information in. @@.nyxaSpeech;"What if they find out? They won't just stop sending you out there, will they? You'd get exiled... or worse. You could even lose this... palace." @@ @@.playerSpeech;"Heh. You don't have to worry about me, I know how to avoid trouble. But what's your stance on this? Do you think its wrong of me to do what I'm doing?"@@ Nyxa shakes her head, her tails swishing gently. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Not... not at all. You are a lot more noble than I thought. The fact you don't mindlessly slaughter these creatures... it speaks volumes. But... It's dangerous, you know that right?"@@ She remains silent for a little, contemplative, and then nods. @@.nyxaSpeech;"I won't tell anyone, your secret is safe with me. And... I would love to accompany you, sometime, if you can find the right, spare contracts."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Thanks. I'll let you know once I have one of these innocuous travels between hands."@@ The rest of the visit goes without a hitch, and by the end of it, you even have her promise to keep her armor off around here. It seems she feels safe enough around you to do so. <<set $nyxaState to "partner">>\ <<set $nyxaPartnered to true>> <div class="important">You have unlocked a new starting class.</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |ContinueNyxaSleep]]</div>\ <<set $newDreams to ["nyxa1"]>> <<set $possibleDreams to $possibleDreams.concat($newDreams)>>You decide not to share the secret with her, you don't know if she can be trusted. @@.playerSpeech;"Oh, I see... what a shame, I would have liked to show you some of the places I've been. I guess it doesn't really matter."@@ The rest of the visit goes decently well, a nice change of pace even if it seems that you won't be spending too much time outdoors. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |ContinueNyxaSleep]]</div>\ <<set $nyxaState to "romancedButFail">>After the visit, you head back to bed, ready to sleep through the rest of the night. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |Bedroom]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedSleep.png"></div>\ <<if $onlyOnceSleep is false>>\ <<run setup.passDay()>>\ <<set $onlyOnceSleep = true>>\ <</if>>\What better opportunity to bring Nyxa along for a quick, peaceful trip, than a contract that has already been forgotten? No one seems to be after this creature anymore, so purposefully failing your duties here is almost irrelevant. You let Nyxa know, and sure enough, that night she is ready to travel with you, the vixen looking rather excited at the prospect. That night, once she's made sure no one should be in need of her services, she makes her way to your home, dressed in her usual attire. @@.nyxaSpeech;"So, where are we going?"@@ She asks, a clear hint of excitement in her voice. @@.playerSpeech;"Im taking a long forgotten contract, assigned to Elise before... well, you know. A harmless deer, and no one has bothered to keep this thing going, so it should be an innocuous trip."@@ She nods, smiling underneath her helmet. @@.nyxaSpeech;"That sounds like a solid plan. No one should care about that, I would think."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"It's the idea. I don't want to draw too much attention to these... outings."@@ With that, you get ready to go. Should be a quick and easy trip, just a hike through a calm, peaceful forest... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Get ready to shift |NyxaTravelsMain][$nyxaTravels +=1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTravelstone.png"></div>\ <<set $currentContract to "deer">>She is fairly close to you, so you have no trouble holding her shoulders and keeping her up, her body leaning onto yours for how long it takes her to stabilize. You dont mind the extra contact, after all… @@.playerSpeech;"Are you alright? These shifts hit hard, huh."@@ She nods, letting out a weak chuckle. It's all she can muster, she is busy trying not to vomit into her helmet, which would be a mess of proportions. It takes a couple of minutes for her to recuperate, breathing deeply and using some of her healing magic to dissipate the sickness that still grips her body, and is finally able to stand up on her own. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Shit, fuck, damn, I had forgotten how horrible those felt..."@@ She says, finally finding her voice. @@.nyxaSpeech;"How do you put up with this every time?"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Heh, well, I've done this a couple times you know? It gets a lot better, I promise"@@ <<switch $currentContract>>\ <<case "deer">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |DeerNyxaContract]]</div>\ <<case "wolf">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |WolfNyxaContract]]</div>\ <<case "orion">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |OrionNyxaContract]]</div>\ <<case "vael">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |VaelNyxaContract]]</div>\ <<case "damlon">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |DamlonNyxaContract]]</div>\ <</switch>>\ Nyxa is perfectly within your reach, but for some reason, you figure it would be funnier to just let her fall onto the floor. And she does. Unable to find her balance, sky and earth mix and swirl around her as she falls face first into the ground, her armor clanking as she lands. She remains there for a couple seconds, groaning and grumbling. You let an amused chuckle as you kneel next to her, giving her a few pats on the back. @@.playerSpeech;"Heh, sorry... should have warned you, the first time is always the worst."@@ She doesn't seem too upset, mostly just disappointed in herself. She is making an absolutely terrible impression, isn't she? Not wanting to embarrass herself further, she quickly rushes back to her feet, brushing the dirt and dust off her while still swaying slightly. @@.nyxaSpeech;"I'm fine, I'm fine... now..."@@ She channels some of her own healing magic onto herself, quickly overcoming the nausea. <<switch $currentContract>>\ <<case "deer">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |DeerNyxaContract]]</div>\ <<case "wolf">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |WolfNyxaContract]]</div>\ <<case "orion">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |OrionNyxaContract]]</div>\ <<case "vael">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |VaelNyxaContract]]</div>\ <<case "damlon">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |DamlonNyxaContract]]</div>\ <</switch>>\@@.title;AUNDUIR, OUTER FOREST@@ <<set $deerJobState to 4>>\ <<set $playerEnergy to "nyxaTravel">>\ <<set $deerStone to false >>\ As expected, there is no trace left of the deer girl, probably alerted by your noisy entrance and already running as far as her delicate hooves can take her. No matter, that's not your objective. The forest is quite a nice place, the trees thick and the foliage dense, and the sounds of birds chirping are quite soothing. It's such a contrast to the usual, desolate interior of the Hunter's Hall that Nyxa quickly finds herself entranced, eyes wide and her tails swishing from side to side, taking in all the details, all the sounds, the smells, the colors... @@.nyxaSpeech;"Oh, right! I forgot, too used to having it..."@@ She reaches for her helmet, taking it away and revealing her face. The slight embarrassment she still feels from being exposed is quickly overshadowed by the warmth of the sun on her fur, something she had quite forgotten. @@.playerSpeech;"Heh, feeling more like yourself ?"@@ She chuckles, her ears swiveling and twitching, taking in all the different sounds that fill the air. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Find a nice spot to lay down |LayDownForest][$nyxaPoints +=1]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Take her hand and stroll around the forest |StrollForest][$nyxaPoints +=2]]</div>\Feeling a little daring, you extend your hand towards her, offering to hold it. It takes a moment for her to understand the gesture, her face flushing the lightest shade of pink once she does, but she accepts, offering you her clawed, steel gauntlet to hold. You lace your fingers together, holding her hand as you guide her around the forest, looking for the most interesting parts, the prettiest flowers and the tallest trees. Your time is limited by the duration of the travelstone, but for as long as it lasts, you want to make the best of it. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |ContinueNyxaTravels]]</div>\Your time in this world is limited by the duration of the travelstone, but you figure the best way to spend it is by finding the most comfortable spot you can, so you and Nyxa can relax and enjoy the nature, together. Its not an easy task, with all the roots and rocks littering the ground, but eventually, you manage to find a little clearing, soft grass, and wildflowers blooming under the sun, surrounded by the thick, leafy branches of several trees. You take a seat on the ground, and Nyxa follows, flattening some of the grass beneath with her armored form. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |ContinueNyxaTravels]]</div>\Eventually, your time in that world runs to a close. You both would have enjoyed it for longer, but alas, it's not a matter of personal desire. You both fade away and reappear in your little home, back to the usual gloominess. <<switch $nyxaTravels>>\ <<case 1>>\ However, you can tell she had quite a bit of fun, with a shy smile on her muzzle. @@.nyxaSpeech;"That was great... All this time, locked inside these dark, stuffy halls... but somehow, I hadn't even noticed how much I missed the free air, until now." @@ She looks at you, almost meeting your gaze but ultimately staying on your chest. She still isn't fully used to having her helmet off, but it's progress. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Thank you... for bringing me out there." @@ It is rather charming seeing her so happy, so excited. It's quite the contrast from the reserved, serious vixen you usually see in the hall, and it makes her a whole lot more endearing. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Tell her that you are happy she had fun |HappySheHadFun][$nyxaPoints +=1]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask her if she would like to go again sometime |AgainSometimeNyxa]]</div>\ <<case 2>>\ As the time ends and the travestone powers down, you and Nyxa find yourselves back in the room, standing over the cold, bland stone, a bit confused and disoriented as always, with the familiar feeling of reality settling back on you. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Almost getting used to this already..."@@ She straightens her posture, stretching her back and arms. <<if $nyxaPoints >= 13>>\ @@.nyxaSpeech;"First thing I'm doing once I'm back home is a good glass of water, I swear these shifts are dehydrating as a marathon through a desert." @@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Offer her to have a drink together |DrinkTogether]]</div>\ <<else>>\ @@.nyxaSpeech;"It's… been a pleasure, again. I’m not sure I’ll be able to do this the following days, quite a bit of unfinished work… but thanks for the invitation. "@@ You nod, a bit disappointed, but you know she can’t entirely neglect her duties… <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |PortalRoom]]</div>\ <</if>>\ <<case 3>>\ As always, Nyxa thanks you for the trip; it's been such a great break from her otherwise bleak days, something that's becoming routine... and something she can look forward to. @@.playerSpeech;"It's nothing, you know that. I'm more than happy to have you around." @@ Nyxa smiles softly, nodding and lowering her head, her eyes avoiding yours. You can see her cheeks reddening a little, as she tries to figure out if what she's about to say is too forward or not. What the hell, why not. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Hey... um... I don't want to be to self-invite, but... would you like to have something for dinner together? If you don't have anything else planned, that is..." @@ You didn't have anything other than a nice encounter with your bed... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Accept |ThirdTimeNyxa2]]</div>\ <</switch>>\@@.playerSpeech;"I can take you out more often if you would like. I'll keep looking for spare travelstones to do so." @@ She seems to like that thought, her tails wagging. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Of course, that would be lovely... I look forward to our next outing, then." @@ You bid each other farewell, and she leaves, returning to her normal life... but she does have quite a bit to think about. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |PortalRoom]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"No need to thank me. I'm glad you enjoyed yourself, really." @@ You smile, and she can't help but smile back. The sincerity of your words... makes her feel some warmth inside, a hard to explain, fuzzy sensation that she can't shake off. You bid each other farewell, and she leaves, returning to her normal life... but she does have quite a bit to think about. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |PortalRoom]]</div>\This contract is quite recent, but you have the gut feeling the creature in question is... not quite as threatening as Damlon paints her to be. Maybe you will regret this choice, but... You let Nyxa know, and sure enough, that night she is ready to travel with you again. She looks as excited as the last time, already knowing that the experience will be a much, much welcome break from her duties, and a nice chance to just have a little bit of peace and quiet. @@.nyxaSpeech;"So, what do you have in mind?"@@ She asks, like a child awaiting her treat. Not being sure if being entirely honest here is the right move -- for not even you know what you are going to find, you opt for a vague answer. @@.playerSpeech;"Probably a wrong mark from The Eye. Damlon is not offering much for this one, so I figured it would be one they won't care about much."@@ She seems a bit worried, but nods nonetheless. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Well, I trust your judgment." @@ With that, you get ready to go. Hopefully, things will go smoothly. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Get ready to shift |NyxaTravelsMain][$nyxaTravels +=1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTravelstone.png"></div>\ <<set $currentContract to "wolf">>You let go, she seems to be doing just fine on her own, and knowing how... iffy the vixen can be in regard to physical contact, you don't want to make her feel awkward. <<switch $currentContract>>\ <<case "deer">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |DeerNyxaContract]]</div>\ <<case "wolf">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |WolfNyxaContract]]</div>\ <<case "orion">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |OrionNyxaContract]]</div>\ <<case "vael">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |VaelNyxaContract]]</div>\ <<case "damlon">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |DamlonNyxaContract]]</div>\ <</switch>>\She doesn't seem to be in need of your support, but why wouldn't you use the chance to get closer to her and show her a little affection, a little caring? @@.playerSpeech;"Here, better this time?"@@ She nods, smiling from underneath her helmet, her tails wagging ever so slightly. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Definitely, yeah."@@ <<switch $currentContract>>\ <<case "deer">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |DeerNyxaContract]]</div>\ <<case "wolf">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |WolfNyxaContract]]</div>\ <<case "orion">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |OrionNyxaContract]]</div>\ <<case "vael">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |VaelNyxaContract]]</div>\ <<case "damlon">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |DamlonNyxaContract]]</div>\ <</switch>>\@@.title;AUNDUIR, SPRINGLEAF GLADE@@ <<set $wolfStone to false >>\ <<set $playerEnergy to "nyxaTravel">>\ <<set $wolfJobState to 2>>\ When you look around, you find yourselves in a cramped forest, with tall trees that scrape a dark, cloudy sky, and a thin carpet of dry leaves and pine needles. It is not entirely inviting, but to Nyxa, who is used to the gloomy, cold halls, this is already a huge improvement. If anything, the fresh, cool air makes her feel invigorated, and ready to explore. You are about to start your journey through the forest, when a sudden, distant scream catches both of your attention. A desperate, scared, shrill cry, a woman's voice begging for help. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Rush to the source of the noise |RushWolfNoise]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Careful, it could be a trap |CarefulNoise]]</div>\@@.title;SHATTERED ELIDIOS@@ <<set $playerEnergy to "nyxaTravel">>\ When the world materializes around you, it's not exactly what you expected. The landscape is bleak, and desolate. Ground blasted to ash, skies painted gray. The wind carries a faint, eerie whisper, and a sense of unease permeates the air. @@.nyxaSpeech;"This must have been a battle, long, long ago..."@@ Bones still stick out from the earth, the desperate last stand of a people long since perished. Tiny shards, all that has managed to endure the pass of time, that is. It's quite a grim sight, the devastation so utter and complete that only the faintest of traces are left, a shadow of a memory, dust and nothing more… @@.playerSpeech;"And quite the war it was, from the looks of it. No wonder the target didn't make it."@@ There's a sense of sadness in the air, a palpable melancholy of something that has long been gone, lost forever. You apologize to Nyxa, who must have surely hoped for a more pleasant location. She doesn't seem mad at all, however, even taking her helmet out to let the wind play with her hair, the cool breeze caressing her fur and ears. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Don't be sorry. It's... beautiful, in its own way."@@ She closes her eyes and takes a deep breath... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Give her a weirded-out stare. You don't think there is much beauty in a landscape like this... |WeirdedStare]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Try to focus on the beauty she is seeing |BeautyNyxaSeeing][$nyxaPoints +=1]]</div>\@@.title;SHANLAAR, THE GLACIAL PEAKS@@ <<set $playerEnergy to "nyxaTravel">>\ The world around you is a refreshing sight for sure. In more than one sense. You find yourself standing on a windy mountain, a snow-covered slope of steep terrain leading all the way down to a heavy fog bank that doesn't allow you to see how far down it goes. You are surrounded by peaks, every which way, covered in snow and ice. A frozen, barren landscape, stretching as far as the eye can see. And yet, the wind manages to reach and cut through your thick clothing and armor, the cold air a stinging bite. @@.playerSpeech;"Fucking Vael... he could have told me his damned expensive piece of rock led to a frozen hell..." @@ Nyxa chuckles, the cold air already making her breath mist. @@.nyxaSpeech;"He surely knew you would hate it here. Probably why he gave it to you."@@ You curse him under your breath, pulling the hood of your coat deeper over your face, the cloth failing to provide you much shelter from the harsh winds. Nyxa is not immune to the cold, her tails fur standing on edge and arms shaking inside the armor, but she seems surprisingly calm. She allows herself to let another puff of breath loose, another cloud of steam forming beneath her eyes before it's taken away by the wind. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Tell her to find some cover, out of the wind |CoverNyxa]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Tell her to come closer, for warmth. |CoverNyxa2][$nyxaPoints +=1]]</div>\ @@.title;REYIL, OUTER FIELDS@@ <<set $playerEnergy to "nyxaTravel">>\ The landscape around you is a surprisingly peaceful, refreshing sight. You find yourself standing in the middle of a vast, flat grassland, each strand of grass reaching about knee-high, swaying in the soft breeze. Endless green extends as far as the eye can see, under a stormy, gray sky, a light rain pouring from the clouds. It's a beautiful, quiet place, with no sign of civilization, just you, Nyxa, and the open fields. @@.nyxaSpeech;"I don't think I have seen so much green since... ever." @@ You nod, taking in a deep breath, enjoying the cool air. Nyxa in turn does the same, removing her helmet and shaking her head a bit, letting her hair mane fully free. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Also been quite a while since I was under the rain..."@@ She holds her face to the skies, closing her eyes and extending her tongue just an inch beyond her muzzle. You can't help but stare as the droplets of water slide down her fur, mostly uninterrupted until they hit her armor. She opens her eyes and gives you a mischievous grin. @@.nyxaSpeech;"What are you looking at, $playerName?" @@ You can't tell whether or not she is teasing you or not. It would be somewhat uncharacteristic of her, and her tone is ambiguous, not exactly friendly or hostile. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Admit that you were looking at her |LookNyxa][$nyxaPoints +=2]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[You were just looking at the fields |LookFields]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Give her an ambiguous answer as well |LookNyxa2][$nyxaPoints +=1]]</div>\Without thinking it twice, you rush towards the source of the noise, stomping on grass and branches, your hand resting on the grip of your weapon. The source of the commotion reveals itself, a young, anthropomorphic worlf. Soft features, fluffy hair, and a delicate, feminine build. Surrounded by a pack of feral wolves, she is pinned on the ground, caked in dirt and grime as the largest of them all, the burly, thick-furred alpha, holds her down, jaws clamped on her shoulder as it tries to take her. His cock is already out of his sheath, an intimidating red, rock-hard member, pulsing with need... and the poor thing underneath him can do nothing but sob, too scared of what the savage beast is going to do. @@.nyxaSpeech;"What is... Oh, oh Gods!"@@ Nyxa can hardly give this any thought before a protective, maternal instinct awakens inside her. With nothing but her fists, the armor-clad vixen steps into the fray, rushing to the rescue. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Step in, weapon at the ready |FightAlongsideNyxa][$nyxaPoints +=1]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Sit back and enjoy the show |EnjoyNyxaShow][$nyxaPoints -=1]]</div>\ Before you both start running, you quickly tell her your thoughts, that it could very well be a trap meant to lure gullible hunters. Nyxa thinks it over and decides to trust your judgment. You are the expert, after all... so you both walk toward the source of the noise as stealthily as you can, ready to pounce in case it is, indeed, a trap. It does not seem to be such a thing, however. The pleading voice grows lower as you approach, until it is brutally silenced by a harsh, wet sound. Weak sobbing follows, and when you get close enough to peek, you see the scene unfold in front of your eyes. A young, anthropomorphic world; soft features, fluffy hair, and a delicate, feminine build. Surrounded by a pack of feral wolves, she is pinned on the ground, caked in dirt and grime as the largest of them all, the burly, thick-furred alpha, holds her down, jaws clamped on her shoulder as it tries to take her. <img src="Images/Monsters/WolfFixed.png" class="resizable-image" alt="WolfImage"> His cock is already out of his sheath, an intimidating red, rock-hard member, pulsing with need... and the poor thing underneath him can do nothing but sob, too scared of what the savage beast is going to do. @@.nyxaSpeech;"What is... With nothing but her fists, the armor-clad vixen steps into the fray, rushing to the rescue. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Step in, weapon at the ready |FightAlongsideNyxa][$nyxaPoints +=1]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Sit back and enjoy the show |EnjoyNyxaShow][$nyxaPoints -=1]]</div>\ The logical thing to do would be to intervene, she is clearly counting on you to follow as she is unarmed and unprepared, but... you figure it would be much more interesting to just sit back and watch the show. His cock is already out of his sheath, an intimidating red, rock-hard member, pulsing with need.... and the newest arrival could work just as fine. She is hiding beneath those obnoxious, hardened steel clothes they have learned to loathe, but that protection can be peeled off easily. In a moment, the pack is upon her, a flurry of fangs and claws and snarling. Most of them simply stay idle, barking and snapping, but a brave few try their chances. They lunge at her, only to be harshly pushed back by the armor-clad vixen. She may not be the best fighter, or even a fighter for that matter, but as a member of your order, it is fair to assume she can at least hold her own. Still, the sheer number of enemies is starting to overwhelm her, and the alpha is quite big... not so easy to dispatch without a weapon. When it finds an opening, it takes it, leaping and slamming the fox into the ground. It's then that she seems to notice your absence, calling for you for help. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Hey, what the fuck are you doing?! He is resolute to fight, to defend his newest toy. Of course, the pack of wolves is no match for the two of you. Her skilled hands roam around the young girl's body, mending scratches, bite wounds, and of course, her pesky leg. It takes only a couple of minutes, and when it's done, the girl seems almost back to full health, with only the dust and dirt that covers her fur to show anything has happened at all. She can hardly believe how good she feels now, full of energy and vigor. <img src="Images/Icons/Heal.png"></div>\ @@.nyxaSpeech;"Feeling better?" @@ She is definitely feeling better, and her gratitude shows in her words. @@.wolfSpeech;"Y-yeah, yes, thank you so much... miss? Oh, uh, I'm sorry, I didn't even ask..." @@ @@.nyxaSpeech;"No no, it's fine. I'm Nyxa, and this here is my partner, $playerName."@@ The wolf girl smiles, thanking you as well. Gathering the broken remains of her bow, she introduces herself, Lyra. The vixen is quick to inquire about the reason she was alone, she doesn't exactly look like a hunter... the bow she is carrying is, in fact, bigger than her, but before they can get too chatty, you realize the situation. This is the supposed fearsome, bow-wielding wolf that Damlon warned you about… You tap Nyxa on her shoulder pad and exchange a quick couple of words, and her demeanor changes drastically, going from friendly and motherly to wary, defensive. @@.nyxaSpeech;"This one? She's not more than a pup. Doesn't look like much of a threat to anyone other than herself."@@ You shrug. @@.playerSpeech;"Told you, The Eye is not always right."@@ Noticing the sudden shift in attitude, the girl looks at you with confusion. @@.wolfSpeech;"Is everything ok...?" @@ Nyxa nods as reassuringly as she can, trying not to scare the poor thing any further, and then looks at you for direction. This is such an odd situation, she has no idea what to do. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Let her go |NoKillWolfGirlNyxa][$nyxaPoints +=1]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[You should kill her |KillWolfGirlNyxa]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png"></div>\@@.playerSpeech;"Right or not, she is the target, we should... deal with her."@@ Nyxa seems horrified by the thought of killing the wolf girl, who you just saved from a brutal fate. @@.nyxaSpeech;"You... are you sure? She's not... a threat, right? You told me you didn't always see your contracts through, don't you think she looks... innocent enough?"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"She is armed, and who knows... she may grow into a problem. If The Eye marks her again, it will be trouble. You don't have to see it."@@ There is a moment of tense silence, the vixen looking at the wolf girl, then back at you. Lyra seems to understand something is about to transpire... something unpleasant, and is visibly worried. @@.wolfSpeech;"Wait, what's wrong, am I in trouble...?"@@ Not being able to take the pressure, Nyxa just steps away and turns her back, walking away from the scene. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Just... get this over with."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |ContinueWolfGirlNyxa]]</div>\ You make it quick, a swift "merciful" death that doesn't even give her the chance to scream. No matter how right you may be, for she was indeed marked to die... Nyxa stays distant, and once you are back, she is quick to excuse herself and retire for the night. The cogs in her mind turn, and you are fairly sure she has changed the way she thinks about you. <<set $nyxaState to "killWolf">> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |PortalRoom]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"I don't think we have to. The wolf girl seems as confused and worried as before, not understanding why you have changed your attitude so out of nowhere. @@.wolfSpeech;"I... my village is not far from here, I... I will just leave if it's better for you, I don't want any trouble..." @@ You nod, and watch as Lyra makes her way back home, clutching the bow to her chest. Once the wolf is out of sight, you turn to Nyxa, who looks at you with a hint of... admiration? It's hard to tell, but the tension seems to have lifted. @@.nyxaSpeech;"So, we just saved the life of a target."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Guess we are together in this, huh?" @@ @@.nyxaSpeech;"Yeah, seems like it. Duo in duty neglection and order defiance. Let's hope this goes over well."@@ She chuckles. Despite her nonchalant facade, you can tell she is still a bit bothered by the whole ordeal. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Reassure her; You did the right thing |NoKillWolf1][$nyxaPoints +=1]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Compliment her; She is quite the exceptional healer |NoKillWolf2][$nyxaPoints +=1]]</div>\You put a hand on her shoulder, shaking her gently. @@.playerSpeech;"We did the right thing. This is supposed to be a fun, free time adventure, not a philosophical, existential crisis about the nature of your order. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Cmon, we should still have some time... I'd like to explore the forest a bit more."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |NoKillWolf3]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"By the way, that was some really fine work back there. I'd like to explore the forest a bit more."@@ You nod, and off you go, back into the thick greenery. The food is nothing fancy, the slop you always have around, but it's the company that makes the difference. Eventually, the plates are empty and the call of bed is becoming stronger, so you bid each other farewell and get ready for a well-deserved rest. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Good night... thank you, for having me over. You hesitate for a moment, then decide to point it out. @@.playerSpeech;"Hold on, you've got a little something... right here."@@ You reach out, gently brushing the spot with your thumb. Traces of that grayish garbage you both just ate... that for some reason, just doesn't leave her fur. Nyxa stop dead in place, eyes widening slightly at the unexpected touch and her cheeks heating up just a tiny bit. It's a soft, chaste kiss, barely a peck as anything more might be way too much. Just as it is, the tiny kiss makes her reaction quite comical. Her ears shoot up, her fur bristles and her eyes go wide with surprise. For a moment, she seems utterly frozen, unsure of what to do, how to respond. It feels great, an accomplishment, a little victory, and yet, she cannot bring herself to reciprocate. It's nothing that could be considered a big deal, and she tells herself that, over and over, knowing she's still making a lot bigger deal of it than it really is. Orion is as stoic as always, not seeming upset or angry in the slightest. Sorry if that came out of nowhere, I should have asked you first." @@ You are about to go on, apologizing for your impulsiveness, when Nyxa cuts you off, waving her hands frantically. @@.nyxaSpeech;"N-no, that's fine, It was nothing! I mean, not that it was not something, it was something, but... I don't know what I'm saying, let me try again"@@ She inhales, steadying herself and regaining her composure. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Shouldn't have run off like that. I tend to overreact a little with these things, and... well..."@@ Even now, she seems to be quite flustered. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Caught me by surprise, that's all."@@ <<if $fromPortal is false>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |Hunters Hall]]</div> <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |PortalRoom]]</div> <</if>>\@@.playerSpeech;"Not much. Just wanted to see how you were doing." @@ @@.nyxaSpeech;"Awh, so kind of you. I'm quite busy, though..."@@ She glances at the potions and medical supplies scattered across her workbench, but her gaze quickly returns to you, lingering just a bit longer than necessary and then turning away. @@.nyxaSpeech;"I can’t neglect my duties during the day, but... I could make some time tonight if you'd like to."@@ <<if $fromPortal is false>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |Hunters Hall]]</div> <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |PortalRoom]]</div> <</if>>\The spare travelstone Orion kindly gave you will come in handy. That way, you won't have to sacrifice one of your contracts to spend some time with Nyxa, and the two of you will have a new place to visit regardless. You notify Nyxa, and sure enough, that night she is waiting for you, ready to travel together. @@.nyxaSpeech;"So... where are we headed?" @@ @@.playerSpeech;"Orion had a spare travelstone lying around, apparently the target perished before anyone could reach it, but the travelstone was still charged. He was kind enough to give it to me." @@ @@.nyxaSpeech;"Orion can be surprisingly kind. I have learned not to judge a book by it's cover... Well then, let's see what lies beyond that gem!" @@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Get ready to shift |NyxaTravelsMain][$nyxaTravels +=1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTravelstone.png"></div>\ <<set $currentContract to "orion">> <<set $orionNyxaStone to 2>>You take a moment to look around, taking in the view, the desolate, barren fields of death and decay, and fail to find anything remotely beautiful about it. Your eyes eventually go back to Nyxa, an eyebrow raised in confusion, and find her smiling softly, a distant, serene look on her face. @@.nyxaSpeech;"I'm not talking about the place. It's the idea, the memory, that I find captivating. I'm... not really good at explaining it." @@ @@.playerSpeech;"It's precisely the idea that I find a bit morbid, but I guess it's just a matter of perspective. I'd much rather spend time with you, in a place with less... death, though." @@ @@.nyxaSpeech;"Well... we are going to be here for a while, so, we might as well take a stroll." @@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Wander through the wasteland |WanderWasteland]]</div>\ It is haunting, yet there's also a beauty to the sight, a kind of poetic tragedy, a glimpse into the past. She smiles, sensing your gaze, eyes squinting on the dark horizon as you try to find that same beauty she does. @@.nyxaSpeech;"It's a tragedy, for sure, but also a story. A story of people who fought, who lived and died, their struggle immortalized here. I'm not saying I like this, but I can appreciate the significance, the weight of the place."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"I get what you mean. Its just... its hard not to think about what actually happened, all this... destruction." @@ @@.nyxaSpeech;"Of course. I'd like to think we'll help prevent such things from happening ever again." @@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Wander through the wasteland |WanderWasteland]]</div>\Nyxa starts walking, her boots sinking on the dusty earth. You follow, taking in the landscape, the endless horizon of gray and black, the wretched spires that rise amidst the fields of ash. There is little gradient, no clear division between sky and ground, all the same dreary hue. With little to stop the wind, the dust swirls in the air, forming intricate, fleeting shapes, and you quickly find yourselves entranced by the display, as if watching a living piece of art. You walk with no direction; for no path can lead you through a land that has none. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Its almost hypnotic..." @@ You look up at Nyxa, the same awe in her eyes as she follows the dance of the swirling dust. Despite the grim, almost foreboding nature of the place, you both end up having a decent time. Nyxa seems to be genuinely enjoying the walk, which, in turn, makes you feel a lot better about it. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |ContinueNyxaTravels]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"I was wondering if you'd have a spare travelstone. No contract, just something to visit other places."@@ Damlon raises en eyebrow, as if the mere suggestion of using a travelstone just to sightsee was completely preposterous. @@.damlonSpeech;"Travelstones are no toy, $playerName. They are costly, and only meant to be used for contracts, not to waste time or visit far-away places for fun. We can't afford to waste them."@@ <<if $reputation >= 3>>\ <div class="failCheck">\ However... you have taken care of quite a bit of contracts already. His expression softens a bit, his voice losing its edge. <img src="Images/Icons/Reputation.png"></div>\ @@.damlonSpeech;"I... trust you know that. With that being said, I do have a spare one. A flawed stone, leading so far away from it's intended target its spark would fade long, long before you reach your destination." @@ He glances you up and down. @@.damlonSpeech;"It has no use. So perhaps you can find it a purpose."@@ <div class="failCheck">\ He produces a crooked smile, and reaches into his desk. The stone he gives you is not a gemstone, but a smooth, irregularly shaped piece of glass, cloudy and filled with cracks, as if a spiderweb had been etched underneath its glossy surface. You thank him, and he waves you off. <img src="Images/Icons/Item.png"></div>\ <<set $damlonNyxaStone to 1>> @@.damlonSpeech;"You've taken care of quite a bit of contracts lately. I know you are no idle hunter. If anyone deserves it, it's probably you."@@ <<else>>\ <div class="failCheck">\ <img src="Images/Icons/FailReputation.png"></div>\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |DamlonIntro][$continueDamlon= true]]</div>\ @@.playerSpeech;"So... Vael. I need to ask you something… I was hoping you'd have a spare travelstone? One I could use for a quick sightseeing trip."@@ Vael's eye lenses light up as they swivel towards you. @@.vaelSpeech;"Sightseeing... a High Hunter such as yourself should not waste their time with such trivialities."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"They help me focus. I'm willing to pay, do you have one I could use or not?"@@ You try your best to not sound too irritated, but there is only so much you can take of the hunter's constant nagging. <<if $feragonJobState ==3>>\ Vael seems to consider a price for a moment. You have helped him with one of his personal projects... @@.vaelSpeech;"You have assisted me, so perhaps you are owed a favor. The price shall be a fair one... then. Twenty gold coins for the stone."@@ It seems as if the old amalgamation of metal was incapable of a proper display of gratitude. If that is a fair price, offered as a token of gratitude, you dread to know what Vael would have demanded if you hadn't done him any favors. <<if $money >=20>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Pay him for the travelstone (20) |PurchaseTravelstone][$money -=20]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Pay him for the travelstone (20) |PurchaseTravelstone][$money -=20]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>@@\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Or... not |DontPurchaseTravelstone]]</div>\ <<else>>\ @@.vaelSpeech;"You are willing to pay, you say? Two hundred is a fair price." @@ You frown. That's a bit steep for a useless stone... but Vael seems more than just amused with the idea of extorting you. <<if $money >=200>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Pay him for the travelstone (200) |PurchaseTravelstone][$money -=200]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Pay him for the travelstone (200) |PurchaseTravelstone][$money -=200]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>@@\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Or... not |DontPurchaseTravelstone]]</div>\ <</if>>\ You bit your tongue, and hand Vael the money. You suppose it's worth it. @@.vaelSpeech;"Here." @@ <div class="passCheck">\ Vael tosses you a small, unassuming rock, and it takes you a moment to make sure that is the travelstone, not some random pebble he has thrown your way. <img src="Images/Icons/Item.png"></div>\ @@.vaelSpeech;"Now go pester someone else, I have work to do." @@ <<set $vaelNyxaStone to 1>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |VaelIntroReduced]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"I'm not paying you that much."@@ @@.vaelSpeech;"Of course you are not. Now, was there anything else you needed, or are you simply here to waste my time?" @@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |VaelIntroReduced]]</div>\ You wait for a bit, growing a little worried, until Nyxa finally shows up, flustered and apologetic. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Hope I'm not too late, got held up in the infirmary. A surprising amount of hunters were injured today." @@ She does sound a bit tired, but also genuinely happy to escape the claustrophobic halls and the endless work for a few hours. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Scold her for making you wait |ScoldMakeWait][$nyxaPoints -=1]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Tell her you didn't wait long |DidntWaitLong]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Tell her she is worth the wait |DidntWaitLong2][$nyxaPoints +=1]]</div>\You can't help but feel a little upset, after you went through the trouble of arranging the whole thing. @@.playerSpeech;"Well, you are late. I would appreciate it if you were more punctual next time." @@ She seems a bit taken aback by the comment, quite clearly not expecting you to be upset about something so minor. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Hey... You know I really appreciate you doing this. Inviting me over, having me break from all the work. But I couldn't get here any sooner, I can't just let someone die to go on a leisure trip. Don't you think it's a bit... Childish to be angry about something like this?" @@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Apologize |ApologizeNyxawait]]</div>\ @@.playerSpeech;"Don't worry, I didn't have to wait for long. Just a few minutes." @@ It is not an entirely truthful statement, but it doesn't seem worth it to point her lateness out. It's not like she had a choice, so why get mad at her. @@.nyxaSpeech;"So, where are we headed?" @@ You hold up the travelstone for her to see. @@.playerSpeech;"Not entirely certain, this one is courtesy of Damlon. It isn't bound to any contract, so..." @@ Nyxa gives the stone an appraising look, and nods. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Lead the way, then." @@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Get ready to shift |NyxaTravelsMain][$nyxaTravels +=1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTravelstone.png"></div>\@@.playerSpeech;"A bit, but it's alright. You are worth the wait~" @@ The comment takes her a little off-guard, chuckling away the compliment. You wonder if she is blushing under her helmet, but it is impossible to tell. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Let's not make you wait any longer then. Where are we going?" @@ You hold up the travelstone for her to see. @@.playerSpeech;"Not entirely certain, this one is courtesy of Damlon. It isn't bound to any contract, so..." @@ Nyxa gives the stone an appraising look, and nods. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Lead the way, then." @@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Get ready to shift |NyxaTravelsMain][$nyxaTravels +=1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTravelstone.png"></div>\You feel your blood boil a little at the accusation. She does absolutely have a point, but you are in no mood to admit you were being unreasonable. @@.playerSpeech;"Oh, so now I'm childish? I'm doing this precisely to get you a break from your shitty job, and yet you can't even manage to get here in time? You are not the only one with obligations." @@ That was probably a bit too much. Nyxa looks visibly hurt, even through all the steel. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Forget it. I'm tired and don't want to argue. Maybe this was all a mistake. Sorry for wasting your time." @@ Even trying to sound harsh and uncaring, there's an undeniable quiver in her voice. She turns her back and leaves without another word, leaving you alone with the travelstone. You realize you have been a bit too impulsive, and you don't really want to ruin the evening over something so petty. @@.playerSpeech;"Yes, of course, sorry. It's just, this also matters to me, I thought you were not coming for a moment."@@ She smiles softly, accepting your apology. In a way she appreciates you being honest, even when your honesty is not so pleasant. Makes sense that you were worried, and makes her feel good that you would miss her company. @@.nyxaSpeech;"I would not dare miss this little trip of ours. I would have been there earlier, if it wasn't for... everything." @@ You let the issue rest, not worth the hassle. @@.nyxaSpeech;"So, where are we headed?" @@ You hold up the travelstone for her to see. @@.playerSpeech;"Not entirely certain, this one is courtesy of Damlon. It isn't bound to any contract, so, might as well see where it leads, right?" @@ Nyxa gives the stone an appraising look, and nods. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Lead the way, then." @@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Get ready to shift |NyxaTravelsMain][$nyxaTravels +=1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTravelstone.png"></div>\<<switch $nyxaTravels>>\ <<case 1>>\ Both you and Nyxa stand atop the obsidian disk, and you wrap your arms around her. It's a necessary precaution, you wouldn't want to end up in two separate places, but at the same time it does bring you a little bit of joy. She seems comfortable within your embrace, too, the fox relaxing into you. You use the travelstone, and you both are swallowed into the void, transported to your destination. You are somewhat used to these longer shifts already, your guts no longer churning and your knees not shaking once you land, but the same cannot be said for the Vixen. As soon as you arrive she seems to be swaying in place, about to fall over. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Catch her before she can fall |CatchNyxaFall][$nyxaPoints +=1]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Let her fall |LetNyxaFall]]</div>\ <<case 2>>\ You stand on the obsidian ring yet again, and yet again you wrap your arms around her armored frame, pulling her close and keeping her steady, your bodies pressed against each other. She seems quite comfortable in the embrace... and rightfully so. Being held feels rather nice, and there is no weirdness or shame in it; Its a necessary precaution to prevent separation during the travel... and so, she finds herself wrapping her arms around you as well. Its a professional, necessary touch, nothing more, she tells herself. ... When you arrive, Nyxa is once again wobbly, but she steadies herself a lot quicker this time, channeling her magic to dissipate the sickness before it becomes an issue. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Hold her regardless |CatchNyxaFall2][$nyxaPoints +=1]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Give her some space |LetNyxaFall2]]</div>\ <<case 3>>\ As it’s become custom, you both take a step into the obsidian ring, the travelstone at the ready, and wait for the device to charge up and bring you to the other world. This time, she wraps her arms around you first, taking a little initiative, and you follow, letting your arms circle her waist, pulling her a little closer. To ensure your safety, of course. ... The void's gaping maw opens up around you, swallowing you both and spitting you into the world of the contract, across infinite distances, in the blink of an eye. This time, Nyxa seems to have gotten used to the trip, or perhaps she is simply better at concealing her uneasiness. You feel one of her tails brushing against your legs as she lets go and straightens her posture. <<switch $currentContract>>\ <<case "deer">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |DeerNyxaContract]]</div>\ <<case "wolf">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |WolfNyxaContract]]</div>\ <<case "orion">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |OrionNyxaContract]]</div>\ <<case "vael">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |VaelNyxaContract]]</div>\ <<case "damlon">>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |DamlonNyxaContract]]</div>\ <</switch>>\ <</switch>>\It did cost a fair coin, but the travelstone Vael gave you seems perfectly functional, and it's not tied to any contract... so it s should be a perfect opportunity to bring Nyxa along for a nice, peaceful stroll. You notify Nyxa, and sure enough, that night she presents herself in your quarters, ready to travel together. She greets you with a warm smile and a wave... though the smile is concealed by her helmet. Better off that way. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Ready to go, then? Where are we headed?" @@ You hold up the travelstone, showing her. @@.playerSpeech;"Vael... sold me this one. Not tied to any contract, so I figured we could just take a look and see where it leads." @@ @@.nyxaSpeech;"Vael, huh? Let's tread lightly then, wouldn't surprise me in the slightest if this thing led straight into a pit of doom and death." @@ You chuckle, but in the end... know that she is probably right. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Get ready to shift |NyxaTravelsMain][$nyxaTravels +=1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTravelstone.png"></div>\ <<set $currentContract to "vael">> <<set $vaelNyxaStone to 2>>The travelstone Damlon was kind enough to lend you seems to be fully functional, if a bit rough and worn out. Regardless, you figure its a good opportunity to bring Nyxa along for a quick stroll, without sacrificing any of your contracts. You notify Nyxa, and she promises to be there that night. Thought, when the night comes, (not like you have any indication of it in your little pocket dimensions other than a magical stone dial) Nyxa is nowhere to be found. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |ContinueDamlonNyxa]]</div>\ <<set $currentContract to "damlon">> <<set $damlonNyxaStone to 2>>You chuckle, and shake your head. @@.playerSpeech;"Just staring at the fields. This is such a beautiful landscape, don't you agree?" @@ She nods, directing her gaze back to the skies. @@.nyxaSpech;"Yeah... really needed a break from all gray and the stale air..."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |ContinueNyxaDamlon2]]</div>\You don't know if Nyxa would find such a direct approach endearing or insulting, but you feel like giving it a try. @@.playerSpeech;"The landscape is beautiful, but... I was looking at something even more so." @@ She lets out a surprised snort, and quickly glances away, clearly not prepared for such a direct compliment. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Come on, you are going to make me regret taking this damn helmet off." @@ @@.playerSpeech;"Please don't. It's rare enough to see your lovely face, I'd much rather not miss the opportunity." @@ @@.nyxaSpeech;"Flatterer..."@@ She tries to keep her face straight, but there is no hiding the blush creeping into her cheeks. @@.nyxaSpeech;"C-come on, let's explore a bit." @@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |ContinueNyxaDamlon2]]</div>\So you, in turn, give her an ambiguous answer as well. @@.playerSpeech;"Just enjoying the sight. Such a gorgeous view... how could I not look~"@@ She chuckles, tails swishing in delight, before looking away with some embarrassment. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Yeah, quite the view..."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |ContinueNyxaDamlon2]]</div>\The travelstone Damlon gave you is an old, worn thing. Its magic doesn't last long, but still, you manage to enjoy it to its fullest, spending a good while wandering around the fields with Nyxa, chatting, and enjoying each others' company. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |ContinueNyxaTravels]]</div>\Whatever her reason may be, you can't stay out in the freezing cold. @@.playerSpeech;"We should try to find somewhere to hide, out of the wind. Maybe a cave of sorts, or at the very least a place that can shield us." @@ Inside her helmet, she is beaming a smile, her excitement barely contained. She knows this is a situation just perfect for her, and she can't wait to show you. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Already ahead of you. Come, a bit closer..."@@ When you are within reach, she places her clawed gauntlet on your shoulder, and channels her magic onto your body. You feel a warm glow wash over you, a soothing sensation, and a pleasant warmth spreads into every nook and cranny of your body. It's as if you were wrapped in a heated blanket, and the terrible cold quickly becomes a distant memory. @@.nyxaSpeech;"How are you feeling, $playerName?" @@ She looks extremely proud of herself, and by the tone of her voice, you can tell she is grinning ear to ear under her helmet. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Thank her, you feel a lot better |CoverNyxa3]]</div>\You dont know about her, but you dont enjoy freezing to death too much. So you turn towards her, and extend your arms invitingly, motioning for her to come closer. @@.playerSpeech;"Come. For warmth." @@ She gives an amused chuckle, lost in the screeching wind. She knows this is a situation just perfect for her, and she can't wait to show you. @@.nyxaSpeech;"That won't be necessary. I'll keep you warm, don't worry." @@ You are not sure what she is talking about, but warmth sounds like a pretty good deal. Even better if she is the one to offer it. There is no tight hug, instead, she places her clawed gauntlet on your shoulder, and channels her magic onto your body. You feel a warm glow wash over you, a soothing sensation, and a pleasant warmth spreads into every nook and cranny of your body. It's as if you were wrapped in a heated blanket, and the terrible cold quickly becomes a distant memory. @@.nyxaSpeech;"How are you feeling, $playerName?" @@ She looks extremely proud of herself, and by the tone of her voice, you can tell she is grinning ear to ear under her helmet. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Thank her, you feel a lot better |CoverNyxa3]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"Wow, that's a neat trick. Can barely feel the cold at all, thank you."@@ @@.nyxaSpeech;"I learned it in case I needed to help someone with hypothermia or frostbite, but I never had the chance to try it until now. I'm glad it works."@@ It works... remarkably well. Even after she withdraws her hand, the warmth remains. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |CoverNyxaContinue]]</div>\With that, you are free to enjoy the freezing climate without worrying too much about losing body parts to the cold. There is enjoyment to be had in a landscape like this when the climate is nothing but an amusing novelty. You can pull your hood down and enjoy the now bearable wind, and Nyxa does the same with her helmet, letting the frozen air ruffle her hair. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Can't remember the last time I saw a snowy mountain. It's... so empty and peaceful." @@ Her eyes are wide and full of awe, gazing at the peaks and slopes. You are not sure you would use the word "peaceful" for this hellish, barren landscape, but whatever works for her. You are just happy she is enjoying herself despite the initial impression. Its as Nyxa is looking around in admiration, oblivious to anything else, that a mischievous thought pops up in your mind. You reach down and scoop a handful of snow, quickly packing it into a ball. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Snowball right into her face |SnowballNyxaFace]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Put the snowball down... |NoSnowballNyxaFace]]</div>\You aim your missile carefully and let the snowball fly. @@.nyxaSpeech;"I'm not saying that-" @@ Thump. Right into her forehead, making her stumble. She stands dazed for a second, not understanding what happened. Your mischievous grin is enough to make the pieces fall together. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Did you just..."@@ You did, of course you did. She doesn't seem irritated at all though, just mad she didn't see it coming. It was a perfect shot, and she must have looked like a total fool. She absolutely did. She crouches down, slowly gathering a snowball herself with a focused frown, as if it was a delicate task that demanded a lot of concentration. @@.playerSpeech;"Oh no, what are you doing there, Nyxa?" @@ She takes her time, making sure it is a perfect, round sphere of packed snow, and then, without warning, hurls it towards your face. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Dodge, if she wants a fight, you are not going to pull any punches |NoMercyNyxa][$nyxaPoints +=1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Don't put up much of a fight, let her have a chance |MercyNyxa][$nyxaPoints +=1]]</div>\ You are a High Hunter, perhaps not the most skilled one, perhaps not the fastest… but a High Hunter at the end of the day. You are used to dealing with deadly threats on a daily, and Nyxa is but a healer, a warrior trained in the fine artistry of the book and not the sword. If you really wanted to, you could really kick her ass, drown her in snow no question about it... but for the sake of healthy, friendly competition, you decide to give her a chance. Her snowball hits you in the chest, and she grins in satisfaction before you start making a second one. She does the same, but it takes a while for her to properly pack the snow. By the time she is done, yours is already exploding on her face, and she lets out a yelp. Snowballs are hurled back and forth, both of you landing some decent hits. Nyxa is quite focused on the fight, a competitive glint in her eyes as she rolls and dives out of the way, like an excited child. It's so cute you don't have the heart to try to dodge her throws, allowing her to hit you in the face a couple of times. Eventually, she has to call for a truce, laughing and gasping for air, trying to catch her breath. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Fine, fine! I surrender. H-how is it that you aren't even tired?" @@ You chuckle, brushing off the snow from your coat. @@.playerSpeech;"Well... I'm not wearing a full plate, nor am I rolling and jumping all over the place with it." @@ That is undeniable; quite a lot of theatrics and effort were put into her dodging. The biggest strain however is the magic she is still channeling to keep both of you warm, a constant stream of energy. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Mhm... yeah, maybe I got a little carried away. Could use the chance to blow some steam..." @@ She slumps on the ground, breathing heavily, staring at the cloudy sky. You take a seat next to her, watching as she catches her breath, the cold air turning her breath to mist. @@.playerSpeech;"You did well, Nyxa. Not bad, for a healer." @@ She snorts, still catching her breath. @@.nyxaSpeech;"I know you weren't going all out, you seemed way too eager to let me hit you. But thanks, I'll take the compliment." @@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |ContinueSnow]]</div>\You dodge the shot effortlessly, you deal with all manners of incoming projectiles on a daily basis, usually quite a bit more deadly... Nyxa is just a soft target, easy to read. Her shot misses, and she is already gathering a second, larger ball. You can see her tails wagging behind her, the excitement in her eyes as she aims and shoots again... which you sidestep with practiced ease. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Stay still, you little-!" @@ Thump. Another snowball hits her right in the face, the white powder covering the tip of her muzzle. She shakes her head, snorting the snow out, and gathers more, her focus undeterred. It's not much of a fight as it is a game of Nyxa catching snowballs with her face while you dodge. It's not that she is a bad shot, she just can't get past your reflexes, and her attempts at improvising are easily predicted. Finally, she decides she is fed up with being buried in snow and charges forth, a bunch of barely packed snow held like a weapon in her gauntlet. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Let her have this winwin, it will be sweeter that way |MercyNyxa2][$nyxaPoints +=1]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Do a quick counterattack and tackle her against the snow |NoMercyNyxa2][$nyxaPoints +=1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\You decide not to put too much effort into evading, allowing her to get a clean hit, snow exploding on your face as her gauntlet smacks you and makes you lose balance. She wastes no time and grabs another handful of snow from the ground, rubbing it on your face, venting the frustration of being unable to catch you with a single strike. @@.nyxaSpeech;"T-there! How do you like that, huh!?" @@ She is huffing, a bit exhausted from the impromptu workout. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Now... we are even." @@ Not even close, but she seems happy to at least have been able to catch you once, so you go along with it, nodding and brushing the snow off. @@.playerSpeech;"Sorry, your face was just screaming for a snowball." @@ She chuckles, not mad at all, and extends her hand, offering to help you up. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |ContinueSnow]]</div>\Nyxa's charge is predictable, and you just take advantage of her momentum to take her off her feet, tackling her against the snow which absorbs the impact. She quickly finds her way underneath you, struggling to push you away and break free... you don't even need your two hands to keep her down, and with your spare hand, you scoop some snow, and hold it over her face, threatening to bury her in the cold powder. She stops her struggle immediately, her eyes wide open. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Fine, fine! I surrender..."@@ There is no point in pushing things, you have already won, so you roll off her and stand, giving her a hand to do the same. She accepts it, and pulls herself up. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Not fair... I guess I'm not built for combat..." @@ @@.playerSpeech;"Hey, never pick a fight you know you can't win..."@@ @@.nyxaSpeech;"Oh, but you started it! And here I was just enjoying the scenery..." @@ She is trying to play her cool, and even if, of course, no offense was taken by little game, you can tell she is a bit pouty about losing SO embarrassingly hard. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |ContinueSnow]]</div>\You put the snowy orb down, dismissing the thought. As great as it would be to see her shocked face, you are not sure how she would react to being snowballed, and it's not a risk worth taking. Nyxa, meanwhile, seems oblivious to the possible threat, looking at the mountains and valleys, taking in the scenery, the wind playing with her hair. You keep your little adventure for as long as the travelstone allows you to, traversing the now harmless environment and enjoying each other's company. You can tell Nyxa is really enjoying herself, and it makes the coins you paid Vael feel slightly less painful. When the travelstone finally spends its last bit of magic, you are pulled back to the familiar sight of your room. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |ContinueNyxaTravels]]</div>\With a truce being called, you and Nyxa go back to exploring, walking around the slope, enjoying the sight of the frozen peaks and valleys... trudging through the snow and ice and the bitter cold wind... and you make sure to express your gratitude towards Nyxa's magic, for it is the only thing keeping you from freezing solid. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |ContinueNyxaTravels]]</div>\Azri'Elis is not a pushover you'd have no problem dealing with; the old demoness is a force to be reckoned with for sure, and one you are not entirely sure how to tackle. You figure Vael may be able to provide you with something useful, something to tip the scales in your favor. @@.playerSpeech;"So. Lauren has tasked me to deal with Azri'Elis' impending... escape, and I'm not really sure how to go about it. I doubt your sword will be enough to stop her. So, if you have anything that could help me deal with the demoness... maybe a talisman or an artifact, I'd get it from you"@@ Vael's lenses gleam and flash as they rotate in their sockets, focusing on you with the same disdain as the first time he laid his eyes on you. This wont be just a quick, simple exchange, will it? @@.vaelSpeech;"The Essence Feaster would be more than enough to deal with a simple Vaaltar, in more capable hands. But I can understand how you would perhaps require... assistance."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Cut the crap, Vael. I would rather ensure I live another day."@@ @@.vaelSpeech;"Azri'Elis... that name I recall. A weak Vaaltar, not even full-blooded. But as all of their ever-shifting ilk, all of the descendants of the primordial darkness, their magic is their biggest strength and weakness. Cut them out from the void that nurtures them, and they wither and perish. I could engineer a device to perform such an act, but the materials will be... costly."@@ You figured that would be the case. @@.playerSpeech;"How costly is… costly?"@@ Vael's eye slits glow a faint red as he seems to be calculating the amount. @@.vaelSpeech;"That would depend on what you'd be looking for exactly. I could create a shard of crystallized anti-magic, forged into an explosive device, or an amulet that would allow you to nullify her magic, for a prolonged amount of time. The explosives... would cost you a considerable amount of wealth, for the amount of moonstone will be great. The amulet, perhaps a fraction of that."@@ <<if $money >= 80 && $azriAmulet ==0>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Buy the amulet (80) |BuyAmuletAzri][$money -=80]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ @@.fadedLink; <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Buy the amulet (80) |BuyAmuletAzri][$money -=80]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>@@\ <</if>>\ <<if $money >= 160 && $azriExplosive ==0>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Buy the explosives (160) |BuyExplosivesAzri][$money -=160]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ @@.fadedLink; <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Buy the explosives (160) |BuyExplosivesAzri][$money -=160]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>@@\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |PurchaseWares]]</div>\The amulet is still quite expensive.
