You hand Vael the payment, who counts the sum of money with the utmost precision before nodding. @@.vaelSpeech;"Very well. Now, if I could request some space. I'll craft this item in no time"@@ With that, Vael disappears into the darkness of his deep forge, leaving you alone. You take a seat on a slab of stone... and wait. In a matter of minutes, Vael reemerged from the dark, with a simple looking dark gem encased in a steel spiral, a fragment of moonstone. It... seems a bit plain. @@.playerSpeech;"You sure this will do? It doesn't seem too impressive..." @@ Vael's lenses flare a bit, his voice a low growl, but decides not to be too harsh. The concern is understandable. @@.vaelSpeech;"A shard of pure moonstone this size should be more than enough to fully nullify the powers of any Vaaltar, let alone one so young and inexperienced."@@ <<if $awakenedMagic is true || $awakenedHeal is true>> <div class="passCheck">\ You are no complete stranger to such magic. Eventually, after what seems like hours, Vael reemerged from the dark, the promised explosive in his grasp. It's a small, metallic object; A semisphere of engraved filigree, containing a glass orb buried away under a myriad of steel petals. Like a closed flower bud, the delicate petals are interlocked together, hiding away the fragile orb that will open to unleash its devastating potential. @@.vaelSpeech;"Here it is. The promised explosive, crafted with the utmost precision."@@ Vael holds out the device, and you take it, feeling the cool metal and intricate craftsmanship. For all his eccentricities, Vael is indeed a masterful smith. Only one concern lingers on your mind. @@.playerSpeech;"Is it really safe to use? I have no desire to be caught up in the blast..."@@ Vael waves his hand dismissively. @@.vaelSpeech;"This little contraption will disrupt the Vaaltar's connection to the void, rendering her magic useless for but an instant. As expected, Lauren sent a competent hunter to deal with her, but she's had quite some time to prepare... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[The fight continues... |FightContinues]]<div class="numberStat">6</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\ <<if $azriExplosive is 1>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Use the explosives as well |UseExplosivesAlso]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedItem.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ @@.fadedLink; <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Use the explosives as well |UseExplosivesAlso]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedItem.png"></div>@@\ <</if>>The fight turns out from a quick, explosive brawl to a battle of attrition. With the demoness' magic, both her shadowy projections and mental tricks completely neutralized, she has no choice but to rely on her natural speed and physical prowess, claws dicing and slicing with precision and speed. It is surprising that she is capable of putting up a fight like that, even as the amulet saps away her strength, and even your sword threatens to cut her down. The two of you dance through the room, the animated guards simply watching. <<if $hunterSkill < 3>>\ Not having much training in the martial arts, you find yourself unable to keep up with the demoness' quick and deadly strikes, growing more and more tired as the fight progresses, your movements slower and less precise... <div class="failCheck">\ Eventually, she manages to find an opening, slashing the amulet away from you and throwing it away... she stands triumphant then, the orb between her horns glowing with renewed strength. <img src="Images/Icons/FailGeneral.png"><img src="Images/Icons/FailGeneral.png"></div>\ @@.azriSpeech;"I was compassionate about your kind, once. As you fall to the ground, a gurgling noise coming from your throat, the last thing you see is her hoofed foot stomping on your skull. <img src="Images/Icons/Death.png"></div>\ Only then do the sentinels interfere, your failure leaving them no choice but to step in. <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Death embraces you...">> <<goto "Death">> <</link>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png" >\ </div>\ <<else>>\ <<if $hunterSkill >=6>>\ The fight stretches for what feels like an eternity, the both of you fighting for your lives... while those damned sentinels do nothing but watch. Nyxa gets up, helping you clean up the table while she ruminates upon something again. You wait for her to bring it up, and eventually, she does, her voice soft. @@.nyxaSpeech;"I'm... awfully tired. The meal was most likely a pretext to ask this, and now, only after the words have left her muzzle, she realizes what a stupid question that was. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Tell her you'd love to have her |ContinueNyxaNight31]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Tease her a bit; Her headquarters are barely two minutes away from here |TeaseNyxaNight3]]</div>\ You raise an eyebrow, pretending to be surprised. @@.playerSpeech;"Stay over? Your room is just a few steps away, is it not? Or did you drink too much water?"@@ She flinches, mortified at what she thinks is a rejection, her ears drooping and her tails stilling. She quickly averts her gaze, her voice trembling slightly as she tries to backtrack. @@.nyxaSpeech;"No, I... I'm sorry. That was a silly thing to ask. I'll just... go."@@ Realizing she has completely misinterpreted the comment, you quickly step forward, gently grabbing her hand before she can retreat any further. @@.playerSpeech;"Hey, Nyxa, wait. I'm just teasing you. Of course you can stay. I would love for you to stay."@@ You can almost feel her relief as her body relaxes, a shaky sigh escaping her lips. Her ears perk up, her tails flicking softly. @@.nyxaSpeech;"You are a bit of an ass, you know?" @@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |ContinueNyxaNight32]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"Of course, Nyxa. You know you are always welcome in my home." @@ You can tell how relieved she is by the answer, but she tries not to show it, just giving a nod and a soft thank you. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |ContinueNyxaNight32]]</div>\You finish cleaning up the kitchen and head back to your room. You've never had anyone over before, so it's a bit strange to have Nyxa in your home, your bedroom... the spacerly decorated walls, the dusty shelves, the unmade bed. Didn't expect a guest, but it's not like she would mind. @@.playerSpeech;"Apologies for the mess. Didn't know I'd be hosting." @@ You turn around and give her a soft punch on the arm. @@.playerSpeech;"Now, absolutely no armor allowed, don't want you punching holes in the matress"@@ Nyxa chuckles softly, her tails swishing behind her as she starts to unbuckle her armor. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Understood, captain. No armor."@@ She teases back, her voice light. Not the first time you see her without it, so not that much of a big deal, she tells herself. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Help her remove the armor |HelpRemoveArmor]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Focus on your own plate |FocusPlate]]</div>\Her hands quickly undo the straps of the plates, working to rid herself of the bulky metal and leather. She does the best she can on her own, but some places are quite hard to reach, so you figure it's only fair you help her out. She freezes up, the touch of your fingers on her body making her shudder, she wasn't expecting it. She says nothing, working alongside you to get it all off, and soon enough, she is standing before you in nothing but her undershirt and trousers, the fabric clinging to her fur like a second layer. You have to admit, she is quite the sight... but you try to avoid staring and focus on your own armor, removing your shoulderpads, chestplate, and boots. Once finished, you turn back to Nyxa, and find her sitting on the bed, looking quite unsure about this whole ordeal. She shifts a bit, glancing down at her paws... The bed... The bed. That will be an issue. You have no guest room, and the bed can hardly fit one person, let alone two. But you are sure as hell not letting her sleep on the floor, or anywhere but on your bed, really. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Have her take the bed and sleep on the floor |ContinueNyxaNight33]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedSleep.png"></div>\You watch as she carefully removes each piece, setting them aside with practiced ease. Without her armor, she seems smaller, more vulnerable, but also more real. She stands there in simple clothes, similar to the ones she wore the day she was so drunk she could barely stand. Not wanting to gawk too much, you take care of your own, simpler armor, removing your shoulderpads, chestplate, and boots. Once finished, you turn back to Nyxa, and find her sitting on the bed, looking quite unsure about this whole ordeal. She shifts a bit, glancing down at her paws... The bed... The bed. That will be an issue. You have no guest room, and the bed can hardly fit one person, let alone two. But you are sure as hell not letting her sleep on the floor, or anywhere but on your bed, really. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Have her take the bed and sleep on the floor |ContinueNyxaNight33]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedSleep.png"></div>\You would like to, at least, be in the same room as her; little point in having her over if you are going to sleep apart. @@.playerSleep;"You can keep the bed, I'll sleep on the floor." @@ Nyxa gives you an incredulous look, as you start to remove your plate armor and hanging it on the stand next to your bed. @@.nyxaSpeech;"On the... floor? Why would you sleep on the floor?" @@ You look over your shoulder, raising an eyebrow at her. @@.playerSpeech;"There is only one bed, and I'm not letting you sleep on the floor." @@ She doesn't press it any further, what else can she say without sounding... weird? <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |ContinueNyxaNight34]]</div>\Nyxa takes the bed, wrapping herself in the blanket, and you take the floor, blowing the candle out and plunging the room in darkness. It's then that you realize the awful idea this was, you can't belive how disgustingly uncomfortable the floor is. Your own weight works against you, making the stone dig into your bone. You try and shift, to no avail, you can't find a single position that isn't at least a tad painful. @@.nyxaSpeech;"How is that floor treating you? Seem's like a great sleeping place, really." @@ You turn around, to find a pair of eyes glowing in the dark. The vixen is peeking at you from the bed, a playful smirk on her face. Now that darkness is obscuring her, she has found her courage again. She can see everything clearly, and she knows you can't. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Like a cloud made of pillows |ContinueNyxaNight35]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Ask if she wants to try it out |ContinueNyxaNight36]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Keep struggling to find a comfortable position |ContinueNyxaNight37]]</div>\ @@.playerSpeech;"Like a cloud made of pillows, thanks for asking." @@ Her amused laughter is a reward of its own. She's having a great time watching you struggle to get comfortable, and she's not even trying to hide it. A bit of time goes on, with you suffering while she gathers the courage to speak, her voice <i>still</i> coming a bit more unsure than she would've wanted. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Hey, no point in torturing yourself. I can make space... it feels unfair to be sleeping in a comfy bed while you are there, suffering."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Are you sure? The bed... is quite small. Don't want to invade your space." @@ @@.nyxaSpeech;"Well... I'm invading yours quite a bit, so..." @@ Her eyes drift away, a faint blush spreading over her cheeks. She's so glad humans can barely see anything in the dark. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Besides... it's a bit cold in here." @@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[No need to ask twice... |NoNeedAskTwice]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"Why don't you come and see for yourself? It's pretty nice." @@ She chuckles, finding your attempt at being sassy amusing. @@.nyxaSpeech;"I'm fine right here, thank you. It seems High Hunters get better beds." @@ A bit of time goes on, with you suffering while she gathers the courage to speak, her voice <i>still</i> coming a bit more unsure than she would've wanted. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Hey, no point in torturing yourself. I can make space... it feels unfair to be sleeping in a comfy bed while you are there, suffering."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Are you sure? The bed... is quite small. Don't want to invade your space." @@ @@.nyxaSpeech;"Well... I'm invading yours quite a bit, so..." @@ Her eyes drift away, a faint blush spreading over her cheeks. She's so glad humans can barely see anything in the dark. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Besides... it's a bit cold in here." @@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[No need to ask twice... |NoNeedAskTwice]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"Ha, ha..."@@ You keep trying to find a comfortable position, but nothing works. Nyxa is watching your futile attempts with growing amusement, like an excited teenager. Her eyes are locked on you, following every move with an eager smile, her tails slowly swishing behind her. A bit of time goes on, with you suffering while she gathers the courage to speak, her voice <i>still</i> coming a bit more unsure than she would've wanted. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Hey, no point in torturing yourself. I can make space... it feels unfair to be sleeping in a comfy bed while you are there, suffering."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Are you sure? The bed... is quite small. Don't want to invade your space." @@ @@.nyxaSpeech;"Well... I'm invading yours quite a bit, so..." @@ Her eyes drift away, a faint blush spreading over her cheeks. She's so glad humans can barely see anything in the dark. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Besides... it's a bit cold in here." @@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[No need to ask twice... |NoNeedAskTwice]]</div>\You groan as you get up, trying not to seem too eager to jump on the bed. You are pretty sure Nyxa can see right through your facade, but it's the principle that matters. Her eyes follow you as you approach, and she scoots over, pressing herself against the wall. The bed is small, sure, but so is she... without the armor, she's actually quite the thin little thing. The bed shifts under your weight, and you settle in, trying not to press too close, but that is of course impossible with the available space. You have no choice but to be pressed up against her, the soft fur of her tails brushing against your legs before they shily retreat. @@.playerSpeech;"Yeah, definitely better than the floor. Thanks." @@ She mumbles a "no problem", the proximity already starting to get to her. Of course she knew this was the only way it could go, but that didn't prepare her for the actual experience. She shifts a bit, trying to get more comfortable by shifting to her side, and somehow, ends up even closer to you. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Sorry, sorry..." @@ She lets out a nervous chuckle, trying not to seem too awkward before shifting again, trying to find that perfect, inexistent position. No avail, she keeps trying for a little while, until she realizes she is just tangling the sheets and being an annoyance. Her muzzle is only inches from your face, her eyes shining bright in the dark. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Um... can we... like, this? Is it ok?" @@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Wrap your arms around her and pull her close |WrapCloseNyxa]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Kiss her |KissNyxa]]</div>\Being so close to her, how can you not? @@.playerSpeech;"Let me help..." @@ You wrap an arm around her waist and pull her closer. Nyxa's breath catches in her throat, and you see her eyes widening slightly in the dark, before she relaxes, her body sinking against yours. She fits perfectly in your arms, her muzzle ending up pressed into the crook of your neck, fur tickling your skin. It feels great. Warm, cozy, comfortable... and she seems to be enjoying the cuddling as well. It feels so natural, so... right, and she quickly relaxes into your embrace. The part about her being tired earlier wasn't a lie, though. It has been a long, busy day... and Nyxa’s eyelids are starting to feel heavy. She however wants to express her appreciation. @@.nyxaSpeech;"You... smell nice." @@ Not exactly the kind of compliment she wanted to give, her mind a bit foggy from sleep. Your scent is indeed all around her, making her feel safe and protected, but that's not what she wanted to say. @@.nyxaSpeech;"I mean... Thank you, for having me over. And for everything, really." @@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Press a kiss to her forehead. |KissForeheadNyxa]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCharisma.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Nuzzle her cheek and wish her goodnight |JustGoodnight]]</div>\Nyxa's muzzle is so close, breath ticking your face as she speaks... how can you resist? Just a quick peck, that's all you want. You lean forward, capturing her lips. She lets out a squeak of surprise, frozen for a moment before giving in, closing her eyes and returning the kiss with clumsy eagerness. Her muzzle shape is a bit strange, which, coupled with her inexperience, makes the kiss an absolute mess. But it doesn't matter, at the end of it, she is smiling, a dazed expression on her face. @@.playerSpeech;"Of course it is." @@ Her tails brush against your legs again, curling around them this time, as she shifts and settles in, pressing herself a bit more into your chest. The part about her being tired earlier wasn't a lie, though. It has been a long, busy day... and Nyxa’s eyelids are starting to feel heavy. She however wants to express her appreciation. @@.nyxaSpeech;"You... smell nice." @@ Not exactly the kind of compliment she wanted to give, her mind a bit foggy from sleep. Your scent is indeed all around her, making her feel safe and protected, but that's not what she wanted to say. @@.nyxaSpeech;"I mean... Thank you, for having me over. And for everything, really." @@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Wish her goodnight |KissForeheadNyxa]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCharisma.png"></div>\ It's hard not to feel a rush of affection when she is thanking you for having her over. The poor thing is so grateful for everything, it's a bit heartbreaking... you can imagine it has been a long time since anyone did anything remotely nice for her, and it shows. You pull her a bit closer, leaning in and pressing a gentle kiss to her forehead, your lips sinking slightly in the coat of thin fur. @@.playerSpeech;"Don't have to thank me, Nyxa. Glad I could make you feel welcome." @@ You keep your hand on the back of her head, softly stroking her hair as you hold her, her eyes sliding shut, and an almost inaudible purr vibrating through her chest. Do foxes purr? It's not something you were aware of, but now that she is pressed against your body, it's impossible to ignore the low, soft rumble emanating from her. Makes you feel... things. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Pet her a little |KissForeheadNyxa2]]</div>\It's hard not to feel a rush of affection when she is thanking you for having her over. The poor thing is so grateful for everything, it's a bit heartbreaking... you can imagine it has been a long time since anyone did anything remotely nice for her, and it shows. Your hand gently caresses her cheek, fingers running over the smooth fur before settling there. You nuzzle the side of her face, stroking her cheek softly until she closes her eyes, and a soft, almost imperceptible purr starts to reverberate through her chest. @@.playerSpeech;"You know you're welcome here anytime, Nyxa. Now, good night, you." @@ She nods softly, not pulling away from the touch. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |ContinueNyxaNight39]]</div>\Your fingers start to slowly run through her hair, petting her softly, the repetitive motion calming and relaxing. You keep doing it, enjoying the soft feeling of the strands under your fingers, and the purring reverberating against your chest. Her eyes are dreamily half-lidded, breathing slow and deep. She's still awake, but barely. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Tease her a little about the purring |TeasePurring]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Just keep petting her hair |NoTeasePurring]]</div>\Her purring is so endearing, and you can't help but tease her a little about it. @@.playerSpeech;"Didn't know foxes could purr, that's adorable." @@ Her ears twitch a little, the comment bringing her out of her stupor for a brief moment. She opens her eyes, palpable embarrassment shining in them. @@.nyxaSpeech;"S-shut up... I can't control it." @@ You keep your hand moving, stroking her softly, and sure enough, the purring resumes. She hides away, burying her face further into your neck, and it tickles a bit, her muzzle brushing against your skin. But you can hardly complain, it's not like you mind it... on the contrary, it's soothing, and a tangible sign of her enjoyment. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |ContinueNyxaNight38]]</div>\You keep the motion going, gently massaging her scalp as you continue petting her, until eventually, her breathing steadies and her body goes slack, peacefully snoring. It's a nice sound to fall asleep to, and you are soon lulled into sleep as well. If only it had been that easy, it would have been the best night's rest in a while. But Nyxa's slumbering form has other ideas... Just like the night she had drank her ass off, the vixen is the embodiment of chaos. She shifts a lot in her sleep, and being as close as she is to you, that means you are constantly being jostled and nudged, the blankets pulled around, and her tails are flicking all over the place. If that wasn't enough, she snores like a bear, loud and rumbling, and the combination of the two makes it... Hard to sleep. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Just suffer through it |SufferThroughNyxa]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Gently wake her up |WakeUpNyxa]]</div>\Eventually, Nyxa's breath slows down, and her purring subsides, replaced by a soft, peaceful snoring. It's a nice sound to fall asleep to, and you are soon lulled into sleep as well. If only it had been that easy, it would have been the best night's rest in a while. But Nyxa's slumbering form has other ideas... Just like the night she had drank her ass off, the vixen is the embodiment of chaos. If that wasn't enough, she snores like a bear, loud and rumbling, and the combination of the two makes it... Hard to sleep. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Just suffer through it |SufferThroughNyxa]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Gently wake her up |WakeUpNyxa]]</div>\Eventually, Nyxa's breath slows down, and her purring subsides, replaced by a soft, peaceful snoring. It's a nice sound to fall asleep to, and you are soon lulled into sleep as well. If only it had been that easy, it would have been the best night's rest in a while. But Nyxa's slumbering form has other ideas... Just like the night she had drank her ass off, the vixen is the embodiment of chaos. She shifts a lot in her sleep, and being as close as she is to you, that means you are constantly being jostled and nudged, the blankets pulled around, and her tails are flicking all over the place. If that wasn't enough, she snores like a bear, loud and rumbling, and the combination of the two makes it... Hard to sleep. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Just suffer through it |SufferThroughNyxa]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Gently wake her up |WakeUpNyxa]]</div>\There is not much you can do, you doubt she can control her movements, if you told her she is snoring and clawing you, she would probably just feel bad while still doing it. So, you resign yourself to a long, long sleepless night... focusing instead on the fact that her body is pressed against yours, her warmth and softness are pleasant, and her scent makes you feel at ease. You can always have a comforting rest tomorrow, but having a chance to spend the night like this, with a cute vixen clawing and drooling on you, might not come again. ... The night stretches on, as every time sleep threatens to claim you, a jolt or a paw or a snore shakes you awake. And just when you think the worst is behind you, and are starting to doze off, Nyxa begins to whimper and twitch more violently. Her claws dig into your skin as she grabs hold of one of your arms, and her breathing becomes rapid and shallow. You open your eyes to see her face contorted in distress, her body shaking a little as if she's fighting off something terrible in her dreams. You realize she's having a nightmare... @@.playerSpeech;"You're okay... it's just a nightmare. I'm here, it's okay..." @@ The words seem to reach her, and after a few seconds, the twitching subsides, and she starts to calm down. She opens her eyes, still a bit unfocused. @@.nyxaSpeech;"$playerName?..." @@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Cuddle her closer; try and comfort her |CuddleCloser]]</div>\It feels a bit rude to disturb her peaceful sleep, but it's either that or you don't get any rest. You nudge her, calling her name, but she is completely dead to the world. @@.playerSpeech;"Nyxa... Nyx, wake up..." @@ Your gentle shaking doesn't seem to have much effect, so you try a little louder, until her eyes flutter open, looking at you, then around, a bit confused. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Hmm? What?... Ishh is it morning?" @@ @@.playerSpeech;"It's still the middle of the night. Just... please stop moving so much, alright?" @@ The drowsy vixen apologizes, knowing to an extent that she is quite the restless sleeper, and soon enough, she is dozing off again. Not even a minute later, the snoring returns, and her sharp claws and paws dig into you. It's going to be a long night... you suppose it's not like she can just control her movements, so you'll just have to endure it. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Cuddle her closer; try and comfort her |CuddleCloser]]</div>\With no fear of her claws or fangs, you gently pull her close, wrapping your arms around her and hugging her tight, letting her know she is safe. Realizing where she is, she presses herself into you, her arms and legs wrapping around you and holding on, as if you were her anchor in reality. Such a mess of emotions... @@.nyxaSpeech;"I... I'm sorry. I didn't mean to wake you."@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Shhh... Don't worry about it, Nyxa. It's alright. Are you okay?" @@ Not like you were getting much sleep anyways, it's the least you can do. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Mnf... Yeah, I think so. I have those... sometimes. I'm glad you are here... though." @@ @@.playerSpeech;"You... sure you're okay? Do you want to talk about your dream?" @@ You feel her shift, a small sigh leaving her. She seems to hesitate a moment before talking, her voice a whisper. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Nothing in particular. Just... the usual." @@ You are not sure what she means by that, but she doesn't seem eager to share much more in regards to her dream... Soon enough, she is asleep once again, though this time, she is a lot calmer, her body resting comfortably against yours. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |WakeUpMorningWood]]</div>\ <<set $onlyOnceSleep = false>>\<div class="passCheck">\ With your arms wrapped around her body and her head on your chest, you manage to get some rest as well. <img src="Images/Icons/Sleep.png"></div>\ ... In the morning, Nyxa's tails are draped all over the place, and her limbs are sprawled out in all directions. Her arms and legs are tangled with yours, and a thin strand of saliva is leaking out of the corner of her mouth. She's also snoring in a similar fashion as during the night, but somehow, it is not quite as annoying. You suppose you have gotten somewhat used to it, passing the sound for white noise. It's then that you notice, as you start to stir, that your cock is painfully hard, a morning wood so strong you're surprised you didn't wake up because of it. Or maybe you did... It's as if having Nyxa's sleeping form pressed against you, has been enough to drive your subconscious wild with sexual fantasies, and this is the result... a throbbing erection trapped under your underwear. @@.playerSpeech;"Shit..."@@ You wouldn't like being seen like this, your relationship with Nyxa is fairly innocent, and the last thing you need is for her to see your dick poking up in the air. She is fast asleep still, but the damned thing doesn't seem like it is going down anytime soon. <<if $onlyOnceSleep is false>>\ <<run setup.passDay()>>\ <<set $onlyOnceSleep = true>>\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Try and wait it out |WaitOutErection]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Get up, maybe a walk will help |WalkOutErection]]</div>\ You take a few deep breaths, trying to calm yourself and relax. <div class="passCheck">\ That usually works, but this time, it's not so easy. It's as if your cock has a mind of its own, and refuses to let go of the images your brain conjured. <img src="Images/Icons/Cum.png"></div>\ Images of Nyxa in different states of undress, or in the midst of the throes of passion... yeah, it's not helping. And her scent... It's everywhere now, surrounding you... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Get up, maybe a walk will help |WalkOutErection]]</div>\With a bit of careful maneuvering, you manage to extract yourself from her, sliding her limbs away and replacing your chest with a pillow to hold. She grumbles a bit but settles soon after, and you slip out of bed, confident you did a good job, and are no longer at risk of being spotted. Until her ears twitch, and her eyes open. She yawns loudly, her jaw cracking. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Is it morning already? Hnnf... I could sleep for another whole day... but I suppose it's not possible, huh." @@ Her tails curl as she stretches, yawning once again, a cute expression on her sleepy face. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Mhm... I hope I wasn't too much of a bother. Did you have a decent sleep as well?" @@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Tell her it was fine and try to cover up your morning wood |WalkOutErection2]]</div>\ <<set $nyxaState to "romanced">>@@.playerSpeech;"Don't worry. A bit hard to sleep, but it wasn't a bother." @@ You turn around, trying your best to conceal the tent in your underwear while you grab a pair of pants and start putting them on. You hope the conversation will help keep your her thoughts on other things. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Yeah... I was under the impression that I move a lot... during..."@@ Her speech slows down as she notices the... prominent bulge in your underwear, her cheeks gaining in a matter of seconds. So that was the odd scent her sensitive nose was picking up... @@.nyxaSpeech;"U-um. $playerName?"@@ She lets out a nervous chuckle, as she tries not to stare too hard. Even in the darkness, her eyes are <i>very</i> obvious. @@.playerSpeech;"Ah, shit. Sorry, just a... natural morning occurrence, I was trying to avoid waking you until I could get something on."@@ She chuckles again, your reaction more entertaining than the actual issue. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Y-yeah, I know that. But... what, is that because of me?~" @@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Say nothing |SayNothingNyxa]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Say you had a dream about her |DreamNyxa]]</div>\A bit embarrassed at the whole situation, you decide to keep quiet, not really knowing how to respond. She gets up from the bed and gives you a gentle, playful nudge, smirking. @@.nyxaSpeech;"I'm just messing with you, I know that's normal. It's just funny seeing you all embarrassed, for a change."@@ Again, no offense seems to have been taken, and that makes you feel a bit more at ease. @@.playerSpeech;"Heh. Yeah, guess that's payback, huh?" @@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |SayNothingNyxa2]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"Actually... well, I can't deny it. I had a vivid dream about you, and the way you were pressed against me was enough to... well, make it happen." @@ As much as you are worried about her reaction, Nyxa still seems uncharacteristically okay with the situation, maybe even a bit flattered. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Really? |NyxaGenericDayNight3]]</div> <<default>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Just have a chat with her |NyxaGenericDay]]</div> <</switch>> <<if $fromPortal is false>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Nevermind... |Hunters Hall]]</div> <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Nevermind... |PortalRoom]]</div> <</if>>\ <</nobr>>You kinda feel the urge to return and try to chat a bit more with the vixen, but you ultimately decide against it. If she is busy with her work, you would probably just end up bothering her... and you don't have any medical emergency to justify a visit. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |Hunters Hall]]</div>\The death of the healer of the order quickly becomes known, even if no one really seems to grieve her. Apparently, she killed herself with some kind of poison, not long after you left her room. Her suicide is something that seems within the realm of the possibility, and while, if that was it, no one would suspect foul play... there are a couple key details that in the end, come to bite you in the ass. It is not hard to realize that she was abused, and after that... its only a matter of hours before you find a couple of hunters coming to apprehend you. @@.hunterSpeech;"Hey, in my opinion you did good, $playerName. Bitch deserved everything that came her way... and hopefully we'll get the healer we deserve. Lauren however, well... she's pretty damn pissed, so... yeah, sorry bud."@@ While they themselves seem to cheerish your crime, they still have to carry out the law of the order, and a swift execution takes place shortly after. <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Continue...">> <<goto "Ending6">> <</link>>\ </div>\ You make your way to the infirmary, and sure enough, Nyxa is there to receive you... a smile on her face even if you can't really see it. Her work requires concentration and composure, and she is still not ready to share her emotions with the rest of the world. @@.nyxaSpeech;"$playerName! Good to see you."@@ You are happy to see her as well, but you know you can't pester her too much during the day. She is on duty, after all... and she knows that as well. @@.nyxaSpeech;"... though maybe, I'll see you at the end of the day? We can share a meal or... well, perhaps something else~" @@ A grin grows on your face. She wants to be around you, and you are not going to say no to that. <<if $fromPortal is false>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |Hunters Hall]]</div> <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |PortalRoom]]</div> <</if>>\You make your way to the infirmary, and sure enough, Nyxa is there to receive you... a smile on her face even if you can't really see it. Her work requires concentration and composure, and she is still not ready to share her emotions with the rest of the world. @@.nyxaSpeech;"$playerName! Good to see you."@@ You are happy to see her as well, but you know you can't pester her too much during the day. She is on duty, after all... and she knows that as well. @@.nyxaSpeech;"... though maybe, I'll see you at the end of the day? We can share a meal or something~" @@ A grin grows on your face. She wants to be around you, and you are not going to say no to that. <<if $fromPortal is false>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |Hunters Hall]]</div> <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |PortalRoom]]</div> <</if>>\You make your way to the infirmary, looking to talk with Nyxa about the little... incident. Perhaps she will understand, or forgive you... The door is sealed shut, and when you knock, no response comes. @@.playerSpeech;"Nyxa? You there?" @@ Her voice, cold as ice, comes from the other side. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Leave. Now."@@ You are not welcome here.... <<if $fromPortal is false>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |Hunters Hall]]</div> <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |PortalRoom]]</div> <</if>>\You make your way to the infirmary, where Nyxa is working on some potions, looking to talk with her about the little... incident in the woods. She looks up at you, clad in full armor as always. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Oh, hey."@@ You try to spark a little conversation, but Nyxa seems to be entirely uninterested, avoiding you or shooting you short, curt answers. @@.playerSpeech;"Is it about what happened with the wolf?"@@ She stops for a moment, hesitating, then continues mixing her ingredients, refusing to look at you. @@.nyxaSpeech;"Look, I... I know it's your duty. It's fine. I just... I don't think I want any more part in it. I have enough with my own responsibilities."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |Hunters Hall]]</div>\You make your way to the infirmary, and sure enough, Nyxa is there to receive you... a smile on her face even if you can't really see it. Her work requires concentration and composure, and she is still not ready to share her emotions with the rest of the world. @@.nyxaSpeech;"$playerName! Good to see you."@@ You are happy to see her as well, but you know you can't pester her too much during the day. She is on duty, after all... and she knows that as well. @@.nyxaSpeech;"... though maybe, I'll see you at the end of the day? We can share a meal or... well, perhaps something else~" @@ A grin grows on your face. She wants to be around you, and you are not going to say no to that. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |Hunters Hall]]</div>\Nyxa didn't seem to be entirely on her right mind... maybe too much work, maybe some of her old scars resurfacing. You decide against bothering her anymore, for now at least... <<if $fromPortal is false>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |Hunters Hall]]</div> <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |PortalRoom]]</div> <</if>>\@@.title;SITE ██@@ <<set $sixEightJobState to 2>>\ <<set $playerEnergy to "tired">>\ <<set $sixEightStone to false>>\ <<set $temporaryImpregnations to $impregnations >>\ <<set $temporaryKills to $playerKills >>\ <<set $angered682 to false>>\ <<if $eyeSeeing >=3>>\ <div class="passCheck">\ Alarms blared, emergency lights, fed from a separate power supply pulsed along the corridors, flashing on and off. Panic ensued as the occupants of Site ██ scurried like ants, some trying to flee, others trying to hide, a few brave ones trying to stop what was happening. Scientists, guards, and administrative staff alike, for this containment breach meant only one thing for them all: Death. They knew it, and even if being incinerated by the highly volatile thermobaric explosives now about to send the whole place to hell was not a particularly pleasant prospect -Many of them had families or loved ones and were at least a little reluctant to die - The alternative wasn't any better. They were all doomed, the cascade of events could only result in the destruction of the site and every single one of its workforce, one way or the other. And if any of the threats contained within managed to escape and reach the outside world, it would spell death to hundred, perhaps thousands of innocent souls. Site ██, thankfully, didn't host too many world-ending horrors; mainly built to contain SCP-682 away to safely study ways to neutralize the monster if push came to shove, but it, like every other facility in the Foundation, was host to a myriad of otherworldly things that were not to be underestimated... especially if they ended up in the wrong hands. The whole place had to be sacrificed, for that was the only way to ensure their many enemies would never get a hold of what lay within its walls. Dr. ███ remained on her chair, glancing at the monitor that was still showing the inside of the cell block where the creature she had just been trying to interview had escaped. That reptile, the T-rex sized monstrosity that was now free and rampaging through the facility. Had she overstepped her bounds? Had she gotten too cocky, or perhaps she had done something wrong in the interrogation process, which could have possibly upset the beast? The man besides her seemed to read her mind. @@.drSpeech;"682 has always been an unpredictable, hostile piece of shit. You did good, Dr. ███. What we have learned here may prove... sufficient, to help the foundation get rid of this pest, once and for all."@@ She nodded absent mindedly, the words bringing her little comfort when she knew the price she and everyone else at the site would have to pay, soon enough. @@.drSpeech;"But now we need to go." @@ he told her, pulling her hand gently. She however remained seated, staring at the screen that had gone dark. Like a captain watching her sinking ship. @@.drSpeech;"There is no point, we wouldn't make it out of the blast zone. I'm sorry it ends this way."@@ Her colleague, the man she had been sharing the last two months with, just slumped back into his own chair. She was probably right. @@.drSpeech;"It's not how I would have liked it to go. But can't say I didn't imagine this is how it could end..."@@ A moment of silence followed, as the screams and cries outside the control room grew more distant, faded. @@.drSpeech;"Dr. ███? It's... been an honor."@@ <img src="Images/Icons/Eye.png"></div>\ <</if>> The void around you fades at last, and when the world comes back into focus, you find yourself standing in a moon-bathed desert, sand and dunes as far as the eye could see, under a sky filled with bright stars. In front of you, a blasted, mangled set of steel ruins, so utterly ruined and broken there is no possible way of telling what they were supposed to be. The iron and concrete have fused together in spots, melted and hardened to give way to a twisted, surreal, and nightmarish sculpture. Whatever has happened here, it has happened recently, for the structure is still hot to the touch. Perhaps some sort of arcane, destructive ritual gone horribly wrong? Whatever the case, this is surely related to your prey, must be. Would be an odd coincidence, otherwise. You try to recall Vael's words, was this creature capable of using magic in this quantity? He has made no mention of such a thing, but he also warned you about the creature's power... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Trudge over the ruins |TrudgeOverRuins]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Dig through the debris |DigOverDebris]]</div>\Your prey must be here, and even if, most likely, she has already been turned to ash by whatever happened here, you feel like you should at least give it a try. What if, indeed, she is the responsible for this... devastation? You are not sure you'd like that better, if that were the case, you would be straight up walking into the maw of the beast. But regardless, you just need to see for yourself, and so you trudge through the debris.... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Descend deeper into the ruin |DescendDeeperComplex]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Conclude that, most likely, your prey is already dead |NothingSurvived]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTeleport.png"></div>\ Your prey is near, and yet you can't see her... perhaps underneath the pile of rubble? If that is the case, you would imagine she is nothing but a charred and burned mess, dead for sure, but you feel like you have to at least confirm that suspicion. And so, you spend the next minutes digging through the debris, flipping over rocks, pieces of concrete and melted steel. You... don't have much luck, not finding any sort of body or even the smallest hint of your prey. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Trudge over the ruins |TrudgeOverRuins]]</div>\ As you move forward, careful not to slip on the unstable terrain, a sound catches your ear. At first, you think it's the wind blowing over the rocks and ruins, but as you listen closer, you realize there is something more to it. Its an almost organic sound, like that of flesh and sinew, rubbing and sliding against one another. The sound grows louder and louder as you press forward, and before long, you are able to make out the noise. It is definitely not the wind, for as you walk over a particularly big panel of steel, the source of the strange noises finally comes into view. Deep below the surface, in the blasted depths, an enormous figure struggles to pick herself up. And even with just the moonlight illuminating her, what you see is... unsettling. The creature seems to be in a terrible shape, with gaping wounds and burns covering her whole body, her skin blackened and crisped at some points, her bones showing through. <img src="Images/Monsters/682Fixed.png" class="resizable-image" alt="682"> Despite all of that... she seems to be healing, <i>extremely</i> quickly. Under your astonished gaze, the wounded creature managing to pull herself out from the wreckage and onto her four clawed limbs. She looks around, palpable satisfaction in her eyeless face... satisfaction at having outlived her jailers, at having brought their pathetic facility to the ground. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Grab your sword and try to finish her off quickly |Kill682quickly]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Calmly make your way towards her |CalmlyMakeWayDown]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCharisma.png"></div>\Let's face it, if she was here, and by the looks of it she was, there is no way she has survived the devastation, right? You'll tell Vael she's dead, even if he insisted she was a rather... sturdy individual. <<set $sixEightJobState to "dead">>\ <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png" ><<link "Continue">> <<set $enterFromVoid = true>>\ <<goto "PortalRoom">>\ <</link>>\ </div>\It's frightening, the speed at which she is recovering, you don't want to imagine how strong she must be already, or what she is truly capable of. The best solution is to finish her off before she gets a chance to fully recover, and some questionable... protrusions in her chest are not going to change that. You pull out your blade and charge her, trudging through the rubble towards her. She seems to have noticed your approach, and turns her head towards you with uninterested curiosity and with palpable hatred. Just another insect to crush, yet another human-shaped nuisance... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Get ready for the fight |Getreadyfight]]</div>\This is, without a doubt, the monster you have been searching for... and while the sight of her flesh slowly regrowing and knitting itself back together is certainly disturbing, you are not too afraid. Perhaps the creature is intelligent; perhaps you can try to reason with her? The huge pair of breasts on her chest may be her only feminine attribute, but its more than enough for you to try. You make your way towards the creature, your boots crunching loudly as pieces of shattered concrete crumble beneath them. The monster, whose attention had been directed elsewhere, now snaps her head in your direction. The eyeless gaze of the creature locks on you as she seizes you up and down, but her face betrays no emotion other than cold disgust. @@.s682Speech;"Don't recall... seeing you." @@ the monster rumbles, her deep voice echoing around the ruins. @@.s682Speech;"How unfortunate... that you're not one of those researchers... I'd enjoy having your blood on my claws." @@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Get ready for the fight |Getreadyfight]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Talk to her, you mean her no harm |MeanNoHarm682]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCharisma.png"></div>\The reptile charges towards you, her massive body building up speed with each passing moment, her claws digging through the dirt and rubble, sending the pieces flying in all directions. It is now, as she is approaching, that you realize just how massive this creature is, all on fours as she is, she already dwarfs you. Hard to tell when her features blur into a shapeless mass of scales and spikes, but she must be at least twice your stature; a veritable tank of muscles, claws and teeth... this is indeed a contract worthy of a High Hunter... she is quite a bit faster than you expected. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Charge back at her |Fight682]]<div class="numberStat">4</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Flee |RunAway682]]<div class="numberStat">2</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\You raise your hands in a calming gesture and speak up, hoping to appear as non-threatening as possible. @@.playerSpeech;"I am not here to harm you. I want to talk."@@ She scoffs at your words, taking a step closer, her claws sinking into the soft, pulverized ground. @@.s682Speech;"You cannot harm me. And I have endured your... repugnant species long enough."@@ Another step forward. Even slightly injured still, she doesn't seem to be intimidated by you at all. @@.s682Speech;"The only sound... I will permit from your lips is the final gasp of breath, as the last vestiges of your life slip away." @@ You gulp, her words not very reassuring... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Insist. You could help her |Help682]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[You do not wish to harm her, you have come here to fuck her |MakeAware682]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Get ready for the fight |Getreadyfight]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\You take a deep breath, steadying your voice, trying to remain calm and collected, despite the clear threat. @@.playerSpeech;"I will not harm you, I'm not sure what happened here, but I want to help. From the looks of it, you were being held prisoner, and I... understand your desire for revenge."@@ The creature scoffs again, a cruel, humorless smirk twisting her lipless face. @@.s682Speech;You take pride in your supposed understanding... the illusion of control. But now, here, you can see how futile your attempts at comprehension are. You can feel how fragile your power really is."@@ The monster takes another step towards you, and then another. @@.s682Speech;"I will kill you. I will not even remember you. Just another corpse."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Get ready for the fight |Getreadyfight]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\There is no point in beating around the bush. What's the worst that could happen at this point, the creature seems decided to kill you regardless. You stand your ground as the massive, naked female draws closer, scales glistening under the moonlight... and let your gaze wander towards her breasts, slowly jiggling with each step she takes. @@.playerSpeech;"I will be honest with you. I'm not here to fight... or to try and bring you back to wherever you came from. I'm here... because I'm interested in you, your body. I wish to mate with you."@@ That does make her stop her advance, if only for a moment. In her eyeless face, the grimace of utter disgust shines through, the thought so vile it actually manages to offend her. For all the time she has been forced to spend with humans, being interogated and tested, this is the first time anyone has shown such a revolting, unadulterated interest in her... and it makes her sick to her core. @@.s682Speech;"How utterly revolting, how disgusting. I would have merely crushed. A quick, efficient death. But now... I think I will take my time with you, human. I will carve out your entrails, break you apart... limb from limb." @@ Well... it seems like the fight was inevitable. Only now, the monster looks even more intent on killing you. <<set $angered682 to true>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Get ready for the fight |Getreadyfight]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\This creature is beyond the scope of a normal contract, and as the charging mass of muscle and claws draws ever closer, you have the clarity of mind to recognize your own limits. You are not sticking around to see what this thing can do... And so you turn on your heel and sprint, climbing up through the mountain of debris and rubble as fast as your legs allow. The ground beneath you shakes as the monster follows close behind, the heavy thumping of her feet and the clatter of rock and steel sending chills down your spine... <<if $hunterSkill >=2>>\ <div class="passCheck">\ Luckily, the creature seems to be too big, too heavy, to follow you through the mountain of rubble, and you manage to reach the top without getting ripped to shreds. You glance back and see the creature struggling to push past a particularly unstable wall, trying her best to follow, her claws sinking into the crumbling mass... and roaring in anger as her target escapes. <img src="Images/Icons/General.png"></div>\ You will live to fight another day... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |RunAway682-1]]</div>\ <<else>>\ <div class="failCheck">\ The creature is fast, much faster than you had anticipated, and before you can make your way up, her hand wraps around your ankle and pulls you back, making you lose balance and dragging you across the debris, slamming your body against every single protruding rock. <img src="Images/Icons/General.png"></div>\ It hurts; bones breaking, muscles torn, you feel the warmth of blood oozing out of the countless cuts and gashes that litter your body. <<if $angered682 is true>>\ The creature is not done yet, as she drags you across the floor, lifting you up and slamming your back into the nearest wall. @@.s682Speech;"You wished to mate with me? Like a wriggling maggot, you squirm and writhe. You will pay, for your... audacity." @@ True to her word, she takes her time killing you. With little effort, she grabs hold of your right arm and yanks, pulling it away from your body in one swift motion. You scream in agony as the pain shoots through your body, the mere instant it lasts before blood loss and shock stretching into a small eternity. Then, the monster moves on, taking hold of the rest of your body, dismembering you piece by piece, until your consciousness finally leaves you. <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Death embraces you...">> <<goto "Death">> <</link>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png" >\ </div>\ <</if>>\Leaving the creature behind, you get more and more distance between the two of you, hoping that the travelstone will deplete whatever energy it has left and you'll be safely returned to your home. You have not been near enough of your target for this to have worked, but you'd much rather hold on to your breath... <<set $sixEightJobState to "escaped">>\ <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png" ><<link "Continue">> <<set $enterFromVoid = true>>\ <<goto "PortalRoom">>\ <</link>>\ </div>\ <div id="progressWrapper"> <div id="hpContainer"> <div id="hpLabel">HP: </div> <div id="hpBar"> </div> </div> <div id="exhaustionContainer"> <div id="exhaustionLabel">Exhaustion: </div> <div id="exhaustionBar"> </div> </div> </div> The fight starts, you are fighting X monster... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go talk with her |Try][$currentMonsterHp -=30]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\ <div id="progressWrapper"> <div id="hpContainer"> <div id="hpLabel">HP: </div> <div id="hpBar"> </div> </div> <div id="exhaustionContainer"> <div id="exhaustionLabel">Exhaustion: </div> <div id="exhaustionBar"> </div> </div> </div><div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Deliver a swift strike with your Essence Feaster |BladeAttack]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Use your Essence Feaster and unleash red lightning |Lightning Attack]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\You have never been one to take a defensive stance. Even if the monster is far more physically imposing, the quicker you subdue her, the less chances she'll have to hurt you. And so, with a snarl, you charge back, no fear of death clouding your judgment. The creature is momentarily shocked by your bold reaction, which is enough for you to find an opening and deliver the first slash to one of her arms. <<if $hunterSkill <=3>>\ <div class="failCheck">\ However... that's about all you are able to do. The slash on her arm means nothing more than an instant of pain for the monster... who is quick to retaliate, slashing at you with her other arm. <img src="Images/Icons/FailGeneral.png"></div>\ Your armor absorbs the worst of the attack, but the strength behind the strike is enough to send you stumbling backward, the pain spreading throughout your chest. And before you can recover, the reptile has already lunged again, with unnatural agility and grace despite her size. Her weight crushes you into the ground, knocking the wind out of you... and breaking a good deal of your ribs in the process. She could bite at your throat, rip your head out of your shoulders... but it seems she opts for a more hygienic approach. Nothing would displease her more than having your revolting blood dripping down her jaws. <<if $angered682 is true>>\ <div class="failCheck">\ And she is not going to let your previous comment slide. True to her word, she takes her sweet, sweet time with you, tearing off your limbs like wet paper, her powerful arms separating them with a single flex. You can do nothing but howl as your bones and muscle are torn apart, the pain blinding, the agony unbearable. <img src="Images/Icons/FailCharisma.png"></div>\ Even after you are gone, death having relieved you of your suffering, the monster keeps going, ripping you apart until you are nothing more than a broken, bloody pulp. <<else>>\ With a quick stomp, the monster crushes your skull into paste, splattering blood and brains all around the place. You are dead before the pain has the chance to fully register... <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Death embraces you...">> <<goto "Death">> <</link>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png" >\ </div>\ <<else>> The beast hisses, enraged, and is quick to retaliate against your attack a speed that betrays her size. You manage to sidestep the oncoming slam just in time, the clawed hand sending shrapnel flying everywhere as it crashes into the ground, mere inches away from you. You take advantage of these openings, delivering a painful, strenght-draining slash everytime you can, but the monster is relentless in her attempts to crush you, no amount of wounds or pain slowing her down. The fight goes on for full minutes, and exhaustion begins to take a toll on you. The creature is bleeding and bruised; patches of skin are missing and her body is littered with wounds, and yet... her flesh keeps knitting itself back together at an incredible pace. It's as if the wounds you are inflicting on her have no effect whatsoever, the damage healing before your very eyes. <div class="passCheck">\ However... the strength-draining effect your blade has seems to be working. The creature's movements are less coordinated, more sluggish, and slow, her attacks losing strength and momentum. Granted, a single swipe of her tail would still have enough force to shatter every single bone in your body, but these seem to be happening less often. <img src="Images/Icons/General.png"><img src="Images/Icons/FailGeneral.png"></div>\ @@.s682Speech;"Cease... your movement!" @@ the monster huffs, slamming her two forearms into the ground, a tiny crater forming where they hit. She then lunges, mouth snapping open with killing intent. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Hold on just a little longer... |Fight682-1]]</div>\ <</if>>\The monster's hide writhes as it heals, her flesh regenerating in real time. No matter how many cuts and slashes you deliver, it's impossible to make any progress. However... she does seem to be tired. The sword is draining her strength. Problem is, she doesn't really have to put any real effort into dodging your attacks, but you absolutely need to stay focused to evade hers. The fight stretches on for several more minutes, and the monster seems to get even more frustrated, now noticing how she is beginning to lose strength and stamina, while you are doing just fine. @@.s682Speech;"Die! Why won't you... die!" @@ <div class="passCheck">\ Finally, as she slams her claws into the ground in yet another failed attempt to crush you, she seems to run out of steam, fully stopping for a moment. It's all you need to sink your sword in her chest and deliver a blast so strong the creature loses her footing and crashes onto her side, a few feet away from you. <img src="Images/Icons/General.png"></div>\ From there, you let the red lightning snake and crackle, binding the noticeably angry monster and draining her remaining power.... For now at least. Seething with hatred, the reptile ceases her struggle, not wanting to debase herself further. It's embarrassing enough having lost this fight. @@.s682Speech;"This... Is meaningless. I am beyond mortality, beyond life. This... <i>victory</i> means nothing"@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Take a chunk of her flesh, as per Vael's request |TakeChunkVael]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedItem.png"></div>\You had been told that this particular creature was capable of healing almost instantly, but a piece of her remarkably tough hide is all you need.
