With a grunt, you slide out of her, and take a step back. It was getting unbearably hot down there, and the fresh breeze is much welcomed. @@.playerSpeech;"Well then... I guess this is a goodbye. Try not to let other mortals kick your ass, will you? I would like to get another piece of this tail~" @@ <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png" ><<link "Continue">> <<set $enterFromVoid = true>>\ <<goto "PortalRoom">>\ <</link>>\ </div>\You decide to accept, even if this doesn't exactly sound like a walk in the park. <div class="passCheck">\ A travelstone is your method of transport. <img src="Images/Icons/Travelstone.png"></div>\ @@.vaelSpeech;"It is attuned to its essence. Bring me back the sample when you have it." @@ <<set $sixEightStone to true>> <<set $sixEightJobState to "accepted">> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |VaelIntroReduced]]</div>\You decide to pass on the offer, this sounds too dangerous. Vael nods, accepting your decision. He will find someone else to carry out his plans. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |VaelIntroReduced]]</div>\<<switch $sixEightJobState>>\ <<case "big">>\ You bring Vael a the sample you so happily retrieved, a big, heavy, writhing mass of flesh that he greedily takes from your hands. He seems very pleased with your sadistic efforts, a rare grin on his metallic face. @@.vaelSpeech;" Yes, this will be more than enough. Thank you for your assistance... and a due reward, of course. " @@ He places a hefty pouch in your hand, a large sum of money that will serve you well. @@.vaelSpeech;"You seem to have gone out of your way to acquire such a big sample. Your efforts have not gone unnoticed. For now... I shall grant you a small boon." @@ He leaves the forge for a moment, and when he is back, a small, odd effigy can be seen in his hands. Doesn't look like much, but he seems very proud of it, and you are not about to antagonize him by showing it any disrespect. @@.vaelSpeech;"This is a relic from the past. I crafted it myself, many years ago. I don't believe in luck, but this will offer you some... protection." @@ You accept the gift, even if you do no know what the effigy will do. Does Vael even know what it does? <<set $vaelEffigy to 1>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue (300)|VaelIntroReduced][$money += 300]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\ <<case "small">>\ You bring Vael a small sample of the creature. The chunk is rather unimpressive; merely a small strip of scaled skin, and Vael seems... disappointed. @@.vaelSpeech;"You could have retrieved a larger sample. This hardly contains enough material for my work."@@ He lets out a tired sight, it is what it is. Maybe he should have gone himself, or perhaps sent someone else. Storing the sample in a flask, he pulls out a handful of coins, placing them in your palm. @@.vaelSpeech;"Your payment."@@ Barely 10 gold coins, a paltry amount for what was a dangerous mission. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Complain, ask for more |MoreMoneyVael]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Fair enough... |MoreMoneyVael][$money += 10]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\ <<case "medium">>\ You bring Vael a sample of the creature. It is a heavy chunk of meat, a large, bloody chunk that seems to writhe and pulse as you hand it over. Vael seems very pleased with the sample, his lenses gleaming with anticipation as he reaches to take it from you. @@.vaelSpeech;"Excellent. Yes, this will be more than enough for my work. Allow me to pay you for your efforts." @@ He hands you a large pouch of coins, the clinking sound of the money a welcome one. @@.playerSpeech;"Pleasure doing business with you. Let me know if you have anything else for me." @@ @@.vaelSpeech;"I will. I have great plans, and perhaps you will be worthy to play a role in them after all..." @@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue (250)|VaelIntroReduced][$money += 250]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\ <</switch>>\ <<set $sixEightJobState to "completed">>You shake your head, the money is a joke. @@.playerSpeech;"Really? I was nearly killed retrieving that sample, and all I get is this?" @@ He turns away from you, not even bothering to keep eye contact. @@.vaelSpeech;"Consider yourself fortunate you got anything at all. You brought me scraps, and scraps is what I pay you." @@ Fair enough, you guess. With a grunt, you shove the money into your pocket and leave. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |VaelIntroReduced][$money +=10]]</div>\<<set $vaelEffigy to 0>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedLevelUp.png">[[Start with +2 |StartingGift][$hunterSkill +=2]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedLevelUp.png">[[Start with +2 in |StartingGift][$charismaSkill +=2]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCharisma.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedLevelUp.png">[[Start with +2 in |StartingGift][$cumSkill +=2]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedPreg.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedLevelUp.png">[[Start with +2 in |StartingGift][$eyeSeeing +=2]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedEye.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedLevelUp.png">[[Start with +2 in |StartingGift][$magicSkill +=2]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedMagic.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedLevelUp.png">[[Start with +2 in |StartingGift][$healSkill +=2]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedHeal.png"></div>\ Perhaps this creature is innocent, perhaps she is not. It doesn't matter, for you have the duty to eliminate her... if not for your order, then for the goddess of death. <div class="passCheck">\ Your sword cuts through her soft, pale flesh, a spurt of blood gushing out as her neck is severed, a profound silence spreading through the forest as her body collapses. <img src="Images/Icons/Devotion.png"></div>\ Your benefactor is satisfied. <<set $playerKills to $temporaryKills +1 >>\ <<set $killedDeer to true>>\ <<set $deerJobState to 2>>\ <<set $playerEnergy to "tired">> <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png" ><<link "Leave">> <<set $enterFromVoid = true>>\ <<goto "PortalRoom">>\ <</link>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedTeleport.png">\ </div>\The Eye has already marked her twice, leaving her alive could be quite a big mistake considering it could happen again. And besides... who are you to deny your benefactor the soul of this creature, who already defied death once? <div class="passCheck">\ She lowers her head, her eyes closing tightly, as she awaits the final blow. Your blade, swiftly drawn, cuts clean through her throat, severing the connection between her body and head. <img src="Images/Icons/Devotion.png"></div>\ <<set $playerKills to $temporaryKills +1 >>\ <<set $killedWolyrn to true>>\ <<set $wolyrnJobState to 2>>\ <<set $playerEnergy to "tired">> <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png" ><<link "Return">> <<set $enterFromVoid = true>>\ <<goto "PortalRoom">>\ <</link>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedTeleport.png">\ </div>\Perhaps this creature is innocent, perhaps she is not. It doesn't matter, for you have the duty to eliminate her... if not for your order, then for the goddess of death. <div class="passCheck">\ Your sword cuts through her soft flesh, a spurt of blood gushing out as her neck is severed, a profound silence spreading through the forest as her body collapses. <img src="Images/Icons/Devotion.png"></div>\ Your benefactor is... satisfied. <<set $playerKills to $temporaryKills +1 >>\ <<set $wolfJobState to 2>> <<set $playerEnergy to "tired">> <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png" ><<link "Continue">> <<set $enterFromVoid = true>>\ <<goto "PortalRoom">>\ <</link>>\ </div>\Perhaps this creature is innocent, perhaps she is not. It doesn't matter, for you have the duty to eliminate her... if not for your order, then for the goddess of death. <div class="passCheck">\ There is nothing she can do to stop you, and a quick swing of your weapon is enough to end her life. <img src="Images/Icons/Devotion.png"></div>\ <<set $playerKills to $temporaryKills +1 >>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Return to your dimension |ReturnWyvernBad]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Take the egg |TakeEggDead]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedItem.png"></div>\Perhaps they are innocent, perhaps they are not. It doesn't matter, for you have a duty... to end them. If not for your order, then for the goddess of death. <div class="passCheck">\ You raise the Essence Feaster high over your head, the cursed blade pulsating with a dark glow as you ready yourself to deliver the final blow, and drive it down with full force. Your sword sinks deep into his skull, and almost immediately, the light fades from his eyes. <img src="Images/Icons/Devotion.png"></div>\ Silence follows for an instant, and then a bloodcurdling scream pierces the air. The white dragon manages to break free from the chains, the pain and suffering of her mate's death giving her a boost of adrenaline that breaks through the magic restraining her. Still, the spell has done its job, and she can barely put up a fight, her moves sloppy and uncoordinated. Her homicidal determination is in complete opposition to the pitiful state she is in... it almost makes you feel sorry for her. Almost. Soon enough, she will be reunited with her beloved... so there's no need to hold back. ... <div class="passCheck">\ Her corpse hits the ground with a thud, and you allow yourself to admire your work for a moment, basking in the feeling of a successful hunt, and a successful act of devotion... <img src="Images/Icons/Devotion.png"></div>\ <<set $playerKills to $temporaryKills +2 >>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Return to your dimension |ReturnFuryBad]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTeleport.png"></div>\ <<set $killedFury to true>>\<<if $darkDevotion < 5>>\ You raise your sword, pointing it towards her. To that, Naafiri just lets out a laugh, and concentrates on the rest of the pack. @@.naafiriSpeech;"Kill. Leave nothing behind."@@ The whole swarm of hounds, including Naafiri this time, pounces on you, the combined fury of the Darkin overwhelming you in but an instant. Shards of tainted daggers pierce through your armor and into your chest, claws tear at your flesh, and teeth rip your limbs to shreds. <div class="failCheck">\ A human on their own has no chance of defeating a Darkin. You will always be prey, never a true match for these powerful predators... <img src="Images/Icons/FailGeneral.png"><img src="Images/Icons/FailDevotion.png"></div>\ <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Death embraces you...">> <<goto "Death">> <</link>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png" >\ </div>\ <<else>>\ You raise your sword, pointing it towards her. To that, Naafiri just lets out a laugh and concentrates on the rest of the pack. @@.naafiriSpeech;"Kill. Leave nothing behind."@@ The whole swarm of hounds, including Naafiri this time pounces on you. A turmoil of teeth, claws, and blades... as the combined power of the darkin is brought to bear on you. An ancient force, corrupted but deadly, one that a human would have no chance to best on their own, no matter their training, their abilities. <div class="passCheck">\ But as the onslaught begins, and you are forced to dance to their tune, evading and countering their moves, you can feel her presence, her dark aura. It envelops you, and as you move, it is as if every move, every action was planned. <img src="Images/Icons/Devotion.png"></div>\ You are a fitting champion, and there is no greater act of devotion towards the dark lady than reminding those who cannot die of their mortality. Your blade, imbued with a dark energy that eclipses its usual crimson shine, slices through the swarm with ease, corpse after corpse piling on the ground until only Naafiri remains, looking incredulous at the sight before her. It's just her against you now, but she still shows no fear. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |EndNaafiri2]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\ <</if>>\@@.naafiriSpeech;"Perhaps I underestimated you, human. You may be a worthy meal, after all."@@ She rushes at you, and this time, her attacks are different, more calculated. There is no frenzied flurry of strikes, instead, she is patient, precise. She's waiting for a mistake, hoping to tire you out. Unfortunately for her, she's met her match. You don't give her time to wear you out, your attacks swift, fast, and unnaturally deadly. <div class="passCheck">\ A mere graze from your blade in her left shoulder is enough to cripple her, the death magic from your patron coursing through the cursed steel and flesh that make up her body. And it hurts, a lot. <img src="Images/Icons/General.png"><img src="Images/Icons/Devotion.png"></div>\ It's then that a thought occurs to her; she might actually die here. She may actually return to her smothering prison, the weapon that for so long had bound her. Begging for mercy is a disgrace, an affront to her pride... but the thought of another untold eon of confinement is terrifying as well. The only option left is to flee, even if it means a shameful retreat. Tail between legs, the darkin flees, limping and alone, leaving behind the remnants of her slaughtered pack, of herself. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Leave her be... |LeaveNaafiriFlee]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Chase after her |ChaseNaafiri]]</div>\You watch her run away, a feeling of satisfaction at having beaten her, but having no desire to pursue her. You have proven yourself, and her pack has been decimated. No point in risking further injuries in a fruitless chase... but rest assured, your patron will not be happy with this wasted opportunity. <<set $darkDevotion to 0>> <<set $naafiriJobState to 2>>\ <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png" ><<link "Continue">> <<set $enterFromVoid = true>>\ <<goto "PortalRoom">>\ <</link>>\ </div>\You can't let her escape, not when you've come so close to ending her. <div class="passCheck">\ The darkin doesn't look back as you give chase, focused solely on escaping with her life. She is twice your size, and, on all fours, she should easily outrun you. Not what it happens—despite her superior speed, you're gaining ground. It's as if the shadows themselves are carrying you forward, propelling you with unnatural swiftness. You feel the cold, reassuring presence of your dark patron, her power flowing through your veins, guiding your steps. <img src="Images/Icons/General.png"><img src="Images/Icons/Devotion.png"></div>\ Naafiri’s panic grows as she glances back, her eyes widening with disbelief. How could a mere mortal be closing in on her? The dark magic coursing through your blade hums with lethal intent, and with every step, you can feel her growing fear. @@.naafiriSpeech;"No... this can’t..."@@ Her voice trembles, the proud, bloodthirsty warrior now a cornered animal. She stumbles, her once graceful strides now desperate, clumsy. She turns, baring her fangs, but it’s clear that she knows she's lost. @@.naafiriSpeech;"Stop! I yield... I... I surrender."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Death is the only answer |KillNaafiri][$darkDevotion +=1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedDevotion.png"></div>\You keep advancing, without hesitation, your eyes locked on Naafiri as she stumbles and pleads. Her once-proud figure, now broken and desperate, does nothing to deter you. In fact, it only stokes the dark flame within you, the pull of your patron urging you toward a single conclusion. Her end. The shadows around you grow thicker, clinging to your figure, making you appear more monstrous than human. Your blade, still humming with lethal energy, drips with malice as you close the distance. And this time, you don't miss your mark. A single thrust pierces through Naafiri's chest, magic surging into her body like a torrent of death. Her eyes widen in shock and pain as the energy tears through her, unraveling her very being from the inside out. When it is all done, and the cloud of shadow dissipates, only a crimson dagger remains on the ground. Clearly a living thing, the weapon that now holds Naafiri's essence pulses faintly with the last remnants of her existence. You also notice something unsettling—a small, bloodshot eye embedded in the blade. It twitches and blinks, darting nervously around as if trying to make sense of its new reality... one so familiar and so terrific she had hoped to never experience again. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Bury the dagger in the sand |KillNaafiri2]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Pick up the dagger |KeepNaafiri]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedItem.png"></div>\As if to do a final insult to the defeated creature, you kick the dagger and bury it into the sand, ensuring it would stay forgotten in the dunes. The eye in the dagger flutters frantically, darting in all directions as it senses its impending fate. Panic radiates from the small, bloodshot orb as it tracks your every movement, helpless in its new form. It seems to plead with you silently, its gaze wide with terror, but no words come. Only the frantic, nervous twitching of the eye as you kick the dagger towards the sand. With a satisfying crunch, the blade sinks into the dunes, swallowed by the unforgiving desert. It is the last anyone will hear of the darkin in a good while. <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png" ><<link "Continue">> <<set $enterFromVoid = true>>\ <<goto "PortalRoom">>\ <</link>>\ </div>\You reach to pick the dagger, if your sword has taught you anything is that demonic weapons can be quite useful. Maybe you could sell it to Vael, or maybe just keep it around for your personal use. However, as soon as your hand makes contact with the handle, a burning sensation runs through your arm, your veins glowing bright red as a voice whispers in your mind. <<if $magicSkill <= 7>>\ @@.naafiriSpeech;"Yes..."@@ The voice is unmistakable, Naafiri. <div class="failCheck">\ You try to let go, but your muscles are locked in place, your body unresponsive to your will. The dagger's crimson hue intensifies, and suddenly, you feel a force invading your mind, like a shadow crawling into the deepest corners of your consciousness. Naafiri’s presence, once desperate and broken, now feels overwhelming, aggressive. The burning spreads, wrapping itself around your very soul, her essence pouring into you like a torrent. <img src="Images/Icons/FailMagic.png"><img src="Images/Icons/FailMagic.png"></div>\ Your body will serve as a vessel for her to return, and your soul crushed and subsumed into her new form. <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "A most gruesome end...">> <<goto "Death">> <</link>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png" >\ </div>\ <<else>>\ The blade tries to invade you, a faint pulse of magic emanating from it. You feel the presence of Naafiri attempting to seep into your mind, her increasingly desperate voice echoing inside your head. <div class="passCheck">\ You however are trained in mental magics, and can resist her intrusion. After a brief struggle, you push the dagger back with a pulse of your own, forcing the darkin's essence out, contained within her steel prison. <img src="Images/Icons/MindMagic.png"><img src="Images/Icons/MindMagic.png"></div>\ @@.playerSpeech;"Nice try, demon. But your tricks won't work on me."@@ The dagger, unable to invade your mind, ceases its struggle and goes silent. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |ContinueNaafiri]]</div>\ <</if>><<set $darkinDagger to true>> With the dagger now securely tied to your belt, you get ready for the inevitable return. Quite a good hunt, it was. <<set $naafiriJobState to 2>>\ <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png" ><<link "Continue">> <<set $enterFromVoid = true>>\ <<goto "PortalRoom">>\ <</link>>\ </div>\@@.playerSpeech;"Took care of that hound. I couldn't fully destroy her, though. The creature seems to be locked within this now."@@ You present Damlon the dagger, the eye glancing around in confusion... in fear. It takes a second for the man to comprehend the situation, and once he does, he looks at the dagger and then back at you, respect and admiration on his face. @@.damlonSpeech;"This weapon... I didn't realize this... How... how did you even manage to fight against such a powerful being, all on your own?"@@ You shrug. You were not alone, but you are not going to tell him that. @@.damlonSpeech;"I will take care of this cursed artifact. The Order will ensure it can't escape again."@@ He puts on a special glove, protective runes glowing on it as he picks the dagger up with the upmost care. He then stores it on a secure steel box; not the final destination, but at least a more secure place to contain it for the time being. He seems to know of her corruptive capabilities, and wont make the mistake of touching the blade without protection. @@.damlonSpeech;"I promised you a reward, but after seeing what you've gone through, I believe it's only fair to offer you more than originally planned..."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |DamlonIntro][$continueDamlon= true]]</div>\ <<set $reputation to $temporaryReputation +3>> <<set $naafiriJobState to 3>> <<set $darkinDagger to false>> <<set $activeDamlonContract to "">>\@@.playerSpeech;"I have something that may be of your interest, Vael."@@ You pull out the darkin dagger, feeling its renewed struggle as she once again tries to gain control of your body... to no avail. The eye embedded in the blade darts around the room, trying to understand her surroundings. Vael's expression turns from one of mild boredom to one of intrigue, and he extends one of his metallic hands towards you. @@.vaelSpeech;"May I?"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"Its dangerous, the creature within it seems to try and manipulate the minds of whoever holds the blade."@@ Vael doesn't seem too fazed by the threat. If you can handle it, he reasons that his own will—along with his mechanical nature—will be more than enough. You place the dagger in Vael's hand. For a moment, his expression shifts; lenses dimming and flickering rapidly, as if his internal systems are struggling against some invisible force. His metal fingers tense around the hilt of the dagger, surely Naafiri is attempting to exert her influence over him. His mechanical body whirrs softly as his internal systems adapt and push back against the Darkin's influence. The flickering stops, his lenses refocus, and he remains perfectly still, standing firm against Naafiri’s attempts to control him. He smiles, as if amused by the whole situation. @@.vaelSpeech;"Trying hard, aren't we?"@@ Vael’s metallic voice hums with interest as he tightens his grip on the dagger. You can see Naafiri’s embedded eye darting around in panic. Nothing good is coming, that's for sure. @@.vaelSpeech;"A remarkable specimen, indeed. There is much we can learn from this one, I am sure of it... I will gladly buy this item from you, at a premium, of course."@@ <<set $darkinDagger to false>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |SellWares][$money += 350]]</div>\@@.vaelSpeech;"Interested in ensuring your peace of mind... I may have something for you."@@ Vael leaves for a moment to return with a dreamcatcher, one unlike any you’ve seen before. Its frame is made of polished steel, intricately wrought with delicate, yet sturdy, metalwork. The webbing is a fine, shimmering silver thread that catches the light with a subtle gleam. @@.vaelSpeech;"Enchanted and reinforced, it will protect your vulnerable mind from any unwanted visitors when you are the most vulnerable..."@@ <<if $money >= 60>><div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Buy the item (60) |BuyDreamcatcher2][$money -=60]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\<<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons">@@.fadedLink; <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Buy the item (60) |BuyDreamcatcher2][$money -=60]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\<</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |PurchaseWares]]</div>\ @@.playerSpeech;"That stone there... what is it?"@@ Vael sighs, his lenses flickering as if this is all a bit too tiresome for him. He picks up the stone with an exaggerated slowness, his metallic fingers holding it delicately. His voice is sharp, laced with mild annoyance. @@.vaelSpeech;"It’s designed to enhance lucid dreaming. Helps those with... less refined mental faculties,"@@ he cant help but to glance at you pointedly@@.vaelSpeech;"to achieve some control over their subconscious. A tool for those that seek enlightenment..."@@ <<if $money >= 40>><div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Buy the item (40) |BuyDreamstone2][$money -=40]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\<<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons">@@.fadedLink; <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Buy the item (40) |BuyDreamstone2][$money -=40]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\<</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |PurchaseWares]]</div>\ There, beneath the looming mass of flesh and steel that is Vael, you spot a curious-looking piece of jewelry, a pendant of sorts. An eye shaped in silver, it has a strange aura around it, one that makes you feel uneasy... and curious. @@.playerSpeech;"That pendant, what is it?"@@ The half-machine pauses his work tilting his head slightly as he considers your question. His lenses flicker as they adjust their focus, his mechanical features remaining expressionless. @@.vaelSpeech;"An artifact... from an older time. I cannot recommend you its purchase, however. It's magic power, albeit faint, its pervasive ... and unpredictable. I doubt it would serve your interests."@@ <<if $money >= 50>><div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Buy the item (50) |BuyPendant][$money -=50]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\<<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons">@@.fadedLink; <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Buy the item (50) |BuyPendant][$money -=50]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCoins.png"></div>\<</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back |PurchaseWares]]</div>\ A weird, wicked idea pops into your mind as you get ready to go to bed. The weapon you have carried all the way here, still pulsing faintly in a desperate attempt to take hold of you... Why don't you give her a show? You slowly, sensually, take your armor and clothes off, enjoying the sensation of the cool air on your bare skin. Naafiri’s eye twitches, darting left and right as it tracks your every movement, her confusion palpable. The faint, desperate pulsing of the dagger intensifies for a moment as if trying to figure out what on earth you're doing. The eye narrows slightly, clearly unsettled, though no words escape her. Not that she can talk in this form of course. She is alone with her thoughts, a prisoner of her cursed weapon, watching helplessly as you start to masturbate in front of her. ... Her pupil trembles a little as she realizes what is going on, shock giving way to disgust. It almost makes you feel pity for the trapped creature within... almost. Reducing her to a mere sexual object is oddly arousing, the thought of her helplessness making your member grow harder, your body shudder with pleasure. You imagine her squirming in the confines of the blade, trying to resist the perverse sight before her, knowing full well that there is nothing she can do. @@.playerSpeech;"You seemed like quite haughty a bitch, I figured you could use some humility."@@ The eye trembles a little more, her gaze focused on your member. Perhaps she is remembering what happened the last time you two fought, and her defeat at your hands. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Cum |CumDagger1]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedCum.png"></div>\With a loud grunt, you finish, the first spurts of your load landing on the blade, splattering all over the dagger's surface, covering the eye and the rest of the blade. Naafiri's eye twitches with disgust, but much to your surprise, the blade seems to absorb your cum, slowly drinking the sticky fluid. @@.playerSpeech;"You like it huh? I'll make sure to keep doing it, then~"@@ The eye in the dagger glares at you, and you can almost feel a sense of indignation, an unspoken threat. But you are not scared, not when she is so helpless, at the mercy of your perverted desires. She seems deep in thought, a faint blush on her cheeks as she scribbles something in her diary. The sound of the pen scratching against the paper is the only noise breaking the silence. With a small sigh, she carefully sets it aside before reaching for the blanket, wrapping herself in it as though to shut out the world. Her ears twitch for a moment, catching the sound of Vael's hammer slamming against his anvil, and then... silence. For a long while, she lies still, darting around with her eyes, taking in every little detail of the room, and then, she finally closes them, and the images go away, taking her dreams with them. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |WakeUpFromDream]]</div>\ <<set $dreamToRemove to "nyxa1">> <<set $possibleDreams to $possibleDreams.filter(function(dream) { return dream !== $dreamToRemove; })>>When the images finally coalesce, you find yourself in some sort of barren wasteland, carefully arranged sand stretching as far as the eye can see. Every boulder, every dead tree, every grain of sand... She has grown intimately familiar with them all. Yet, despite her obvious frustration, her eyes still shine with a glint of hope. She knows she will soon be free. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |WakeUpFromDream]]</div>\ <<set $dreamToRemove to "azri1">> <<set $possibleDreams to $possibleDreams.filter(function(dream) { return dream !== $dreamToRemove; })>> This time, your sleep is not as peaceful as you would have hoped. When you open your eyes, you are not in your bed, but rather, engulfed by an overwhelming darkness. You try to move, but your body seems to have been left behind, lost somewhere in the waking world. You are nothing but a mere thought here. As you float there, helpless and alone, the shifting pitch black surrounding you begins to take form, revealing a non-euclidian nightmare of angles and shapes. Dark and desolate, the obsidian dimension twists and spins in patterns that make your head hurt. <<if $eyeSeeing < 2>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Try to wake up |TryWakeUpNightmare1][$eyeSeeing +=1]]<div class="numberStat">1-</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedEye.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedCross.png">[[You can't wake up |TryWakeUpNightmare1][$eyeSeeing +=1]]<div class="numberStat">1-</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedEye.png"></div>@@\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Make your way towards one of the structures |MakeWayStructure][$eyeSeeing +=1]]</div>\ You try to focus your mind, gather your strength, and find a way out of this nightmarish realm. This is all just an illusion, a dream born of wooziness, and you force yourself to calm down despite the growing dread. <div class="passCheck">\ Despite the unnerving feeling that you are being observed. <img src="Images/Icons/Eye.png"></div>\ Slowly, you feel a change in the void around you, as if something is responding to your efforts. The shifting world coalesces into the same dark void you already know. Gradually, you feel your body begins to materialize, your limbs and senses returning to you. You hold on to the light and use it to pull yourself back to reality. @@.important;It has noticed you.@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |ContinueNightmareBase]]</div>\Desperate for some kind of direction or purpose, you fixate on one of the towering structures in the distance, trying to make your way toward it. As your consciousness swims through the void, you can feel a presence watching you from the darkness. <div class="passCheck">\ Finally, you reach one of the structures, a massive obsidian tower that seems to stretch on forever. As you stare up at it in awe, something stirs in the shadows around you. You try to concentrate on it, but there is nothing there. <img src="Images/Icons/Eye.png"></div>\ Suddenly, you jolt awake in your safe haven, drenched in sweat and gasping for air. The memory of the nightmare is still fresh in your mind, haunting you with its vivid and unsettling details. You shake your head, trying to clear your consciousness. @@.important;It has seen you.@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |ContinueNightmareBase]]</div>\ <<set $newNightmares to ["house", "thing", "maze"]>> <<set $possibleNightmares to $possibleNightmares.concat($newNightmares)>> <<set $nightmareToRemove to "base">> <<set $possibleNightmares to $possibleNightmares.filter(function(nightmare) { return nightmare !== $nightmareToRemove; })>>It has all been a nightmare, you are back in your bed and everything seems fine, if... a bit different. <<if $quickSleep is false>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |Bedroom]]</div>\ <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |PortalRoom]]</div>\ <</if>>\You awaken in your bed, blinking groggily at the familiar surroundings. At first, everything seems normal, just the quiet stillness of the night. But as you sit up, something feels… off. You glance around, your eyes tracing over your belongings, and an uneasy feeling settles in your chest. Your things... they're not where they should be. Your clothes and armor, once draped over the chair, now lie scattered on the floor. Your sword, which you left by your desk, now lies in a strange, unfamiliar position. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Inspect your room for any threats |InspectRoomThreats]]</div>\ <<set $nightmareToRemove to "house">> <<set $possibleNightmares to $possibleNightmares.filter(function(nightmare) { return nightmare !== $nightmareToRemove; })>>The discomfort grows. You frown, eyes narrowing as you look closer. The desk itself seems slightly out of place, as if it’s shifted a few inches to the side. The chair, too, doesn’t sit quite right. You tell yourself it’s nothing, maybe you’re just tired, but the nagging sensation lingers, creeping deeper into your thoughts. Then you notice the door. It’s… wrong. The door to your room is always directly opposite your bed. But now, it’s at an odd angle, as if someone moved it while you slept. Your heart starts to race as you realize it’s not just the angle—the doorframe is taller, narrower, and the wood seems darker, older. You could swear it wasn’t like that before... or was it? <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Pinch yourself to see if you are still dreaming |PinchYourself]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Make your way to the door |MakeWayDoor]]</div>\You could still be dreaming, so you pinch yourself, physical pain could wake you up. Pain spikes up your arm, a most definitely real pain. You seem to be awake, but this isn't the same room. <<if $magicSkill >= 3>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Close your eyes and will yourself to sleep |CloseEyesSleep]]<div class="numberStat">3</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedMagic.png"></div>\ <<else>>\ @@.fadedLink;<div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedCross.png">[[Close your eyes and will yourself to sleep |CloseEyesSleep]]<div class="numberStat">3</div><img src="Images/Icons/RedMagic.png"></div>@@\ <</if>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Make your way to the door |MakeWayDoor]]</div>\You decide you can’t just lie there. With a deep breath, you push yourself out of bed, the creak of the mattress unnervingly loud in the stillness of the room. Your feet hit the cold stone floor... the texture beneath your soles isn’t quite how you remember it; slightly too smooth, too polished. You ignore the feeling, its obvious that something is deeply wrong with this place, and instead, focus on reaching the door. Sure enough, the door opens and the rest of the house seems unchanged, at first sight at least. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Explore the house a little |MakeWayHunter]]</div>\ You shut your eyes tightly, forcing your mind to ignore the unsettling changes around you. Your pulse quickens as you try to calm yourself, focusing on the rhythm of your breathing, willing yourself to accept that this is just some twisted dream. If you can just fall back asleep, everything will be fine. You tell yourself to relax, to let your thoughts drift away, but the nagging sense of wrongness claws at your mind. The cold air clings to your skin, and despite your best efforts, your body refuses to loosen its grip on reality. Inhale... exhale. It takes a while, but you finally manage to calm yourself, and then you awaken. The room looks just as it did before. It is gone. For now. <<if $quickSleep is false>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |Bedroom]]</div>\ <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |PortalRoom]]</div>\ <</if>>\You decide to make your way to the Hunter's Hall, if anyone would know what's going on, it would be Orion or Nyxa. Grabbing your blade, you head towards the portal room. On your way there, everything seems perfectly fine... too fine. Every proportion and every angle seems exactly as it should be, as if a conscious effort had been made to ensure this place would look normal. To your surprise, the obsidian disk on the floor, the one that will carry you to the Hunter's Hall is already active, calling to you. @@.important;You just need to step in and you will immediately be safe...@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go to the Hunters Hall |MakeWayHunter2]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Maybe go somewhere else |MakeWayHunter3]]</div>\You step atop the disk, and instantly, the portal activates, pulling you in. Instantly you are flung into the void... but you don't appear where you should. Instead of the familiar sight of the Hunter's Hull greeting you, you find yourself in a dark, twisted place as before. The obsidian dimension stretches endlessly around you, a cold, empty expanse of strange, non-Euclidean shapes, twisting into patterns that defy logic. Jagged angles stretch in every direction, spiraling and folding upon themselves, warping your perception. It feels oppressive, suffocating. Ahead, barely distinguishable from the pitch-black surroundings, stands a figure. Its form is hidden in shadows, draped in darkness so thick you can barely make out any details, save for the faint outline of a somewhat feminine silhouette. Something about her feels ancient, powerful, and deeply, unnervingly wrong, a hatred that has persisted through the ages. You can’t move. Your legs feel like they’ve been frozen in place, every muscle locked in fear. The figure turns slowly, her movements smooth, almost hypnotic. Then her eyes, glowing faintly through the shadows, meet yours. A wave of overwhelming dread washes over you. You try to look away, but you can't—your gaze is trapped in hers. For a brief moment, everything stops. Time, your breath, your thoughts. All you can feel is an indescribable, soul-crushing terror as she looks through you. <img src="Images/Monsters/Watcher2.png" class="resizable-image" alt="Shard"> And then... darkness. This is her domain, and you, her prey. <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Eternal, dreamless sleep...">> <<goto "Death">> <</link>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png" >\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedEye.png" >\ </div>\... ... Are you sure? The portal will take you with your fellow hunters, who better to help you... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go to the Hunters Hall |MakeWayHunter2]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go to the Hunters Hall |MakeWayHunter2]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go to the Hunters Hall |MakeWayHunter2]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Do not step through the portal |MakeWayHunter4]]</div>\Something feels awfully wrong, and you have the feeling that, should you enter that portal, you will never return. You turn back, and head the other way. The rest of the house feels a lot more hostile now that you have stepped away from the portal, but before you can even make your way downstairs, the illusion already starts to dissipate, and the next thing you know, you are lying in your bed. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |ContinueNightmareBase]]</div>\You are jolted awake by a sudden chill running down your spine. Your eyes snap open and you instinctively scan the room for danger, your mind racing. The shadows seem deeper, darker. You feel watched. Slowly, as your vision adjusts to the gloom, your eyes are drawn to a corner of the room. At first, you see nothing. Just shadows. But as your gaze lingers, you notice a figure, hunched over in the corner. It’s tall, unnaturally so, its form bent and twisted as if too large to fit properly in the space. It doesn’t move, doesn’t make a sound—just watches you with eyes that glint faintly in the dark. Your heart pounds in your chest as you blink, hoping it’s just a trick of the light. But no, it’s still there. The longer you stare, the more defined its shape becomes. There’s something horribly wrong about the way it crouches, as though it’s waiting for something. And it's smiling. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Get the fuck out |GetOutNightmare]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Close your eyes and will it away |WillAwayNightmare]]</div>\ <<set $nightmareToRemove to "thing">> <<set $possibleNightmares to $possibleNightmares.filter(function(nightmare) { return nightmare !== $nightmareToRemove; })>>You get up and sprint towards the door, grabbing your sword as you rush past it, but the figure does not chase after you. Instead, it just... keeps.... smiling, unmoving. You make your way downstairs, and wonder just what exactly you are going to do, what kind of nightmarish entity just appeared in your room. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Get ready to fight it |FightNightmare]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\You have been trained in the ways of the hunter, you are one. It is not your first time dealing with dangerous monsters. You will stand and face it, even if this one does not seem quite the same. However, by the time you have made your way to your room... The creature is nowhere to be seen. The shadows are no longer as deep, the air no longer as cold. You stand still, gripping the hilt of your sword, but the threat is gone. @@.playerSpeech;"The fucking... fuck was that... Did I really... I mean, what the fuck." @@ Was it a nightmare? You are not quite sure. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Wander around the house, keep an eye out for threats |WanderKeepEye]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedGeneral.png"></div>\You decide it's better to just wander around the house, keeping an eye out for threats, until you feel it's safe enough to go back to bed. You check everywhere, but you don't find anything out of the ordinary. Eventually, after a long while of checking and double-checking, you decide that it's safe and return to bed. The rest of the night is peaceful, but the memory of the monster remains. <<if $quickSleep is false>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |Bedroom]]</div>\ <<else>>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |PortalRoom]]</div>\ <</if>>\It's not real, it's but a trick of the mind. You force yourself to ignore it, closing your eyes and willing it away, telling yourself to calm down. It was not just a trick of the mind. You fall asleep to never wake again. <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Eternal, dreamless sleep...">> <<goto "Death">> <</link>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png" >\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedEye.png" >\ </div>\You have to bring Vael the bad news, the creature he sent you after is most likely dead. @@.playerSpeech;"The travelstone carried me to a smoldering ruin, no sign of any kind of life."@@ The Forgemaster looks upset, but not as much as you expected him to. @@.vaelSpeech;"Impossible, this creature cannot die... perhaps the travelstone was not calibrated correctly..."@@ He goes on a rant about how his travelstones are almost always perfectly calibrated, but eventually agrees that the travelstone could indeed, not be set correctly. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |VaelIntroReduced]]</div>\ <<set $sixEightJobState to "completed">> <<set $dissapointedVael to true>>You have to bring Vael the bad news, and tell him you were forced to run away from the creature. @@.playerSpeech;"I wasn't expecting a ten-foot-tall abomination. I'm sorry but it was way beyond my abilities, it could have killed me!"@@ The Forgemaster looks angry, but it quickly gives way to cold disappointment. @@.vaelSpeech;"I see. I'll be more careful choosing my agents in the future."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |VaelIntroReduced]]</div>\ <<set $sixEightJobState to "completed">>A sample of flesh from the regenerating monstrosity. Vael will gladly take it off your hands... [[Go back |Inventory]]Install this item in your bedroom to reduce nightmare chance by 30% [[Go back |Inventory]]A smooth, warm stone of unknown origin. Having it in your inventory will increase dream chance by 20% [[Go back |Inventory]]<<if $pendant is 1>> [[Put the pendant on |PutOn][$eyeSeeing +=1]] <<elseif $pendant is 2>> [[Take the pendant off |PutOff][$eyeSeeing -=1]] <</if>> [[Go back |Inventory]]You put the pendant on, its weight a reassuring presence around your neck. Its magical aura is a bit unnerving, but also somehow familiar. <div class="passCheck">\ <img src="Images/Icons/Eye.png"></div>\ <<set $pendant to 2>> [[Go back |Inventory]]You remove the pendant, its strange aura leaving with it. <<set $pendant to 1>> <div class="failCheck">\ <img src="Images/Icons/Eye.png"></div>\ [[Go back |Inventory]]You pay him and take the item, you should put it in your bedroom if you wish to use it. <<set $dreamcatcher to 1>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |PurchaseWares]]</div>\You pay him and take the item, you should equip it from your inventory if you wish to use it. <<set $pendant to 1>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |PurchaseWares]]</div>\You purchase the small piece of jewelry and store it in your pocket. <<set $dreamstone to 1>> <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue |PurchaseWares][$baseDreamChance += $dreamstoneChance]]</div>\A mysterious little trinket... can be used to gain additional points at character creation, and it persists through death. [[Go back |Inventory]]The small dagger squirms and wriggles against your palm, seeking to free itself, but the iron grip of your fingers only tightens. You feel its heart beating frantically as you hold it in front of your face, a tiny, helpless creature, trapped by you, a mortal... [[Go back |Inventory]]<<nobr>> <div class="header-container"> <div class="middle-section">SCP-682</div> </div> <div class="long-container"> <div class="left-long-container-2"> <<if $sixEightJobState != "">> <img src="Images/Monsters/682Fixed.png" alt="IMG"> <<else>> <img src="Images/Monsters/682Dark.png" alt="IMG"> <</if>> </div> <div class="right-long-container-2"> @@.titleSmall;STATUS@@ <br><br> <<switch $sixEightJobState >> <<case "">> <<if $eyeSeeing >=2>> <div class="passCheck"> You can get this contract from Vael, after succesfully bringing him the feragon. <img src="Images/Icons/Eye.png"></div> <<else>> <div class="failCheck"> You have not yet found this creature, or anything related to her. <img src="Images/Icons/FailEye.png"></div> <</if>> <<case "accepted">> You have accepted this contract, and you have the travelstone to make the trip. You may warp to her world from the portal room in your home. <<case "escaped">> You ran away from the creature, and didn't manage to get a sample. <<case "dead">> The creature must be dead, nothing should be able to surivive such devastation... <<case "completed">> The questline is over. <<case "small">> You managed to get a small sample of the creature, should be enough for Vael... <<case "medium">> You managed to get a big sample of the creature, Vael will be satisfied. <<case "big">> You managed to get a big sample of the creature, Vael will be satisfied. <<default>> This should be impossible, what kind of disgusting spagetti code brought us here... <</switch>> </div> </div> <div class = "close-Inventory"> <<link "Go Back">> <div class="stat-container"> Go back </div> <<goto Contracts>> <</link>> </div> <</nobr>><<nobr>> <div class="header-container"> <div class="middle-section">Oblivion</div> </div> <div class="long-container"> <div class="left-long-container-2"> <<if $exalted == 1>> <img src="Images/Monsters/Death.png" alt="IMG"> <<else>> <img src="Images/Monsters/DeathDark.png" alt="IMG"> <</if>> </div> <div class="right-long-container-2"> @@.titleSmall;STATUS@@ <br><br> <<switch $exalted >> <<case 0>> <<if $eyeSeeing >=2>> <div class="passCheck"> The younger of the three sisters of death, claim enough souls and then die, she may be interested in you... <img src="Images/Icons/Eye.png"></div> <<else>> <div class="failCheck"> You have not yet found this creature, or anything related to her. <img src="Images/Icons/FailEye.png"></div> <</if>> <<case 1>> To bring death to the deathless is the greatest act of devotion... <<default>> <</switch>> </div> </div> <div class = "close-Inventory"> <<link "Go Back">> <div class="stat-container"> Go back </div> <<goto Contracts>> <</link>> </div> <</nobr>>You don't really care how she got here, or why. The fact that she is here, is enough reason for you to end her life... You let go for a moment, standing up to be able to properly swing your sword. She thanks you as you let go, assuming the interrogation is over and her words were deemed adequate. She tries to stand on her four feet with what little energy your weapon hasn't drained off her, but before can, you swing the blade, slicing through the air with a sharp, whistling sound, and ending her life in a single cut. <div class="passCheck">\ Her body falls back down on the floor, motionless, the crimson staining the gray sand beneath. <img src="Images/Icons/Death.png"></div>\ <<set $playerKills to $temporaryKills +1 >>\ <<set $killedYuumi to true>>\ Without any fanfare, you take her corpse by the tail and drag her towards the edge, throwing it into the infinite depths below. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Go back to sleep |SleepYesYuumi3]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedSleep.png"></div>\Perhaps this creature is innocent, perhaps she is not. It doesn't matter, for you have the duty to end her... if not for your order, then for the goddess of death. <div class="passCheck">\ Without giving her proper time to realize what's about to happen, you sink your sword deep into her hardened skull and end her life. <img src="Images/Icons/Devotion.png"></div>\ <<set $playerKills to $temporaryKills +1 >>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Get ready to return |ReturnDeathclaw3]]<img src="Images/Icons/RedTeleport.png"></div>\You have spilled plenty of blood in her name, but now is the time for the greatest sacrifice, a willing death. You kneel and pray, begging her to accept your sacrifice. She doesn't answer, but her presence fills you, and you can feel her approval. With a deep breath, you draw your blade and slit your throat, collapsing to the ground. The pain is not as bad as you thought it would be, and the more blood spills from the wound, the less you can feel. Your vision blurs and fades, and then... <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "Death embraces you...">> <<goto "Death">> <</link>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png" >\ </div>\When the images finally come into focus, you find yourself in a cave, the wind raging outside as rain pelts the entrance. A massive creature, her scales shimmering like sheets of polished steel, her horns curved and sharp, stands before a much smaller one, her daughter. <img src="Images/Monsters/FeragonFixed.png" class="resizable-image" alt="Dream Image"> @@.feragonSpeech;"But mom... I promise it's safe! It's a wreck, who would suspect anything? I'm sure I can find some tasty gold there, no one will see me"@@ The younger feragon's eyes gleam with excitement, her claws digging impatiently into the rocky floor. The mother, however, stands her ground, her massive form blocking the cave's exit. Her voice is deep, steady, and filled with concern. @@.feragonSpeech;"No. And it's final. It doesn't matter if the ship is a wreck or not, humans are way too dangerous. If they notice as little as a single scale of yours, a clawprint, they will jump to conclusions, they will track you down. They’re relentless. They don't need a reason to kill us, just an excuse."@@ The mother leans down, her voice softening just a little, but still firm. @@.feragonSpeech;"I know you're fast, but it's a risk not worth taking. Promise me you will not go looking for that wreck. Promise me."@@ The young feragon's excitement fades as she looks up into her mother's serious eyes. She opens her mouth to protest again, but the weight of her mother's words presses down on her. She exhales sharply, her claws still twitching with frustration. @@.feragonSpeech;"...Fine, I promise. I won't go."@@ That seems to be enough for the older feragon. She smiles and nods, gently rubbing her snout against her daughter's. @@.feragonSpeech;"Thank you. I will be back before you even notice, but stay in the cave. The war against the hellborn has humans particularly jumpy... we'll get through this."@@ The visions begin to fade, the darkness swallowing them whole. <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Continue... |WakeUpFromDream]]</div>\ <<set $dreamToRemove to "feragon1">> <<set $possibleDreams to $possibleDreams.filter(function(dream) { return dream !== $dreamToRemove; })>>An amalgamation of humanoid remains, twisted and piled high in a macabre monument. The titanic creature is both awe-inspiring and horrifying, its form more shadow than substance, a formless silhouette given terrifying detail only by the deep hollows where its eyes would be. At times, its form flickers, shifting from a mass of dark mist to something more skeletal, its bony limbs protruding through the ephemeral shadows. Sensing your gaze, it slowly rises from its seat, its form expanding and growing, stretching to completely fill the horizon. Then, she assumes a more comprehensible form, bones from the throne forming a somewhat humanoid body. <img src="Images/Monsters/Death.png" class="resizable-image" alt="682"> In this new form, the dark goddess is no less imposing, still a being of immeasurable power, but now she appears more... familiar, more relatable. Whatever that means, considering you are looking at a primordial, eldritch being. @@.oblivionSpeech;"How wonderful, been a while since I last got a soul as devout as yours. The Watcher had her eye on you, didn't she? Let her rot for another thousand years, her blood claim means nothing here."@@ Her voice is a chorus of whispers, yet somehow, you understand them perfectly. She however is not the calm, self-assured deity you expected, instead, her words betray a hint of excitement. She continues speaking. @@.oblivionSpeech;"Your gorgeous skull will be a great addition to my throne, but..."@@ She shifts slightly, her movements both graceful and slightly awkward, as if she's reacquainting herself with the act of addressing another sentient being... instead of simply feasting on their souls. @@.oblivionSpeech;"But the way you handled that... swine, it was magnificent. A masterful performance, the mighty, eternal Wyrm of Ruin brought low and bred by a lowly, mortal hunter."@@ Oblivion's tone becomes increasingly enthusiastic, a giddy smile spreading across her ever-shifting face. @@.oblivionSpeech;"I think you have at least earned a little bit of a reward, a final boon before the end. What would you wish for, dear hunter?"@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[You would rather not die |RatherNotDie]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Lewd until the very end: You wish to have some fun with her |FuckDarkLady]]</div>\The reward part sounds tempting, but the 'final boon' part leaves a bitter taste in your mouth. You tell her that, if possible, you would rather keep on living. She laughs, a cacophony of whispers echoing across the endless plains. @@.oblivionSpeech;"I do very much enjoy your kind... always so amusing."@@ The dark goddess raises a skeletal arm, pointing into the distance. @@.oblivionSpeech;"Look around. You stand in the grave of gods, the festering, bleeding fracture in the fabric of reality that is the Phantom Wastes."@@ Her gaze is intense, almost predatory, as she continues her rant. @@.oblivionSpeech;"Mine is but a shard, a tiny piece of the whole... but this domain still contains enough noxious power to wither even the most stalwart of souls. Now, granted... you are not only human, but the blood of the now corpse-god is not enough to resist such a place. The death I offer you will be a blissful one, far more pleasant than the slow, agonizing rot you will be subjected to here. Pure, utter oblivion..."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[The blood of a god runs through your veins? |BloodGod]]</div>\The comment raises a lot of questions, about the nature of this place, and about your very own being. @@.playerSpeech;"What... what do you mean?"@@ Oblivion stares at you, tilting her head slightly to the side, as if trying to figure out whether you're messing with her or if you're just incredibly stupid. @@.oblivionSpeech;"What puzzles you, exactly?"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"The blood of the corpse-god part, I'm pretty sure I don't have any gods in my family."@@ The creature stares at you for a moment, before letting out a short chuckle. @@.oblivionSpeech;"You never noticed? Why do you think Xa'Rdrath has always had such a particular interest in you?"@@ Who... is even that? You are sure you have heard this name before, but your memory in this place is blurry at best. Indeed, this place seems to drain the very soul. Should not be that interested in you, if you cant even put a face to the name. @@.oblivionSpeech;"I couldn't have manifested to you like I did, had she not gifted you some of her essence. Your body would have fallen apart instantly."@@ You nod, the information slowly sinking in. @@.playerSpeech;"Well... Im not sure it matters much now, right?"@@ @@.oblivionSpeech;"No, it doesn't... now that you are mine~"@@ Seeing your expression, the Dark Lady's form flickers with a mixture of dark amusement and a touch of genuine regret. @@.oblivionSpeech;"Ah, don’t look so despondent. I assure you, death at my hands is far more pleasant than what awaited for you had she managed to take hold of your soul. Here you shall have a silent, tranquil end. My realm will be your eternal rest."@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Accept your fate |AcceptFate]]</div>\You have already killed yourself, without any promise of a reward of afterlife. What saddens you is not having been aware of your potential until now that the end is near. ... @@.playerSpeech;"I am ready... for whatever is to come."@@ Oblivion's form ripples and shifts, her skeletal face giving away no emotion, but the whispers are tinged with satisfaction. @@.oblivionSpeech;"Good. Rest then, my champion... until the next life."@@ Her hand gently strokes your cheek, her touch cool and gentle, a soothing sensation, a last moment of peace. There is no pain, no suffering, as your body dissolves and blends into the endless wastes. Your mind drifts away, into the eternal darkness. <div class="linkIcons"> <img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png"><<link "A silent, peaceful death">> <<goto "Ending7">> <</link>>\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png" >\ <img src="Images/Icons/RedSkull.png" >\ </div>\All you did, you did for her, and now you want her to grant you this final request. You look at her, eyes full of longing. @@.playerSpeech;"You are very powerful and beautiful. I would like to know what you feel like... before you take me."@@ The Dark Lady’s form flickers, a mix of confusion and curiosity playing across her skeletal features. Her gaze narrows slightly, as if trying to fully comprehend your request. Finally, she speaks, her tone tinged with intrigue. @@.oblivionSpeech;"I have never been asked such a thing before... but perhaps I can indulge you."@@ The brutal mating session with the loathed reptile is still fresh in her mind, it will for sure be an interesting experience... but of course, there are some complications. @@.oblivionSpeech;"This current form is, however... well, rather limiting in terms of physical interaction. I could... shift into something else, perhaps?"@@ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[This form is fine |FormFine]]</div>\@@.playerSpeech;"No need for that, I find your form intriguing and appealing."@@ Oblivion stares down at you, her expression unreadable, her skeletal form flickering slightly as bones twist and move, forming an approximation of a smile. @@.oblivionSpeech;"So shall it be. Though, if I may ask, why is it you desire this so? Is my visage truly so appealing to you?"@@ @@.playerSpeech;"You are very different, powerful, otherworldly, and yet you are willing to spend time with me, a mortal. It's quite flattering."@@ She nods, her expression thoughtful. @@.oblivionSpeech;"Mortal, and yet so much more... I can see why she would take an interest in you."@@ With gentle, fluid movements, she sits besides you, carrying you with her so that you end up sitting on her lap, her bony arms holding you close. She feels cold, but not unpleasant... its as if her very presence is a paradox, a blend of chilling detachment and eerie comfort. Your head rests against her ribcage, the bones surprisingly polished and smooth. Her touch is light, but her movements are deliberate, methodical. Her hands run along your sides, caressing your skin, leaving goosebumps in their wake, and eventually, one of them reaches your naked crotch, rubbing your already semi-erect member. Her movements are slow and gentle, her skeletal fingers wrapped around your length. They are so polished, almost as if they are made of glass, and her touch is surprisingly tender. Soon enough, you are completely hard, your member throbbing against her cold, smooth fingers. It is such a bizarre, almost surreal experience... sha has not even started to move her hand, yet the simple act of being held like this, of her delicate, skeletal fingers touching you, is almost enough to send you over the edge. @@.oblivionSpeech;"It is fascinating, so pliable, so... reactive, the flesh. It can be molded, shaped, bent... an ever-changing, dynamic canvas, in which life blossoms and withers."@@ Her grip tightens, and she starts moving her hand, stroking your length with a steady rhythm, her bony digits wrapped around you. From the base all the way to the tip, again and again, her smooth fingers gliding over your sensitive flesh, sending jolts of pleasure through your body. @@.oblivionSpeech;"It is but an empty vessel, a container for the true treasure within... a temporary shackle that holds the spirit captive. And yet I must give credit, such a wondrous work of art..."@@ You are not even sure what she is talking about, most of your focus is on the forbidden pleasure that comes from the Dark Lady's touch. She may only be stroking your member, but the feeling is intense, overwhelming... <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Reach to her, touch her |ReachTouch]]</div>\ <div class="linkIcons"><img src="Images/Icons/RedArrow.png">[[Let her be in control |RemainControl]]</div>\You don't want to simply stay there and receive her touch, you want to feel her as well. It is here, at the end of all things, that you meet your final fate. Your true physical body has already been left behind, and now, all that is left is the soul, the essence of what you truly are. Here, under the gaze of the Dark Lady, it shall truly fade, its last echoes disappearing in the winds of the wastes, safe from she who would take it.
